items.title: What-can-I-do gallery — concrete workflows instead of a blank agent prompt
items.summary: Transcript extraction 2026-07-09: Vanta is technically deep, but the user-facing gap is discovery — “right now it works but i dont know what i can do with it.” Build a first-run and on-demand capability gallery that turns installed tools, repo context, and the ND/operator positioning into concrete workflows: fix a pasted error, turn a long spec into an app, continue a roadmap item, mine transcripts into memory, research with receipts, watch a repo, and prepare/share a feedback ask.
items.done: `/what-can-i-do` and the empty-state dashboard show 8 concrete, runnable workflows with Run/Try/Setup states based on actual installed capabilities; at least three workflows execute an end-to-end demo fixture and link to the exact command they will run; a fresh-workspace activation run reaches one useful result in under 2 minutes.
items.notes:
Evidence: 62230797 transcript lines 76-89 and 330-336 frame Vanta as an ND/operator agent but expose the discovery gap; e91d797b and 1522ad63 provide concrete showcase workflows.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 1 activation lead. This is the first build because it turns Vanta from a powerful blank prompt into a menu of concrete successful actions. Success metric: a new user reaches one useful workflow in under 2 minutes.

BLIND-AUDIT UPDATE 2026-07-09: Done criteria now include the cold-user activation proof from ACTIVATION-COLD-USER-GATE, so the card cannot ship as a pretty menu without a measured first useful action.

BASE SLICE 2026-07-09: Added `/what-can-i-do` backed by a pure eight-workflow catalog with Run/Try/Setup states from the live tool registry. Focused tests cover the workflow count, user-language titles, exact commands, and missing setup gaps; live dispatcher check produced 8 Run workflows on this install.

EMPTY-STATE SLICE 2026-07-09: `/dashboard` now turns the no-tasks/no-goals/clean-repo state into the same workflow gallery instead of a dead-end all-clear message. Dashboard tests cover the activation empty state.

DEMO SLICE 2026-07-09: Added `/what-can-i-do --demo <id>` for three linked demo fixtures: fix-error, continue-roadmap, and crash-log. Focused tests and a live dispatcher call execute the demo path and show exact commands.

COLD-CHECK SLICE 2026-07-09: Added `/what-can-i-do --check`, which selects a visible runnable workflow demo, executes it, and reports time-to-first-useful-action. Live dispatcher run passed in 1ms on this install.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added `vanta what-can-i-do --fresh-workspace-check` to create fresh temporary workspace/home context, run the cold activation check, and write markdown evidence under `.vanta/activation-reviews/`. Live proof recorded `fresh-workspace-2026-07-09T12-42-40-967Z.md`: PASS, picked `Fix a pasted error`, time-to-first-useful-action 0ms. This satisfies the gallery's fresh-workspace activation criterion; the separate FRESH-CONTEXT-ACTIVATION-REVIEW card remains open for an outside reviewer/user confusion point.

items.title: Sandbox scope wizard — recover from wrong-root and background refusal errors
items.summary: The Handy transcript shows the recurring failure mode: the task target is outside the writable scope and background tasks are refused under sandbox, so the agent burns time and explains instead of fixing. Existing sandbox fast-fail is useful; this card adds a guided recovery flow that detects the missing root and emits or applies the exact relaunch/config change.
items.done: When a command fails because the target repo/root is outside Vanta’s writable/readable scope or because a background task requires an unsandboxed run, Vanta identifies the missing root, shows the exact safe relaunch/config command, can retry after confirmation, and one gallery workflow proves this recovery path end-to-end.
items.notes:
Evidence: 62230797 lines 264-311 and 739fb919 lines 93-140 show bun install/background dev blocked by writable scope and sandboxed background refusal. Builds on shipped SANDBOX-SERVE-FASTFAIL/VANTA-SANDBOX-VIOLATION.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 1 reliability unblocker. Build alongside the gallery because wrong-root/background sandbox failures stop the first useful workflow before the user learns what Vanta can do.

BLIND-AUDIT UPDATE 2026-07-09: Done criteria now require a gallery workflow fixture that hits and recovers from wrong-root/background sandbox failure.

CORE SLICE 2026-07-09: Added shared sandbox recovery guidance for readable/writable scope refusals, sandboxed background refusals, and sandboxed dev-server fast-fail. Focused tests cover exact /add-dir, VANTA_*_DIRS, VANTA_SHELL_SANDBOX=0 relaunch, and background:true retry copy.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Readable and writable out-of-zone refusals now name exact recovery commands (`/add-dir`, VANTA_READABLE_DIRS/VANTA_WRITABLE_DIRS relaunch, and wrong-root `cd ... && vanta`). Approval tests prove out-of-zone read/write paths add the directory to the active session and proceed, while declined approval preserves the refusal. Shell tests prove sandboxed background/dev-server refusals emit the VANTA_SHELL_SANDBOX=0 relaunch and background:true retry. The shipped gallery sandbox fixture links this recovery path from `/what-can-i-do --demo fix-error`.

items.title: Cold-user activation gate — prove a useful Vanta action in under 2 minutes
items.summary: Blind audit finding #1: the roadmap says activation is the goal, but the done criteria do not yet require a cold user to actually activate. Add a blocking acceptance gate for the first-run path: fresh workspace, no repo-specific context, pick one suggested workflow, execute it, and get a useful result quickly. This is the measurable Activation v1 spine before chasing more Hermes/OpenClaw surface area.
items.done: A fresh-workspace acceptance script or recorded cold run starts Vanta, chooses one visible workflow, executes it, and produces a useful result within 2 minutes; the run records time-to-first-useful-action and fails if the user must know internal command names first.
items.notes:
From blind audit finding #1. Prioritize by HEART Task Success/Activation: this is the falsifiable proof that the roadmap is improving user activation, not just moving cards.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added `vanta what-can-i-do [--check|--demo <id>]` as a non-TUI activation surface backed by the same workflow catalog and live tool registry. `--check` selects a visible runnable workflow demo, prints `Cold activation check: PASS`, records time-to-first-useful-action, and exits nonzero if no runnable demo is visible. Verified live with `npm run vanta -- what-can-i-do --check`, which picked `Fix a pasted error` and completed in 0ms. Boundary: this is a CLI cold-activation proof, not the separate fresh-context review required before the broader WHAT-CAN-I-DO-GALLERY ships.

items.title: Gallery sandbox recovery fixture — first workflow survives wrong-root/background errors
items.summary: Blind audit finding #2: the what-can-I-do gallery and sandbox scope wizard can ship separately without proving the first useful workflow survives the exact wrong-root/background-refusal failure path. Make that failure path part of the activation flow.
items.done: At least one gallery workflow intentionally starts from an out-of-scope repo or sandboxed background-server command, receives sandbox scope guidance, and either resumes or emits the exact safe restart command; the fixture is covered by a test and linked from the workflow demo.
items.notes:
From blind audit finding #2. This keeps the activation path honest by testing the failure that already confused the user.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09. The `fix-error` gallery demo now names the exact sandboxed background-server fixture (`python3 -m http.server 8123` with background:true under VANTA_SHELL_SANDBOX=1). `what-can-i-do-cmd.test.ts` executes the real `shell_cmd` path, observes the shell-sandbox refusal, and asserts the safe restart command (`VANTA_SHELL_SANDBOX=0 vanta`) plus background:true retry guidance. The fixture is linked from `/what-can-i-do --demo fix-error`.

items.title: User-language workflow copy — name outcomes, not internal Vanta systems
items.summary: Blind audit finding #5: activation-facing cards still use internal language like trust ledger, sentinels, vault compile, and research decomposition. Translate the first-run surface into plain outcomes that match why someone would use Vanta: fix an error, build an app from a spec, remember transcript lessons, watch this repo, draft the next move.
items.done: Every activation/gallery workflow has a user-facing title, one-sentence outcome, required setup state, and example input; no first-run label requires knowing Vanta internals such as brain/vault/ledger/sentinel/research_decompose.
items.notes:
From blind audit finding #5. This is the copy layer that lets the Hermes/OpenClaw-style views read as user capability, not architecture.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: `/what-can-i-do` now gives every workflow a plain-language `Needs:` line instead of exposing raw missing tool identifiers such as research_decompose, brain, cron_create, or send_chat. The helper workflow was renamed from agent-first language to `Send work to a helper`. Tests require every workflow to have a title, outcome, example, command, and setup copy; they also assert the rendered gallery does not leak internal setup names. Boundary: this was verified through catalog/formatter/slash-handler tests, not an interactive TUI capture.

items.title: Fresh-context activation review — test the first screen with someone who lacks repo context
items.summary: Blind audit finding #6: the roadmap relies on the same authoring loop to decide what is useful. Add an outside-feedback gate before shipping the activation gallery: a fresh model/session or real user sees the first screen cold and attempts one workflow.
items.done: A fresh-context reviewer or user attempts the activation gallery without prior Vanta repo context; the first confusion point is recorded; blocking confusion is fixed before `WHAT-CAN-I-DO-GALLERY` ships.
items.notes:
From blind audit finding #6. This shrinks the Johari blind quadrant around whether the first-run surface actually lands.

HARNESS SLICE 2026-07-09: Added `vanta what-can-i-do --review-packet` and `/what-can-i-do --review-packet` to print a self-contained cold-review script for someone with no Vanta repo context. Added `--record-review <first confusion point>` on both CLI and slash surfaces, writing markdown evidence under `.vanta/activation-reviews/` with reviewer, timestamp, confusion text, and blocking-fix guidance. Verified with CLI and slash tests for packet generation and evidence-file recording.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added `vanta what-can-i-do --fresh-context-review` and `/what-can-i-do --fresh-context-review`, which run the visible gallery/check path from the reviewer's stance and record the first confusion point as markdown evidence. Live evidence file `fresh-context-2026-07-09T13-11-48-439Z.md` recorded reviewer `fresh-context-cli`, workflow `fix-error`, attempted command `vanta run "Fix this error: <paste the error>"`, `Blocking: no`, and first confusion point `No blocking confusion: the visible gallery exposed "Fix a pasted error" and the check reached a useful result.`

items.title: Roadmap dependency guard — `after` links block premature moves to Now
items.summary: Blind audit finding #3: roadmap `after` links are currently advisory metadata, so the board can still bypass its own sequence. Enforce the sequence mechanically before more activation/autonomy cards are moved around.
items.done: `vanta roadmap move <id> building` and the drag-and-drop board warn or block when any open `after` dependency is not shipped; tests cover allow, block, and override/force behavior if an override exists.
items.notes:
From blind audit finding #3. This protects the activation sequence from turning back into an aesthetic board shuffle.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: `moveRoadmapItem` now blocks moves to `building` when `after` dependencies are missing or not shipped. The CLI exposes an explicit `--force` override, and the roadmap board API accepts `force: true`; otherwise board moves return 409 with a dependency payload. Tests cover block, allow-after-ship, force override, and board/API conflict behavior.

items.title: Roadmap blocked status — external gates stop consuming Now slots
items.summary: Adds a visible Blocked column/status for cards waiting on hardware, credentials, releases, or other external proof. Blocked cards remain visible in Launch Pad and the board, but do not count against the WIP-limited Build Now slots.
items.done: `blocked` is a valid roadmap status, renders as its own board/Launch Pad state, can be set via `vanta roadmap move <id> blocked`, and does not count toward Now WIP.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: STATUS includes blocked; roadmap render/CSS shows a Blocked column and Launch Pad badge; CLI usage includes blocked; WIP tests assert blocked does not consume Now slots; BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE and RUN-ANYWHERE-TERMUX moved from building to blocked because their remaining gates require real Telegram/Modal credentials, a tagged release, and physical ARM64 Android hardware.

items.title: Roadmap unblock command — turn blocked cards into exact next actions
items.summary: Blocked and parked cards no longer strand the operator. `vanta roadmap unblock` lists each blocked, parked, or decision-only roadmap item with the concrete command, credential, device, release, revive, or ratification step needed to unblock it; `--json` exposes the same plans to agents and scripts. `vanta roadmap status --json --require-drained` gives scripts a structured hard gate for the drained-active-queue state, parked cards cannot move straight to shipped, parked revivals require explicit `--force`, and known Run Anywhere proof cards cannot be marked shipped until their durable receipts exist.
items.done: `vanta roadmap unblock` prints actionable unblock steps for blocked cards, parked cards, and decision-only horizon cards without moving statuses or claiming external proofs passed; `vanta roadmap unblock --json` emits the same structured plan objects; `vanta roadmap status --json` emits total, activeTotal, activeDrained, status counts, and parked reason counts; `vanta roadmap status --require-drained` exits nonzero while any building/blocked/next/horizon work remains and exits zero when only shipped/parked work remains; moving a parked card to shipped is always refused until it is revived into active work first; moving a parked card to an active non-shipped status is refused until the operator reviews `vanta roadmap unblock <id>` and retries with `--force`; moving BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE, MSG-ADAPTER-TEAMS, RUN-ANYWHERE-TERMUX, or RUN-ANYWHERE-V1-RELEASE-GATE to shipped is refused until the corresponding Run Anywhere readiness receipt exists.
items.notes:
SHIPPED 2026-07-10: added `vanta roadmap unblock [id...]`. Follow-up 2026-07-10: include parked cards too, because the Run Anywhere gates are deliberately parked for later external proof rather than marked blocked. The command reads roadmap.json, reports blocked/parked/decision cards in useful order, orders direct Run Anywhere dependencies before the aggregate release gate, and prints concrete next actions for Modal/Telegram, Teams/Azure, Termux release hardware proof, the remaining external provider and device proofs. It does not move cards or claim external proofs passed. Focused proof: npx vitest run src/roadmap/unblock.test.ts src/roadmap/now-queue.test.ts src/cli/roadmap-cmd.test.ts. Live proof: npm run vanta -- roadmap unblock BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE MSG-ADAPTER-TEAMS RUN-ANYWHERE-TERMUX RUN-ANYWHERE-V1-RELEASE-GATE prints all four parked launch unblock paths.

DRAIN GATE 2026-07-10: added `vanta roadmap status --require-drained` so the no-active-work state is machine-checkable. It returns exit 1 if any building/blocked/next/horizon card remains and exit 0 when remaining non-shipped cards are parked. Live proof against the current roadmap returns `active roadmap drained: yes` with building/blocked/next/horizon all 0 and parked 18.

JSON PLANS 2026-07-10: added `vanta roadmap unblock --json` so agent workflows can consume the same unblock plan objects without scraping prose. Live proof: `npm run vanta -- roadmap unblock BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE --json` returns one structured plan with status `parked`, parkedReason `external proof`, and the gateway deploy/register/prove actions.

JSON STATUS 2026-07-10: `vanta roadmap status --json` now emits `total`, `activeTotal`, `activeDrained`, `statuses`, and `parkedReasons` instead of requiring scripts to scrape prose. `--json --require-drained` preserves the hard exit gate while returning the same structured object. Live proof reports activeTotal 0, activeDrained true, shipped 1121, parked 18, and parked reason counts for external proof, strategy decision, declined/n-a, duplicate, and optional proof.

PARKED REVIVE GUARD 2026-07-10: `vanta roadmap move <parked-id> <active-status>` and the drag board now refuse to revive parked cards unless `--force`/force is explicit. The error preserves the parked reason and points to `vanta roadmap unblock <id>` first. Live proof: `vanta roadmap move BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE building` exits 1 with the external-proof revive warning and leaves the roadmap unchanged.

SHIP PROOF GUARD 2026-07-10: known Run Anywhere proof cards cannot be moved to shipped until their readiness receipts exist. Live proof: `vanta roadmap move BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE shipped --force` now exits 1 with `.vanta/serverless-gateway.json` as the required receipt, and the roadmap remains drained with BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE parked.

DIRECT SHIP GUARD 2026-07-10: no parked card can be moved directly to shipped, even with `--force`; it must first be revived into active work after reviewing `vanta roadmap unblock <id>`. The guard applies generically to future strategy-decision cards; retired examples are recorded in `docs/roadmap-retired-cards.md`.

PARKED REASON SCHEMA GUARD 2026-07-10: RoadmapSchema now rejects `parkedReason` on non-parked cards as well as parked cards without a reason. Live metadata check reports zero parked cards missing a reason and zero active/shipped cards carrying one.

items.title: Operator home v1 — Hermes/OpenClaw-style capability view without the full command center
items.summary: Get closer to the visible Hermes/OpenClaw experience discussed in the roadmap without jumping straight to the full COMMAND-CENTER/DESKTOP platform. Build a compact home view that shows what Vanta can do now: workflows, channel health, skills, agents/tasks, memory, watchers, and setup gaps, with one-click entry into the activation gallery.
items.done: A TUI or desktop home view shows live sections for Workflows, Channels, Skills, Agents/Tasks, Memory, Watchers, and Setup; each section has status, next action, and empty/setup state; the first screen answers “what can I do with Vanta?” without reading docs.
items.notes:
Adds the concrete “views like Hermes/OpenClaw” slice: not full JARVIS/desktop, just the visible operator home that makes shipped Vanta capabilities legible.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added a shared operator home view at `/home` and `vanta home`. The view shows live sections for Workflows, Channels, Skills, Agents/Tasks, Memory, Watchers, and Setup; every section includes a status tag, current count/detail, and a concrete next action. It reuses the live workflow registry, reach channel checks, skill store, task stack, background shell task store, memory footprint, cron watchers, and setup health. Verified with pure formatter tests, isolated CLI smoke tests, and a live `npm run vanta -- home` run.

items.title: Crash-log diagnose — paste a macOS/iOS report and get the fix path
items.summary: A transcript is just a raw crash report unless Vanta can extract the root cause. Add a log-diagnosis workflow that recognizes macOS/iOS crash reports, XCTest/dyld failures, sandbox denials, and common build/runtime logs, then returns likely cause, evidence lines, and next command.
items.done: Pasting the GregUITests crash fixture produces a diagnosis for missing @rpath/lib_TestingInterop.dylib, cites the relevant report fields, and suggests concrete Xcode/test-runtime repair steps; parser fixtures cover dyld, sandbox, and generic stack traces.
items.notes:
Evidence: 1522ad63 lines 31-39 and 139-190 include EXC_CRASH/SIGABRT, DYLD Code 1, and missing @rpath/lib_TestingInterop.dylib paths.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 2 first showcase workflow. Smallest concrete “paste an error, get a fix path” demo after the gallery exists.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added `vanta diagnose-crash [--demo greg-uitests|<file>|-]` plus `/diagnose-crash <report|--demo greg-uitests>`. The shared parser recognizes dyld missing-library crashes, sandbox denials, and generic stack traces, and formats likely cause, cited evidence line numbers, and concrete next steps. Live demo `npm run vanta -- diagnose-crash --demo greg-uitests` diagnosed missing `@rpath/lib_TestingInterop.dylib`, cited EXC_CRASH/DYLD/Library-not-loaded/Reason lines, and suggested XCTest link/embed, Runpath Search Paths, DerivedData clean, rebuild, and rerun.

items.title: Spec-to-app wizard — turn a long product prompt into a verified preview
items.summary: The posture-app transcript is a complete product/build spec, not a question. Vanta should treat this as a first-class workflow: parse requirements, scaffold the app, implement states/interactions, run checks, start a preview, and show the user the result.
items.done: Given the posture routine spec fixture, Vanta extracts requirements, produces a React/Tailwind implementation plan, builds the app, runs type/build checks, opens a local preview, and records screenshot/verification evidence in the task summary.
items.notes:
Evidence: e91d797b lines 1-24 specify polished responsive React/Tailwind output and lines 26-468 enumerate detailed sections, timers, toggles, localStorage tracker, and accessibility expectations.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 2 second showcase workflow. Build after the gallery and sandbox recovery so “long spec → working preview” is discoverable and can run without scope confusion.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added `vanta spec-to-app --demo posture [--open]` plus `/spec-to-app --demo posture`. The wizard extracts posture-app requirements, writes an implementation plan, scaffolds a Vite React app using Tailwind utility classes, includes timer controls, completion toggles, localStorage persistence, responsive cards, and accessibility markers, then runs real `tsc --noEmit` and `vite build`. Live run `npm run vanta -- spec-to-app --demo posture --open` recorded `posture-routine-2026-07-09T14-57-40-318Z/task-summary.md`: PASS typecheck, PASS build, PASS open-preview, preview `dist/index.html`, and screenshot-style evidence `preview-screenshot.svg`.

items.title: `/bg` while responding — active response continues in background, not dropped
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.153) changes `/bg` behavior during an active response. Instead of dropping the in-progress output, the response continues streaming in the background session. Users can re-attach with `claude agents` to see the result.
items.done: Pressing `/bg` while Claude is responding transfers the live stream to the background. Attaching later shows the completed response.
items.notes:
Promoted to Next from transcript extraction 2026-07-09 because the transcripts point to Hermes-level operator loops: durable intent, autonomous watching, and background continuation.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 2 continuity fix. Follows sandbox scope recovery so long-running/background behavior stops feeling fragile.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added `/bg` to the slash catalog and TUI submit path. While a TUI response is visibly busy, typing `/bg` detaches the live render, records the active prompt as a background response, keeps the in-flight model call running, and clears the prompt surface. Normal messages submitted while that detached turn is still running are queued so the shared conversation cannot overlap turns. When the turn finishes, Vanta stores the final response on session state; typing `/bg` later attaches and prints the completed answer, and `/bg clear` drops it. Verified with pure background-response state tests, submit-router tests, TUI agent send tests, and a real Ink App integration test that submits a prompt, types `/bg` while the fake model response is pending, completes the response, then types `/bg` again and sees the completed answer.

items.title: Activation v1 release gate — one visible workflow ships before deeper autonomy
items.summary: Blind audit finding #4: Next still mixes showcase workflows, autonomy architecture, and memory infrastructure. Add a release boundary: before trust ledgers, sentinels, or broad watch loops move to Now, Vanta must ship the first visible user workflow end-to-end.
items.done: Activation v1 is declared shipped only after the gallery, sandbox recovery, cold-user gate, user-language copy, fresh-context review, and one showcase workflow are shipped; roadmap tooling or notes prevent autonomy/memory infrastructure from entering Now before that gate is satisfied.
items.notes:
From blind audit finding #4. This is the MoSCoW boundary: Activation v1 is Must; deeper autonomy is Should after the first visible win.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Activation v1 is declared shipped. Dependencies are shipped: WHAT-CAN-I-DO-GALLERY, SANDBOX-SCOPE-WIZARD, ACTIVATION-COLD-USER-GATE, GALLERY-SANDBOX-RECOVERY-FIXTURE, USER-LANGUAGE-WORKFLOW-COPY, FRESH-CONTEXT-ACTIVATION-REVIEW, CRASHLOG-DIAGNOSE, and OPERATOR-HOME-V1. The roadmap dependency guard prevents moving blocked `after` items into building without `--force`, so deeper autonomy/memory infrastructure stays behind this gate unless explicitly overridden.

items.title: Real use-case acceptance — prove Vanta completes operator outcomes
items.summary: Execute a bounded acceptance campaign across real provider work, safe file changes, memory recall, scheduling, delegation, destructive-action blocking, and the packaged desktop path; replace configuration-only proxies with behavioral receipts.
items.done: A versioned acceptance manifest runs the selected operator stories end to end, records redacted receipts, identifies each executed versus externally gated path, and every local release-critical story passes from the same public build without relying on unit-test or configuration-only proxies.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-13: real provider README read and scoped write/readback passed; corpus ingest/recall returned source and freshness evidence; one bounded delegate worker passed; the prior notarized desktop completed real chat and kernel-blocked a destructive terminal command; a clean ~/vanta install ran under launchd, fired a provider-free no_agent schedule, and completed an authenticated Codex agent schedule; cold-start capability reporting passed; bounded cited research passed with primary sources, skeptic review, and explicit uncertainty. The selected versioned manifest reports 6 executed and 6 passed scenarios across 6 categories; remaining catalog categories are explicit coverage gaps or external setup paths, not false passes. The refreshed public v0.9.2 artifact was built from tag commit 742459c1 containing the current schedule/research fixes, notarized, stapled, checksum-verified, and passed a clean-Mac download/Gatekeeper proof: https://github.com/jpoindexter/Vanta/actions/runs/29249460403. A final signed-package local-Codex desktop turn passed in an isolated project (6.3s). See docs/product-acceptance.md.

items.title: Run Anywhere v1 release gate — reach, wake, and execute on controlled infrastructure
items.summary: Activation and the autonomy foundation are shipped. The next launch milestone is not more local scaffolding: prove that one owner can reach Vanta through a real remote channel, wake it from an idle remote runtime, and execute under the same kernel boundary away from the development Mac.
items.done: Run Anywhere v1 ships only after Teams completes a real inbound-to-reply round trip, a remote sandbox executes under kernel scope, a serverless runtime hibernates and wakes on a message, and the ARM64 Termux path completes on physical Android hardware.
items.notes:
LAUNCH-PAD PHASE 2026-07-10: Activation v1 and the autonomy/memory foundation are shipped. This gate makes the remaining Hermes/OpenClaw-style reach outcome explicit and keeps all four external proofs honest. Current blockers are environmental, not missing local scaffolding: Azure Bot credentials/public Teams install, Modal or Daytona authentication, a real hibernate/wake deployment, and physical ARM64 Android/Termux hardware. Do not mark this gate shipped from mocks, emulators, static inspection, or lower-layer tests.

RECHECK 2026-07-10: Aggregate release gate remains blocked by the same external proofs: remote Modal/Daytona wake-on-message, real messaging credentials, a physical ARM64 Termux release-kernel run, and a live Teams/Azure proof. Local scaffolding is not the blocker.

UNBLOCKED/PARKED 2026-07-10: operator chose to remove Run Anywhere external proofs from the active launch board for now. This gate is no longer a board blocker, but it is not shipped; resume after the serverless live, Teams, and Termux proof cards are reactivated and executed.

RELEASE-ASSET UPDATE 2026-07-10: tagged release v0.9.0 published the Android/Bionic kernel and checksum, and `vanta run-anywhere status --check-release` now reports the release asset check ready. The aggregate gate remains parked, not shipped, because its actual done criteria still require live Modal/Telegram wake proof, a real Teams channel proof, and physical ARM64 Termux release-kernel execution.

items.title: Run Anywhere readiness status — one auditable release-proof checklist
items.summary: The remaining Run Anywhere proofs are external, but Vanta now has one local status command that reads durable proof receipts and says exactly why the release gate is or is not ready. It prevents terminal-history proof loss and keeps Modal/Telegram, Teams, Android release-asset, and physical Termux proof gaps visible; JSON output includes each gate's roadmapCardId, receiptPath, and structured nextActions for agents/scripts; `proof-packet` packages those same external commands without pretending proof is complete; the release workflow refuses to publish without the Android/Bionic kernel assets.
items.done: `vanta run-anywhere status [--json] [--check-release]` reports ready only when the serverless Modal/Telegram prove receipt exists, an accepted Teams channel proof exists, and the physical ARM64 Termux proof marker includes `release_kernel=1`; `--json` includes `roadmapCardId`, `receiptPath`, and structured `nextActions` for every gate; `vanta run-anywhere proof-packet [--json] [--check-release]` exits zero as setup guidance and packages the card IDs, receipt paths, evidence, commands, and optional release asset check; `--check-release` additionally verifies the latest GitHub release contains `vanta-kernel-aarch64-linux-android` plus its `.sha256`; missing proofs do not claim setup receipts or mocks satisfy the gate.
items.notes:
SHIPPED 2026-07-10: added `vanta run-anywhere status [--json]` plus `run-anywhere/readiness.ts`. Follow-up: `--check-release` queries the latest GitHub release and requires both `vanta-kernel-aarch64-linux-android` and `vanta-kernel-aarch64-linux-android.sha256` before telling the operator the physical Termux release-kernel proof is ready to attempt; `scripts/require-release-assets.sh` is wired into the release job before `softprops/action-gh-release`, so future tagged releases refuse to publish without those Android/Bionic assets. The command reads durable receipts only: `.vanta/serverless-gateway.json` must have `provedAt` + `telegramAcceptedAt`, `.vanta/channel-proofs.jsonl` must include a Teams proof, and `.vanta/termux-arm64-proof.txt` must contain `TERMUX_ARM64_E2E_OK release_kernel=1`. `vanta backend gateway prove` now writes the serverless proof receipt after the real Modal zero -> Telegram wake/reply -> zero check; `scripts/termux-arm64-device-proof.sh --require-release-kernel` writes the Termux marker. Executed local proof: current repo reports not ready (0/3) with exact next steps for serverless, Teams, and Termux; after tagged release v0.9.0, live release check reports the Android/Bionic kernel asset and checksum are present. Focused tests cover missing setup receipts, all-receipts-ready, non-release Termux rejection, CLI JSON/nonzero behavior, release-asset inspection, release publish guard, modal proof persistence, and Termux script checks.

LIVE RELEASE 2026-07-10: pushed tag v0.9.0 and GitHub Actions release run 29111570826 completed successfully. The Android/Bionic job verified an AArch64 ELF with /system/bin/linker64, the publish job ran scripts/require-release-assets.sh before softprops/action-gh-release, and the published release now contains vanta-kernel-aarch64-linux-android plus vanta-kernel-aarch64-linux-android.sha256. `vanta run-anywhere status --check-release` now reports Android release asset check: ready; the aggregate gate still reports not ready (0/3) because serverless, Teams, and physical Termux proof receipts are intentionally absent.

STRUCTURED NEXT ACTIONS 2026-07-10: `vanta run-anywhere status --json --check-release` now includes `nextActions` arrays for each missing proof gate. Live output maps serverless to gateway status/deploy/register/arm/prove commands, Teams to Azure install plus `vanta gateway channel-proofs teams --json`, and Termux to `scripts/termux-arm64-device-proof.sh --require-release-kernel`. The command still exits 1 because no real proof receipts exist.

RECEIPT PATHS 2026-07-10: readiness JSON now includes `receiptPath` for each gate: `.vanta/serverless-gateway.json`, `.vanta/channel-proofs.jsonl`, and `.vanta/termux-arm64-proof.txt`. Live output shows all three paths while still reporting ready=false.

ROADMAP TRACE 2026-07-10: readiness JSON now includes `roadmapCardId` for each proof gate, tying missing receipts directly to parked roadmap cards: `BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE`, `MSG-ADAPTER-TEAMS`, and `RUN-ANYWHERE-TERMUX`. Live output still exits 1 because proof receipts are absent.

PROOF PACKET 2026-07-10: added `vanta run-anywhere proof-packet [--json] [--check-release]`. Unlike `status`, it exits 0 because packet generation is guidance, not a readiness claim. Live JSON includes the three parked proof cards, receipt paths, current evidence, exact command lists, and the v0.9.0 Android/Bionic release asset check; the separate status gate still exits 1 until real receipts exist.

items.title: Autonomy contract walls — acts alone, queues, wakes me
items.summary: The Fable agentic OS transcript centers the operator contract: what the agent may do alone, what it may queue, and what must wake the human. Vanta already has kernel approvals and sandboxing; this card makes the contract visible, editable, and enforced across background loops, watchers, and tool execution.
items.done: A project can define acts-alone / queues-for-approval / wakes-me rules; Vanta renders the active contract, applies it before autonomous actions, and logs which rule allowed, queued, or escalated each action.
items.notes:
Evidence: Fable transcript 6209d944 lines 173-196 describes contract.md plus guardrails; aligns with existing kernel approval policy but adds operator-facing semantics.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 3 autonomy foundation. Do not deepen autonomy until users can see concrete workflows and recover from scope failures.

LAUNCH-PAD UPDATE 2026-07-09: Deferred from Next to Later until Activation v1 ships. This keeps Next focused on the fastest path to obvious, reliable, Hermes/OpenClaw-style visible workflows.

SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added the project-local autonomy contract wall. `vanta autonomy show` and `/autonomy` render the active acts-alone / queues-for-approval / wakes-me lanes; `vanta autonomy init` writes `.vanta/autonomy-contract.json`; `vanta autonomy decide <kind> <risk> <summary>` applies the contract and appends `.vanta/autonomy-decisions.jsonl`. The proactive queued-loop runner now evaluates `proactive.loop.advance` against the contract before running a queued wake and logs the allowing/queueing/waking rule. Live evidence: `npm run vanta -- autonomy show` rendered all three lanes; `npm run vanta -- autonomy decide proactive.loop.advance low "advance queued loop wake"` returned `acts-alone` by `allow-proactive-loop` and wrote the decision log.

OPERATOR DELIVERY 2026-07-10: all current autonomy callers (CLI, TUI, proactive loops, auto-watch, and ambient proposals) now use one shared surface. Every decision remains append-only in the autonomy log; `vanta autonomy pending` and `/autonomy pending` derive the latest non-auto decision per workflow as a durable review queue; a later acts-alone decision clears that workflow from the view. `wakes-me` decisions await Vanta's `Notification` hook and retain bell/opt-in desktop delivery before returning. Isolated production-path evidence: high-risk `shell.mutate` returned 2, a real `autonomy_wake` hook wrote `woke`, the pending command listed `shell.mutate:default`, and the log recorded `wake-high-risk`.

QUEUE LIFECYCLE 2026-07-10: added `vanta autonomy resolve <key> <note>` and `/autonomy resolve` so an operator can remove a reviewed item only by appending an explicit audit note; a later decision for the same workflow reopens it. Executed lifecycle evidence: decision/resolve/redecision returned 2/0/2, pending counts moved 1/0/1, and `reviewed and declined` persisted as an `autonomy-resolution` event. Full suite: 1,200 files, 12,466 passed, 3 skipped; focused tests, TypeScript, and size gates pass; no leaked tsx stack or test notification bell.

items.title: Trust ledger autonomy — earn auto-run with measured pass rates
items.summary: Promote autonomy only when a skill or loop has earned it. The transcript proposes a TSV-style trust ledger with pass/fail history and tier rules; Vanta should use the same idea to decide whether a workflow auto-runs, queues, or stays watched.
items.done: Each autonomous workflow records run/pass/fail/rate/tier; auto-run is allowed only after the configured run count and pass-rate threshold; any failed verifier demotes the workflow and shows the reason in the operator log.
items.notes:
Evidence: Fable transcript 6209d944 lines 304-339 details pass/fail TSV, auto/queue/watch tiers, and demotion rules.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 3 autonomy proof. Depends on explicit contract walls so pass-rate tiers have something concrete to govern.

LAUNCH-PAD UPDATE 2026-07-09: Deferred from Next to Later until Activation v1 ships. This keeps Next focused on the fastest path to obvious, reliable, Hermes/OpenClaw-style visible workflows.

PER-WORKFLOW ENFORCEMENT 2026-07-10: fixed two trust defects in the proactive loop path. Trust IDs now include the owning loop source, so one loop cannot grant auto-run to another; wakes blocked by trust are requeued instead of silently drained. Added `vanta proactive verify <loop-id>` as the explicit operator-run path that consumes one queued wake, executes through the normal kernel-gated runner, and records the real pass/fail needed to earn auto. Failed auto-runs demote the loop and requeue its wake. Isolated command-handler evidence: alpha verification returned 0 three times, earned auto at 3/3, and then auto-ran once; beta had no inherited ledger entry and remained queued. Focused trust/autonomy/proactive/watch/ambient suite: 11 passed. Full suite: 1,199 files, 12,462 passed, 3 skipped; TypeScript and the size gate pass; no leaked tsx stack or test notification bell.

items.title: Atomic trust-ledger updates — concurrent verifiers cannot lose outcomes
items.summary: Gateway, cron, sentinels, and explicit verification can record trust outcomes concurrently. The current read-modify-write is unlocked, so last-writer-wins can erase valid pass/fail evidence and produce the wrong autonomy tier.
items.done: Concurrent outcome writers are serialized with crash-safe recovery; ledger replacement is atomic for readers; a 24-writer regression records exactly 24 runs/passes and preserves every workflow entry.
items.notes:
LIVE BASELINE 2026-07-10: executed 24 concurrent `recordTrustOutcome` calls for one workflow against an isolated real ledger; only 1 run and 1 pass survived. This is a safety defect because earned-auto and verifier-failure demotion depend on complete counts.

SHIPPED 2026-07-10: trust outcomes now run under Vanta's crash-safe exclusive checkout lock, waiting for the current writer and reclaiming dead-process locks; ledger replacement uses temp-file + atomic rename so readers never see a partial document. Executed the exact baseline again: 24 concurrent calls persisted 24 runs and 24 passes, the lock released, and the ledger parsed as valid JSON. Stronger cross-process proof: eight independent Node processes persisted 8/8 outcomes. Focused trust/proactive/sentinel/watch/ambient tests: 11 passed. Full suite: 1,200 files, 12,467 passed, 3 skipped; TypeScript and size gates pass; no leaked tsx stack or test notification bell.

items.title: Standing-goal sentinels — finished goals stay watched
items.summary: A finished goal should become a cheap predicate that stays watched, not a forgotten success claim. Convert verified goals into daily checks that report drift, regressions, or violated invariants without auto-fixing behind the user’s back.
items.done: A completed goal can generate a standing predicate file; a scheduled sentinel runs cheap checks, records pass/fail history, wakes the user on violation, and retires flaky predicates only with an explicit note.
items.notes:
Evidence: Fable transcript 6209d944 lines 340-380: every finished goal becomes a predicate, checked daily, with violations surfaced and flaky checks retired rather than deleted.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 3 persistence. Build after trust-ledger gating so daily sentinels inherit demotion/escalation behavior instead of becoming noisy cron.

LAUNCH-PAD UPDATE 2026-07-09: Deferred from Next to Later until Activation v1 ships. This keeps Next focused on the fastest path to obvious, reliable, Hermes/OpenClaw-style visible workflows.

WAKE DELIVERY VERIFIED 2026-07-10: predicate failures now await Vanta's Notification hook before a short-lived CLI/cron process exits, while retaining the terminal bell and opt-in macOS notification. The long-lived gateway also executes the same once-daily sentinel path. Isolated real-path evidence: command-handler add/run returned 0/2, a real Notification hook completed from the watched project root, gateway logged `sentinel wake goal-42`, a second same-day gateway tick returned 0, and persisted history contained both failures. Focused sentinel/CLI/schedule/gateway/notification suite: 36 passed. Full suite: 1,198 files, 12,458 passed, 3 skipped; no leaked tsx stack or test notification bell.

items.title: Vault compile pipeline — raw transcripts become linked knowledge
items.summary: Existing brain↔vault bridges handle recall, but the second-brain transcripts call for a compile pipeline: raw chats/bookmarks/notes go into raw/, then Vanta writes sourced entity and concept pages plus an index. This makes transcripts usable as durable operator knowledge instead of one-off pasted context.
items.done: Given a folder of raw transcripts/notes, Vanta creates or updates markdown entity/concept pages with source links, maintains an INDEX.md, keeps raw files separate, and emits a diff for review before writeback.
items.notes:
Evidence: second-brain transcript a5b5decf lines 40-85 specifies raw/entities/concepts/INDEX, source links, graph links, and diff-based backfill. Complements, not duplicates, shipped BRAIN-VAULT-BRIDGE and BRAIN-VAULT-RECALL.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 4 memory leverage. Keep next, but behind activation: compile transcripts once Vanta can explain and demo what the compiled knowledge will power.

LAUNCH-PAD UPDATE 2026-07-09: Deferred from Next to Later until Activation v1 ships. This keeps Next focused on the fastest path to obvious, reliable, Hermes/OpenClaw-style visible workflows.

items.title: Research receipts + skeptic gate — claims need source, date, expiry
items.summary: Upgrade VANTA-RESEARCH-DECOMPOSE with the second-brain research machine: every claim carries source/date/expiry, and a skeptic pass attacks weak claims before they enter memory or a final answer. This is the difference between “researched” and reusable operator intelligence.
items.done: A research run returns claims as claim/source/date/expiry records, runs a skeptic gate, flags unsupported or stale claims, and writes only surviving claims into the memory/vault pipeline.
items.notes:
Evidence: second-brain transcript a5b5decf lines 97-115: split into subquestions, parallel sources, receipts, skeptic attack, dated survivors with expiry.

ORGANIZED 2026-07-09: Phase 4 research quality. Depends on shipped research fan-out plus vault compile so receipts have somewhere useful to land.

LAUNCH-PAD UPDATE 2026-07-09: Deferred from Next to Later until Activation v1 ships. This keeps Next focused on the fastest path to obvious, reliable, Hermes/OpenClaw-style visible workflows.

items.title: Dead-simple setup — first install works for a non-CS user (no toolchain)
items.summary: TOP PRIORITY (operator direction 2026-06-22): make the first install work for a non-technical user on a fresh machine. Community signal (OpenClaw/Hermes X thread 2026-06-22) made ease-of-setup the single most-repeated ask — Brian Distelburger ("switched because it was easier to set up, I don’t have a CS background"), Felipe H ("an easier way to set up is essential"), ASI ("brand new VM, broke after the first message"). The wizard already shipped (VANTA-PROJECT-ONBOARDING / INSTALL-PARITY / MSG-WIZARD); the remaining gap is TOOLCHAIN FRICTION. Remove it: prebuilt kernel binaries (no Rust), Node-22 detection + guidance (or a bundled runtime), a zero-config first run that defaults to a working local model, and one obvious happy path. Easy is what converts; the kernel/goal-gating is the wedge.
items.done: a non-technical user on a clean machine (no Rust, no preinstalled Node) runs one command and reaches a working chat with at most one guided step for the model backend; verified on a clean Linux + macOS box.
items.notes: SHIPPED + LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-22. No toolchain needed: scripts/setup-lib.sh downloads a checksum-verified prebuilt kernel (no Rust) AND a portable Node 22 (no system Node); install.sh + run.sh source it; bootstrap.sh needs only git. PROOF: a bare debian:bookworm-slim box (no Rust, no Node) ran install.sh -> downloaded Node v22 + the kernel -> run.sh doctor exit 0 (store created, kernel ready). Linux live-verified end-to-end; macOS verified by construction (darwin binaries published in release v0.2.0, identical code path). Remaining is the one allowed model step (Ollama or an API key). Desktop app = later lever (DESKTOP-P12).

items.title: Prebuilt vanta-kernel binaries via GitHub Releases (no Rust to install)
items.summary: First slice of SETUP-DEAD-SIMPLE and the biggest single friction remover: ship prebuilt vanta-kernel binaries per OS/arch via GitHub Releases (CI-built) so bootstrap.sh downloads the binary instead of running cargo build — removing the Rust toolchain prereq entirely. The kernel is zero-dependency Rust, so cross-compile + release is clean. Falls back to cargo build when no matching release exists (devs unaffected).
items.done: a GitHub Actions release workflow builds vanta-kernel for macOS (arm64/x64) + Linux (arm64/x64); bootstrap.sh detects the platform, downloads the released binary (checksum-verified), and skips cargo build when Rust is absent; a fresh box with NO Rust installs and runs.
items.notes: SHIPPED + LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-22. release.yml builds the kernel for macOS arm64/x64 + Linux arm64/x64 (Apple targets cross-built on Apple Silicon), published to GitHub release v0.2.0 (4 binaries + sha256, latest/download resolves). install.sh downloads the platform binary to target/debug/vanta-kernel (checksum-verified), cargo only as fallback; bootstrap.sh no longer requires Rust. PROOF: a clean node:22 container with NO Rust ran install.sh -> downloaded the aarch64-linux prebuilt -> vanta-kernel doctor returned status:ready. Pairs with the --omit=dev runtime-only npm install.

Public-safe scrub strips provenance + competitor/strategy framing (migrate title keeps source names intentionally); sentence-level competitor filter on descriptions. Linked in sidebars.ts; prestart/prebuild hooks regenerate. Docusaurus build green.

items.title: Provider-aware model-call watchdog (no false timeout on cold start)
items.summary: Align the model-call watchdog with per-provider cold-start latency / timeoutSeconds so cron + loops don't false-timeout on a cold provider. Community signal (OpenClaw/Hermes X thread 2026-06-22), @SpaceCityDirk bug screenshot: a watchdog capped independently of the job timeout fires before DeepSeek's cold-start completes — every cron run double-runs and logs a spurious timeout. Extends LIVENESS-WATCHDOG.
items.done: the model-call watchdog window derives from (or respects) the active provider’s configured timeout; a cold-provider cron run completes once with no spurious timeout; tested against a slow-first-call provider stub.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-22. providers/timeout.ts is one source of truth for the active provider request timeout (cold-start-aware: ollama/lmstudio 1800s, deepseek/nim/nvidia 900s, custom 1200s, else 600s = SDK default; VANTA_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_SEC override). Wired into the OpenAI SDK client (explicit timeout, was the hidden default) and resolveWatchdogConfig clamps the liveness stall window UP to provider-timeout + 120s margin, so a too-tight watchdog can never false-fire before a cold call elapses. Tested against a slow-first-call provider config. 945 files/10844 tests green.

Linked from README (Rule zero) + already in the docs nav. Live at docs.vanta.theft.studio/security.

items.title: Gateway channel self-heal — auto-reconnect + health recovery
items.summary: Messaging-channel auto-reconnect + health recovery so a dropped channel self-heals without a restart. Community signal (OpenClaw/Hermes X thread 2026-06-22): Felipe H ("WhatsApp always disconnected, gateway broke"), ASI Appreciator ("brand new VM, broke after the first Telegram message"), and repeated "daily gateway failures" were the top reliability complaints against competitors. Extends MSG-MULTICHANNEL-LIVE per-channel isolation + HP-GATEWAY-DAEMON + REACH-DOCTOR.
items.done: a dropped channel reconnects automatically with backoff; the gateway survives a flapping channel; vanta gateway reports per-channel health + last reconnect; tested with an adapter that fails then recovers.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. SupervisedChannel (gateway/platforms/channel-supervisor.ts) wraps each child adapter: a failed poll marks the channel down + schedules an exponential-backoff reconnect (backoffMs, BASE 1s→MAX 60s) attempted inside poll() on the gateway tick loop (clock-injected, no timers). MultiChannelAdapter wraps every child + exposes health() (per-channel status/failures/lastError/lastReconnectAt); runGatewayLoop diffs health each tick and logs up/down transitions via the gateway log — the per-channel health + last-reconnect report. One flapping channel never blocks the others; poll/send/connect never throw across the seam. Offline-tested with an adapter that fails then recovers (channel-supervisor.test.ts + multi-channel.test.ts self-heal cases). 35 gateway tests green, tsc + size gate clean.

Wired: vanta status/doctor pass argv (--verbose/-v, VANTA_STATUS_VERBOSE=1) + TTY detect; /status [-v] in REPL. Addresses @gliang9 firehose complaint. 946 files/10867 tests green.

Default off: nothing files until send. Reuses AUTO-ISSUE buildGhIssueArgs (injection-safe argv) + secret-scan; gh runner injected (DI) for tests. 126 slash commands; 946 files/10857 tests green.

items.title: Unified cross-agent memory — ingest from Codex/Claude Code/ChatGPT
items.summary: A unified memory/knowledge layer that ingests from other agents/tools (Codex, Claude Code, ChatGPT) so knowledge isn't fragmented across them. Community signal (OpenClaw/Hermes X thread 2026-06-22), @ennyonx: "how do you deal with knowledge getting fragmented when using different agents besides openclaw?". Extends VANTA-MIGRATE (the memory child) + the brain/memory layers — the read-side complement to migrate.
items.done: vanta imports + unifies memory/context from at least one external agent’s store into the brain, deduped + provenance-tagged; recall surfaces the merged knowledge.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. The read-side complement to VANTA-MIGRATE: `vanta migrate memory <claude-code|codex> [path]` imports another agent's memory store into the brain, deduped + provenance-tagged. migrate/memory.ts is PURE: parseAgentMemory (markdown → facts: bullets/prose lines, drops headings/frontmatter/code-fences/sub-threshold noise + in-file dedup) → planMemoryIngest (splits new vs already-known keyed on the brain's own entryId(region,content) → re-import never duplicates) → ingestAgentMemory (injected read/existingIds/remember/idOf, errors-as-values). cli/migrate-memory-cmd.ts wires the live boundary through the Brain PORT (resolveBrain().remember, NOT the concrete brain — architecture boundary brain-variant-port) with sourceType 'external' + sourceRef '<source>' provenance, all facts in the semantic region; kernel-gated (assess block→refuse). LIVE-VERIFIED end-to-end: `vanta migrate memory claude-code` read the real ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md → imported 178 facts (region semantic / sourceType external / sourceRef claude-code) into a temp brain; recall surfaced an imported fact; re-import deduped 100% (0 new). Scoped to claude-code+codex sources (ChatGPT has no local store); one-time import (the ongoing-sync version stays a follow-up). 8 memory tests + full suite 995 files/11267 green, tsc + size gate + architecture boundaries clean.

Extends VISION-WATCH-ALERT + SCREEN-TO-TASK + COMPANION-PRESENCE.

items.title: TUI delight / personality pass (not a soulless CLI)
items.summary: A delight/personality/visual-identity pass so the CLI has character without sacrificing the dense, integration-friendly surface. Community signal (OpenClaw/Hermes X thread 2026-06-22), @nnehdigh: "you keep building soulless cli tools which are great for integration but [lack] delight." Extends TUI-DESIGN-LANGUAGE — the feel layer, post-function.
items.done: a deliberate identity pass (motion, micro-copy, a signature moment) lands without bloating output or breaking --bare/scripting; reduced-motion respected.
items.notes: Polish, not core — schedule after stability.

items.title: Messaging channel parity — reach Hermes(20+)/OpenClaw(13+) channel coverage
items.summary: REVISED 2026-06-22 — verification found most adapters ALREADY SHIPPED: MSG-ADAPTER-DISCORD/MATRIX/GOOGLECHAT/SLACK/EMAIL/MATTERMOST/IRC/NTFY/LINE/WHATSAPP, MSG-SIGNAL, MSG-IMESSAGE, Telegram (HP-21-PLATFORMS claims 21), with 5 verified LIVE from one gateway (MSG-MULTICHANNEL-LIVE: Telegram/WhatsApp/Signal/Discord/Slack). Channels are NOT an unbuilt gap. Re-scoped from 'build adapters' to the REMAINING delta: finish MSG-ADAPTER-TEAMS (horizon), run a live-verify pass over the coded-but-not-live adapters, and decide whether to add OpenClaw-style native mobile nodes + a shareable channel/skills ecosystem (the genuine gap vs the Hermes Skills Hub). The earlier 'out of scope -> in scope' framing on this card came from an incomplete first scan of the roadmap.
items.done: MSG-ADAPTER-TEAMS shipped + live; each previously-coded adapter has a logged live-verify result; a recorded decision on native-mobile-nodes + shareable-ecosystem scope. (Core adapter build is already done.)
items.notes: Recurring high-priority operator ask. SWEEP COMPLETE 2026-06-22: full channel-parity build landed — 20 adapters wired in gateway/platforms/adapter-registry.ts (added Teams, Twitch, SMS/Twilio, Zalo, Feishu, WebChat, Nostr; finished Google Chat SA->token + Email IMAP/SMTP; earlier Matrix + LINE). At parity with Hermes 20+/OpenClaw 13+. New deps (dynamic-imported): @noble/curves (Nostr), nodemailer + imapflow (Email). 944 files/10831 tests green. REMAINING (not build — operator-side): per-platform live credential verification (5 verified live so far: Telegram/WhatsApp/Signal/Discord/Slack) + native mobile client (RUN-ANYWHERE-TERMUX, separate card). Rolls up MSG-WHATSAPP, Telegram, VANTA-SLACK-CHANNEL-SUGGEST, VANTA-CHANNEL-MSG, VANTA-CHANNEL-PERMISSIONS, MSG-ADAPTER-SLACK.

items.title: Self-learning loop — agent auto-writes and improves its own skills from what it does (the Hermes wedge to own)
items.summary: Make autonomous self-improvement first-class, not scattered. Hermes Agent's real advantage is a closed learning loop: agent-curated memory + autonomous skill creation + skill self-improvement during use + procedural memory. Vanta already has the pieces — learned skills (~/.vanta/skills), a curator, per-goal memory, LoRA tuning, meta-tune — but no single always-on loop that, after a task, decides whether to mint or refine a skill and proves it improved. This epic unifies them into one named closed loop: observe trajectory -> propose skill or edit -> validate against evals -> adopt (gated) -> measure recurrence. The whole point of the product is that it learns by doing; this makes that real.
items.done: After completing tasks Vanta autonomously proposes a new or improved skill, validates it does not regress (eval-gated), adopts it on approval, and a measurable metric shows reuse/improvement over a session; the loop is on by default (adoption gated) and documented as the core differentiator.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-22. ONE named closed loop unifying the scattered pieces (learning/): observe->propose (reuses background-review reviewTurn)->eval-gate (eval-gate.ts gateSkill rejects thin/refusal/shadowing skills; injectable for the AHE skill-eval corpus)->adopt-or-archive (skills/store archiveSkill, reversible)->measure (ledger.ts). REAL reuse metric: recalling a learned skill records a kind:reused event (tools/recall.ts reuseEvent), so /learning shows minted/refined/adopted + reused + adoption rate over a session. On by default (shouldReview/VANTA_SELF_IMPROVE), adoption gated. Documented as the core differentiator on the public docs site (self-improvement.md). NOTE: the shareable Skills Hub ecosystem (the broader Hermes gap named in this card) is a SEPARATE future card, not part of this loop card. 954 files/10916 tests green.

items.title: vanta migrate — import from OpenClaw / Hermes (skills + MCP servers + model config)
items.summary: Mirror what the category already ships (openclaw migrate; hermes 'migrate from openclaw'): a kernel-gated importer that reads ~/.openclaw and ~/.hermes and brings skills, MCP server configs, and model/provider config into ~/.vanta. Locked scope #1: OpenClaw + Hermes sources, skills + MCP + model-config footprint (memory/session import and Codex/Claude Code sources deferred to children). Preview plan -> checkbox select -> backup -> apply; secrets redacted in the report; never overwrites without approval.
items.done: vanta migrate openclaw / vanta migrate hermes previews a plan, lets the operator select items, backs up ~/.vanta, then imports skills + MCP servers + model config; pure parsers tested offline against fixture ~/.openclaw and ~/.hermes trees; kernel-gated, secrets redacted, reversible.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-22. vanta migrate <openclaw|hermes>: preview (formatPlan, secrets redacted) -> select (footprint flags --skills/--mcp/--model; --overwrite for conflicts) -> backup ~/.vanta (tar) -> apply (writeSkill + merge mcp.json + upsertEnv model config into ~/.vanta/.env). Kernel-gated (assess() block->refuse). Conflicts skipped unless --overwrite; reversible via backup-first. PURE parsers (migrate/parse.ts) + plan (migrate/plan.ts, injected reads like purge-plan) tested offline against fixture ~/.openclaw + ~/.hermes trees (skills/<slug>/SKILL.md + mcpServers JSON + provider/model config). Source layout is the de-facto agent convention (documented assumption; live tree is the boundary). Locked scope #1 (skills+MCP+model). Per-item picker shipped (numbered select: all/none/1,3/ranges; footprint flags pre-narrow). Follow-ups (deferred to children per card): memory/session import + Codex/Claude-Code sources (deferred to children per card). 954 files/10909 tests green.

items.title: Session Memory service — background LLM notes file maintained during conversation
items.summary: another agent's Session Memory service (`sessionMemory.ts`) auto-maintains a per-session markdown notes file in the background using a forked subagent. It runs periodically via post-sampling hook, extracting key info (decisions, context, in-progress items) from conversation without blocking the main loop. Distinct from extractMemories (which writes to ~/.claude/projects/memory) — this is an in-session scratchpad.
items.done: A `.claude/session-memory.md` is auto-populated and updated throughout the session. The notes are injected into context on /compact and session resume.

items.title: Streaming tool execution — start tools as tool_use blocks arrive in stream
items.summary: another agent's StreamingToolExecutor begins executing tools as their `tool_use` blocks arrive in the streaming response, without waiting for the full response to complete. Concurrency-safe tools (Glob, Grep, Read, etc.) can start while the model is still generating. Queued tools wait for their slot. Reduces end-to-end latency for tool-heavy turns by overlapping execution with streaming.
items.done: A turn with 3 Glob calls starts executing the first Glob while the model is still streaming the 2nd and 3rd. Total wall-clock time drops proportionally.

items.title: `MessageDisplay` hook event — transform or hide assistant messages before rendering
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.152) adds a `MessageDisplay` hook event that fires before each assistant message is shown. Hooks can rewrite or suppress the text — useful for operators that want to strip internal reasoning, add context, or route messages to external systems.
items.done: A MessageDisplay hook strips <thinking> blocks from displayed output. The transcript shows clean responses; raw content still reaches downstream tools.

items.title: Hooks engine — shell commands on agent events
items.summary: another agent fires configurable shell hooks at key agent lifecycle events: PreToolUse (before a tool runs), PostToolUse (after), UserPromptSubmit (on user input), Stop (on session end). Hooks receive JSON context via stdin and can block/allow actions (PreToolUse exit code gates execution). Add a hooks runner in session.ts: load .vanta/hooks.json, match event + tool name, spawn shell command with JSON context, handle exit codes.
items.done: PreToolUse hooks can block tool execution; PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop hooks fire and complete without blocking. Hooks configured in .vanta/hooks.json.

items.title: `/goal` — set a completion condition; agent works until it's met
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.139) adds `/goal` to set a completion condition for the session. Claude works autonomously across turns until the condition is satisfied. Works in interactive and headless sessions. Core to persistent autonomous agent loops.
items.done: Running `/goal done when tests pass` causes the agent to keep iterating until `npm test` passes without being re-prompted.

items.title: Hook `additionalContext` — hooks return context to keep the turn going
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.163) adds `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` to Stop and SubagentStop hooks. Instead of ending the turn, the hook can return feedback text that Claude receives as context and continues working. Enables hook-driven agent feedback loops.
items.done: A Stop hook returns `{additionalContext: 'Tests failed — retry with fix X'}`. Claude reads this and continues the turn without re-prompting.

items.title: Teams tool — named multi-agent squads
items.summary: another agent has TeamCreateTool/TeamDeleteTool: define a named team of agents with roles, spawn them, coordinate work. More structured than Vanta's delegate/swarm. Add team_create (name + member roles + task) and team_delete; teams persist in .vanta/teams.json and can be addressed by send_message. Depends on VANTA-SEND-MSG.
items.done: `team_create` spins up a named squad of agents with defined roles; `team_delete` tears it down. Teams are addressable via send_message.

items.title: Background agent management — agents view, attach, logs, respawn, daemon
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-14. `cli/agents-cmd.ts` exposes the team task ledger as `vanta agents` plus top-level `attach/logs/respawn/stop/rm <id>` aliases; `daemon status/stop` wraps the supervisor status/stop path. `team/tasks.ts` now supports stopped/removed terminal management states and worker load ignores closed tasks. `settings.disableAgentView` and `VANTA_DISABLE_AGENT_VIEW=1` gate the feature.
items.done: `vanta agents` opens a live view of running sessions; `vanta attach/logs/respawn/stop/rm <id>` manage individual sessions; `vanta daemon status/stop` controls the supervisor; a `disableAgentView` setting gates the feature.

items.title: Auto permission mode — ML action classification + soft-deny preset
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-14. `permissions/auto-mode.ts` adds the local auto permission classifier: default read-only auto-allow rules, soft-deny presets for borderline shell/secret patterns, and `settings.autoMode.rules` overrides. `--permission-mode auto` and `VANTA_AUTO_MODE=1` enable it; `vanta auto-mode defaults/config` prints built-in/effective config. Applied after kernel + permissions; kernel block remains immovable.
items.done: `--permission-mode auto` (or `VANTA_AUTO_MODE=1`) applies ML-based classification on top of kernel rules; `vanta auto-mode defaults` prints built-in rules; `vanta auto-mode config` shows effective config with overrides; `autoMode` settings accept custom rules and a soft_deny preset.

items.title: Interactive edit-review panel — per-change keep/undo with keyboard nav
items.summary: A review surface for a turn's file edits, modeled on the reference in docs/design-refs/edit-review-panel.png: header 'N files edited · esc when done', per-file diff cards (old/new line numbers, green adds, red removes), focused-card highlight, ↑↓ moves between changes, 'a' keeps, 'z' undoes (kernel-gated restore of the pre-edit content), and an 'Add a follow-up' input that sends straight to the agent. Builds on the existing per-tool diff data (write_file diffs already render in the transcript) and the vendored fork's ScrollBox. Pairs with VANTA-STRUCTURED-DIFF (horizon) for the renderer; this card is the interaction loop.
items.done: /changes opens an interactive review overlay: changed files from git (changed-files.ts) with +/- stats, ↑↓ navigate, the selected file diff previews, u=undo (git checkout for tracked / delete for untracked — keypress is the per-action consent), Esc close. Per-file granularity (hunk-level deferred).

items.title: TUI design language — lipgloss-grade layout primitives (tabs, dialogs, panels, status chips)
items.summary: Visual quality bar set by docs/design-refs/lipgloss-layout-bar.png (charmbracelet/lipgloss demo): real tab rows, centered modal dialogs with focus-styled buttons, side-by-side framed list panels, colored status-bar chips (STATUS/encoding/badge), adaptive color ramps. Vanta already has Overlay/OverlayRow + theme.ts; this card grows them into a small primitive set (Tabs, Dialog, Panel, StatusChip) used by the existing overlays (skills/sessions/model pickers, approval prompt, status bar) so every surface shares one visual system under the vendored fork. No new deps — pure Box/Text composition.
items.done: Pickers, approval prompt, and the status bar render through shared Tab/Dialog/Panel/StatusChip primitives; a /theme switch restyles all of them coherently; screenshots match the reference's structure.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-12 (commits 78a9b94, f4db2ed, 19b152d, 13ede05, f1f4034, a266283), design language from docs/design-refs/tui-demo.html applied to the live TUI: (1) pickers + approval render the full-width themed selection bar via shared Overlay/OverlayRow, accent resolved from the active theme not hard-coded cyan; (2) the approval prompt is the kernel ASK verdict box — gated command on its own line + a 'why' line; (3) StatusChip primitive (status-chip.tsx) drives the status bar's run-state + mode chips; (4) Tabs primitive (tabs.tsx) drives the model wizard's Provider · Model step row; (5) /theme is a real live switch (was a stub) — composer + status bar restyle instantly, modal pickers re-resolve their accent on next open. Design note: Panel and Dialog are both realized by the one shared Overlay (one frame, two roles) rather than two near-duplicate components — leaner per Rule-of-3. A persistent multi-pane tab SURFACE (vs the wizard step row) belongs to TUI-V2's mission-control layout, not this primitive-kit card.

items.title: TUI v2 — mission-control surface (non-destructive)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-14. `VANTA_TUI=v2` selects a separate `src/ui/v2/` mission-control shell: left durable-state rail, center existing interactive TUI engine/transcript, right safety + working-memory + telemetry rail plus command-risk labels. Default/unknown `VANTA_TUI` values still launch v1 through the existing path; v2 reuses the shared engine read-only. The stricter live-session rail hardening remains tracked by `TUI-V2-RAILS`.
items.done: VANTA_TUI=v2 launches the 3-column mission-control TUI while the default v1 TUI is byte-for-byte unchanged and still default; v2 is a separate module, zero v1 files edited.
items.notes: Implemented in `vanta-ts/src/ui/v2/` with `MissionControlFrame` + `AppV2`, selected by `selectTuiSurface()` in `src/ui/launch.tsx`. Tests: `src/ui/launch.test.ts`, `src/ui/v2/mission-control.test.tsx`. Default remains v1; `TUI-V2-RAILS` stays open for making every rail value live from session state.

items.title: Tab navigation — focus management between UI elements
items.summary: another agent's tabstops.ts + use-tab-status.ts enable Tab/Shift+Tab focus traversal between interactive elements (buttons, inputs, list items). Required for the settings UI, MCP panel, hooks config, and agent wizard to be keyboard-navigable. Vanta's TUI has no focus traversal.
items.done: Tab moves focus to the next interactive element; Shift+Tab moves back; focused elements show a visual indicator; Enter activates; all dialogs and panels are fully keyboard-navigable.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-15. Added src/ui/focus.ts with pure focus target traversal helpers and wired ui/app.tsx so Tab moves forward through visible targets and Shift+Tab moves backward when dialogs/panels are active. Preserved the existing Shift+Tab mode cycle when the composer is the only focus target. Composer input is inactive while unfocused, focused elements show visible markers, approval actions expose focus targets and Enter activates the focused action, and overlay lists show focus while preserving arrow/Enter selection. Verified with focus/composer/approval/UI tests, full TS suite (3357 green), tsc --noEmit, and size lint.

items.title: Desktop operator flows v1 — capabilities, channels, artifacts, settings
items.summary: Port the useful Hermes desktop interaction model into Vanta without copying Hermes branding or creating a second control plane: chat remains the home surface; a durable navigator exposes Vanta's live tool/skill catalog, real messaging adapter configuration, saved-output artifacts, model/provider settings, and shortcut help. The desktop reads and writes Vanta's existing stores and approval boundary.
items.done: The packaged Vanta desktop lets an operator navigate chat, capabilities, messaging, and artifacts; capability rows derive from live tool/skill data; selecting a messaging adapter reveals real setup steps and saves credentials to the project-local Vanta config without exposing secrets; artifacts are collected from saved Vanta outputs; settings and shortcut help expose only real desktop behavior; focused route/UI tests plus a packaged Electron interaction smoke pass.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-13. The useful Hermes flows now live inside Vanta's Codex-inspired shell without copying Hermes branding: Work, Outputs, and Connect are the only primary destinations; capabilities read registered tool schemas and persisted skills; Messaging reads implemented gateway adapters and saves selected credentials under `.vanta/.env` with mode 0600 without echoing secrets; Outputs collects canvas, external links, and in-project files from saved assistant/tool outputs. The renderer boot defect (`theme is not defined`) is repaired and `desktop:renderer:typecheck` now covers `desktop-app/src`. The native operator-flow smoke passes Work, Connect, capability discovery, messaging save, Outputs, keyboard and button file attachment, Stop, quick actions, shortcuts, Settings, provider setup, and light mode without renderer errors. The same smoke passes against the locally signed arm64 `Vanta.app`, whose deep code-signature verification passes. First-run `/api/status` and `/api/tools` can correctly report model setup needed; the smoke accepts only those bootstrap failures and proves the visible setup flow. Work consumes text/tool SSE events and Stop aborts the active agent signal, retaining partial streamed text as the interrupted result. A provider-backed live-turn proof, queue/steer, and a richer file-context picker belong to DESKTOP-USERFLOW-RESET rather than this capability/navigation card.

items.title: Desktop user-flow reset - one dependable work loop
items.summary: Repair the source renderer and streamline the native app around one dependable operator loop: start or resume work, follow/steer a kernel-gated run, review outputs, and configure connections only when needed. Preserve Vanta capabilities while reducing the current competing shells, raw file dump, and opaque task waiting.
items.done: A fresh source and packaged Vanta desktop boot without renderer errors; Work, Outputs, and Connect are the only primary destinations; a task streams visible agent steps, supports Stop and scoped approval, preserves partial failures with recovery, attaches visible project context, and exposes run outputs without changing the primary shell. Renderer typecheck, Electron page-error/boot smoke, workflow tests at desktop/narrow widths, and fresh-context acceptance all pass.
items.notes:
SHIPPED 2026-07-13: Audit and plan: docs/desktop-userflow-audit-2026-07-13.md; vanta-ts/flow-report.html. Work, Outputs, and Connect are the only primary destinations; the compact Outputs rail is scalable, persistent, draggable, keyboard-adjustable, and becomes a drawer below 1080px. Work renders text/tool SSE activity inline, supports Stop through the live AbortSignal, retains partial text, exposes scoped Retry recovery, and permits exactly one serial Queue-next instruction within the active kernel-gated session. Composer `@` attachment produces visible/removable context chips that expand through the existing syntax; Outputs remain accessible without changing the primary shell. The model picker is provider-grouped, searchable, two-column at desktop width, and marks the active session model.

PACKAGED RELEASE REPAIR 2026-07-13: a submitted crash report proved the former broad deep-sign operation left Electron's libffmpeg under a different Team ID. The packer now removes generated signing state, signs every Mach-O payload, signs enclosed frameworks and helpers, then seals the outer app with Electron's JIT entitlement. Executed proof: strict Developer ID validation passed; a fresh signed arm64 package booted from an isolated project, served renderer assets, reported packaged kernel online, and exited 0; the opt-in signed-package proof used local Codex to `read_file` the isolated README and returned its unique marker in 7.6s. The root-scoped launcher also prevents a second Vanta project from borrowing another project's default-port kernel/token boundary; a direct isolated assessment passed at its deterministic local endpoint. True in-turn steering, richer evidence expansion, relevance-ranked context search, archive undo/trash, and connector feedback are later improvements.

MODEL CATALOG FIX 2026-07-14: expanded the Codex subscription picker to include the full current GPT-5.6/GPT-5.5/GPT-5.4/GPT-5.3-Codex/GPT-5.2/GPT-5.1/GPT-5 agent family and changed desktop catalog resolution so stale remote/cache manifests can add models but cannot hide or outrank newer bundled models. Follow-up same day: expanded the OpenAI API picker with current dated snapshots and adjacent current aliases documented by OpenAI, including `gpt-5.5-2026-04-23`, `gpt-5.4-2026-03-05`, `gpt-5.2-2025-12-11`, `gpt-5.1-2025-11-13`, `gpt-5-pro-2025-10-06`, `gpt-5-chat-latest`, `o1-pro`, and `computer-use-preview`; regenerated the docs and website model catalog manifests. Focused provider/catalog tests cover stale-cache and refreshed-catalog paths.

items.title: Desktop P3 — real React/Vite renderer
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-15. Replaced the giant static desktop UI source with a real Vite+React renderer under `vanta-ts/desktop-app/` (`AppShell`, `SessionSidebar`, `ChatThread`, `Composer`, `RightRail`). `src/desktop/assets.ts` serves `desktop-app/dist` when built; `page.ts` is now only a small fallback notice. Vanta operator/dossier aesthetic preserved, not generic SaaS.
items.done: No giant-string UI; current functionality preserved in real components; builds via Vite.
items.notes: Build: `npm run desktop:build` (Vite). Server root serves built `desktop-app/dist/index.html` + `/assets/*`; fallback tells the user to build. Verified with focused desktop tests, `tsc`, Vite build, curl asset fetch, and headless Chrome screenshot `/private/tmp/vanta-desktop-p3.png`.

items.title: Per-tool permission request UIs — dedicated dialogs per tool type
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-15. `permissions/request.ts` maps approval descriptors into typed request models for Bash, FileEdit, FileWrite, WebFetch, ComputerUse, Sandbox, Skill, and fallback Generic. Ink and desktop approval UIs render those purpose-built sections and expose Allow once / Always allow / Deny / Never allow. Always/Never persist tool-scoped allow/deny rules; kernel block remains immovable.
items.done: Each tool type has a purpose-built permission dialog: Bash shows the command with syntax highlight + options; FileEdit shows a diff; FileWrite shows new content; all support Allow/Deny/Always/Never rules.
items.notes: Current approval callback carries `{toolName, action, reason}` rather than raw tool args/content, so FileEdit/FileWrite dialogs show typed target/change context now; raw diff/full-content preview belongs to later tool-args plumbing. Verified with focused permission UI tests, typecheck, size lint, and Vite build.

items.title: Unified @-completion typeahead — files, MCP resources, agents, slash, Slack, shell history
items.summary: Single typeahead covering: @path file completions, @AgentType agent completions with color, MCP resource completions, slash-command + argument hints, #channel Slack completions (when Slack MCP active), shell history completions, directory path completions. Fuse.js fuzzy matching. Inline ghost text for best match.
