items.done: Files edited by another agent tracked with hashes; git commits include proper Co-Authored-By attribution; generated files excluded; remote URL included in attribution metadata.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-09. End-to-end wire is complete: ToolContext/AgentDeps carry optional sessionId; readline/TUI/desktop hosts pass it through; call_agent coding streams collect Claude Write/Edit paths, read final file contents after the subprocess exits, skip generated/out-of-root files, persist .vanta/attribution/<session>.json with hashes and remote.origin.url; git_commit loads trailersForSession and appends Co-Authored-By plus Vanta-Attribution metadata to the commit message. Verification: attribution/claude-stream/call-agent/git/git-write focused tests green (37 tests) and npx tsc --noEmit clean.

items.title: Concurrent session management — track multiple running another agent instances
items.summary: another agent tracks all running instances (session ID → PID → timestamp). On startup, dead sessions are cleaned up. Session switching triggers onSessionSwitch callbacks. Prevents stale session locks. Cross-platform process health check.
items.done: Multiple another agent instances run without conflicts; dead session entries cleaned on startup; isProcessRunning checks prevent orphaned state; session IDs unique.

items.title: Prompt hook type — hooks backed by a single LLM query
items.summary: another agent supports a 'prompt' hook type: each hook event fires a single non-streaming LLM query (small/fast model). The hook receives the event JSON as `$ARGUMENTS` substituted into its prompt, and returns a structured `{decision, reason}` response. Lighter-weight than agent hooks — useful for classification/labeling tasks without multi-turn logic.
items.done: A hook configured with `type: prompt` fires one LLM call per event. Returns a structured `{decision: 'block'|'allow', reason}` that determines hook behavior.
items.notes: Already shipped by VANTA-HOOK-TYPES: hook type enum includes prompt; runPromptHook(hook,ctx,{provider}) evaluates a provider prompt + returns block/allow. Verified live + tested.

items.title: Frontmatter hooks — agents and skills declare hooks in YAML frontmatter
items.summary: another agent agents and skills can declare hook configurations in their YAML frontmatter (`hooks:` key). These hooks are automatically registered when the agent/skill activates and deregistered when it exits. Sub-agent Stop hooks are converted to SubagentStop events. Session hooks share the same lifecycle as the agent.
items.done: A skill with a `hooks.PreToolUse` frontmatter entry runs that hook for every tool call while the skill is active. The hook is removed when the skill session ends.
items.notes: Pure extractFrontmatterHooks (flat array OR event-keyed map, zod via the canonical ShellHookSchema, invalid dropped) + mergeFrontmatterHooks (dedupe by structural identity). Extractor built+tested; load-site = SessionStart hook assembly per active skill — needs the skill frontmatter re-parsed retaining the hooks key (parseMeta strips it today). Named follow-up.

items.title: Once-only hooks — `once: true` hooks auto-deregister after first fire
items.summary: another agent skill hooks support a `once: true` flag that causes the hook to automatically deregister itself after its first successful execution. Useful for one-shot setup hooks (e.g. 'on the first tool call after this skill activates, do X') without polluting subsequent tool calls with unnecessary hook processing.
items.done: A skill hook with `once: true` fires exactly once, then disappears from the hook registry. Subsequent tool calls in the same session don't trigger it.
items.notes: Already shipped by VANTA-HOOK-TYPES: shell-hooks.ts once:z.boolean().optional() + shell-hook-run.ts seenOnce() skips a once-hook after first fire ([hook skipped: once]). Verified live + tested.

items.title: Hook exit code semantics — exit 0 silent, exit 2 blocks + shows model, other shows user
items.summary: another agent defines specific semantics for hook exit codes: exit 0 = success (stdout/stderr not shown), exit 2 = show stderr to model AND block the tool call (the model sees the message and can change strategy), any other non-zero = show stderr to user only but continue with the tool call. This gives hook authors precise control over whether the model or user sees the hook's output.
items.done: A PreToolUse hook returning exit 2 with a message causes the tool call to be blocked and the model receives the hook's stderr as a constraint.

items.title: CLI auto-updater — version notification + background update with semver comparison
items.summary: another agent's AutoUpdater/AutoUpdaterWrapper components check for new CLI versions and notify the user via a bottom-right notification. `useUpdateNotification` tracks the last-notified semver to avoid repeat notifications for the same version. The update is downloaded in the background and the user is prompted to restart.
items.done: When a new version is available, a notification appears in the corner. User can dismiss or restart to apply the update.
items.notes: Pure compareSemver/isUpdateAvailable/buildUpdateNotice/checkForUpdate (injected fetch, fail-closed) + handleUpdate (prints notice + upgrade command, never auto-runs). runUpdateCommand exported but NOT wired — the `update` CLI name is already taken by an existing command; a follow-up can wire it as a subcommand or `vanta version --check`. Live npm-registry URL is illustrative (package is private).

items.title: Deferred SessionStart hooks — async hook execution without blocking REPL startup
items.summary: another agent's `useDeferredHookMessages` runs SessionStart hooks asynchronously so the REPL renders immediately (~500ms savings). Hook messages are stored in a pending promise and merged into the conversation before the first API request (ensuring the model always sees hook context). The deferred pattern avoids REPL startup blocking on slow hooks.
items.done: SessionStart hooks with slow shell commands (~500ms+) don't delay the initial REPL render. Their output appears as the first hook attachment when the user sends their first message.

items.title: Memory usage monitor — high/critical heap warnings at 1.5GB / 2.5GB
items.summary: another agent polls Node.js heap usage every 10 seconds via `useMemoryUsage`. At 1.5GB (high) or 2.5GB (critical) thresholds, a visible warning appears so the user can /compact or restart before hitting an OOM crash. Returns null while normal so there's no UI noise in typical sessions.
items.done: When heap reaches 1.5GB, a warning appears in the status area. At 2.5GB it escalates to critical with a recommendation to /compact.
items.notes: Pure classifyHeap/buildMemoryWarning/checkMemory NOW LIVE: runPostTurnGates calls memWarnGate (checkMemory with readHeap=process.memoryUsage().heapUsed, cooldown on GateState.memWarnLastAt) best-effort each turn, emits the HIGH(>=1.5GB)/CRITICAL(>=2.5GB) warning via the shared note sink. Injectable heap seam for tests. Below-threshold/within-60s-cooldown = silent.

items.title: Token budget directives — parse +500k / use 2M tokens from user messages
items.summary: another agent parses token budget directives from user messages: shorthand at start/end (`+500k`, `+2M`) and verbose anywhere (`use 500k tokens`, `spend 2M tokens`). Parsed budgets are passed to workflow/agent execution as spending caps. Supports k/M/B multipliers with decimal values.
items.done: User types 'Review all these files +500k' — the session sets a 500,000-token budget ceiling. `budget.total` and `budget.remaining()` are available to orchestration code.
items.notes: Pure parseTokenBudget(text) (+500k/+2M/use 2M tokens/budget N tokens/1.5m; requires a k/m suffix OR the word token(s); $/bare-number/0 -> null). Unwired this slice; intended pre-turn consumer = interactive-turn.ts where the sent text is read (alongside mode-detect/clarity gates).

items.title: Parallel tool execution — concurrent read-only tool calls with configurable cap
items.summary: another agent's tool orchestration batches tool calls marked `isConcurrencySafe` and runs them concurrently (up to `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY`, default 10). Read-only operations (Glob, Grep, Read, WebSearch, WebFetch) run in parallel; write/mutating tools run sequentially. Speeds up multi-file reads and search-heavy turns significantly.
items.done: A turn with 5 parallel Glob calls completes in ~200ms instead of ~1s. `VANTA_MAX_TOOL_CONCURRENCY=10` controls the cap.
items.notes: Pure planToolExecution (consecutive concurrency-safe reads batch up to cap; mutating calls solo+ordered) + resolveParallelCap (VANTA_PARALLEL_TOOLS, default 1=sequential, clamp [1,8]) + reassembleResults (restores call order) + isConcurrencySafe (reuses CONCURRENCY_SAFE_TOOLS). Planner built+tested; wire = turn-loop.ts processToolCalls for-loop (named follow-up; default cap 1 = byte-identical current behavior).

items.title: VCR record/replay — record API request/response cassettes for test replay
items.summary: another agent has a VCR (cassette recorder) mode that records all API requests and responses to disk when `FORCE_VCR=1` (for ants) or in `NODE_ENV=test`. Cassettes are keyed by content hash; on replay, matching requests return cached responses instantly without hitting the API. Used in integration tests and for debugging.
items.done: `VANTA_VCR=1` records a session's API calls to `.vanta/cassettes/`. Re-running with the same inputs replays from cassette without network calls.
items.notes: Pure cassette (requestKey sha256 body-hash, record/find, tolerant load/save) + makeVcrFetch({off|record|replay}) + resolveVcrMode(VANTA_VCR). off=passthrough; replay never hits network (hit or miss). NOT wired into live fetch — follow-up wraps each provider fetch (providers/openai.ts etc.) so a suite can record once + replay offline.

items.title: API preconnect — pre-warm TCP+TLS during startup for 100-200ms first-request savings
items.summary: another agent fires a fire-and-forget fetch to the Anthropic API endpoint during init (after CA certs and proxy are configured), warming the TCP+TLS connection pool before the first real request. Bun's keep-alive pool reuses the connection. In `-p` mode this saves ~100-200ms; in interactive mode the savings are the full 'user is typing' window. Runs AFTER settings.json env vars are applied so proxy/mTLS settings are respected.
items.done: First API request in a session completes 100-200ms faster because the connection was pre-warmed during startup.

items.title: Adaptive thinking mode — let model self-budget tokens + `ultrathink` max-budget keyword
items.summary: another agent supports three thinking modes: `adaptive` (model decides how much thinking budget to use), `enabled` with a fixed `budgetTokens` value, and `disabled`. The `ultrathink` keyword in a prompt triggers maximum thinking budget (feature-gated). Users control mode via /think toggle or settings. Adaptive mode avoids token waste on simple tasks while allowing deep reasoning on complex ones.
items.done: `/think` cycles through disabled → adaptive → max. Typing 'ultrathink' in a prompt activates maximum extended thinking for that turn only.
items.notes: Pure hasUltrathinkTrigger/stripUltrathinkTrigger/ultrathinkEffortLevel (max) + adaptive-effort (additive adaptive level → self-budget sentinel; existing levels unchanged). Detectors built+tested; consumers = mode-detect pre-turn host bumps effort to max on the keyword + providers/effort.ts reads the adaptive self-budget disposition (named follow-up).

items.title: Hook timing indicator — show elapsed time when hooks take >500ms
items.summary: another agent shows a timing indicator in the hook progress display when a hook takes longer than `HOOK_TIMING_DISPLAY_THRESHOLD_MS` (500ms). Helps users identify slow hooks that are delaying tool execution, with elapsed time shown as e.g. '1.2s' in the hook progress pill.
items.done: A PreToolUse hook that takes 800ms shows '800ms' next to the hook name in the tool call progress display.

items.title: Undercover mode — safety strip for public/open-source repo contributions
items.summary: another agent's 'undercover mode' activates when working in public/external repos (auto-detects unless repo is in internal allowlist, or `CLAUDE_CODE_UNDERCOVER=1`). When active: adds safety instructions to commit/PR prompts to avoid leaking internal codenames or Anthropic-internal context, and strips all commit attribution. The model is not told its own identity.
items.done: Working in a public GitHub repo, another agent adds a safety caveat to generated commit messages and omits model attribution. `VANTA_UNDERCOVER=1` forces it on.
items.notes: Pure undercoverEnabled (VANTA_UNDERCOVER=1, off by default) + stripInternalMarkers (drops inline .vanta/ paths, internal card-id tags, Vanta provenance/Co-Authored-By lines; tidies whitespace; leaves prose + real identifiers intact) + applyUndercover. Cleaning internal bookkeeping, NOT impersonation. Stripper built+tested; consumers = batch buildGhPrArgs --body + the git-commit message build site (named follow-up).

items.title: /env — set session-scoped environment variables for child processes
items.summary: another agent has an `/env` command that sets session-scoped environment variables. These are stored in `sessionEnvVars` and applied only to spawned child processes (via the bash provider env overrides) — NOT to the REPL process itself. Useful for temporarily setting API keys, feature flags, or tool configuration without modifying shell profile.
items.done: `/env FOO=bar` sets FOO for subsequent bash tool calls in the session. `/env` with no args shows current session env vars. Vars are cleared on session end.
items.notes: /env set|unset|list + session-env store + applySessionEnv merge into shell_cmd child spawns (no session env = byte-identical, kernel still gates). run_code currently inherits process.env without the session-env merge — extending the merge to run_code is a small follow-up.

items.title: Privacy levels — default/no-telemetry/essential-traffic to control network traffic
items.summary: another agent supports three privacy levels (ordered by restrictiveness): `default` (everything enabled), `no-telemetry` (disables analytics/telemetry but other network traffic allowed), and `essential-traffic` (only API calls — no telemetry, no update checks, no marketplace refreshes, no bootstrap). Configurable via settings or `CLAUDE_CODE_PRIVACY_LEVEL`.
items.done: `VANTA_PRIVACY=no-telemetry` disables all usage tracking. `essential-traffic` mode blocks all non-API network calls for air-gapped environments.
items.notes: PrivacyLevel (default|no-telemetry|essential) + resolvePrivacyLevel (env>settings>default) + pure isAllowed(category,level) matrix + settings.privacyLevel field. PRIVACY_CALL_SITES names the provider/kernel/search/fetch/update/other sites; gating each network path with isAllowed is the follow-up (Vanta ships no telemetry sender today). Default = unchanged.

items.title: Interleaved thinking — thinking tokens between tool calls, not just before response
items.summary: another agent supports an `interleaved_thinking` capability mode where extended thinking tokens are interleaved between tool calls, not just in the initial response block. This allows the model to reason after each tool result before deciding the next action. Listed alongside `thinking`, `adaptive_thinking`, `effort`, and `max_effort` as a model capability override.
items.done: With interleaved thinking enabled, thinking blocks appear in the transcript after each tool result as the model reasons about next steps.

items.title: Model tier env overrides — pin opus/sonnet/haiku to specific model versions
items.summary: another agent reads `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL`, and `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` env vars to pin each model tier to a specific version. Combined with `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL_SUPPORTED_CAPABILITIES` to declare what capabilities the override model supports. Useful for enterprise deployments pinning to a specific API version.
items.done: `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6` pins the sonnet alias to that exact version. `/model` and startup banner reflect the override.

items.title: Shadowed permission rule detection — warn about unreachable allow/deny rules
items.summary: another agent's `detectUnreachableRules()` analyzes the permission rule set and identifies rules that will never match because a broader rule with the same tool already shadows them. Surfaced as a `ContextWarning` in `/doctor`. Prevents confusion where users add rules that silently have no effect because an existing `Allow Bash(*)` already covers them.
items.done: `/doctor` reports 'Rule Allow Bash(ls) is unreachable — shadowed by Allow Bash(*)'. Helps users maintain a clean permission set.
items.notes: findShadowedRules (author-intent lint: a later narrower rule whose target space an earlier broader rule fully covers, + exact dups) + buildShadowWarning; wired into /permissions list (silent when clean). Note: live resolver is specificity-based not pure declaration-order — detector flags intent-shadowing robustly regardless.

items.title: Auto tool-search mode — defer tools only when they exceed the context threshold
items.summary: another agent supports `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto` (tst-auto mode): tools are only deferred to ToolSearch when their total schema size exceeds `getAutoToolSearchCharThreshold(model)`. Below the threshold, all tools are exposed inline as usual. `auto:50` enables for 50% of sessions for gradual rollout. Prevents unnecessary overhead for simple sessions while still protecting context on complex ones.
items.done: With `VANTA_TOOL_SEARCH=auto`, simple sessions see all tools inline. Sessions with many plugins/MCP tools automatically defer the overflow to ToolSearch.

items.title: `--bare` mode — minimal output format for scripting and pipelines
items.summary: another agent supports a `--bare` CLI flag that enables bare mode: minimal output formatting suitable for scripting and pipes. In bare mode, decorative UI elements (spinners, boxes, colors, status line) are stripped — only the essential response text is output. Complements `--print` / non-interactive mode.
items.done: `claude --bare 'summarize this file' < file.txt` outputs only the summary text, no terminal UI chrome. Suitable for shell pipelines.
items.notes: Pure bareEnabled (--bare in argv OR VANTA_BARE=1, default off) + BareEvent union {text|tool|result|decoration} + formatBareEvent (text/result -> stripped+trimmed+newline, empty dropped; tool -> [tool] name minimal; decoration banner/cost/spinner -> suppressed) + bareLines (joined non-empty). Reuses bash-io stripKeepNewlines (newly EXPORTED, additive) -> strips ALL ANSI/CSI/OSC/C0/C1/DEL so a tool result cant inject escapes into a downstream pipe (machine-safe). Built+tested; wire = runInstruction routes to bare callbacks when bareEnabled instead of buildCallbacks rich (named). Default = current rich output.

items.title: Prompt cache break detection — detect invalidation, trigger microcompact cleanup
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `PROMPT_CACHE_BREAK_DETECTION`) detects when the Anthropic API prompt cache is invalidated (cache miss after a hit sequence). On detection, triggers cleanup: subagents are notified via `notifyCompaction`, and /compact can respond reactively. Prevents unnecessary re-billing from repeated cache misses. Feature flag: `BREAK_CACHE_COMMAND`.
items.done: When the cache breaks mid-session, a microcompact runs to shrink the prompt before the next request, avoiding a large uncached prefix resend.
items.notes: Pure stablePrefixHash (sha256) + detectCacheBreak + cacheBreakState (threaded, first-seen=no break, A->B->A breaks each transition). Detector built+tested; wire follow-up: buildSystemPrompt per-turn holds CacheBreakState, on break triggers time-microcompact clearStaleToolResults.

items.title: --dump-system-prompt — CLI flag to print the assembled system prompt and exit
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `DUMP_SYSTEM_PROMPT`) adds a `--dump-system-prompt` flag. Running another agent with this flag assembles the full system prompt (including operator/user layers, tool descriptions, context attachments) and prints it to stdout without starting a session.
items.done: Running `claude --dump-system-prompt` prints the full 3-tier system prompt to stdout. Useful for debugging operator prompt injection.

items.title: `/cd` — change working directory mid-session without breaking prompt cache
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.169) adds `/cd <path>` to move the active session to a new directory. Unlike shell `cd`, it updates another agent's root context and tool paths without invalidating the prompt cache, preserving accumulated context.
items.done: Running `/cd ~/other-project` mid-session switches tool resolution to the new root. The prompt cache remains intact.

items.title: `PermissionDenied` hook — fire on auto-mode classifier denials with retry option
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.89) adds a `PermissionDenied` hook that fires after auto-mode classifier denials. Returning `{retry: true}` tells Claude to try an alternative approach instead of stopping. Enables operator-controlled recovery from permission blocks.
items.done: A PermissionDenied hook logs the denied action and returns `{retry: true}`. Claude rewrites the command to avoid the denied pattern.

items.title: Hook exec form — `args: string[]` spawns hook directly without a shell
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.139) adds `args: string[]` to hook config (exec form). The hook binary is spawned directly without a shell, so path placeholders don't need escaping and the command can't be injection-bypassed via shell metacharacters.
items.done: A hook configured as `{command: '/usr/bin/notify', args: ['--title', '$TOOL_NAME']}` spawns directly. No shell wrapping, no injection risk.

items.title: Hook `continueOnBlock` — feed PostToolUse rejection reason back to model
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.139) adds `continueOnBlock: true` to `PostToolUse` hook config. When the hook blocks a tool, instead of hard-stopping, the rejection reason is fed back to Claude as a tool result so it can adapt its approach.
items.done: A PostToolUse hook blocks `rm -rf` and sets `continueOnBlock: true`. Claude receives 'destructive rm blocked' as the tool result and chooses a safer alternative.

items.title: `sandbox.network.deniedDomains` — block specific domains under a broader allowlist
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.113) adds `sandbox.network.deniedDomains` to deny specific domains even when a broader `allowedDomains` wildcard would otherwise permit them. Enables allowlist + denylist layering: allow `*.api.com` but deny `internal.api.com`.
items.done: With `allowedDomains: ['*.api.com']` and `deniedDomains: ['internal.api.com']`, requests to `internal.api.com` are blocked.
items.notes: Pure isDomainDenied (exact+subdomain) + networkDecision (deny-wins) + sandbox deniedDomains setting + resolveNetworkAccess decision site. Note: the OS sandbox is coarse net on/off; this is the per-host policy + persisted setting + decision fn — enforcing per-domain deny at runtime needs a proxy (bounded follow-up).

items.title: Ultracode trigger keyword — `ultracode` in prompt launches dynamic workflow

items.title: Hook `sessionTitle` output — `UserPromptSubmit` hooks can set the session title
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.94) adds `hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle` to `UserPromptSubmit` hook output. A hook can return a title string that becomes the session's name. Useful for auto-titling sessions based on the first prompt's content or intent.

Extractor built+tested; consumption = the 3 UserPromptSubmit fireHooks sites parse stdout -> applyHookTitle -> saveSession({title}) like /title (named follow-up).

items.title: `/less-permission-prompts` — scan transcript and propose read-only allow rules

items.title: `/proactive` alias for `/loop` — proactive autonomy mode discoverability

items.title: `.husky` in acceptEdits protected dirs — git hooks directory is write-protected

items.title: Monitor tool — reactive line-by-line command monitoring
items.summary: another agent has a Monitor tool that runs a shell command in the background and fires a callback on each output line, enabling event-driven loops (watch a build, react to log lines, stream deploy output). Vanta runs BG tasks but has no line-by-line reactive tool.
items.done: A `monitor` tool runs a command and routes each output line as a tool result; the agent can act on each line before the process finishes.
items.notes: Pure parseMonitorPatterns (string ERROR -> alert pattern; object validated, label/kind defaults, garbage dropped, PATTERN_MAX 200) + matchLine (ANSI-stripped line, LITERAL substring test, no patterns/no match -> []) + accumulateMatch (append matches, bump per-label counts, set ready on a ready-pattern; pure) + summarizeMonitor (✓ ready · N alerts · …). SECURITY: literal substring NOT regex = O(n·m) no backtrack so a hostile (a+)+$ cant ReDoS (verified 5000 a in <100ms); LINE_MAX 4000. Reuses bash-io stripKeepNewlines. Built+tested; wire = a monitor tool / shell-cmd streaming feeds each output line through accumulateMatch (named, process stream = boundary). No patterns = none.

items.title: --exec — run a shell command as a background job alongside the session
items.summary: another agent's `--exec <cmd>` starts a shell command as a tracked background job concurrent with the agent session (e.g., a dev server, a test watcher). The agent can monitor its output. Vanta has no way to co-launch a background process with a session.
items.done: `--exec <cmd>` starts the command before the first turn; its output is available to the Monitor tool; the process is tracked and killed on session end.
items.notes: Pure parseExecFlags (extract every --exec <cmd>, rest unchanged, trailing-no-value ignored) + buildExecBgPlan + launchExecBg (injected bg-task starter, best-effort, executes nothing itself). Built+tested. WIRE DEFERRED for SECURITY: the auto-launch must go through kernel assess() per command, but startInteractive has no kernel client until prepareRun runs inside runChat — so the launch must move INSIDE runChat (after prepareRun creates safety) and assess() each --exec command before spawnBackground. An initial wire that called spawnBackground at startup ungated was reverted (Rule Zero — never execute a shell command without the kernel gate). Reuses bg-tasks machinery.

items.title: --exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections — prompt cache optimization
items.summary: another agent's `--exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections` strips volatile sections (goals, time, current memory) from the cache-prefix portion of the system prompt, dramatically improving LLM cache hit rate on long sessions. Vanta's prompt has stable + volatile tiers (PROMPT-STABILITY) but no per-section exclusion flag.
items.done: `--exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections <list>` (or comma-sep env `VANTA_EXCLUDE_PROMPT_SECTIONS`) moves specified tiers out of the stable cache prefix; the cost footer shows estimated cache-hit improvement.

Vanta's memory is always on with no per-instance controls.

items.title: Organize-code tool — agent-callable file decomposition
items.summary: Steal rocket.new '/Organize Code': an agent-callable refactor tool that splits an oversized file into cohesive modules, operationalizing the size gate (lint/size.ts, file<=300/fn<=50) as an ACTION the agent can take, not just a warn-only lint. Behavior-preserving extraction, test-gated. (Exactly the app.tsx decomposition done by hand 2026-06-10.)
items.done: The agent can call organize_code on a file over the size limit and get back a behavior-preserving multi-file split that passes the existing tests.
items.notes: Pure ExtractSuggestion{symbolName,kind,lineCount,targetModule} + analyzeDecomposition (REUSES analyzeSource/LIMITS as the single size source; walks sf.statements via TS compiler API for top-level exported fn/class/const/interface/type/enum + line spans; overGate from the file violation) + planDecomposition (greedy largest-first extract until remaining <= LIMITS.file; co-located target names <basename>-<group>.ts; [] when not over gate) + formatOrganizePlan (Extract N decls to get <file> under <limit> + per-decl bullets + the export * re-export reminder + This is a plan only — no files changed). PLAN ONLY — read-only analysis, NO file write; the extraction is a named kernel-gated operator/agent edit. Built+tested; wire = an organize_code tool (describeForSafety read-only -> Allow) returns formatOrganizePlan as a PROPOSAL, agent then does kernel-gated write_file edits (named). Under gate = [].

items.title: Swarm permission routing — workers forward permission asks to lead agent
items.summary: Swarm workers serialise permission-ask actions and forward them to the lead agent via mailbox IPC. Poll every 500 ms for allow/reject response. Schema-validates incoming PermissionUpdate entries; drops malformed ones. Coordinator workers run hooks + classifier sequentially before falling through to the interactive dialog.
items.done: Worker agent permission asks appear in the lead agent's permission queue; lead's allow/reject propagates back to the worker within ~500 ms; malformed entries logged and dropped.
items.notes: Pure immutable router: ForwardedAsk{askId,workerId,request,askedAt} + buildForwardedAsk (mirrors permissions/request shape, carried verbatim) + PermRouterState{pending[]} + enqueueAsk (append, dedupe by askId so a re-forwarded A2A send doesnt double-queue) + routeDecision (find+remove the ask -> {state, target:{workerId,decision}|null}; unknown/already-resolved askId -> unchanged + null) + pendingForWorker + nextPendingAsk (oldest by askedAt). SECURITY: FORWARD NOT BYPASS — kernel assess already ran (the ask is real) + the lead still surfaces a real operator prompt; never auto-approves. Concurrent workers queue independently. Built+tested; wire = worker requestApproval buildForwardedAsk -> A2ABus -> lead enqueue+nextPendingAsk (one prompt) -> routeDecision back (named, transport+prompt = boundary).

items.title: Team memory directory — shared memory namespace for swarm agents

Store built+tested; swarm read/append points named = fleet executeWorker (read digest into instruction on start, append outcome on done/blocked, null-gated on resolveTeamId).

items.title: Magic Docs — auto-maintained markdown files updated after each turn
items.summary: Any .md file starting with `# MAGIC DOC: [title]` is automatically updated by a background subagent after each turn that contains tool calls. The doc accumulates learnings from the conversation (architecture, decisions, patterns) without interrupting the main flow. Uses post-sampling hook + sequential update guard.

Built+tested; post-turn wire = runPostTurnGates resolves magicDocs + builds summary from where.ts/closure-gate + writes (named, best-effort like voice/proactive). No docs = no writes.

items.title: Coordinator mode — env-controlled lead-agent persona with restricted tool set

Built+tested; consumers = buildRegistry({exclude}) (the same seam subagents use to drop delegate) + prompt persona note, both gated on coordinatorEnabled (named). Off = full set + normal persona.

Multi-language via the LSP server manager (not just TS). ENABLE_LSP_TOOL env var gates it.

Reuse the exported buildService factory + pure mappers; real TS-service calls tested. tools.test exact name-list updated; doc tool counts corrected to the re-counted live surface 116 built-in / 119 registered. Existing diagnostics/definition unchanged.

items.summary: Suite of agent-accessible task management tools for the TodoV2 task system: TaskCreate (spawn background task), TaskGet (check status), TaskList (list all tasks), TaskUpdate (update title/description), TaskStop (cancel task), TaskOutput (stream task output). Enables agent orchestration of background work.

Built+tested; Task* tool family (register in all-tools, deps from ~/.vanta/tasks.json) = named follow-up.

items.title: Shell snapshot — capture aliases, functions, PATH for shell completions
items.summary: Shell snapshot captures the user's current shell environment (PATH entries, aliases, functions) by running a probe script in the user's shell. The snapshot feeds the shell completion engine in the typeahead. Uses embedded ripgrep/bfs/ugrep via argv0 dispatch for completions.

READ-ONLY capture — captured aliases/functions never executed. Built+tested; consumer = a setup/prepareRun step runs it with a real execFile runner + persists ~/.vanta/shell-snapshot.json; the completion UI card reads it (named). Live shell = boundary.

Built+tested; wire = delegate runDelegate resolves agent_type + passes the complement as buildRegistry exclude + injects persona (named, kernel still gates).

Remote memory dir for cloud (CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_MEMORY_DIR). Colons in plugin-namespaced types replaced with dashes for path safety.

Mirrors team-memory. Store built+tested; spawn read/append points named (subagent spawnSubagent, null-gated).

items.title: In-process swarm backend — agents share Node.js process via AsyncLocalStorage
items.summary: Alternative to tmux backend: agents run in same Node.js process with isolated context via AsyncLocalStorage. Share API client and MCP connections. File-based mailbox IPC. Killed via AbortController. Enables swarms without tmux dependency.
items.done: Swarm agents spawn in-process when tmux unavailable; share API/MCP connections; isolated via AsyncLocalStorage; mailbox IPC works same as tmux backend.
items.notes: WorkerContext{workerId,goal?,dataDir?,scopeRoot?} backed by a module-level node:async_hooks AsyncLocalStorage. runInWorkerContext(ctx,fn)=store.run (returns fns promise, errors propagate + auto-unwind) + currentWorkerContext/currentWorkerId (undefined outside) + isInWorkerContext + withWorkerOverride (merge current+partial, nested auto-restore). CONCURRENT no-bleed PROVEN: Promise.all of two interleaved staggered-await runs each read their own id at every checkpoint + a 12-worker test + a failing-worker-throw-doesnt-corrupt-a-concurrent-healthy-one test. ALS = ISOLATION/observability ONLY, NOT a security boundary — kernel assess still gates every worker tool call. Built+tested; wire = spawn runWorker wraps convo.send in runInWorkerContext({workerId,goal,dataDir}) so concurrent in-process workers isolate + perm-routing/logging read currentWorkerId (named). Outside = undefined.

items.title: Read-only command validation maps — git/gh/shell safe-flag allowlists
items.summary: Comprehensive allowlist maps for read-only validation: GIT_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS (all git subcommands + safe flags + danger callbacks), GH_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS (gh CLI read-only ops), EXTERNAL_READONLY_COMMANDS (cross-shell). containsVulnerableUncPath detects Windows UNC paths that can leak credentials.
items.done: In read-only mode, only allowlisted git/gh commands pass; any unsafe flags rejected; UNC paths blocked; PowerShell and bash share the same validation logic.
items.notes: Pure classifier: READ_ONLY_GIT/GH/SHELL allowlists + isReadOnlyCommand/classifyCommand (read-only|unknown|mutating). Conservative — any pipe/redirect/&&/;/backtick/$()/process-sub anywhere -> never read-only (a mutation behind a safe head can never pass). Built+tested; consumer = auto-mode classifyAutoModeAction for shell_cmd (may only LOOSEN an ask, never a block) + less-perms seeding (named).

items.title: Bash security blocks — process substitution, Zsh expansions, heredoc injection
items.summary: BashTool blocks dangerous shell patterns: process substitution `<()` `>()` `$()` `${}`, Zsh equals expansion `=cmd` (bypasses deny rules), heredoc in substitution, malformed token detection, PowerShell comment syntax. Each pattern has a specific error message. Defense-in-depth on top of the permission system.
items.done: Commands using `$(cmd)` are blocked with a specific error; `=curl` is blocked explaining Zsh equals expansion; all patterns surface the reason they're blocked.
items.notes: Pure detectShellInjection -> {flagged,risks[]} for 4 classes: process-substitution <()/>(), command-substitution-payload $()/backtick wrapping a destructive/exec token, heredoc-injection (<< feeding a shell/eval/exfil sink, not a plain-file redirect), zsh-glob-qualifier (N)/(om) on a destructive op. Conservative/low-FP (plain ls, echo $(date), benign heredoc NOT flagged). ADDITIVE to the kernel — never weakens it. Built+tested; surface = shell-cmd describeForSafety appends risks so assess() escalates (named).

items.title: Fork subagent — implicit context inheritance when subagent_type omitted
items.summary: When the Agent tool is called without `subagent_type`, it forks the current conversation: the child inherits the parent's full context and system prompt. All spawns run async for unified task-notification model. `/fork <directive>` slash command. Mutually exclusive with coordinator mode.
items.done: Calling Agent without subagent_type spawns a fork with full parent context; `/fork fix the lint errors` creates a focused fork; task-notification pattern used for all async agents.
items.notes: Pure isForkSpawn (undefined/empty/fork -> true) + buildForkPreamble (caps last 6 user/assistant turns, gisted 200-char, system/tool excluded so no secret dump) + resolveSubagentSeed (fork -> preamble+instruction; typed -> instruction only = current behavior). Built+tested; call points named = delegate runDelegate + spawn runWorker pass parent context through it when type omitted (clarity-gate pattern). Explicit type unchanged.

items.title: Stalled spinner animation — visual state change when agent is stuck

Empty text -> []. Mirrors spinner-stalled/verbs. Built+tested; busy wire = app-regions LiveRegion renders bright runs whiteBright + normal dim when glimmerEnabled, else plainSegments (named). Off = plain.

items.summary: another agent's ShellTimeDisplay + ShellProgressMessage show real elapsed time for each shell command, updating live during execution. Helps identify slow commands, understand build times, and debug performance issues. Vanta shows no timing.

items.summary: another agent renders UserChannelMessage as a visually distinct message type showing the source platform (Telegram, Slack, etc.) and sender alongside the message content. Vanta's gateway handles incoming messages (E2 shipped) but they're rendered as plain user text.

items.notes: Pure formatChannelMessage(msg, platform, opts) + channelMessagePrefix (telegram/discord glyphs, unknown -> generic 📨) + sanitizeForLine. SECURITY: strips ANSI/CSI/SGR/OSC + control chars to one line (no terminal escape injection), then truncates to 280 chars + ellipsis. platform passed separately (InboundMessage has no platform field; comes from adapter.id at pollPlatform). Built+tested; surface = gateway/run.ts pollPlatform (format each InboundMessage) + ui/transcript NoteView (named).

items.title: Effort indicator — visual effort level callout in TUI

Default medium/unset = no chip (byte-identical to before); render-tested via the Ink harness. VANTA_EFFORT_INDICATOR forces medium visible.

items.title: BypassPermissions confirmation dialog — explicit gate for dangerous mode
items.summary: another agent shows BypassPermissionsModeDialog when entering bypassPermissions mode, requiring explicit confirmation and showing a risk summary. Prevents accidental activation of the most permissive mode. Vanta allows mode switching without a dedicated confirmation step.
items.done: Switching to bypass/bypassPermissions mode shows a modal: risk summary, list of what will be allowed without prompts, explicit confirmation; confirmed mode is shown with a persistent 'dangerous' badge.

SECURITY (both invariants asserted hard): (1) un-enterable without the exact typed token, (2) bypassAutoApproves(block)===false even when active = immovable kernel block floor. Built+tested; wire = permission-mode requires resolveBypassActivation before flipping to bypass + approval path calls bypassAutoApproves never on a block (mirrors auto-mode block short-circuit). No confirm = off.

items.title: Cost threshold dialog — alert when approaching budget cap
items.summary: another agent's CostThresholdDialog alerts when cumulative session cost approaches a threshold (80% and 100% of --max-budget-usd). Pairs with VANTA-BUDGET-CAP (next). Without this UI, the agent silently stops at budget without user awareness.
items.done: At 80% of the budget cap, a non-blocking warning shows in the status; at 100%, a dialog offers to continue with a new cap, stop now, or ignore; actual stop behavior is configurable.
items.notes: Pure CostAlertLevel none|warning|exceeded + CostAlertState{lastAlerted} + costAlertLevel (REUSES budget statusFor DEFAULT_WARN_FRACTION 0.8: <80% none, >=80% warning, >=100% exceeded; non-positive/non-finite limit -> none) + nextCostAlert (alert ONLY on escalation past lastAlerted via LEVEL_RANK, immutable; warning once then exceeded once, same/lower -> null) + formatCostAlert (⚠ approaching budget $X of $Y (80%) / ✖ budget cap reached $Y — actions may pause). WARNING surface BEFORE the existing PCLIP-BUDGET-HARDSTOP. Built+tested; wire = post-turn-gates runPostTurnGates adds a costAlert GateState field reading sessionCost + getBudget cap -> nextCostAlert -> onNote (named; cost footer secondary). No cap = none; same crossing never re-alerts.

items.title: Terminal window title — session name / active task in title bar
items.summary: another agent's use-terminal-title.ts sets the terminal window/tab title to the current session name or active task, so users can identify Vanta sessions in a terminal multiplexer or across multiple windows. Vanta sets no terminal title.
items.done: The terminal title updates to 'Vanta — <session name>' at session start and '<active task>' while working; resets to 'Vanta' on idle; disabled with an env var.

SECURITY: a goal name cannot inject a second escape (tests assert one OSC opener + one BEL). Built+tested; wire = interactive.ts runChat on session start + setNewGoal on active-task change (named).

items.title: Status notices — notices area above the status line

Separate from the status line so notices don't clutter the primary info. Vanta has no notices layer.

Built+tested; render point = ui/app.tsx renders visibleNotices as dim rows immediately above <StatusBar/> (named). No notices = empty render.

items.title: ThinkingToggle — dedicated expand/collapse control for reasoning

items.title: ShutdownMessage — graceful session end display

items.title: /copy — copy last response as markdown, HTML, or RTF to clipboard

Built+tested; wire = existing /copy handler (media-cmds.ts) calls resolveCopyFormat(arg)+formatForCopy before the pbcopy write + catalog desc /copy [md|html|rtf] (named). Unknown = md.

Absent/blank activeForm or pending/done = byte-identical display.

Empty/unicode ids safe. Built+tested; fleet-UI wire = ui/teammate-tree buildTeammateTree rows + ui/task-rows /agents panel colored by teammateColor(id) (named). Presentation-only.

Built+tested; execute point = a /mcp <server> enable|disable|reconnect handler runs resolveMcpAction then dispatches the plan to mount.ts mountOneServer/kill+unregister (named). Pure, no IO.

Print-only — handler returns {output} only, NO file writes (asserted). Registered in catalog+handlers (169 lines).

items.title: Away summary — auto-generate context recap when terminal regains focus after 5+ min

The model sees this as context when the user returns, reducing the need for manual context re-establishment.

Built+tested; trigger = next-user-submit reads the proactive last-activity stamp (post-turn-gates markProactiveActivity) + builds the recap from where.ts breadcrumb (named). Under threshold = nothing.

items.title: OS system notification — desktop alert after long tool runs when terminal is inactive

Uses `sendNotification` via the system notifier. Also respects terminal focus events via `useTerminalNotification`.

Built+tested; loop point = agent dispatchTool post-tool with the proactive inactivity signal + tool label (named). Fast/active/disabled = no notify.

items.title: Copy-on-select — auto-copy terminal text selection to clipboard (iTerm2-style)

items.notes: Pure TerminalKind + detectTerminal (mirrors osc8 isKnownHyperlinkTerm signatures: TERM_PROGRAM iterm/apple_terminal, WEZTERM_PANE, WT_SESSION, KITTY_WINDOW_ID/TERM, VTE_VERSION>=5000; more-specific first; none -> unknown) + copyOnSelectInstructions (REAL honest per-terminal steps: iTerm2 Settings>General>Selection>Copy to pasteboard on selection; Kitty copy_on_select yes in kitty.conf; Windows Terminal copyOnSelect:true; WezTerm ON by default; GNOME automatic via PRIMARY; Apple Terminal NO built-in setting; unknown -> generic) + copyOnSelectHelp (detect+format). BY DESIGN help-text-only: Vantas TUI uses native terminal selection (no mouse capture) so copy-on-select is a terminal setting Vanta cant intercept. Built+tested; wire = /copy-on-select handler (mirrors terminal-setup-cmd) prints copyOnSelectHelp (named). Unknown = generic note.

No image/disabled = no hint.

items.title: AI session title — auto-generate session name via Haiku from conversation content

Default off = deriveTitle unchanged.

Reuses ui/teammate-color (same name -> same color). Built+tested; banner wire = ui/banner WORDMARK labels with identity.name in identity.color when custom (named). Unset = default Vanta/white.

items.title: Auto-mode denial history — /permissions tab showing recently blocked commands
items.summary: another agent tracks commands recently denied by the auto-mode transcript classifier (up to 20, newest-first) via `recordAutoModeDenial()`. These are surfaced in a 'RecentDenials' tab in the `/permissions` UI, showing tool name, display description, reason, and timestamp — helping users understand and tune what the classifier blocked.

items.notes: Pure/injectable extractDenials (PREFERENCE-SIGNALS human approval-deny rows -> DenialRecord{tool,action,reason,ts}, newest-first, cap 20, non-denial skipped) + formatDenials (✘ tool action — reason (relative time via message-time), control/ANSI stripped, empty -> no recent denials) + readRecentDenials (injected reader, [] on failure; defaultDeps reads ~/.vanta/preferences.jsonl read-only). READ-ONLY — never alters a decision. Built+tested; /permissions listRules append wire named.

items.title: Chrome automation GIF recorder — capture multi-step browser interactions as GIF
items.summary: During Chrome browser automation (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*` tools), another agent can record multi-step browser interactions as animated GIFs via `mcp__claude-in-chrome__gif_creator`. The agent is prompted to capture extra frames before/after actions for smooth playback, and to name the file meaningfully. GIFs are saved locally for review or sharing.

NO dep added. Labels from action data (URL/selector/typed) control-stripped (ANSI-injection guard). Built+tested; wire = browser_act when gifRecordEnabled captures one screenshot/step via screenshot tool (frame SOURCE) -> buildAssemblyPlan -> a gif encoder (DEP BOUNDARY, named). No steps = [].

items.title: Smart first-run example commands — git-history-aware prompt suggestions on empty input
items.summary: On first launch or empty-state input, another agent generates contextual example commands by sampling from git history (recent commit messages, changed files) filtered through a pattern list that removes lock files, build artifacts, generated files, and config. Examples like 'Explain the changes in <recent-file>.ts' are shown as tappable suggestions instead of generic placeholder text.
items.done: Empty input shows 3-4 smart example commands drawn from the current repo's git history. They update per-project, not per-install.
items.notes: Pure/injectable buildExampleCommands (changed file -> Review/Write tests (source only); commit subject -> Continue: <subject>; >=1 evergreen reserved; dedupe + cap 4) + genericExampleCommands (no-signal safe read-only fallback) + gatherGitSignals (injected runner, parses diff --name-only HEAD~3 + log --oneline -5, failure -> empty) + sanitizeSignal (control-strip so a hostile filename/subject cant inject escapes) + formatExamples (Try: block). Never suggests destructive verbs. Built+tested; banner/empty-input wire named (mirrors project-onboarding).

items.title: Auto-run /issue — proactive GitHub issue creation with ESC cancellation window
items.summary: When another agent detects conditions that warrant filing a GitHub issue (bug, unexpected behavior), it shows an `AutoRunIssueNotification` banner proposing to auto-run `/issue`. The user has a brief window to press ESC to cancel; otherwise the issue command runs automatically. The banner shows the reason for the proposed issue.
items.done: After a crash or repeated error, a banner appears: 'Running /issue: unexpected tool failure [ESC to cancel]'. On ESC timeout, `/issue` runs and opens a draft in the browser.
items.notes: Pure IssueDraft{title,body,labels} + buildIssueDraft (concise title from summary; body = Summary/Error-signal/Recent-tools/Git-state sections, control-stripped + capped 120/4000) + autoIssueEnabled (VANTA_AUTO_ISSUE=1, default OFF) + CancelWindowState{remainingMs,cancelled,fired} + tickCancelWindow (counts down, fires at 0 UNLESS cancelled, terminal once fired/cancelled never resurrects) + cancelWindow (ESC) + buildGhIssueArgs (issue create --title --body --label ARGV, each value a discrete item even injection-shaped x; rm -rf /). SECURITY: outward-facing -> off by default, ESC-cancellable, never silent; title/body control-stripped; argv NOT shell-interpolated. Built+tested; wire = error-detect recurring signal (>=3) -> buildIssueDraft -> gate autoIssueEnabled -> ESC countdown -> on fired&&!cancelled spawn gh issue create kernel-gated (named, gh exec = boundary). Default off = nothing.

items.title: External prompt editor — open $EDITOR to compose multi-line input
items.summary: another agent lets users press a keybinding to open the current prompt input in their external editor (`$VISUAL`, `$EDITOR`, or detected IDE). Supports VS Code (`code -w`), Sublime Text (`subl --wait`), and any GUI editor. Pasted content refs from the editor are expanded back into the prompt on save. Enables composing complex multi-line prompts outside the terminal.

items.notes: Already shipped: the composer already opens $EDITOR to compose multi-line input via ^G (ui/composer.tsx:156 — ^G edit-in-$EDITOR). Verified live. Reconciled.

items.title: Shell output JSON auto-format — pretty-print JSON lines in bash output
items.summary: another agent's shell output renderer (`OutputLine`) attempts to parse each output line as JSON and re-serializes it for readability when successful. Checks for precision loss on large integers before substituting. Turns compact JSON API responses in bash output into human-readable formatted output without user action. [MODULE BUILT — formatJsonInOutput shipped + tested (term/json-format.ts); wiring deferred: Vanta summarizes tool output rather than showing raw lines, so there is no high-value display surface yet — needs a raw-output/expand view first.]

Default off = byte-identical join (existing shell-cmd tests unchanged). Closes the long-standing built-not-wired item.

items.title: Terminal hyperlinks — OSC 8 clickable links in tool output and messages
items.summary: another agent creates OSC 8 terminal hyperlinks (`createHyperlink`) in shell output and messages. In supporting terminals (iTerm2, WezTerm, Kitty, modern terminals), URLs become clickable links that open in the browser. The feature is guarded by `supportsHyperlinks()` for terminals that don't support OSC 8.

items.notes: Pure osc8Link + sanitizeLinkUrl (strips ESC/BEL/C0/C1 so a hostile url cant break out of the OSC-8 sequence) + hyperlinksEnabled (VANTA_HYPERLINKS=1 or known TERM_PROGRAM, default off) + linkOr/fileLink. Off/unsupported = plain text. ALSO HARDENED THE LIVE PATH: the already-wired term/osc8.ts osc8() was interpolating the url RAW (a real OSC-8 breakout vector) — now routed through sanitizeLinkUrl (clean urls unchanged). Render wire (transcript linkify/linked-text) named.

items.title: /stats sparkline charts — ASCII activity charts and heatmap in stats view

NaN/Infinity/negatives clamped to 0. Built+tested; wire = /usage (context-cmds) + dashboard costLine append labeledSparkline/heatmapRow (named). No data = empty.

items.title: History picker — searchable input history overlay in the prompt bar

No history -> empty. Built+tested; composer overlay wire = ^R reverse-search opens openHistoryPicker(props.history), keys drive update/move, Enter sets buffer (named).

Malformed content best-effort, never throws. Built+tested; mount wire = mcp/mount mcpToolToVantaTool pass the raw content blocks through richMcpOutput instead of the joined string (named). Small = current behavior.

items.title: Idle return dialog — re-engagement prompt after session inactivity

Complements away-summary (recap) with next-actions. Built+tested; next-submit wire alongside away-summary named.

items.title: Sandbox violation expanded view — detailed breakdown of blocked sandbox actions

READ-ONLY. Built+tested; error-path wire = dispatch-tool tool boundary on non-zero exit under VANTA_SANDBOX=1 (named). Non-sandbox error = plain.

items.notes: Pure/immutable MultiSelectState{items,cursor,checked} + openMultiSelect(items,preChecked) + moveCursor(clamp no-wrap) + toggleChecked(only cursor item) + setAll(true|false) + chosenItems(checked NAMES in list order) + formatMultiSelect (▸ [x]/[ ] rows + [space] toggle · [a] all · [enter] confirm, names control-stripped). Never mutates input. Empty handled. Built+tested; mount-dialog wire = open with readMcpConfig server keys when .mcp.json >1, confirm -> chosenItems -> mountOneServer batch (named).

items.title: MCP project server approval — per-project approval dialog for .mcp.json servers
items.summary: another agent auto-shows `MCPServerApprovalDialog` for project-scoped servers listed in `.mcp.json` when they haven't been approved yet. The `handleMcpjsonServerApprovals()` call in setup runs before the session starts, collecting approvals for all pending project servers.

items.notes: Pure/injectable ServerApprovalRecord{serverName,commandHash,approved} + serverCommandHash (djb2 over canonical JSON of {command,args,url}, base-36, no crypto dep) + serverApprovalStatus (approved=record matches name+hash+approved | changed=name matches but hash differs | needs-approval=no record) + recordServerApproval (immutable upsert by name binding the current hash) + serversNeedingApproval (needs-approval OR changed names). RECONCILED: existing resolveMcpTrust (.vanta/trust.json mcp?) is per-project+per-server but keyed on NAME ONLY — no command binding, so a server whose command silently CHANGES after approval is still recalled trusted (bait-and-switch hole). This is the COMMAND-BOUND delta closing it (changed command beats a prior approve AND deny). INVARIANT TESTED: unapproved/changed server ALWAYS in serversNeedingApproval, never auto-mounts; empty records -> all need approval. SECURITY: approval is a mount SIGNAL — kernel assess still gates every tool. Built+tested; wire = mount mountMcpServers per-server checks serverApprovalStatus before mountOneServer, skip/ask on needs-approval|changed (named, complements resolveMcpTrust).

Built+tested; composer wire = composer.tsx ghost (swap historyTypeahead -> suggestGhost) + composer-view dim Text + handleGhostAccept on rightArrow (named). FOLLOW-UP: the existing live historyTypeahead doesnt control-strip (lower severity, own history) — adopting this module closes it.

items.title: Status line GitHub PR info — repo and PR data in status line JSON input

items.title: `Ctrl+U` clears entire input; `Ctrl+Y` restores — readline kill-buffer update
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.111) changes `Ctrl+U` from 'delete to start of line' to 'clear entire input buffer'. The cleared content goes into the kill ring and can be restored with `Ctrl+Y`. Matches zsh/bash Emacs kill-line behavior.

Built+tested; wire = composer-keys ^U->killWholeLine + ^Y->yank threading KillYankState (named).

Non-plan = plain. Built+tested; render = transcript plan Entry kind + key handler -> /planmode approve (named).
