items.title: Observation compression pipeline (raw event -> facts -> memory)
items.summary: AgentMemory-inspired 3-stage pipeline: (1) raw hook observations (tool calls, user prompts, assistant responses); (2) compressed observations (type, title, facts[], narrative, concepts[], importance); (3) consolidated memories (pattern/preference/architecture/bug/workflow/fact). Today reviewAfterTurn extracts skills. This extends it to extract all typed knowledge. Each tool result that produces insight generates a CompressedObservation; the consolidator promotes recurring patterns to long-term Memory entries. ND fit F5 (Vanta builds a real mental model, not just a skills list).
items.done: A file edit, decision, or discovery generates a typed CompressedObservation; recurring patterns crystallize into Memory entries; the brain grows from experience.

items.done: A coverage matrix exists; genuine gaps are promoted to roadmap items.

items.done: A scheduled research task produces a cited brief with an explicit confidence/uncertainty note, stored as one durable memory rather than a link dump.

Distinct from the generic skills library (D1/D2) -- these are Jason's personal procedures, captured from repeated solutions (ties to REFLECT-CORRECT).

items.title: Curator umbrella consolidation + pin
items.summary: The bundled curator does an umbrella-building pass Vanta's doesn't: it groups many overlapping/related skills under CLASS-LEVEL umbrella skills (e.g. apple-services, github-operations, research-workflows), archives the children (69 in one observed run), prunes stale ones, then shows a once-per-run digest with `curator status` (view anytime) and `curator pin <umbrella>` (freeze a good umbrella so the next pass won't churn it). Vanta's curate() is non-destructive archive of stale LEARNED skills only — it reports overlaps but never consolidates them. Add an LLM consolidation pass that proposes umbrellas from overlap clusters, archives children reversibly, supports pin, and emits a once-per-run digest. Reversible + approval-gated (Rule zero).
items.done: `vanta curator` proposes class-level umbrellas from overlapping skills, archives children reversibly with pin support, prunes stale, and surfaces a once-per-run digest viewable via `vanta curator status`.

Output points to roadmap_add to promote it to a card on triage. Wired into HANDLERS + catalog. 5 tests; tsc clean; handlers.ts under the 300-line cap.

items.title: Early fail-detect + safe retry + honest report
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. tool-retry.ts: shouldRetryTool = isRetryableTool (a curated set of idempotent reads — read_file/web_*/git_status/gmail_read/vision/… ; writes/shell/commit/run_code/spawns excluded, unknown tools default off) AND isTransientFailure (network/timeout/rate-limit/5xx patterns; a deterministic ENOENT is NOT retried). Wired into agent.ts dispatchTool: a transient read failure re-runs up to resolveToolRetries() times (VANTA_TOOL_RETRIES, default 1, clamp 0..5), emitting a retry note; the final result is returned as-is so success is never faked. Early-detect + honest-report already existed via EF-ERRORDETECT (isErrorResult + consecutive-failure pause). 10 unit tests; agent suite green; tsc clean;.env.example documents the knob.
items.done: A failed tool call is surfaced immediately; only safe operations auto-retry; no false success claims.

items.title: Action previews + post-action proof
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. The 'before' half already existed (write_file returns a computed diff → TUI-DIFF). Added the missing 'after' half: write_file now RE-READS the file post-write and confirms the bytes actually landed — 'verified N lines, M bytes on disk' (or a ⚠ if on-disk content differs / re-read fails), instead of reporting only the intended byte count. Real post-action proof, not assumed success. 1 added test (tools.test.ts); full suite 1201 green; tsc clean.
items.done: A file write shows the diff before and a verification readout (re-read / test result) after, both within the turn.

items.title: Visible cost + latency per call (local vs frontier)

Both hosts now print the cost/latency footer per turn and accumulate ReplState.sessionCost; /usage shows the session split. Prices are approximate-for-visibility (documented). 8 unit tests; full suite 1182 green; tsc clean.
items.done: Each turn shows model, token counts, and latency; /status shows session cost split by local vs frontier.

Closes the triage->done loop so the backlog reflects reality automatically. Ties directly to the KANBAN item (Vanta moving cards as it works).
items.done: A shipped factory slice automatically closes/moves its source work item; the board reflects what was actually shipped without manual edits.

items.title: Stable prompt prefix for LLM cache hit rate
items.summary: AgentMemory issue #720: per-turn system prompt mutation (injecting current time, session-specific context) drops prompt cache hit rate ~10pp because prefix matching fails on any change. Vanta buildSystemPrompt() injects now: timestamp in the volatile tier every turn, invalidating the stable prefix. Fix: freeze the stable tier (SOUL + tools + rules + skills index) as a constant string computed once per session; inject now/goals/moim only in a trailing volatile block that never touches the prefix. Anthropic ephemeral caching matches on the first N tokens -- keep those stable.
items.done: Stable prompt prefix never changes within a session; volatile suffix changes freely; cache hit rate measurably improves.

items.title: Memory freshness caveat — staleness annotation for memories > 1 day old
items.summary: Memories older than 1 day get a human-readable age string ('47 days ago') and a staleness caveat injected alongside their content: 'This memory is N days old. Memories are point-in-time observations — file:line citations may be outdated. Verify against current code.' Prevents stale facts from being asserted confidently.
items.done: Fresh memories (today/yesterday) inject no caveat; older memories prepend the staleness note; age shown as human-readable string, not raw timestamp.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). memory/freshness.ts: pure humanAge(ageMs) ("today"/"yesterday"/"N days ago") + freshnessCaveat(ageMs) (null when <2 days, else a point-in-time caveat) + annotateMemory(content, ageMs). Wired into memory/store.ts recentMemory: each block's own ISO timestamp → age → stale blocks get the caveat prepended at the injection path (per-block granularity). +14 unit + 1 store integration test; full suite 2019 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Shell task stall detection — notification when background command awaits input
items.summary: Background shell tasks get a stall watchdog: if output stops growing for 45s, the last tail is pattern-matched against interactive prompt patterns (y/n, yes/no, 'Press any key', directed questions like 'Are you sure?'). If matched, a notification fires so the agent can unblock it.
items.done: Long-running commands that ask y/n surface a notification; slow commands that don't ask anything stay silent; notification fires once, not repeatedly.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). tools/shell-stall.ts: pure detectInteractivePrompt(tail) (matches the trimmed/lowercased LAST non-empty line against a needle list + a ?-ending-with-y/n branch; near-zero prose FP) + StallState + checkStall(prev, curLen, tail, nowMs, idleMs=45000) (resets on output growth, fires once after idle+interactive, never refires; nowMs injected). Wired into bg-tasks.ts spawnBackground: startStallWatchdog polls the live in-memory chunks buffer every 5s (the log file is empty until close, so the watchdog lives at the spawn site), notify()s once when stalled-on-prompt, cleared on close. +32 tests; full suite green, tsc clean. (checkStall has 5 params — card-mandated signature, size gate warn-only.)

items.title: Tool batch summary — Haiku-generated ~30-char label for completed tool batches

items.done: Tool batch summarization utilities available; scaffolded for agent-loop integration via dispatchTool().

items.title: Time-based microcompact — auto-clear stale tool results after cache TTL expiry
items.summary: Runs before each API call: if the gap since the last assistant message exceeds the cache TTL (default 60 min), clears old tool results from the prompt to shrink what gets rewritten. Keeps the N most recent tool results. Main thread only (subagents have short lifetimes). GrowthBook-configurable.
items.done: After 60+ minutes idle, the next request sends a compacted prompt; old tool results are cleared; the N most recent are kept; no visible disruption to the conversation.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent verified-integration). context/time-microcompact.ts: pure clearStaleToolResults(messages, idleMs, opts?) — under idle threshold (default 60min, VANTA_MICROCOMPACT_IDLE_MS) returns the same array ref; over it, stubs old role:"tool" contents to "[old tool result cleared after idle]" IN PLACE (position preserved → tool_call/tool_result pairing valid), keeps the last N (default 4, VANTA_MICROCOMPACT_KEEP). + resolveIdleConfig(env). Wired into agent.ts runTurn: a GC-safe WeakMap<messages,number> tracks last-turn time; on iter 1 (the genuinely-idle turn start) clearStaleToolResults feeds the transient compress/trim copy — stored messages keep full content (no visible disruption). Agent's sandbox couldn't run tests; parent verified (typecheck + 12 module tests + full suite 2096 green). Completes the auto-context set: persistent size-compaction + >50k offload + idle clearing.

items.title: /rename — set session title
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. repl/rename-cmd.ts: /rename [name] shows current title or sets a new one (max 100 chars). Modifies ctx.state.title. Registered in HANDLERS + catalog.

items.title: Fix: image drag-drop from Desktop fails ENOENT (path not in scope)
items.summary: Dragging an image from ~/Desktop into the TUI fails with ENOENT. describe_image resolves the path via resolveInScope which rejects Desktop paths even though ~/Desktop is in resolveReadableZones. Tilde expansion may also be missing from the image tool's path handling.
items.done: Dragging ~/Desktop/image.png into the TUI attaches it correctly; no ENOENT; tilde paths expanded before scope check; Desktop is treated as readable zone for image attach.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. Root cause: describe_image (and compare_vision) called resolveInScope WITHOUT expandHome — '~/Desktop/x.png' resolved to a bogus '<root>/~/Desktop/x.png' that passed the in-scope test then ENOENTed; an absolute Desktop path was refused outright though ~/Desktop is a default readable zone. Fix mirrors read_file exactly: expandHome() first, then allow out-of-root paths that isInZone(resolveReadableZones()). Tool descriptions updated to say ~-paths / readable zones are OK. Fixed BOTH describe_image and compare_vision (same bug). The drag-drop attach path (repl/format.ts maybeDroppedImage) already expanded ~ and was fine. +~6 tests across describe-image/compare-vision; suite green, tsc clean. (screenshot.ts/lsp.ts keep the old in-root-only message by design — not image-from-Desktop.)

items.title: Destructive bash command warnings — informational notes in permission dialog
items.summary: Permission dialogs for commands like `git reset --hard`, `git push --force`, `git clean -f`, `git checkout .` show an informational warning ('Note: may discard uncommitted changes'). Purely informational — doesn't affect permission logic or auto-approval. Curated list of dangerous git patterns.
items.done: Running `git reset --hard` shows the warning note in the dialog; `git push -f` shows the force-push warning; non-destructive commands show no extra note.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. tools/destructive-warn.ts: pure destructiveWarning(command) → curated note for allowed-but-destructive git commands (reset --hard, push --force [but NOT --force-with-lease], clean -f, checkout . / -f, restore ., branch -D, stash drop/clear), else null. shell-cmd.ts prepends "⚠ <note>" to the result (success or failure) so the user/agent sees it in the transcript. Purely informational — never blocks (rm -rf etc. stay hard-refused by the DESTRUCTIVE gate). +7 tests; suite green, tsc clean.

items.title: /context — show context window usage breakdown with microcompact option
items.summary: The /context command analyzes and displays context window usage breakdown (analyzeContextUsage): how many tokens are used by system prompt, conversation, tool results, etc. Optionally runs microcompact to clear old tool results and show the savings.
items.done: Running /context shows current context breakdown by category; shows % of window used; optional compact button clears old results; savings displayed.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. Replaced the untested inline /context handler with repl/context-cmd.ts: pure contextBreakdown(messages, window) → per-category token estimates (system/user/assistant[+tool-call args]/tool results) + total + pct, and formatContextBreakdown() rendering a usage bar + each category's token count and % share. Richer than the old role-count version AND now unit-tested; also shrinks handlers.ts (inline handler removed, registered via import). +5 tests; full suite 1985 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Bash per-command exit code semantics — grep exit 1 = no-match, not error
items.summary: another agent maps per-command exit code semantics: grep/rg exit 1 = 'No matches found' (not an error), find exit 1 = partial success, diff exit 1 = 'differences found'. Without this, these commands falsely appear as failures and the agent may retry unnecessarily.
items.done: grep/rg returning exit 1 shows 'No matches found' as info, not error. diff exit 1 shows 'Differences found'. find exit 1 shows 'Some directories were inaccessible'.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. tools/shell-cmd.ts: pure lastCommandWord() (first token of the last pipeline/chain segment, git-aware, basename-stripped) + classifyExitCode() — grep/rg/egrep/fgrep exit 1 → 'No matches found', diff exit 1 → 'Differences found', find exit 1 → 'Some paths were inaccessible', all ok=true; exit ≥2 and unrelated commands stay errors; ENOENT/timeout (non-numeric code) stay errors. Returns the info note as the result so the TUI renders it as a normal (non-error) outcome — no display-layer change needed. +11 tests in tools.test.ts; suite green, tsc clean.

items.title: NVIDIA NIM provider — cheap worker routing for summarize/classify/code
items.summary: Add an NVIDIA NIM provider adapter (OpenAI-compatible API at build.nvidia.com) so Vanta can route low-cost subtasks (summarize, classify, first-pass code) to NVIDIA-hosted models like meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct. Uses NVIDIA_API_KEY + VANTA_ROUTE_SUMMARIZE=nvidia:model config. Since NIM is OpenAI-compatible, implementation is a baseURL swap in the OpenAI adapter — minimal new code.
items.done: VANTA_PROVIDER=nvidia with NVIDIA_API_KEY resolves the OpenAI adapter to build.nvidia.com. /routes shows nvidia as a configured route. A summarize task auto-delegates to the configured NIM model.
items.notes: Provider adapter + /routes command done. Auto-delegation (VANTA_ROUTE_SUMMARIZE) parked — needs routing infra, scope exceeds S/low-effort.

items.title: Model deprecation warnings — show retirement date when user is on a deprecated model

When the active model is deprecated, another agent surfaces a warning with the model name, retirement date, and a suggestion to upgrade. Deprecation data is provider-aware (same model may have different retirement timelines on different providers).

items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). providers/model-deprecation.ts: pure modelDeprecationNotice(modelId, now) over a curated DEPRECATIONS map of clearly-legacy ids (claude-1/2/2.0/2.1/instant, gpt-4-0314/0613, gpt-3.5-turbo-0301/0613, gemini-1.0-pro/pro) → "model 'X' is deprecated (retires DATE) — switch to Y"; case-insensitive + prefix match (gpt-4-0314-preview matches). modelDeprecationNotices(env, now) reads VANTA_MODEL. Wired into status.ts notices: [...detectAuthConflicts, ...modelDeprecationNotices] → shown by vanta status/doctor under "⚠ notices:". Dates are best-effort/illustrative. +7 tests; full suite 1985 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Auth conflict notice — warn when both API key and OAuth credentials are configured
items.summary: another agent surfaces a startup status notice (`apiKeyConflictNotice`, `bothAuthMethodsNotice`) when both an API key and OAuth login are simultaneously active. These can conflict — the user may not realize they're using the wrong credentials. The notice suggests which to remove and links to auth docs.

items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. providers/auth-conflict.ts: pure authConflictNotices(env, {claude,codex}) + detectAuthConflicts(env) (detects OAuth via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN/ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN env or ~/.claude/.credentials.json, and ~/.codex/auth.json). Flags ANTHROPIC_API_KEY+Claude-OAuth and OPENAI_API_KEY+Codex-OAuth coexistence with a 'remove one' notice naming which provider uses which. Surfaced in StatusReport.notices → rendered by `vanta status`/`doctor` under "⚠ notices:". Reused existing path helpers (exported credentialsPath from claude-code-auth.ts; defaultCodexAuthPath already exported). Whitespace-only keys treated as unset. +9 tests; suite green, tsc clean.

items.title: /compact with custom instructions — control what the summary preserves
items.summary: another agent's `/compact` accepts an optional argument: `/compact <instructions>` to guide the summarization. E.g. `/compact focus on the authentication flow` tells the summarizer what to emphasize. Also supports `DISABLE_COMPACT` env var to block compaction. Works in non-interactive mode for scripted compaction.

New repl/compact-gate.ts: compactionDisabled(env) (VANTA_DISABLE_COMPACT/DISABLE_COMPACT === "1") short-circuits the manual /compress with a "compaction disabled" notice (auto-compaction not gated — out of scope). +7 tests; full suite 2032 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Dangerous paths blocklist — hardcoded files/dirs Claude never writes to
items.summary: another agent maintains `DANGEROUS_FILES` and `DANGEROUS_DIRECTORIES` constants — a hardcoded blocklist of system-critical paths (SSH keys, shell configs, credentials, OS directories) that Claude will never write to, regardless of permission rules. This is distinct from user-configured deny rules and cannot be overridden.
items.done: Attempting to edit `~/.ssh/id_rsa` or `/etc/passwd` always returns a permission denial, even in bypassPermissions mode.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. tools/writable-zones.ts: isDangerousPath(abs) — an unconditional floor beneath zones, scope AND approval mode. Blocks DANGEROUS_DIRS (~/.ssh ~/.gnupg ~/.aws ~/.config/gcloud /etc /private/etc /System /var/db/sudo) + DANGEROUS_FILES (~/.netrc ~/.npmrc ~/.pypirc ~/.docker/config.json ~/.kube/config ~/.codex/auth.json ~/.claude/.credentials.json ~/.vanta/google-tokens.json + shell rc files). Checked FIRST in both resolveReadablePath (reads) and a new resolveWritablePath (writes), so write_file/edit_file/read_file/describe_image/compare_vision all refuse these paths even in auto-approve mode. Also DRY'd write_file+edit_file onto resolveWritablePath (mirrors the read helper; the security floor now lives in one place per direction). Prefix-collision safe (~/.ssh-backup not blocked). +~15 tests; suite green, tsc clean.

items.title: Compaction reminders — inject compaction_reminder attachment into context
items.summary: another agent (feature-gated `COMPACTION_REMINDERS`) adds a `compaction_reminder` resolver to the context attachments pipeline. When included, the reminder attachment nudges the model to use compaction tools when context is getting large, without hard-coding the instruction into the system prompt.
items.done: When context approaches limits, a `compaction_reminder` attachment appears in the context, prompting the model to consider compaction.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). repl/compaction-remind.ts: pure compactionReminder(estTokens, contextWindow, env?) → null below 0.70 fill (VANTA_COMPACTION_REMIND_FRAC override) else "[context ~NN% full — consider /compress …]" (display clamped ≤99%). Wired into context.ts compressMessages: injected as a transient interior message in the compression branch (after the summary note, before the tail) so it never displaces head/goalNote at index 1. Used the MINIMAL safe seam (compression branch only) — not the agent's 70-75% band early-return — to avoid any reminder-accumulation risk if a caller stores the result. +9 tests; full suite 2080 green, tsc clean.

items.title: /restart command (reload the instance)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. /restart (repl/restart-cmd.ts) exits with the sysexits sentinel 75; run.sh now wraps the launch in a relaunch loop (export VANTA_RELAUNCH=1; re-runs tsx on code 75, passes any other code through) so edited source reloads in place without quitting to the shell. Both hosts wired: readline (interactive.ts, process.exitCode=75 + forced exit after cleanup) and TUI (app.tsx app.exit() + launch.tsx forced 75 exit after waitUntilExit). Kernel reconnects via the existing ping/auto-start. Refuses with a hint when VANTA_RELAUNCH is unset (run via npm/old launcher) so it never surprise-quits. 3 unit tests; full suite green; tsc clean. The actual TTY relaunch is Jason's to confirm in a terminal (the loop is offline-reasoned + shell-syntax-checked, like the TUI streaming verify).
items.done: /restart relaunches Vanta in place with fresh code; the kernel reconnects.

items.title: Operator voice + verify-before-claim discipline

SOUL.md already on-voice (operator, honest, no-fake). Locks the framing with prompt tests asserting both rules present. NOTE: the aesthetic half of done ('reads like the brand guide examples') is Jason's to confirm on real replies; VOICE-NATURAL tunes warmth on top of this base, TRUST-LABELS renders the epistemic markers.

Emission is model-driven (verified live).

items.title: Esc to interrupt a running turn

Vanta HAS the mechanism (createConversation signal + AbortController, stoppedReason 'interrupted') but the TUI never wires a key to it — you can't stop a runaway turn from the Ink UI (only the one-shot CLI has SIGINT). Wire Esc → controller.abort() in app.tsx. ND fit F6 (control/trust) + F3 (stop the firehose).
items.done: Pressing Esc during a busy turn aborts it cleanly between iterations and returns control to the composer.

items.title: Prefix shortcuts: bash (!) + memory (#)
items.summary: another agent: '!' runs a shell command inline (output into context), '#' appends a line to memory without a full command. Vanta has shell_cmd as a tool + /memory as a command but no fast prefixes. Add composer prefixes: leading ! → shell (kernel-gated), leading # → brain/memory append. ND fit F1 (lower friction for the two most common quick actions).
items.done: Typing '! <cmd>' runs a kernel-gated shell command; '# <note>' appends to the brain — no slash command needed.

items.title: Persistent rich status line
items.summary: Gemini + another agent show a persistent status line (model · context % · tokens · sometimes cost). Vanta has a one-line hint + /context + /usage commands but no always-on status. Add a calm persistent footer: model, context-fill %, token/cost (ties to FAC-ESCALATE cost ledger). ND fit F2/F6 (ambient state without asking).
items.done: A persistent footer shows model + context% + tokens; updates live, stays calm (no flicker).

items.title: Themes incl. accessibility (high-contrast / dyslexia)
items.summary: Gemini ships selectable themes. For an ND product this is accessibility, not decoration: a high-contrast theme, a low-arousal muted theme, and a dyslexia-friendly option (spacing/weight). Token-drive the TUI colors + VANTA_THEME. ND fit F3 (sensory regulation — let the user tune arousal/contrast).
items.done: VANTA_THEME selects among calm / high-contrast / dyslexia-friendly palettes; persists like the model choice.

items.title: Thinking / reasoning display (collapsible)
items.summary: another agent shows (collapsible) thinking; Codex exposes reasoning levels. Vanta's loop discards/ignores reasoning in the TUI. When a provider returns reasoning, show it dim + collapsed (expand on key) so the user can see WHY without the firehose. ND fit F3 (optional depth, calm by default) + F6 (transparency).
items.done: Reasoning, when present, shows as a dim collapsed line that expands on demand; off by default to stay calm.

items.title: Vim-mode composer editing
items.summary: Gemini + another agent offer vim-mode line editing. Lower priority but cheap given the custom composer already owns key handling (composer.tsx). Add an opt-in modal (normal/insert) editing mode behind VANTA_VIM. ND fit F1 (muscle-memory for vim users = lower friction).
items.done: VANTA_VIM enables modal editing in the composer (normal/insert, basic motions); off by default.

items.title: Keyboard shortcut help overlay
items.summary: Most TUIs offer a '?' / shortcut to a key-binding cheat sheet. Vanta has /help (commands) but no key-binding reference and the custom composer keys (ctrl a/e/u/k/w, etc.) are undiscoverable. Add a '?' overlay listing keys + modes. ND fit F2 (don't make me remember the bindings).
items.done: A '?' overlay lists every composer key, slash command, and mode toggle; dismiss with esc.

items.title: Worktree-aware project identity for memory scoping
items.summary: AgentMemory issue #728: git worktrees return the worktree root from git rev-parse --show-toplevel, not the main repo. Memory fragments across worktrees of the same project. Vanta uses worktrees extensively (superpowers:using-git-worktrees). Fix: for memory and session scoping, use git remote get-url origin (canonical repo URL) as the project identity, falling back to git rev-parse --show-toplevel for the main checkout. Worktree sessions share memory with the main branch.
items.done: A session run from a worktree of Vanta reads/writes the same memory as a session run from the main checkout.

items.title: Conversation timestamps in memory (not ingest time)
items.summary: MemPalace issue #1649: the miner writes filed_at (ingest time) but the transcript already carries per-message timestamps. A session from 3 weeks ago mined today shows filed_at=today, breaking time-based recall. Vanta sessions store messages as Message[] with no timestamp. Fix: add timestamp to the Message type (record at send time); propagate to memory store entries. Enables: vanta memory search --since 7d, temporal graph queries, correct time anchoring for what-did-we-do-last-Tuesday recall.
items.done: Every memory entry carries the conversation timestamp (not ingest time); time-scoped search works.

items.title: Canonical project identity from git remote URL
items.summary: AgentMemory issues #716/#733: projects named by directory basename collide across machines and clones (~/work/foo vs ~/dev/foo = same project, different memory silos). Fix: resolve project identity from git remote get-url origin (e.g., github.com/jpoindexter/Vanta) and use that as the memory namespace key. Fall back to directory name for non-git roots. Enables memory portability: clone the repo on a new machine and existing memories surface. VANTA_PROJECT_ID env override for non-git use.
items.done: Memory scoping uses the git remote URL; cloning to a new path preserves memory continuity.

items.title: Complexity gate — auto-prompt to plan before executing complex requests
items.summary: PMC4455841 planning/organization: difficulty breaking complex tasks into steps, sequencing, anticipating obstacles. Currently Vanta has /planmode as an opt-in toggle. But the user must know to use it. Fix: lightweight complexity classifier on each incoming user message — estimate task complexity by indicators (multi-file, schema change, migration, cross-service, ambiguous spec). If complexity > threshold, auto-suggest plan-first mode before any tool call. Same principle as the kernel ambiguity gate (FAC-PREFLIGHT) but for the interactive session. User can dismiss with one key to proceed directly. ND fit F1+F5 (breaks the blank-page paralysis, provides structure before confusion).
items.done: Complex requests trigger a one-line plan prompt before tool use; user can dismiss; simple requests are not interrupted.

items.title: Explicit task boundaries — clean context handoff on topic switch
items.summary: PMC4455841 set shifting: context pollution from previous tasks bleeds into current work ("set" persists when it should be cleared). Vanta has /clear but no lightweight mid-session boundary. Design: when the agent detects a significant topic shift mid-session (classifier on user message vs active goal), propose a task-boundary event: summarize the previous task state, archive it to brain/session memory, and begin fresh with the new task context. Unlike /clear (full wipe), task boundary preserves the conversation but explicitly marks the shift, preventing the prior approach from contaminating the new task. Optional: /boundary slash command as manual trigger. ND fit F4+F5 (one control that says "new thing now", structured handoff).
items.done: Topic shifts generate a task-boundary event; prior task state is archived; new task starts clean without full context loss.

items.title: Backlog choice reduction gate (pattern: initiation paralysis)
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: 11-item backlog → paralysis. Case 4-2 analog: paralyzed when faced with multifaceted problems. The backlog is the multifaceted problem. Counter-measure: when the next queue has > 3 items and the user asks what to do next, present ONLY the top 3 (ranked by: dependencies_met + time_since_last_touched + smallest_size). Do not show the full list. One question: which of these 3? This is occupational therapy Environmental Manipulation (simplify the task environment). Pairs with /next which reduces to a single micro-step. KANBAN WIP limit prevents adding while paralyzed. ND fit F1+F5 (structure reduces the blank-page-of-100-options problem).
items.done: When next queue > 3, /next and agenda-style prompts show only the top 3 candidates; the rest are hidden until one ships.

items.title: Velocity tracker — capture:ship ratio (pattern: ideas-rich/finish-poor)
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: roadmap grew from 0 to 100 items in 4 sessions, shipped items grew much more slowly. Fluency excess masks planning deficit (MMSE 28/30 analog: backlog looks productive, shipping rate is low). Counter-measure: rolling 7-day capture:ship ratio stored in ~/.vanta/velocity.json. When ratio > 5:1 (5 ideas captured per 1 shipped), surface on /status and at session start: capture/ship ratio is 8:1 this week — consider closing before opening. Not a block. A visible metric that makes the pattern legible. ND fit F2+F5 (ambient awareness without having to remember to check).
items.done: /status shows the rolling capture:ship ratio; ratio > 5:1 surfaces a one-line note at session start.

items.title: Scope delta tracker — topic count per turn (pattern: while-we-are-at-it)
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: while implementing FAC-INTENT, also checked MemPalace issues, added 8 roadmap items, wrote two design docs, analyzed clinical papers. Each is individually justified; together they are 6 topics in one turn. Inhibition failure: prepotent response to each interesting thing fires. Counter-measure: at the end of each assistant turn, count distinct topics/files/goals touched. If > 3, add a dim annotation: n topics this turn. Not alarming — just visible. Over a session, the annotation pattern becomes its own signal. ND fit F3+F6 (calm ambient signal without interrupting flow).
items.done: Multi-topic turns show a dim topic count annotation; annotation accumulates across a session into a visible pattern.

All documented in usage. 11 tests; all files pass the size gate; full suite 1227 green; tsc clean. Deferred sub-items (not in done): `vanta send`, `vanta recap`/`/recap` — promote if wanted.

items.title: Compact boundary message — visual separator after context compaction
items.summary: another agent renders a distinct '✻ Conversation compacted (Ctrl+O for history)' message at the compaction point, with a dynamic shortcut hint. Vanta compacts context (VANTA-S-COMPACT shipped) but shows no in-transcript marker where compaction occurred.
items.done: After compaction, a separator line appears in the transcript at the compaction boundary with the shortcut to view full history; it uses the active keybinding, not a hardcoded key.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. New transcript Entry kind "compactBoundary" (magenta ──── ✻ … ──── separator) + reducer action { t: "compactBoundary"; text }. app.tsx onAutoCompact now dispatches the boundary (was a plain note) with "compacted N messages · <summary preview>", so the transcript shows a distinct visual marker where the model's context was summarized. +2 tests (reducer + EntryRow render); full TUI suite 226 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Interrupted message — distinct visual when user interrupts a turn
items.summary: another agent renders a distinct InterruptedByUser message when Esc aborts a turn, showing what partial work was done before the stop. Vanta aborts cleanly but shows no marker in the transcript that a turn was interrupted vs completed.
items.done: When a turn is aborted via Esc, an 'Interrupted' marker appears in the transcript with the partial tool-use count and a prompt to continue or ask again.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. New transcript Entry kind "interrupted" (yellow ⎋ marker) + reducer action { t: "interrupted" } that preserves partial streamed text as an assistant entry, appends the marker, and ends the turn (busy=false, idle). use-agent-send.ts Esc handler now dispatches "interrupted" (was a generic note) and sets interruptedRef; the send .catch short-circuits on interruptedRef so the AbortError no longer also surfaces as a noisy "error:" note. interruptedRef reset at each turn start. +3 tests (reducer preserves-partial + EntryRow render); full TUI suite 224 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Token warning — approaching context limit alert
items.summary: another agent's TokenWarning renders a visible alert when the conversation is approaching the context window limit (e.g. >85% full), prompting the user to compact or start a new session. Vanta tracks context % (VANTA-S-CONTEXT) but has no alert threshold UI.
items.done: When context fill crosses a configurable threshold (default 85%), a persistent warning appears near the composer with context % and a /compact shortcut; at 95% the warning turns urgent.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. tui/status-bar.tsx: pure tokenWarningLevel(estTokens, contextWindow, env) → none/warn/urgent + tokenWarnFractions(env) (VANTA_TOKEN_WARN_FRAC default 0.85, VANTA_TOKEN_URGENT_FRAC default 0.95). The status bar (already below the composer, receiving estTokens+contextWindow) colors the % yellow and appends '▲ /compact' at warn, red 'context full · /compact' at urgent — zero app.tsx changes (StatusBar self-computes; env read at render time, not module load). +12 tests; full TUI suite 218 green, tsc clean. Documented in .env.example.

items.title: InvalidConfigDialog + InvalidSettingsDialog — actionable config error UX
items.summary: another agent renders dedicated dialogs for invalid config (.mcp.json parse errors, settings.json schema violations) showing the specific error, file location, and a suggested fix instead of crashing or silent fallback. Vanta logs parse errors to console with no UI.
items.done: Config parse errors show InvalidConfigDialog with: which file, what's wrong, the offending line, and a 'Fix in editor' button; settings schema violations show ValidationErrorsList with all violations.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). config/validate.ts: pure describeConfigError(file, raw, err) → "<file>: invalid JSON at line N — <reason> — fix it or remove it" + validateConfigFiles(env) which independently re-parses the known config files (./.mcp.json, ~/.vanta/mcp.json, the three settings scopes via settings/store path exports) and returns one notice per broken file ([] when clean/absent). Surfaced via status.ts notices → shown in vanta status/doctor under "⚠ notices:" (same mechanism as auth-conflict/model-deprecation) instead of a silent fallback. Reader injected for fs-free tests. Adapted to Vanta (no Ink dialog system — doctor surfacing, not a another agent-style InvalidConfigDialog). +8 tests; full suite 2104 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Secret scanner — block credentials from cloud memory sync
items.summary: Client-side secret scanner that checks memory file content before cloud upload. Uses curated high-confidence gitleaks rules (GitHub PAT, AWS access/secret keys, etc.) with near-zero false positives. Secrets are detected and blocked from leaving the machine.
items.done: Memory files containing GitHub PATs or AWS credentials are blocked from cloud sync; clean files sync normally; matched rule ID logged for diagnosis.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). store/secret-scan.ts: pure scanForSecrets(text)→deduped rule ids (never the values) + hasSecrets(), 7 high-confidence near-zero-FP rules (github-pat, github-fine-grained-pat, aws-access-key-id, slack-token, google-api-key, private-key, openai-key). Wired into memory/store.ts appendMemory: scans the summary FIRST and returns {skipped:true, rules} without writing/committing if a secret is found — credential content never reaches ~/.vanta or its git store. Caller (session.ts) ignores the new return (void→object compatible). +17 unit + 2 store integration tests; full suite 2019 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Tool result disk storage — persist large outputs (>50K chars) to disk
items.summary: Tool results exceeding 50K characters (DEFAULT_MAX_RESULT_SIZE_CHARS) are persisted to disk; the model receives a preview and file path reference instead of the full content. Individual tools can declare a lower maxResultSizeChars. Prevents context window explosion from large tool outputs.
items.done: Shell commands producing >50K output store to disk; model gets preview + path; subsequent Read can retrieve the full content; per-tool limit respected.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). compress/result-offload.ts: offloadResult(text, {toolName, dataDir}) — when a tool output exceeds resolveMaxResultChars (default 50k, VANTA_MAX_RESULT_CHARS / per-tool override) it stashes the full text via the existing CCR store (stashOriginal → .vanta/ccr/<id>.txt, reused — no parallel system) and returns a ~2k preview + retrieval id (the model's existing retrieve_original tool reads it back). Wired into agent.ts dispatchTool AFTER compressOutput, so it backstops non-allow-listed oversized outputs (read_file/shell_cmd/grep). Best-effort (stash failure → original text). +7 tests; full suite 2080 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Context suggestions — actionable recommendations when context window fills up
items.summary: another agent's `contextSuggestions.ts` generates actionable suggestions when context fills: identifies which tools (bash, read, grep, web-fetch) are consuming tokens, shows estimated savings, and suggests /compact or specific file removal. Surfaced in the ContextVisualization component (VANTA-CONTEXT-VIZ) with severity levels (info/warning).
items.done: When context is 70%+ full, the context visualization shows specific items to remove with token counts: 'Remove 3 bash outputs (~4k tokens)'.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). repl/context-suggestions.ts: pure contextSuggestions(messages, window) → ContextSuggestion[] — returns [] below 70% fill; at ≥70% groups role:"tool" messages by name and emits "Remove N <name> output(s) (~Xk tokens)" for each ≥1k-token group (savedTokens desc), always appends a /compress entry; severity warning ≥85% else info. Wired into the /context command (context-cmd.ts contextCmd): appends the suggestions (⚠/· prefixed) under the breakdown when context is high. +6 tests; full suite 2032 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Prompt keyword detection — 'keep going' resumes, negative keywords stop agent
items.summary: another agent detects semantic keywords in user prompts: `matchesKeepGoingKeyword()` detects 'continue', 'keep going', 'proceed' to signal the agent should resume without stopping; `matchesNegativeKeyword()` detects 'no', 'stop', 'cancel' to signal interruption. Used in multi-turn flows and agentic sessions to understand user intent without full LLM inference.
items.done: Typing 'keep going' after an agent pause resumes without generating a new full response. 'stop' or 'no' in a message flags the intent for the agent loop.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). repl/prompt-keywords.ts: pure classifyPromptKeyword(text) → "continue"|"stop"|null. Normalizes (trim, lowercase, strip one trailing .!?) then WHOLE-STRING set-matches so a bare phrase fires but an embedded one doesn't ("keep going" → continue; "keep going on the refactor" → null). continue set: keep going/continue/go on/proceed/keep at it/carry on; stop set: stop/never mind/nevermind/cancel that/forget it. CONTINUE_NUDGE exported. Wired into tui/app.tsx submit (after the busy-enqueue guard): a bare continue phrase sends the nudge to resume the prior task instead of the literal text. No auto-action for "stop" (Esc already interrupts). +7 tests; full suite 1985 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Autonomy ladder (L1–L4, kernel-bounded)
items.summary: factory/run.ts now runs at a configurable autonomy level: L1 suggest (plan, no branch) · L2 implement (branch+execute+verify, stop for review) · L3 commit (no push) · L4 push. resolveAutonomyLevel(sub,env): improve=L1, approve=VANTA_AUTONOMY_LEVEL (default 4). Split commitAndPush→commitSlice+pushBranch; new CycleResult statuses implemented + committed{pushed}. Kernel is_protected_path still blocks skeleton/brainstem at every level. L5 auto-merge + low-risk classifier deferred (riskiest rung).
items.done: vanta factory approve with VANTA_AUTONOMY_LEVEL=2|3|4 stops exactly at implement/commit/push; 15 run.test.ts incl. resolveAutonomyLevel.

items.title: Autonomy L5 (auto-merge low-risk)
items.summary: factory/merge.ts: assessMergeRisk fails-closed through 3 gates — armed (VANTA_AUTONOMY_ALLOW_MERGE, default OFF), safe target (resolveMergeTarget default factory/integration, never main/master, --no-ff never force), low-risk (limbs/reflexes/memory only via O11, no dep/env/config/migration file, ≤20 files & ≤400 diff lines). MAX_AUTONOMY_LEVEL 4→5. run.ts: after L4 push, an armed+low-risk slice merges into the integration branch (mergeIntoTarget restores HEAD, aborts on conflict/missing target → stays L4). The git lifecycle runs outside the kernel assess() gate, so the classifier is the whole safety story; it's pure + 11 merge.test.ts. Merge git sequence smoke-verified (clean + conflict-abort + missing-target).
items.done: A low-risk verified slice auto-merges when armed; anything failing the classifier stops at L4 push. Default OFF (riskiest rung — opt-in).

items.title: Compartmentalized self-repair (body model)
items.summary: factory/compartments.ts: classifyCompartment(file)→tier (skeleton/brainstem/limbs/reflexes/memory), compartmentMaxAutonomy(tier)→max L (skeleton 0 · brainstem 2 · limbs/reflexes/memory 5), autonomyCapForFiles(files)→most-restrictive cap. run.ts clamps the requested autonomy level to the cap of the files a slice ACTUALLY touched (mixed slice → minimum). A brainstem fix can't auto-commit even at L4; only limbs/reflexes/memory reach the full ladder. is_protected_path stays the hard boundary; this is the soft policy it can't express.
items.done: A tier map exists + the factory refuses to exceed a compartment's max autonomy: limb fixes flow autonomously, brainstem/skeleton don't. 12 compartments.test.ts.
