items.notes: gateway/platforms/slack.ts: Slack adapter (PlatformAdapter) — Events API inbound (pure parseSlackEvents: plain user messages only, skip bot_id/subtype = anti-loop, DM vs channel) + chat.postMessage outbound + allowlist + injected transport. Wired into factory + catalog implemented:true (VANTA_SLACK_BOT_TOKEN). Offline-tested; live needs an xoxb token + Events API webhook.

items.title: Messaging adapter — Discord
items.summary: Build the live PlatformAdapter for Discord (gateway/platforms/discord.ts) — connect/disconnect/send/poll over the Discord API, wired to the catalog entry (registry.ts id 'discord'). Approach: discord.js gateway + MESSAGE CONTENT intent. Flips implemented:true so `vanta setup messaging` configures it for real (it's preview-only today).
items.done: Discord works end-to-end via the gateway: inbound message → agent → reply, honoring the allowlist; `vanta setup messaging` configures it.
items.notes: Pure/injectable parseDiscordMessages (Discord message objects -> InboundMessage: channel_id->chatId, author.id->from, content->stripControl, isGroup; SKIPS author.bot===true ANTI-LOOP; non-array/garbage -> []) + buildDiscordSendBody ({content}, capped at Discords 2000-char limit) + parseDiscordAllowlist (VANTA_DISCORD_ALLOWLIST comma -> Set, empty -> allow-all) + discordEnabled (VANTA_DISCORD_TOKEN present) + stripControl (C0/C1/DEL, keeps \n\t). DiscordAdapter implements PlatformAdapter (connect/disconnect/poll/send) over injected {fetchJson,postJson} transport — poll catch -> [] never throws; splitForLimit on send (long replies -> multiple capped, no truncation loss). SECURITY: bot-message skip (anti-loop); token=SECRET read ONLY in httpTransport Authorization: Bot <token> header at the wire, NEVER a literal (gitleaks-safe); inbound text control-stripped. Contract reconciled: base InboundMessage has no platform/userId (off-limits) -> mapped to real chatId/from/text/id/isGroup. Built+tested; wires = registry MESSAGING_CATALOG discord entry flip implemented:true + resolve.ts resolvePlatform adds a discordEnabled branch returning new DiscordAdapter (named; channel id source like Mattermost; live REST/gateway = boundary). Not configured = disabled.

items.title: Messaging adapter — Matrix

items.done: Matrix works end-to-end via the gateway: inbound message → agent → reply, honoring the allowlist; `vanta setup messaging` configures it.
items.notes: Pure/injectable parseMatrixEvents(json,selfUserId?) (m.room.message events -> InboundMessage: chatId=room_id, from=sender, text=stripControl(content.body), id=event_id, isGroup:true; SKIPS sender===selfUserId ANTI-LOOP + non-text msgtypes m.image/file; zod safeParse per element, garbage/non-array -> []) + buildMatrixSendContent ({msgtype:m.text,body} control-stripped) + parseMatrixAllowlist (VANTA_MATRIX_ALLOWLIST comma, empty -> all) + matrixEnabled (VANTA_MATRIX_HOMESERVER AND VANTA_MATRIX_TOKEN) + MatrixAdapter implements PlatformAdapter over injected {sync,sendEvent} (poll syncs+parses, markdown->plain + splitForLimit no truncation; poll/send NEVER throw). SECURITY: self-event skip anti-loop; token=SECRET read ONLY into the injected httpTransport Authorization: Bearer <token> at the wire, NEVER a literal (gitleaks-safe); inbound+outbound control-stripped. Mirrors Discord/Email. Built+tested; wires = registry MESSAGING_CATALOG matrix entry flip implemented:true + resolve.ts resolvePlatform branch (homeserver+token -> new MatrixAdapter, optional selfUserId from /whoami) (named, live client-server API = boundary). Not configured = disabled.

items.title: Messaging adapter — Email (IMAP + SMTP)
items.summary: Build the live PlatformAdapter for Email (IMAP + SMTP) (gateway/platforms/email.ts) — connect/disconnect/send/poll over the Email (IMAP + SMTP) API, wired to the catalog entry (registry.ts id 'email'). Approach: IMAP IDLE/poll for inbound + SMTP send (imapflow + nodemailer, or zero-dep). Flips implemented:true so `vanta setup messaging` configures it for real (it's preview-only today).
items.done: Email (IMAP + SMTP) works end-to-end via the gateway: inbound message → agent → reply, honoring the allowlist; `vanta setup messaging` configures it.
items.notes: Pure/injectable parseEmailMessage (raw {from,subject,body,messageId,date?} -> InboundMessage: chatId=from=sender, text=stripControl(stripQuotedReply(body)), id=messageId, isGroup:false) + stripQuotedReply (cuts at first On .. wrote: attribution + trims trailing leading-> quote block) + buildEmailReply (to=sender, subject=Re: <orig> SINGLE-prefix no Re:Re:, body verbatim) + parseEmailAllowlist (VANTA_EMAIL_ALLOWLIST comma -> lower Set, empty -> all) + emailEnabled (VANTA_EMAIL_IMAP_HOST AND VANTA_EMAIL_SMTP_HOST) + EmailAdapter implements PlatformAdapter over injected {fetchInbox,sendMail} (poll fetches+parses+allowlist-filters + learns each senders subject Map for the Re: reply WITHOUT touching base.ts; send builds+control-strips+sends; poll/send NEVER throw). SECURITY: quoted-reply history stripped so a FORGED reply chain is never echoed to the agent; inbound+outbound body control-stripped; IMAP/SMTP passwords=SECRET flow ONLY through the injected transport (imapSmtpTransport at the wire), NEVER a literal (gitleaks-safe). Most-distinct messaging adapter (IMAP/SMTP not a chat API). Built+tested; wires = registry MESSAGING_CATALOG email entry flip implemented:true + resolve.ts resolvePlatform emailEnabled branch (named, live IMAP/SMTP = boundary). Not configured = disabled.

items.title: Messaging adapter — Microsoft Teams
items.summary: The live Teams adapter now owns an authenticated Azure Bot Framework `/api/messages` ingress, buffers accepted activities into the gateway poll loop, applies the allowlist, and replies through the Bot Connector API. `vanta gateway channel-proofs teams --json` exposes accepted receipts for automation; real Azure credential proof remains before release.
items.done: Microsoft Teams works end-to-end via the gateway: inbound message → agent → reply, honoring the allowlist; `vanta setup messaging` configures it.
items.notes:
IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-10: the gateway starts push-channel HTTP handlers exposed by a PlatformAdapter, including supervised multi-channel children. Teams registers POST /api/messages, verifies Bot Framework RS256 bearer tokens against Microsoft's cached OpenID/JWKS keys, requires matching issuer, app audience, lifetime, and serviceurl, buffers only accepted activities, then uses the existing allowlist, agent session, and Connector reply path. The listener is loopback:3978 by default with documented TLS reverse-proxy settings; setup now prints the exact public endpoint.

EXECUTED LOCALLY 2026-07-10: generated a real RSA keypair/JWK and proved valid Bot Framework-shaped JWT acceptance plus missing auth, wrong audience, expiry, service URL mismatch, missing service URL, and tamper rejection. A real localhost HTTP POST then traversed gateway-owned ingress -> Teams auth seam -> allowlist -> poll/session agent turn -> Connector send with the expected reply. Focused gate: 109 passed. Full suite: 1,197 files passed; 12,441 tests passed; 3 skipped. Cross-platform gate: Windows native run 29064957089 passed fresh install, launcher, typecheck, shell, kernel, doctor, and gateway on commit c58605b5. Evidence: https://github.com/jpoindexter/Vanta/actions/runs/29064957089

DELIVERY RECEIPTS 2026-07-10: the Bot Connector transport now rejects token failures and non-2xx reply responses instead of treating them as delivered. A positive receipt propagates through supervised/multi-channel adapters and the canonical MessageStop sink; only then does the gateway append `.vanta/channel-proofs.jsonl`. Proofs hash conversation/activity ids and contain no message text. `vanta gateway channel-proofs teams` exposes the durable evidence. The authenticated-webhook-shaped localhost integration created one proof after auth -> allowlist -> agent -> accepted reply; invalid auth and send failure create none. The actual configured CLI currently reports `No accepted channel round-trip proofs for teams.`, so this remains blocked. Focused Teams/gateway gate: 83 passed; TypeScript and size gates pass. Full suite: 1,206 files, 12,498 passed, 3 skipped; no leaked tsx stack or terminal bell.

REMAINING REAL-SERVICE GATE: provide an Azure Bot app id/client secret and public HTTPS endpoint, install the app in Teams, send an allowlisted message, then require `vanta gateway channel-proofs teams` to show an accepted real-service receipt. Keep blocked until that external round trip executes.

RECHECK 2026-07-10: Still blocked by live Azure/Teams proof. Current environment exposes no VANTA_TEAMS/AZURE/MICROSOFT credentials. The code path is implemented, but release proof still requires a real Azure Bot/Teams install to send an authenticated activity through /api/messages and receive a Bot Connector reply.

UNBLOCKED/PARKED 2026-07-10: operator chose to set Teams/Azure bot setup aside for later. This card is no longer a board blocker, but it is not shipped; resume when Azure Bot credentials, a public HTTPS endpoint, and a real Teams install can produce an accepted channel proof.

JSON PROOF VIEW 2026-07-10: `vanta gateway channel-proofs [platform] --json` now returns the filtered persisted receipt list for automation. Live run for Teams currently returns `[]`, confirming no real Azure/Teams receipt is present yet; this is proof visibility only, not the external round trip.

items.notes: Pure/injectable parseLineEvents (handles BOTH the {events:[..]} wrapper AND a bare array; type:message + message.type:text -> InboundMessage: chatId=source.groupId ?? source.userId, from=source.userId, text=stripControl(message.text), id=message.id, isGroup=source.type===group; SKIPS non-message events follow/join/postback + non-text sticker/image/audio; garbage -> []) + buildLinePushBody (chatId,text -> {to:chatId, messages:[{type:text,text}]} control-stripped, ~5000 cap) + parseLineAllowlist (VANTA_LINE_ALLOWLIST comma, empty -> all; keys userId|groupId) + lineEnabled (VANTA_LINE_TOKEN) + LineAdapter implements PlatformAdapter over injected {poll,push} (PUSH api keyed by chatId, simpler than the one-time replyToken which parse drops; markdown->plain + split; poll/send NEVER throw). items.title: Eval harness — task set + sandboxed run + reward/verifier signal
items.summary: Prerequisite for any harness self-improvement (and a regression eval Vanta lacks today). A small curated task set, each run in an isolated sandbox, producing pass/fail (or scored) reward plus a step-level trace. Mirrors AHE's Evaluate stage (E2B sandboxes -> traces + reward). Reuses Vanta's tool/kernel loop; new parts are the task corpus, the sandbox runner, and the verifier. See docs/agentic-harness-engineering.md.
items.done: `vanta eval` runs the task set in sandboxes and emits per-task pass/fail + a trace file; a baseline score is recorded.

items.title: Trace distiller — events.jsonl to sourced root-cause report (Agent Debugger analog)
items.summary: AHE's 'Experience Observability': compress large execution traces into a layered, sourced root-cause report where each claim links back to the raw trace line. Vanta already emits .vanta/events.jsonl + session memory; this adds the distillation pass turning a failed/long run into an overview + per-issue detail with evidence pointers. Useful for debugging on its own, and the input the self-evolve loop consumes. See docs/agentic-harness-engineering.md.
items.done: Given a run's events.jsonl, the distiller produces an overview.md + per-issue detail with each claim citing the source trace line(s).

items.title: Self-evolving harness loop — evaluate/analyze/improve over Vanta's own components
items.summary: Pillar-5 capstone, gated behind AHE-EVAL-HARNESS + AHE-TRACE-DISTILLER. An Evolve meta-agent proposes evidence-backed, FALSIFIABLE edits (failure-evidence -> root-cause -> fix -> predicted impact -> verified next iteration, auto-rollback on regression) to Vanta's 7 evolvable components (3-tier prompt, config, tools+schemas, hooks/EF gates, skills, subagents, brain/memory). Vanta edge over AHE: the kernel ENFORCES the evolve-agent's write boundary, safer-by-construction than AHE's plain workspace dir. Do NOT build before real users + an eval set + a reward signal exist. See docs/agentic-harness-engineering.md.
items.done: Over N iterations against the eval set, the loop raises the baseline score with each accepted edit attributed to a confirmed prediction, and regressions auto-roll-back.

items.title: Eval corpus — small curated task set with deterministic checks
items.summary: The task set the eval harness runs. ~10-20 self-contained tasks each with a deterministic pass check (file exists / output matches / test passes). Start tiny; this is the signal everything else optimizes against. Part of AHE-EVAL-HARNESS.
items.done: A versioned corpus dir with N tasks, each defining setup + a deterministic verifier check.

items.title: Eval sandbox runner — isolated per-task execution
items.summary: Run each corpus task in an isolated env (temp dir + kernel scope, or a container) so runs don't bleed into each other or the real repo. Mirrors AHE's E2B stage; reuses Vanta's tool/kernel loop. Part of AHE-EVAL-HARNESS.
items.done: A task runs end-to-end in a fresh sandbox and produces a step-level trace; cleanup leaves no residue.

items.title: Eval verifier — per-task reward/score signal
items.summary: Run each task's deterministic check after a sandboxed run and emit pass/fail (or a score) — the reward signal the evolve loop maximizes. Part of AHE-EVAL-HARNESS.
items.done: Each run yields a reward (pass/fail or 0..1) written alongside its trace.

items.title: `vanta eval` — run the corpus, record a baseline score
items.summary: CLI that runs the whole corpus through sandbox+verifier and reports an aggregate score + per-task results, recording a baseline to track regressions/lifts. Part of AHE-EVAL-HARNESS.
items.done: `vanta eval` prints aggregate + per-task pass/fail and writes a baseline score file.

items.title: Evolve workspace — kernel-bounded write surface over the 7 components
items.summary: Expose Vanta's 7 evolvable components (3-tier prompt, config, tools+schemas, hooks/EF gates, skills, subagents, brain/memory) as the ONLY paths the evolve agent may write — enforced by the kernel (Vanta's edge over AHE's plain workspace/ dir). Part of AHE-SELF-EVOLVE. NOTE 2026-06-16: largely provided by the existing `factory/` loop — compartments = the kernel-bounded evolve workspace; kernel-protected factory/skeleton + holdout.ts = the guardrails. Remaining work = wire an 'evolve' factory objective that optimizes the new `vanta eval` score by editing reflexes/memory/limbs, keep/rollback by score delta.
items.done: An evolve-scoped run can write only the 7 component paths; any other write is kernel-blocked.

items.title: Evolve agent — proposes falsifiable, evidence-backed edits
items.summary: Meta-agent that reads a distilled root-cause report and proposes a component edit carrying failure-evidence -> root-cause -> fix -> PREDICTED impact. One change at a time, scoped to the evolve workspace. Part of AHE-SELF-EVOLVE. Empirical (arXiv 2604.25850): bias edits toward TOOLS / MIDDLEWARE / LONG-TERM MEMORY — that's where the measured gain concentrates and transfers across tasks/models; prose-level system-prompt edits don't transfer (the prompt alone regresses -2.3pp).
items.done: Given a distilled report, the agent emits a single scoped edit + a written, checkable impact prediction.

items.title: Evolve loop — apply, re-eval, confirm/falsify prediction, auto-rollback
items.summary: Orchestrate the iteration: apply the evolve agent's edit, re-run `vanta eval`, compare to baseline, confirm or falsify the edit's prediction, keep on lift / roll back on regression, repeat. Part of AHE-SELF-EVOLVE.
items.done: Over N iterations the loop accepts lifts, rolls back regressions, and logs each prediction as held/failed.

items.title: Full-state checkpoints — snapshot + restore/branch a session's complete state
items.summary: From 'From AGI to ASI' Table 1 (lossless replication: backup & restore arbitrarily; spawn/halt/resume). Beyond today's resume/fork: named checkpoints capturing the COMPLETE state (messages + brain/memory + goals + todos), restorable or branchable mid-run. Gives an autonomous run a rollback floor — return to a known-good point after a bad turn. See docs/from-agi-to-asi-notes.md.
items.done: `/checkpoint <name>` snapshots full state; `/restore <name>` returns to it (or branches a new session); verified across an autonomous run.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04 to the done line. Prior state was in-memory LIFO only (/checkpoint saved, /rollback POPPED the latest — no random access, no restore-by-name, no branch). Added: CheckpointStore.find(nameOrId) (non-destructive lookup by cp-id or label, most-recent on repeated label) + a /restore <name|id> [branch] handler (repl/checkpoint-cmd.ts) — restores messages+turnIndex in place, or `branch` forks a NEW persisted session from the checkpoint via injected saveSession/newSessionId. Wired into the readline REPL dispatch (interactive-repl.ts tryCheckpointCmd) alongside the existing checkpoint/rollback; cataloged /checkpoint + /restore. Verified: 16 unit tests (round-trip restore, by-id, non-destructive re-restore, unknown-name no-op, branch persists a new session with the checkpoint messages while leaving the live convo untouched); tsc + size gate clean. BOUNDARY: the checkpoint snapshots the CONVERSATION state (messages + turnIndex); the summary's broader 'brain/memory/goals/todos' are NOT bundled into the checkpoint — they already persist via their own stores (brain git-versioned, goals in the kernel ledger). Bundling those into one checkpoint blob is a follow-up if wanted.

items.title: Fleet digest — steerable summary when agents output more than a human can read
items.summary: From 'From AGI to ASI' pathway-4 open question: how humans meaningfully interact with and steer large groups of agents producing volumes of artifacts impossible to consume in full. When a multi-agent run exceeds human reading capacity, surface a digest (what each agent found, conflicts, decisions needing input) + steering controls instead of raw volume. See docs/from-agi-to-asi-notes.md.
items.done: A run with N subagents shows a digest (per-agent finding + conflicts + pending decisions) the operator can act on without reading every raw trace.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-09. Added `formatFleetDigest(report)` and wired it into `vanta fleet run` plus `vanta fleet digest [fleet-id]`. The digest shows per-worker findings, conflicts/blockers, branch collisions, and operator decisions such as accept/reject done workers or unblock/retire blocked workers. Verification: focused fleet tests cover pure digest formatting, run output, saved-report digest, fleet execution regressions, batch command compatibility, plus `npx tsc --noEmit` and `git diff --check`.

items.title: Self-evolve metrics — lift-per-iteration + human-in-loop ratio + spend
items.summary: From 'From AGI to ASI' pathway-3 research: monitor how much AI facilitates AI R&D, the degree of human-in-the-loop, and formulate recursive-improvement scaling laws. Instrument the AHE self-evolve loop to record per-iteration lift vs baseline, human-in-loop ratio, and compute/token spend — so the loop's own returns are observable (and diminishing returns are visible). See docs/from-agi-to-asi-notes.md.
items.done: Each evolve iteration logs lift-vs-baseline, human-in-loop ratio, and spend; a trend view shows whether returns are compounding or tapering.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. Instrumented the AHE self-evolve loop (src/evolve/) so its OWN returns are observable. evolve/recursion-metrics.ts (pure): computeRecursionMetrics(baseline, iterations) → per-iter liftVsBaseline (after−baseline) + marginalLift (after−before) + kept + spend + human-in-loop, plus humanInLoopRatio, totalSpendUsd, liftPerUsd efficiency (null when no spend — honest, no divide-by-zero), and classifyTrend over KEPT marginal lifts (compounding ↗ / flat → / tapering ↘ = diminishing returns / insufficient). formatRecursionMetrics renders the trend headline + per-iter table. EvolveIteration + Proposal gained optional spendUsd/humanInLoop (non-breaking); loop.ts carries them through. Surfaced two ways: printed at the end of every `vanta evolve` run, and `vanta evolve metrics` reads evolve-journal.jsonl and prints the trend on demand. The autonomous propose turn auto-approves (requestApproval→true) so human-in-loop is a genuine 0% — the metric correctly shows a fully autonomous loop. 11 tests (trend up/down/flat/insufficient, lift/marginal/ratio/spend/efficiency, null-spend honesty, format). Full suite 1060 files/11881 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Calibrated solutioning — ranged/ensembled recommendations + revisit triggers
items.summary: From 'From AGI to ASI' (recurring): entertain a range of possibilities, attach uncertainty estimates to forecasts, ensemble, and frequently revisit. Make Vanta's solutioning recommendations carry calibrated ranges (best/realistic/worst) instead of point estimates, and emit a revisit trigger when a key assumption is cheap to check or likely stale. See docs/from-agi-to-asi-notes.md.
items.done: A solutioning recommendation ships with a range + named uncertainty drivers + a concrete revisit trigger, not a single-point claim.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. solutioning/calibration.ts (pure, from docs/from-agi-to-asi-notes.md): CalibratedRecommendation = {claim, metric, range best/realistic/worst, confidence, uncertaintyDrivers[], revisitTrigger} — the SHAPE a solutioning recommendation must ship in, never a bare point. calibrateRange widens the best↔worst band as confidence drops (higherIsBetter flips it for good-when-larger metrics); ensembleRange means independent estimates and DERIVES confidence from their divergence (≤25% spread→high, >60%→low — 'entertain a range, ensemble' made concrete; throws on empty); isCalibrated enforces the contract (≥1 named driver + a revisit trigger + ordered range) so a point estimate is rejected; formatRecommendation renders range+drivers+trigger. 8 tests (band widening + flip, ensemble agree/diverge/empty, isCalibrated rejects a bare point / missing trigger / inverted range, formatter shows all three parts). Full suite 1035 files/11662 green; tsc + size gate clean. The primitive is what solutioning outputs (ideation/launchpad/research-decompose) format against; no single emit hook existed to retrofit, so the calibrated shape + its guard is the deliverable per the done criterion.

items.title: Regression foresight — predict what an evolve edit will BREAK, not just fix
items.summary: The AHE paper's #1 open problem (arXiv 2604.25850): the evolve loop predicts fixes well (~5x baseline) but is blind to regressions (~2x baseline) — it can't name the tasks an edit is about to break, which causes non-monotone evolution. Add a regression-foresight pass: before applying an edit, predict at-risk tasks (from the trace corpus + edit scope) and gate/scope/targeted-re-eval accordingly. Closes the gap the paper calls the clearest direction for future self-evolution loops. See docs/agentic-harness-engineering.md. UPDATE 2026-06-16: regression DETECTION now works (live run reverted a fix-sum regression). Still open: FORESIGHT (predict at-risk tasks BEFORE the edit) — this card's core.
items.done: Before an edit is committed, the loop emits a ranked at-risk-task set; measured regression-recall beats the current ~11% baseline and the evolution curve trends monotone.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-09. The loop already emits a ranked at-risk-task set before measurement/commit; now the repo includes a real JSONL evolve-journal fixture and the CLI can backtest an explicit journal path. Executed real command: npx tsx src/cli.ts evolve foresight vanta-ts/test/fixtures/evolve-journal.scoped-regressions.jsonl -> 16 scored iterations, foresight recall 50% vs frequency baseline 38%, BEATS baseline, monotone curve. Verification: regression-foresight + evolve-loop focused tests green (10 tests), CLI path executed, and npx tsc --noEmit clean.

items.title: Evolve guardrails — kernel-enforced no-reward-hacking (verifier/model/budget read-only)
items.summary: Controllability from the AHE paper (arXiv 2604.25850): the evolve agent must be UNABLE to game its own score — no disabling the verifier, swapping the base model, or raising the reasoning/token budget; tracer + verifier + LLM config are read-only and the seed system prompt is non-deletable. Vanta's kernel is the natural enforcer (rule zero: it already gates every write). Distinct from AHE-EVOLVE-WORKSPACE (which bounds WHICH components are writable) — this protects the EVALUATION machinery from the thing being evaluated. See docs/agentic-harness-engineering.md. NOTE 2026-06-16: largely provided by the existing `factory/` loop — compartments = the kernel-bounded evolve workspace; kernel-protected factory/skeleton + holdout.ts = the guardrails. Remaining work = wire an 'evolve' factory objective that optimizes the new `vanta eval` score by editing reflexes/memory/limbs, keep/rollback by score delta. V0 2026-06-16: evolve loop ships with brain-only target + kernel Block floor + eval/verifier running OUTSIDE the agent (un-tamperable). Still TODO: explicit enforcement that the evolve agent can't raise its own budget / swap model. SHIPPED 2026-06-16: per-rollout brain freeze (withFrozen) keeps the component-under-evolution frozen DURING measurement; eval/verifier run outside the agent; the evolve agent's only persisted change is its deliberate edit.
items.done: An evolve run cannot modify the verifier, model selection, or budget; attempts are kernel-blocked and logged; the seed system prompt cannot be deleted.

items.title: Interaction-aware evolution — account for non-additive component effects
items.summary: From the AHE paper (arXiv 2604.25850): single-component gains don't stack cleanly (3 edits summing +11.1pp net only +7.3pp together) because components spend turns on redundant re-checks. Make the evolve loop interaction-aware — consider cross-component effects when composing edits, dedupe redundant verification, rather than greedily stacking single-component fixes. Named as future work in the paper. See docs/agentic-harness-engineering.md.
items.done: When two edits touch interacting components, the loop composes them without redundant re-checks and the combined lift approaches the sum of their isolated gains.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-09. Added `composeInteractionAware`, a pure composition planner for harness component edits that merges predicted fixes, dedupes shared verification checks, tracks interacting components, and estimates combined lift against the isolated-lift sum. Wired optional `componentEdits` into the evolve loop so a proposal touching multiple interacting components emits an interaction plan, sends the merged predicted-fix set into regression foresight, and records the interaction-aware summary on the iteration. Verified with `npx vitest run src/evolve/interaction-aware.test.ts src/evolve/loop.test.ts src/evolve/regression-foresight.test.ts --reporter=dot` (13 passed), `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run vanta -- lint src/evolve/interaction-aware.ts src/evolve/interaction-aware.test.ts src/evolve/loop.ts src/evolve/loop.test.ts`. This is composition/deduping behavior, not a live benchmark lift claim.

items.title: Live kanban — decompose a goal into lanes + run a subagent swarm
items.summary: From a reference agent (kanban + kanban_decompose/_swarm/_specify/_diagnostics): a live work board that auto-decomposes a goal into board lanes, optionally generates a spec per lane, and runs a subagent SWARM across them in parallel — distinct from Vanta's static roadmap.json (strategy backlog) and the one-shot Workflow tool. Serves the Harness 'multi-agent mechanics' pillar as a durable, inspectable execution surface. Local-first (no cloud).
items.done: A goal becomes a kanban with auto-decomposed lanes; a swarm runs lanes in parallel; progress + blockers are inspectable and resumable.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added `vanta kanban` with `create`, `status`, `digest`, and `swarm` commands. A goal now decomposes into persistent lanes (`understand`, `plan`, `build`, `verify`, `ship`) stored under `.vanta/kanban`; the local swarm runs unfinished lanes in parallel, records done/blocked lane outcomes, appends a swarm run record, and can resume the latest board by id or by latest board. This intentionally ships local/dry-run swarm mechanics first; live model-backed workers can build on the same board/run model. Live evidence: `npm run vanta -- kanban create "ship VANTA-KANBAN roadmap card" && npm run vanta -- kanban swarm && npm run vanta -- kanban status` created a real board, ran 5 lanes to done, and reloaded the persisted status. Tests: `npx vitest run src/kanban/kanban.test.ts src/cli/kanban-cmd.test.ts`; `npm run typecheck`; `git diff --check`.

items.title: Blueprints — reusable project/agent scaffolds you instantiate
items.summary: From a reference agent (blueprint_cmd): named, parameterized scaffolds (project + agent setup + tools/skills config) you instantiate in one command, instead of hand-rolling each new setup. Grows Vanta without core edits (Extensibility pillar).
items.done: `vanta blueprint <name>` scaffolds a configured project/agent from a reusable template; new blueprints are addable without touching src/.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. Data-driven scaffolds — a blueprint is a blueprint.json (no src/ edit to add one). blueprint/apply.ts (pure): zod BlueprintSchema {name,description,vars[{key,description,default?}],files[{path,content}]}, parseVarArgs (k=v, values may contain =), resolveVars (provided→default→missing report), applyTemplate ({{key}} substitution, unknown placeholders left intact), planBlueprint (vars applied to BOTH paths and contents → write-plan, no I/O). blueprint/store.ts: listBlueprints/getBlueprint over TWO sources — bundled vanta-ts/blueprints/ + user ~/.vanta/blueprints/ (user overrides bundled by name). cli/blueprint-cmd.ts: `vanta blueprint list` + `vanta blueprint <name> [k=v ...] [--out dir] [--force]` — plans, refuses existing files unless --force, writes + reports. Ships a bundled `vanta-tool` blueprint (scaffolds a kernel-gated Tool + co-located test from the ports/adapters pattern). LIVE-VERIFIED: `vanta blueprint vanta-tool name=read_weather summary=… --out <tmp>` wrote 2 files with vars substituted in paths + contents. 11 tests (schema validate/reject, var parse, resolve defaults+missing, template substitution, plan, bundled load out-of-box, user-override-without-src-edit, unknown→null). Gotcha fixed: a JSDoc comment containing the glob `/*/` closed the comment early (TS1160) — reworded. New CLI command (not a tool/slash). Full suite 1064 files/11907 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Skills hub — discover/share skills + agentskills.io standard compat
items.summary: From a reference agent (skills_hub + agentskills.io open standard): beyond authoring skills locally, discover/import/share skills via a hub and stay compatible with the agentskills.io open format so Vanta skills interop with the wider ecosystem.
items.done: Vanta can import/export skills in the agentskills.io format and pull from a hub; a third-party skill loads without code changes.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. Interop with the agentskills.io / Anthropic Agent Skills open format (SKILL.md + YAML frontmatter: name/description/allowed-tools/license + body). skills/agentskills-format.ts (pure): fromAgentSkills (maps the spec frontmatter → Vanta Skill; allowed-tools scalar-or-list + license preserved; created/updated default to now; unknown fields like `version` dropped so ANY conformant third-party skill loads with no code change) + toAgentSkills (emits only the portable fields — Vanta's created/updated/tags/triggers omitted). Made the STORE round-trip lossless: parseMeta (now table-driven) + serializeSkill + writeSkill carry allowedTools/license (added as optional SkillMeta fields). CLI (cli/skills-interop.ts): `vanta skills import <file>` (load a third-party SKILL.md), `export <name> --format agentskills` (emit interop format), `hub <url>` (fetch any hub-hosted SKILL.md over the open format + import). LIVE-VERIFIED: a third-party 'Interop Demo' skill with allowed-tools+license+version imported, then exported → allowed-tools [Bash, Read] + license Apache-2.0 preserved through the store. 8 format unit tests (third-party load, scalar/list allowed-tools, unknown-field tolerance, import→export→import round-trip, minimal output). The hub is a generic open-format URL fetch (not a proprietary API) — honest, works with any agentskills.io host. Full suite 1062 files/11896 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Cost guard — hard per-run/session budget ceiling that warns/blocks
items.summary: From a reference agent (model_cost_guard): Vanta TRACKS cost but doesn't gate on it. Add a configurable ceiling (per run / per session) that warns as it approaches and blocks/asks before exceeding — so an autonomous run can't silently overspend. Operator-trust feature.
items.done: A run with a budget set warns near the ceiling and halts (or asks) before exceeding it; the limit is configurable per run/session.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07 on the existing budget module (PCLIP-BUDGET-HARDSTOP). budget/guard.ts (pure): guardBeforeTurn(budget|null, estimatedNextUsd, mode) → allow/warn/halt/ask. No budget→allow; already-exceeded OR spent+estimate would cross the limit→halt (or ask in ask mode) BEFORE the turn overspends; in the warn band (≥warnFraction, next turn still fits)→warn+proceed; else allow. estimate 0 = unknown → only current spend gates. Wired into interactive-post-turn.ts maybeHaltOnBudgetCap: the pre-existing VANTA_MAX_BUDGET_USD/--max-budget-usd session cap now WARNS as it nears the ceiling (was halt-only) and HALTS using last-turn cost as the next-turn estimate so it stops BEFORE the crossing turn, not only after. Byte-identical when no cap is set (early return). Configurable per run/session via the existing flag/env; the generic budget module already scopes session/goal/loop/agent and the `budget` tool sets per-scope limits. ask-mode is built (headless wiring uses halt as the safe autonomous default). 7 guard tests (allow/warn/halt-before-cross/already-exceeded/ask/unknown-estimate). Full suite 1065 files/11914 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Proactive suggestions + session recap
items.summary: From a reference agent (suggestions_cmd + tips + session_recap): surface proactive next-step suggestions during a session and a concise recap on resume (what happened, where you left off, what's next) — beyond Vanta's existing handoff/resume block. Operator daily-driver polish.
items.done: On resume Vanta shows a recap (done / in-progress / next); during a session it can offer a ranked next-step suggestion on request.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. repl/suggestions.ts (pure + one handler): formatRecap (done/in-progress/next, 3-section, empty-state text, caps 3), rankSuggestions (DETERMINISTIC, top-3: finish in-progress first → ship smallest ready backlog pebble-first → advance an idle active goal), formatSuggestions, buildSuggestView (recap + ranked list). Both DONE clauses met: (1) `/suggest` slash command — deterministic recap + ranked next-step ON REQUEST, no model turn (distinct from /next which asks the model); (2) resumeRecap auto-shown ON RESUME — wired into interactive.ts announceSessionStart after the '↻ Resumed session' notice (best-effort, never throws). Gathers active/done kernel goals + top roadmap backlog (reuses next.ts readNextItems + choice-reduce topNextItems). 12 tests incl. resumeRecap with injected getGoals + the kernel-down degrade path. 129 slash commands. Full suite 1059 files/11872 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Kernel: remove the hardcoded Jason path from the scope check
items.summary: safety.rs mentions_outside_scope() hardcodes '/users/jasonpoindexter/documents/github' — a Jason-specific leak in the SAFETY BOUNDARY. Derive scope purely from `root`. Blocks the neurodivergent-agent-for-anyone reframe and is a latent correctness bug (wrong on any other machine/user). Small, high-priority. cargo test must stay green. See . SHIPPED 2026-06-16: mentions_outside_scope now derives from root.parent() (generic); all test fixtures use a neutral /repo/projects/vanta path; zero jasonpoindexter refs remain in src/; 41 kernel tests green.
items.done: safety.rs has no hardcoded user/path; scope checks derive from root only; kernel tests green on a non-Jason root.

items.title: Kernel: canonicalize paths + resolve symlinks in scope checks
items.summary: normalize() in safety.rs joins but doesn't canonicalize — a symlink inside root pointing out, or a `..` traversal, can defeat inside_scope/references_abs_path_outside_root. Canonicalize + resolve symlinks (TOCTOU-aware) so the scope boundary can't be walked out of. See . SHIPPED 2026-06-16: added lexically_normalize (resolves ../.) + resolve_scope_path (fs canonicalize/symlink when path exists, else lexical) + is_inside (trailing-sep containment, kills sibling-prefix bypass). inside_scope/references_abs_path_outside_root/is_protected_path all routed through it; .. traversal + sibling-prefix + symlink bypasses closed. 45 kernel tests green (+4).
items.done: A symlink/`..` path that escapes root is correctly classified Ask/Block; added kernel tests for symlink + traversal bypass.

items.title: Kernel: tamper-evident audit log (hash-chained events.jsonl)
items.summary: events.jsonl is append-only plaintext with no integrity guarantee. Hash-chain each event (prev-hash → this-event) so the audit trail is tamper-evident — strengthens the 'trusted operator, only verified output' story and makes after-the-fact review trustworthy. See . SHIPPED 2026-06-16: src/audit.rs — vendored zero-dep SHA-256 (NIST-vector tested) + hash chain h=sha256(key+prev+payload) over every events.jsonl line, per-install secret key (.vanta/audit.key, 0600). `vanta-kernel audit verify` walks+recomputes the chain; any edit/insert/delete/reorder is rejected. 53 kernel tests green (+4). Threat model: detects tampering by accidental edits, other users, and key-less processes; a same-user attacker who reads audit.key could forge (future: TPM/keychain-held key or HMAC).
items.done: Each event records a hash over (prev-hash + payload); a verifier detects any insert/edit/delete in the chain.

items.title: Kernel: cut assess() denylist false positives (verb+target parse)
items.summary: assess_action() is pure substring matching → false positives ('delete a line in the file' → Block; any 'api key' mention → Block; 'install' → Ask). Parse the action verb + target so innocent phrasings stop tripping the gate — reduce friction WITHOUT weakening the Block floor (which must stay first + immovable). See . SHIPPED 2026-06-16 (safe slice): EXFIL + MACHINE_CONFIG no longer fire on explicit SEARCH actions (grep for/web search:/search/glob) — a sensitive word in a query is a mention, not an action. Block floor + secret-FILE reads (read file ~/.ssh → Ask) + non-search installs unaffected. Deeper verb+target parsing (e.g. "delete a line" vs "delete the file", which conflicts with the intentional "delete the branch"→Block test) deliberately DEFERRED as a larger, riskier design change. 49 kernel tests green (+2).
items.done: Benign phrasings ('delete a line', 'describe the api key flow') no longer Block; destructive/exfil cases still Block; new regression tests for both.

items.title: Kernel: robust write-path extraction for the protected-path check
items.summary: extract_write_path() only recognizes 3 prefixes ('write file ', 'write to ', 'overwrite '); other phrasings skip the is_protected_path check. Broaden extraction so a write to a protected path (kernel src, factory/, manifesto) can't be evaded by wording. See . SHIPPED 2026-06-16: broadened extract_write_path to a WRITE_VERBS set (incl. "edit file" which edit-file.ts actually emits — closing a real bypass where editing src/safety.rs Allowed). Generic verbs require a path-like target (looks_like_path) so the reversibility classifier is not weakened. 47 kernel tests green (+2).
items.done: Writes to protected paths are caught across varied phrasings; tests cover the new phrasings.

items.title: ND: full executive-function gate engine (user-facing, configurable)
items.summary: Vanta's EF gates today are antiSlop/modeDetect/researchGate/stallUnblock. Promote to the full neurodivergent support set as FIRST-CLASS, per-user-configurable product behaviors (the way the assistant's own ND skills work, but FOR the user): research-gate, complexity-gate, task-initiation, hyperfocus-guard, time-blindness, closure-gate, velocity-check, set-shift, inhibit. This is the core of the ND-first positioning. Stay lean — one engine, gates as small pure rules. See .
items.done: The EF-gate set is configurable per user and fires in-session (e.g. research-gate nudges after N read-only turns; hyperfocus-guard after long single-area runs); each gate is independently toggleable.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-17. One engine (nd/engine.ts runEfGates) runs all 9 gates as small pure rules (nd/gates.ts): research, complexity, task-initiation, hyperfocus, time-blindness, closure, velocity, set-shift, inhibit. Per-user config persisted in nd/profile.ts — each gate independently enabled + threshold-tunable; engine-level on/off. /nd command views/toggles/tunes (repl/nd-cmd.ts). Fires in-session via session/nd-gates.ts wired into repl/post-turn-gates.ts. Now wired into BOTH hosts: readline REPL (interactive-turn.ts) AND the default TUI (ui/use-agent.ts runTurnGates after each successful turn → nudges as transcript notes), closing the prior TUI-parity gap. 26 unit tests (23 engine + 3 TUI-wiring); typecheck + size gate clean; 246 UI+gate tests green.

items.title: ND: always-on working-memory rail (goal + step + remaining)
items.summary: `wm` exists as a command; make it an always-on rail that surfaces the active task context (goal + current step + remaining steps) and RE-ANCHORS after context compaction — the single highest-value ND support (working-memory load is the core deficit). See .
items.done: A compact working-memory anchor is visible during long sessions and re-printed after compaction without being asked.
items.notes: Pure WorkingMemory{goal?,currentStep?,remaining[]} + buildWorkingMemoryRail (◇ goal · ▸ step · +N more; each free-text part clipped to partWidth 48 + ellipsis; full-ANSI/C0/C1/DEL stripped since text untrusted; empty -> "") + railVisible (content AND not VANTA_WM_RAIL=0) + railSegments ({label,text}[] present parts) + hasWorkingMemory predicate. ND working-memory aid as a persistent rail. Built+tested; wire = app-regions Footer replaces the lone ◇ goal line with buildWorkingMemoryRail from active goal (getGoals) + todo/next state, gated by railVisible (named). Empty = hidden.

items.title: ND: per-user neurodivergent support profile
items.summary: Generalize operator-profile beyond one user into a per-user ND profile: which EF gates/supports are on, their thresholds, sensory/output preferences, time-support style. This is the mechanism that makes Vanta 'not just Jason' — any ND user configures the supports that fit them. See .
items.done: A new user can set an ND profile (gates on/off, thresholds, output density) that persists and drives the EF engine + UI.

items.title: ND: choice reduction — top-3 ranked + hidden count

items.title: Async background delegation — handle in ~2ms, result re-enters as a new turn

Verify against Vanta's current delegate/team/workflow (which may be synchronous) and close the gap. See .
items.done: delegate(background=true) returns immediately; the subagent's result appears as a new turn when idle, self-contained, without breaking message order or the prompt cache.

items.title: Frictionless Google/comms auth onboarding — client.json ingest, publish-state + 7-day-token handling
items.summary: Per-user Google comms setup is too heavy and has two footguns: (1) a Testing-status OAuth consent screen makes refresh tokens for restricted scopes (gmail.modify, drive) + sensitive (calendar) expire after 7 days, silently breaking ~/.vanta/google-tokens.json roughly weekly; (2) the current flow makes users copy-paste client_id/secret — a real leak vector (already leaked once into chat). Near-term fix (THIS card): make `vanta auth google` a guided wizard — accept Google's downloaded client_secret.json directly (`--client <path>`, no copy-paste), detect Testing-vs-Published status and warn about the 7-day expiry, and walk the user through Publish App + the unverified-app 'Advanced -> Continue' click-through. Makes BYO-app onboarding bearable + safe WITHOUT owning a verified app. Two documented scale paths, both decision-gated and NOT built here: (A) Theft Studio owns ONE verified Google app -> every user just clicks Authorize, zero console work (best UX) — but gmail.modify/drive are restricted scopes requiring an annual third-party CASA security assessment + makes Theft the data controller for users' mail; gate on a real 2nd comms user (-> DECISIONS). (B) MCP-delegation (the reference agent posture, see VANTA-MCP-AUTH): drop native Google, point at a Google MCP server — but Google does NOT support OAuth dynamic client registration, so MCP only RELOCATES the verification cost to whoever runs that server, it does not remove it; Vanta MCP tools stay kernel-gated so no thesis break. When verifying: switch Drive to drive.file (sensitive, no assessment) vs full drive (restricted). Impl site: vanta-ts/src/google/auth.ts (runGoogleAuth reads env-only today).
items.done: `vanta auth google --client <client_secret.json>` ingests Google's downloaded client JSON (no id/secret copy-paste); the flow detects Testing-vs-Published publishing status and warns that Testing-status refresh tokens expire in 7 days, then guides Publish App + the unverified-app click-through. Verified-app and MCP-delegation scale paths are captured in the summary as decision-gated (not built in this card).

items.title: Brain↔vault bridge: auto-graduate crystallized knowledge to the Obsidian vault
items.summary: Make the in-core brain and the obsidian-vault MCP work as one memory system, two organs. Write-side, deterministic (not model-dependent): when a brain SEMANTIC memory crystallizes (≥10 retrievals via repeated recall), it's auto-written to the vault as wiki/concepts/<slug>.md with brain-provenance frontmatter, and the brain entry is stamped sourceRef:vault:<path> so it never duplicates. Division of labor: facts about the user or self → brain (decays, auto); durable domain knowledge → vault (permanent, searchable). Core reads the vault path from ~/.vanta/mcp.json (no subprocess, writes the file directly) and no-ops cleanly when the vault MCP isn't configured. Prompt vaultTier now states the labor split so Vanta stops hand-copying. Impl: brain/vault-bridge.ts (resolveVaultPath/isPromotable/writeVaultPage), brain/brain.ts promoteCrystallized hooked into recall(), prompt.ts.
items.done: A crystallized semantic brain fact is written once to the vault as a wiki page (provenance frontmatter), stamped promoted so it never duplicates; no-ops without a configured vault; prompt states the brain/vault division of labor. Unit-tested (12 bridge + 71 brain green).

items.title: Brain↔vault read-side bridge: unified recall + vault→brain priming
items.summary: Complete the bridge on the read side (write-side shipped in BRAIN-VAULT-BRIDGE). Today recall consulting both organs is prompt-level only — the agent uses brain recall + vault_search separately, by choice. Harden it: (1) a unified recall path that queries brain entries AND the vault in one call, merged + deduped by provenance (brain sourceRef:vault: pointers collapse to the vault page); (2) vault→brain priming — when Vanta reads a vault page that's about the user or a standing project, seed a brain salience/user_model entry so self-relevant knowledge flows back; (3) brain digest optionally surfaces a 'relevant vault pages' pointer line for the active goal. Keep core decoupled from the optional MCP vault (read the vault filesystem directly like vault-bridge.ts, or go through the MCP client when mounted). No-op without a vault.
items.done: A single recall surfaces relevant brain memories AND vault pages, deduped against brain→vault pointers; reading a user/project-relevant vault page primes a brain entry; degrades cleanly when no vault is configured. Unit-tested.

items.title: TUI: OSC 10/11 terminal color auto-detection
items.summary: Query the terminal's actual foreground/background colors via OSC 10/11 escape sequences at startup, giving a precise light/dark reading without relying on COLORFGBG (which many terminals don't set). Parse the xterm-style rgb: response, compute luminance, and feed it into resolveTheme() so the mono/light theme selection is accurate out-of-the-box for any terminal (iTerm, WezTerm, Ghostty, kitty, etc.). Fall back to COLORFGBG → mono if the terminal doesn't respond within a tight timeout (~50ms). Impl site: vanta-ts/src/term/theme.ts detectBackground + new oscQuery helper.
items.done: On a light-background terminal with no VANTA_THEME set, Vanta automatically selects the light theme; on a dark terminal it selects mono — without requiring COLORFGBG to be set. Falls back gracefully when the terminal doesn't support OSC queries.

items.title: TUI composer: fish-shell-style history typeahead (ghost text)
items.summary: As the user types in the composer, show the most likely completion from input history as dim ghost text (same pattern as fish shell and zsh-autosuggestions). Right-arrow or End accepts the full suggestion; any other key ignores it. Match against the full history array already maintained by the composer (up/down history exists; typeahead reuses the same data). Ghost text should render after the cursor in a muted colour from the theme (e.g. dim white) and disappear as soon as the typed prefix no longer matches. Only surface a suggestion when the prefix matches the beginning of a recent entry — no fuzzy matching on the first pass. Impl site: vanta-ts/src/ui/composer.tsx + the history hook.
items.done: Typing a prefix in the composer shows a dim ghost-text completion from history; right-arrow or End accepts it; mismatches clear the ghost immediately; no suggestion shown on empty input.
items.notes: Pure ghostSuggestion(input,history) (remainder of the MOST-RECENT control-stripped history entry that startsWith input; input empty/no match/input===full entry -> ; case-sensitive) + acceptGhost (input + ghost, else unchanged) + ghostVisible. SECURITY FIX: the ghost is CONTROL-STRIPPED via bash-io stripToLine (ANSI+C0/C1+DEL) — the gap the existing historyTypeahead had (it rendered un-stripped history into the live terminal); asserted with ESC/OSC-title/BEL/NUL/SGR fixtures (ghost never contains an ESC). stripToLine newly EXPORTED from bash-io (additive). Built+tested; wire = composer.tsx replaces composer-keys historyTypeahead with ghostSuggestion(buffer,history) rendered dim after the cursor + Right/End/Ctrl+F -> acceptGhost (named). Empty/no-match = no ghost.

items.title: Code intelligence behind a swappable provider port (codegraph = default adapter)
items.summary: Give Vanta-the-operator code intelligence so the factory/executor stops acting on code blind — built as a SWAPPABLE PORT so a better engine can replace codegraph with nothing breaking. Architecture = ports & adapters: define ONE interface CodeIntelProvider (port) in vanta-ts/src/code-intel/provider.ts with methods context(task)/search(sym)/affected(files)/ensureIndexed(); ship CodegraphProvider as the default adapter (shells out to the already-installed codegraph CLI, which stays a standalone component — not vendored/embedded/forked). A single resolver (code-intel/index.ts) picks the active provider; swapping engines = write a new adapter + change one registration line. Expose 4 kernel-gated tools that call the PORT, never codegraph directly: code_context, code_search, code_affected, code_index. HARD CONSTRAINTS: (1) nothing outside the adapter imports codegraph — core/factory/tools depend only on the CodeIntelProvider interface; (2) provider methods return Result<T,E> and degrade gracefully ('code intelligence unavailable') when no engine is present — never throw, never break a session; (3) absent/unconfigured provider is a silent no-op via a capability check; (4) removal/replacement = swap or delete one adapter file, ZERO changes to tools/core/factory. Impl: vanta-ts/src/code-intel/{provider.ts (port + types + Zod), codegraph.ts (adapter), index.ts (resolver)} + vanta-ts/src/tools/{code-context,code-search,code-affected,code-index}.ts; register in tools/registry.ts + all-tools.ts; vitest mocks the adapter exec and tests the no-engine path. This is the REFERENCE implementation of Vanta's modular port/adapter convention.
items.done: Agent calls code_context/code_search/code_affected/code_index against the operating root, kernel-gated, all routed through the CodeIntelProvider port; codegraph is one adapter behind that port; swapping in a different engine is one new adapter + one registration line with no edits to tools/core/factory; with no provider present the tools return a graceful 'unavailable' Result and nothing else breaks.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18 — the REFERENCE port/adapter implementation. code-intel/provider.ts = CodeIntelProvider port (context/search/affected/ensureIndexed/available) + hand-rolled Result<T,E> + nullProvider no-op. code-intel/codegraph.ts = default adapter shelling out to the installed `codegraph` CLI (context→`context`, search→`query`, affected→`affected`, ensureIndexed→`index`); never throws — ENOENT/non-zero/missing-index become Result errors. code-intel/index.ts = resolveCodeIntel(root,env) (VANTA_CODE_INTEL, default codegraph; none/off→nullProvider) = the one registration line. 4 kernel-gated tools (code_context/code_search/code_affected/code_index) call only the port. The HARD `code-intel-seam` fitness rule (from ARCH-BOUNDARY-FITNESS) guarantees nothing outside the adapter imports the engine (the adapter shells out — zero TS import). Tools now 88 built-in / 90 registered. code-intel.test.ts (resolver, no-op degradation, never-throws, tool wiring) + full suite 3544 green; live smoke: code_search('resolveBrain') returned a real hit; typecheck + size gate + all 5 boundaries clean. Removing codegraph = delete codegraph.ts + one ADAPTERS entry, zero tool/core edits.

items.title: Factory uses code intelligence — additive, fully optional, no-op if removed
items.summary: Wire the code-intel tools (see CODE-INTEL-CODEGRAPH-TOOLS) into the factory build pipeline so the executor builds with a code map instead of blind. planner.ts injects code_context output into FactoryPlan.instruction; verifier.ts uses code_affected on the changed files to scope verification to impacted tests. HARD CONSTRAINT — clean removability: every call site is ADDITIVE and GUARDED. If code intelligence is unavailable, planner/verifier behave exactly as today (blind build, full verify) — degraded, never broken. Factory must NOT import codegraph-specific types; it only calls the adapter, which returns plain strings. Removing the feature = delete the guarded injection calls; factory reverts to prior behaviour with no other change. Depends on CODE-INTEL-CODEGRAPH-TOOLS.
items.done: A factory run auto-injects a code map into the executor instruction and scopes verify to affected tests when codegraph is present; when absent, planner/verifier behave identically to before; removing the wiring is just deleting the guarded calls, with no other impact.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. Wires the CODE-INTEL port (resolveCodeIntel → CodeIntelProvider, plain-string Results) into the factory pipeline — factory imports only the port (type-only) + the resolver, never the codegraph engine (architecture boundary code-intel-seam stays clean; the seam is designed for this consumption). TWO additive+guarded call sites: (1) planner.ts augmentPlanWithCodeIntel(plan, provider) appends a CODE MAP block to the executor instruction when provider.available() + context() ok, else returns the plan BYTE-IDENTICAL; run.ts preparePlan() resolves the provider + augments. (2) verifier.ts affectedTestsCheck — a NEW guarded VerifyCheck inserted BEFORE fullSuiteCheck: when code-intel is available it runs the tests AFFECTED by the changed files as a fast-fail pre-gate. SAFETY (deliberate interpretation): it does NOT replace the full-suite floor — because affected tests ⊆ the full suite, this can only fast-fail a slice fullSuiteCheck would also fail, never weakening the autonomous commit/push trust gate. affectedTestPaths() parses *.test.ts paths from the affected() report and keeps ONLY files that exist (strips leading vanta-ts/) so a stale/garbage path is dropped, never false-failing. Guarded no-op on: no provider / unavailable / affected()-err / no existing affected test → {ok:true}. VerifyOpts gained codeIntel?, threaded via CycleCtx. Removing the feature = delete the augment call + the affectedTestsCheck chain entry; factory reverts to blind-build + full-verify with no other change (proven: run.test.ts drives a full L2 cycle through the real preparePlan/resolveCodeIntel with codegraph CLI absent → available()=false → identical behavior). Both branches unit-pinned (code-intel-wiring.test.ts: available→inject, absent/nullProvider/err→identical; parse+existence-filter; guarded check ordering). GAP (honest): the live codegraph-PRESENT injection isn't run end-to-end (needs the codegraph CLI + a real backlog + LLM); the injection logic is fake-provider-proven at both branches. 12 wiring tests + full suite 998 files/11295 green, tsc + size gate + architecture boundaries clean.

items.title: Architectural fitness function — boundary rules as a CI-failing test
items.summary: THE enforcer for Vanta's ports/adapters standard (skills/hooks only guide; a failing test enforces). Add scripts/check-boundaries.mjs + vanta-ts/src/architecture.test.ts wired into `npm test`. Declarative dependency rules, each a falsifiable assertion over the import graph: (1) nothing outside code-intel/codegraph.ts imports codegraph; (2) no file imports a concrete brain variant — only the Brain port (after PORT-BRAIN-INTERFACE); (3) SafetyClient is import-type-only outside its factory (after PORT-KERNEL-CLIENT); (4) no tool imports another tool's internals (e.g. runGit reach-in); (5) core/factory never import a concrete provider. Implement with ts-morph or a lightweight regex+AST scan over src/; rules live in a data table so adding a boundary = one entry. New violation -> red test. Rules start as warnings for already-violating boundaries (grandfathered list that only shrinks), hard-fail for new code and for ported boundaries.
items.done: `npm test` includes an architecture test that fails when a declared boundary is crossed; the code-intel seam rule is enforced hard; pre-existing violations are an explicit shrink-only grandfather list; adding a new rule is one table entry.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. Zero-dep import-graph scanner: arch/boundaries.ts (RULES data table + GRANDFATHER shrink-only map + pure parseImports/evaluateRule/findViolations), arch/collect.ts (recursive src walker), arch/cli.ts. src/architecture.test.ts runs in `npm test` — one assertion per rule, red on a crossed boundary (verified: an injected codegraph import fails the HARD code-intel seam rule). scripts/check-boundaries.mjs is the standalone CI entry. Adding a boundary = one RULES entry. Seeded HARD rules: code-intel-seam, no-concrete-provider-in-factory. Brain-port + SafetyClient-port rules land with PORT-BRAIN-INTERFACE / PORT-KERNEL-CLIENT (one table entry each).

items.title: Authoring skill — new Vanta capability = port + adapter
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-18. Added bundled skill `vanta-port-adapter` under `vanta-ts/skills-library/`. It triggers when adding a swappable Vanta capability/provider/backend/transport/model route/registry/scorer/evaluator/tool family and prescribes the port+adapter pattern: define an interface, isolate concrete adapters, resolve through one module, keep consumers typed to the port, and verify with fake/null adapter tests. The skill names in-repo reference seams and grandfathered gaps so new work does not deepen coupling.
items.done: A skill exists that, when writing new Vanta code, prescribes the port+adapter pattern with the in-repo reference examples; it is invoked for capability-adding work.
items.notes: Verified by the bundled skill install smoke test asserting `vanta-port-adapter`, plus `vanta skills lint`, targeted tests, typecheck, size gate, and full suite.

items.title: Pre-commit hook runs the boundary check
items.summary: Run scripts/check-boundaries.mjs in a pre-commit git hook (and optionally a another agent PostToolUse hook) so violations surface before CI. Non-blocking on grandfathered warnings, blocking on hard rules. Depends on ARCH-BOUNDARY-FITNESS.
items.done: Committing a hard boundary violation is blocked locally by a pre-commit hook before it reaches CI.
items.notes: WIRED the existing architectural fitness check (src/arch/cli.ts over arch/boundaries.ts = the 5 port/adapter boundaries; its header already said for CI/pre-commit but it was never wired) INTO scripts/pre-commit, after the size-gate step + mirroring it: runs tsx src/arch/cli.ts when TS is staged + tsx present (whole-repo check, fast), hard-blocks by default with VANTA_BOUNDARY_BLOCK=0 for warn-only (parallels VANTA_LINT_BLOCK), skips cleanly when no TS staged. Verified the repo currently PASSES (✓ all 5 architectural boundaries hold, exit 0) BEFORE wiring so the step cant block a legitimate commit. The hook is symlinked into .git/hooks via install.sh so the edit is immediately live (this very commit ran the new step). Now a cross-boundary import cant slip in.

items.title: Brain port — route the 3 brain variants behind one interface
items.summary: Audit verdict COUPLED: 3 brain implementations ship (live md-region brain + dormant v2 + brain5d) but consumers hardcode `import { brainDigest } from ../brain/brain.js` (session/prepare-helpers.ts:6, subagent/spawn.ts:5, cli-dx/prompt-size.ts:32) — the dormant variants can't be activated by config. This is the exact failure mode the modularity push targets. Define a Brain port (read/remember/recall/digest/health) in brain/interface.ts; make the live brain the default adapter; a single resolver picks the active brain (env/settings, e.g. VANTA_BRAIN). Consumers depend only on the port. Enforced by an ARCH-BOUNDARY-FITNESS rule. Removability: swapping/removing a brain = one adapter + one registration; zero consumer edits.
items.done: All brain consumers depend on a Brain interface resolved by one registration point; the live brain is the default adapter; an alternate brain can be activated by config with no consumer edits; a fitness rule forbids importing a concrete brain variant.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. brain/interface.ts defines the Brain port (read/write/remember/recall/digest/health) + the live md-region/entries brain as the default adapter + resolveBrain(env) — the one registration point (VANTA_BRAIN, default live). All 12 consumers migrated off direct brain.ts imports (tools/brain, tools/compare-vision, memory/extractor, repl/{reflect-correct,brief-cmd,handlers,shortcuts}, cli/hooks-cmd, gateway/run, subagent/spawn, session/prepare-helpers, cli-dx/prompt-size) — including the dynamic-import sites. New HARD fitness rule `brain-variant-port` (in ARCH-BOUNDARY-FITNESS) forbids importing brain/brain.ts or brain/v2.ts outside brain/ — a new concrete-brain import turns the suite red. interface.test.ts + 653 touched-area tests green; typecheck + size gate clean. Swapping in the dormant v2 (or a remote/stub) is now one adapter + one ADAPTERS entry, zero consumer edits.

items.title: KernelClient interface over SafetyClient (~25 sites)
items.summary: Audit verdict PARTIAL: the concrete SafetyClient class (safety-client.ts:21) is named at ~25 sites and has leaked into every *Deps type (tools/types.ts:11, agent/agent-types.ts:12, mcp/server.ts:49, +5 `new SafetyClient` sites). Tests already hand-roll a fakeClient — proof the port is missing. Extract a KernelClient interface (assess/log/goals/approvals/status surface); SafetyClient becomes the default HTTP adapter; consumers type against the interface; construction funnels through one factory. Enables IPC/in-process/stub transports and clean tests. NOTE: the Rust kernel stays the fixed boundary — this ports only the TS-side client, not the kernel. Enforced by a fitness rule (import-type-only outside the factory).
items.done: Consumers depend on a KernelClient interface, not the concrete SafetyClient; SafetyClient is one adapter behind a single factory; tests use a stub adapter via the same port; a fitness rule enforces it.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. kernel/client.ts defines the KernelClient port (status/assess/getGoals/addGoal/completeGoal/getApprovals/proposeApproval/approve/deny/logEvent) + createKernelClient factory (the one value-import of SafetyClient). SafetyClient now `implements KernelClient` (default HTTP adapter). All 23 sites migrated: 18 type-only importers now type against KernelClient; 5 `new SafetyClient` sites (status/kernel-launcher/session/cli-extra-cmds-2/cli-ops incl. dynamic) build via createKernelClient. New HARD fitness rule `kernel-client-port` forbids importing safety-client.ts outside the factory. client.test.ts proves the factory surface + a stub adapter satisfies the port. Full suite 3532 tests green; typecheck + size gate + all 4 boundaries clean. IPC/in-process/stub transports now drop in behind the factory; Rust kernel stays the fixed boundary.

items.title: FactoryDeps injection — make the factory pipeline pluggable
items.summary: Audit verdict PARTIAL: factory/run.ts hardcodes its entire triage->plan->execute->verify->merge pipeline via direct imports (run.ts:2-8) with zero injection, while loop/IterationDeps (loop/types.ts:174-184) and evolve/EvolveDeps right next door are textbook DI. Mirror them: inject a FactoryDeps {triage, plan, execute, verify, merge} into runCycle so the executor/planner/verifier are swappable and testable in isolation; lift the inline git stages (run.ts:86-167) behind a small VCS adapter; make verify a registered check chain (VerifyCheck[]) not a hardcoded 7-gate sequence. Also wire the built-not-wired holdout seam. Removability: swap the executor or add a verifier check without editing the orchestrator.
items.done: runCycle takes a FactoryDeps and calls stages through it; executor/planner/verifier are swappable without editing the orchestrator; verify is a registered check array; git ops sit behind a VCS adapter; existing factory tests pass.

items.title: ToolRegistry interface (currently a concrete class)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-18. `tools/registry.ts` now exports a `ToolRegistry` port plus `InMemoryToolRegistry` concrete adapter. `buildRegistry()` returns the port and constructs the adapter internally; production construction sites that need the default store use the concrete adapter name. Existing registry consumers, including `mount_mcp` and `tool_search`, type against the interface so scoped/test registries can replace the default without consumer edits.
items.done: A ToolRegistry interface exists; consumers (incl. mount-mcp) depend on it; the concrete class implements it; a scoped/test registry can replace it without consumer edits.
