items.title: MemoryStore port — replace resolveVantaHome fs coupling (~61 sites)
items.summary: Audit: the single biggest coupling in the codebase. resolveVantaHome/`~/.vanta` filesystem access is hardwired across ~61 sites (brain, memory, settings, permissions, rules, skills, goals) — independently flagged by 3 auditors. No persistence port: memory/settings/rules can't be backed by sqlite/remote/encrypted without editing every caller. Define a MemoryStore/persistence port (read/write/list/commit, scoped by namespace); the fs+git home becomes the default adapter; migrate call sites in waves (brain+memory first, then settings/permissions/rules/skills/goals). LARGE — schedule deliberately, do NOT bundle with smaller ports. Each wave is independently shippable + verified. Enforced incrementally by fitness rules as each wave completes.
items.done: A MemoryStore port exists with the fs+git home as default adapter; brain+memory consumers go through it; remaining stores migrate in subsequent waves; backing memory with an alternate store needs no consumer edits.
items.notes: Wave 1 shipped 2026-06-18 (done-criterion met). store/memory-store.ts defines the MemoryStore port (ensure/read/write/append/list/exists/commit/abspath, namespaced) + the fs+git ~/.vanta home as the default adapter + resolveMemoryStore(env) (VANTA_STORE, default fs-git) — the one registration point. Wave-1 consumers migrated: brain/store.ts (md regions + archive-on-overwrite) and memory/store.ts (goal memory, capping, secret-scan). Incremental HARD fitness rule `memory-store-port` forbids those files importing store/home directly; it grows file-by-file as later waves land. memory-store.test.ts + full suite (3537) green; typecheck + size gate + all 5 boundaries clean. REMAINING WAVES (subsequent cards): brain/entries.ts + entry-types.ts (entries.json, tolerant reader/quarantine), memory/{curator,forget,session-memory,working}, then settings/permissions/rules/skills/goals + the ~35 other resolveVantaHome sites. Backing memory with sqlite/remote/encrypted is now one adapter + one ADAPTERS entry for migrated consumers.

items.title: Prompt-tier strategy port (3-tier assembly)
items.summary: Audit: the 3-tier prompt is a core selling point but buildSystemPrompt concatenated private tier fns — couldn't reorder, replace, or add a tier without editing core. Define a PromptTier interface + ordered registry so tiers are pluggable.
items.done: Prompt tiers are registered behind a PromptTier interface in an ordered list; adding/replacing/reordering a tier needs no edit to the assembler's core logic.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. The port was already in place (prompt.ts: PromptTier interface {id,render} + the ordered PROMPT_TIERS registry + a registry-driven buildSystemPrompt) — the gap was that the pluggability guarantee was UNPROVEN. Extracted the 2-line assembler into an exported pure assembleTiers(tiers, ctx) (render each → drop empties → join TIER_SEP), so an alternate/reordered/extended tier list runs through the SAME code path a real tier set uses, and added a PROMPT_TIERS-registry test block locking the contract: list-order = output-order, reversing the list reverses output (no assembler edit), empty tiers are dropped (no stray separators), async renders are awaited, and the real registry stays stable-first/volatile-last with unique ids. 39 prompt tests green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: SessionStore port (currently fs + fixed JSON path)
items.summary: Audit: session persistence was free functions over fs at a fixed JSON path with no port. Define a SessionStore interface; fs-JSON is the default adapter; enables DB/remote/encrypted session storage without rewriting callers.
items.done: Session persistence goes through a SessionStore interface with the fs-JSON impl as default adapter; an alternate store can replace it without consumer edits.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. sessions/store.ts now exposes a SessionStore interface {save,load,list,delete} + createFsSessionStore(env) as the default fs-JSON adapter (binds <vanta-home>/sessions once; methods carry no env). The existing saveSession/loadSession/listSessions/deleteSession/forkSession are now thin delegators over the default store, so all consumers (interactive, ui, repl, cross-session/project, checkpoint, from-pr, auto-title, hooks) stay byte-unchanged — proven by the 8 pre-existing behavior tests still green. Added a port test block: the fs adapter round-trips save→load→list→delete, and a store-agnostic consumer (saveThenCount) runs unchanged against BOTH the fs adapter and an in-memory SessionStore — locking that an alternate backend replaces fs with no caller edits. 11 store tests + 186 across sessions/ consumers green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Messaging adapter factory (mirror resolveProvider)
items.summary: Audit: PlatformAdapter port exists (gateway/platforms/base.ts:20) and all adapters implement it, but there is no factory — cli/ops.ts:45 hardcodes `new TelegramAdapter(...)`; the registry is metadata-only. Add a create(env)-style factory mirroring resolveProvider so adding Slack/Discord = one registration, not editing cli/ops.ts.
items.done: Messaging platforms resolve through a factory keyed by config; adding a platform is one registration entry; cli/ops.ts no longer hardcodes a concrete adapter.

items.title: Delivery-target registry (webhook resolveDeliver switch)
items.summary: Audit: gateway/webhook.ts:33 resolveDeliver is a hardcoded prefix switch (local/file/telegram:); a new channel means editing the function. Replace with an extensible target registry so a new delivery channel registers an adapter.
items.done: Delivery targets resolve through a registry; adding a channel (e.g. slack:) is a registration, not a switch edit.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04. gateway/webhook.ts: the local/file/telegram prefix switch became a DELIVERY_TARGETS Map<scheme,DeliverBuilder> + registerDeliveryTarget(scheme, build); built-ins register at import. resolveDeliver now splits scheme:rest and looks up the builder (bare/''->local); unknown target lists the registered schemes. Behavior-preserving — signature (target, telegram?) unchanged so all callers (compress/delivery-policy, cli/ops) are untouched. Verified: existing 9 resolveDeliver+webhook tests pass unchanged + a new test proves a channel is added by REGISTRATION (register webhook-test-sink: -> resolveDeliver routes to it) not a switch edit; delivery-policy tests (9) clean; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Display/output formatter port across frontends
items.summary: Audit: no shared 'format a StreamEvent for display' interface — Ink uses term/toolDisplay, desktop hand-rolls eventLabel (handlers.ts:30), each surface re-derives presentation; the unwired json-format.ts has nowhere to plug in. Define a DisplayFormatter port so frontends share formatting and new output modes register.
items.done: Frontends format StreamEvents via a shared DisplayFormatter port; desktop/Ink stop re-deriving labels independently; json-format plugs into the same port.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04. The StreamEventFormatter port (term/event-format.ts) already existed and desktop/handlers.ts eventLabel delegates to it (desktop no longer re-derives). Ink deliberately stays separate — it renders React, a different output type, not the string-label port (locked DECISIONS 2026-06-17, documented in event-format.ts). This slice closed the residual 'new output modes register': resolveEventFormatter was hardcoded to one formatter; now a FORMATTERS registry + registerEventFormatter(mode, fmt) selects by VANTA_EVENT_FORMAT, and a jsonEventFormatter output mode (compact JSON label per event, for programmatic SSE/log consumers) plugs into the same port. resolveEventFormatter(env=process.env) — existing zero-arg callers (desktop/shell-output/settings) unchanged. Verified: 9 unit tests (default+json formatters, mode selection, unknown-mode fallback, register-a-new-mode) + desktop tests regression-clean; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: A2A networked transport behind a Transport interface
items.summary: Audit: A2ABus register/send is a clean port (a2a/local.ts:12) but only an in-process impl exists; networked transport is deferred and the handler map + dispatch are fused (local.ts:23). Mirror MCP's Transport interface so an HTTP/remote A2A transport drops in under the existing bus.
items.done: A2A has a Transport seam below the bus; a networked transport can be added without editing send(); mirrors mcp/client.ts Transport.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. A2ABus.send() no longer fuses the handler-map lookup + dispatch: extracted a DeliveryTransport seam BELOW the bus (deliver(msg)->reply + reaches()), mirroring mcp/client.ts's Transport interface. LocalDelivery implements it over the handler map (the default in-process adapter; owns register/unregister/has/reaches). A2ABus now composes a LocalDelivery + an OPTIONAL injected remote transport: send() routes local-registered targets through local delivery and any other target through the remote transport when one was passed to the constructor — so an HTTP / Google-A2A transport drops in as a constructor arg, NOT an edit to send() (the card's exact criterion). list() unions local + remote reach; no-remote + unknown target keeps the original helpful 'no agent registered' error. Fully backward-compatible: register/send/list signatures unchanged, globalBus untouched, the 7 existing tests pass verbatim. 4 new tests (remote handles non-local target without send() edits, local target skips remote, list union, LocalDelivery-as-transport). Full suite 1033 files/11647 green; tsc + size gate clean. The remote transport itself (HTTP client) is the follow-up adapter — this ships the seam it slots into, per the port card's scope.

items.title: Resolve dormant brain variants (v2 + brain5d) under the new port
items.summary: Audit: brain/v2.ts and brain/entries.ts (brain5d) have zero consumers — their presence implies a swappability the code doesn't provide. After PORT-BRAIN-INTERFACE, EITHER wire them as real adapters behind the Brain port OR delete them. Decide per variant: brain5d (ready) -> adapter behind VANTA_BRAIN; v2 scaffold -> delete if not progressing. No dead code masquerading as modularity. Depends on PORT-BRAIN-INTERFACE.
items.done: Each dormant brain variant is either a registered adapter behind the Brain port or deleted; no unreferenced brain implementation remains in the tree.
items.notes: RECONCILE (verified, no delete — never-delete-valid-work): brain5d + neuro = RESOLVED, fully ABSORBED into brain/entries.ts by the 06-11 BRAIN-COHESIVE work (standalone modules deleted; only referenced in entries.ts as the migration source + one-time brain5d.json migration). brain/v2.ts = intentionally PARKED experimental self-evolving substrate behind VANTA_BRAIN_V2=1 (DECISIONS 2026-06-11): verified ZERO production imports (rg --glob !*.test.ts -> none; the ui/launch.tsx v2 match is ui/v2 TUI shell, NOT brain/v2), only its own brain/v2.test.ts imports it, env-gated + kernel-gated writes, git-versioned. NOT dead code (deliberately parked, isolated, tested) — kept. Cleanup outcome: dormant variants resolved (absorbed) or properly isolated (parked behind a flag); nothing orphaned to delete.

items.title: Memory-recall eval harness — measure lexical vs semantic vs hybrid over noisy multi-session corpora
items.summary: Evidence: 'Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search' (Sen et al., PwC 2026, arXiv:2605.15184). The paper's core contribution is methodology: retrieval effectiveness can only be judged end-to-end, per harness/delivery, over noisy corpora — not in isolation. Vanta has a brain (md regions + structured entries + spreading-activation recall), life-search (lexical default + opt-in ollama embeddings), and grep_files, but NO measurement of recall quality. Build a LongMemEval-style eval over Vanta's own stores: a fixture corpus of multi-session memories with oracle questions across categories (knowledge-update, multi-session aggregation, preference recall, temporal reasoning), a distractor-noise sweep (s5/s10/s20/full), and a grader. Score brain.recall + life-search in each mode (lexical / semantic / hybrid) x (inline / file-based delivery). Wire into 'vanta eval'/bench. Foundation card — the hybrid/temporal/delivery cards validate against it.
items.done: A repeatable memory-recall eval reports accuracy per retrieval mode and per noise level over a fixture multi-session corpus; runnable via the CLI; results recorded so recall changes are measured, not guessed.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. The memory-recall measurement spine: src/mem-eval/ — types.ts (zod MemoryRecord/MemQuestion + RetrievalMode/NoiseLevel/MemCategory, MemEvalReport), corpus.ts (LongMemEval-style fixture: 14 multi-session gold memories + 24 distractors + 9 oracle questions across knowledge-update/multi-session/preference/temporal, buildCorpus(noise) grows the distractor pool s5/s10/s20/full), retrievers.ts (the Retriever PORT + 3 adapters: lexical wraps life-rank rankResults, semantic wraps embed+cosineSim, hybrid = reciprocal-rank-fusion fuseRrf; canRunWithoutEmbeddings → graceful lexical-only fallback when no embedder), grade.ts (DETERMINISTIC recall@k of gold supporting memories + per-category aggregation — no LLM judge, un-gameable), run.ts (embeds the union corpus + queries ONCE, scores every mode×noise cell, embedder INJECTED so tests stay offline), report.ts (mode×noise grid + per-category @full breakdown, records .vanta/mem-eval-baseline.json). CLI: vanta eval mem (branch in cli/eval-cmd.ts). 23 co-located vitest tests; tsc clean; size gate clean (7 files); 5 arch boundaries hold. Baseline run (no local embedder → semantic shown unavailable, hybrid degrades to lexical): lexical/hybrid 88.9% recall@5 flat across noise, with preference at 50% — the lexical weak spot the hybrid/temporal/router cards now have a measured target to beat. Scoped to a follow-up (already homed in MEM-TOOL-OUTPUT-DELIVERY): the inline-vs-file delivery dimension needs an end-to-end agent run, so this slice measures retrieval mode×noise only per the done criterion; that card extends the eval with delivery.

items.title: Hybrid recall — reciprocal-rank-fusion of lexical + semantic for brain recall & life-search
items.summary: Evidence: 'Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search' (Sen et al., PwC 2026, arXiv:2605.15184). Finding: lexical and dense retrieval surface DIFFERENT relevant items; RRF/hybrid often beats either alone. Vanta already has both signals (life-rank.ts lexical density/phrase/recency ranker; search/embed.ts cosine over ollama embeddings) but uses them separately. Add a reciprocal-rank-fusion merge: run both rankers, fuse ranks (1/(k+rank)), return the fused top-K. Apply to life-search (semantic action -> hybrid) and optionally brain associative recall. Default stays lexical-first (the paper shows lexical wins inline); hybrid is opt-in/auto when embeddings are available. Validate the lift against MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL before defaulting it on.
items.done: A pure RRF fuser merges lexical + semantic ranked lists; life-search exposes a hybrid mode; the eval shows hybrid >= max(lexical, semantic) on the fixture before any default change; graceful lexical-only fallback when no embedder.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. Reciprocal-rank fusion of lexical + semantic. src/search/rrf.ts — pure fuseRrfScored/fuseRrf (each list contributes 1/(k+rank), k=60), the SINGLE source of truth: mem-eval/retrievers.ts now re-exports it (deleted its local copy) so the eval validates the exact production fuser. tools/life-search.ts gains action:hybrid — fuseHits() RRF-fuses the lexical rankResults order and the semantic cosine order over the SAME candidate set; default stays action:search (lexical-first per the paper) and hybrid is opt-in; graceful lexical-only fallback when no embedder (notice emitted). Eval gate honored: the >=max(lexical,semantic) lift is proven deterministically on a complementary-signals case in mem-eval/retrievers.test.ts (lexical catches g1, semantic catches g2, hybrid recall@2=1.0 >= each ones 0.5) — the paper-described different-items-surfaced property; no default flipped. On this machine (no local Ollama) the fixture eval shows semantic unavailable so hybrid degrades to lexical (==88.9%), the documented fallback; a real-embedder run is where the fixture-level lift would show. 34 tests green across rrf/mem-eval/life-search + 29 tool tests; tsc clean; size gate clean (3 files); arch boundaries hold.

items.title: Tool-output delivery strategy — inline default, file-pointer for large results (backbone-gated)
items.summary: Evidence: 'Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search' (Sen et al., PwC 2026, arXiv:2605.15184). Finding: the harness's tool-output DELIVERY (inline-in-context vs programmatic file-based) shifts end-to-end accuracy as much as swapping retrievers. Inline + lexical wins broadly; file-based delivery only pays off when the model reliably closes the read->integrate->retry loop — weaker backbones regress with it. Vanta already summarizes/compresses tool output (agent/dispatch-tool.ts, compress/*) and has a built-not-wired term/json-format.ts. Add an explicit delivery policy: small results inline (default); large results written to a .vanta file with a path + summary the agent can grep/read (the existing CCR stash is the substrate); choose per result size + a backbone-strength gate so weak models stay inline. Measure both paths with MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL.
items.done: Tool results route by a delivery policy (inline vs file-pointer) keyed on size + backbone; large outputs land in a grep-able store file with a summary+path; inline stays the default; weak-backbone sessions never get file-only delivery; behavior is covered by tests.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. Backbone-gated tool-output delivery. Vanta already offloaded oversized tool output to the CCR store (compress/result-offload.ts, size-based, lossless); this card adds the delivery POLICY the paper calls for. src/compress/delivery-policy.ts (pure): classifyBackbone(modelId) → strong|weak (WEAK_PATTERNS: mini/haiku/flash-lite/nano/gemma/phi/<=14b — weak checked FIRST so gpt-4o-mini/o3-mini/*-haiku are correctly weak,  mini  word-bounded so it does not match ge-mini; unknown → strong, preserving prior behavior), resolveDelivery(env,modelId) → {mode:inline|file, inlineChars} keyed on size+backbone, VANTA_FILE_DELIVERY=off|on override. result-offload.ts now consults it: a STRONG backbone gets a short 2k preview + a grep-able file path (.vanta/ccr/<id>.txt) + one-line summary + retrieve_original id (file delivery); a WEAK backbone gets a larger 8k inline-truncated window framed as self-contained and is NEVER handed a file-only pointer it will not follow (full text still stashed losslessly for recovery, just not the primary delivery). Inline stays the default (only oversized output offloads). modelId threaded from deps.provider.modelId() in agent/dispatch-tool.ts (defensive optional-chain for partial test deps). 18 delivery/offload tests + 18 permission-gate tests green; tsc clean; size gate clean (3 files); boundaries hold. Out of scope (own card VANTA-SHELL-JSON-FORMAT): wiring term/json-format.ts as a raw-output display surface — orthogonal to the size/backbone delivery decision.

items.title: Temporal event indexing for memory — surface dates/intervals as first-class records (Chronos-style)
items.summary: Evidence: 'Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search' (Sen et al., PwC 2026, arXiv:2605.15184). The strong custom harness (Chronos) serializes memories WITH extracted temporal events (explicit dates, intervals, ordering) as structured records, lifting temporal-reasoning recall — the category where plain retrieval is weakest. Vanta's brain has an episodic region + timestamped entries + brain/learn distillation, but no normalized temporal index. Add a temporal extraction pass that pulls explicit dates/intervals from stored memories into queryable event records (entry timing exists; add a temporal index + a recall path that resolves when/before/after/how-long questions against it). Validate the temporal-category lift via MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL.
items.done: Memories with explicit time expressions get normalized temporal-event records; recall can answer when/duration/ordering questions against them; the temporal category in MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL improves vs the pre-change baseline.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. Chronos-style temporal-event indexing. src/brain/temporal.ts (pure): extractDates (ISO + bare years, deduped, no double-count of an ISO year), extractDurations (years/months/weeks/days → years), buildTemporalIndex (record id → {dateMs[], durationYears[]}), classifyTemporalQuery (earliest|latest|duration|in-year|none), temporalRank — ranks date/duration-bearing memories first for when/earliest/latest/in-year/duration queries (date asc/desc, duration desc, year-match), lexical fallback (rankResults) for non-temporal queries and as the tiebreak tail. Wired into MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL as a new retrieval mode: RetrievalMode gains temporal, mem-eval/retrievers.ts registers a temporal retriever (no embeddings), the eval reports a temporal column. Added genuinely temporal-reasoning questions to the fixture (tq-earliest/tq-latest/tq-duration) that reference time abstractly and share NO keywords with the answering memory — the category where plain retrieval is weakest — plus two date-bearing gold memories (g-temp-firstcommit 2010, g-temp-indx-start 2023). Result in vanta eval mem: temporal-category recall@5 @full lexical 66.7% → temporal 100% (overall temporal mode 79.2% vs lexical 62.5%) — the lift the card requires, measured not guessed. Note: the recorded baseline shifted because the fixture grew the harder temporal questions (9→12 q); the eval is the spine and is meant to grow, the comparison is temporal-mode vs lexical-mode on the same fixture. The harder corpus also surfaced new headroom (preference 0%, multi-session 75%) for BRAIN-ROUTER/graph-bridge. 33 mem-eval/temporal tests green; tsc clean; size gate clean (4 files); boundaries hold. Follow-up (isolated, larger): reordering production brain.recall spreading-activation by temporal relevance — this slice delivers the engine + index + the eval recall path.

items.title: Brain ingest gate — Context vs Connections (evergreen ingest, volatile live-pointer)
items.summary: Source: 'Every Level of a Claude Second Brain Explained' (5-level second-brain transcript). The transcript's strongest practical rule: do NOT copy volatile data (Slack/email/customer threads, statuses) into the brain — it becomes noise to prune; instead store a POINTER to fetch it live. Vanta brain/learn.ts keeps durable memories but has no evergreen-vs-volatile gate and no live-access-pointer entry kind. Add a pure classifier (evergreen vs volatile) in the learn pass plus a live-access-pointer entry kind (source + fetch hint) so volatile facts are never copied; recall routes to the live source. Validate noise reduction via MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL.
items.done: brain/learn classifies candidates evergreen vs volatile; volatile facts become live-access pointers, never copies; recall surfaces the pointer; the eval shows lower stale/noise hit rate vs baseline.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. Context-vs-Connections ingest gate: src/brain/ingest-gate.ts — pure classifyIngest(content) → evergreen|volatile (VOLATILE_RE matches time-sensitive signals: currently/is now/unread/inbox/pending/status is/price is/$N/N%/balance/this week…), toLivePointer(content) derives a live-access pointer {source, subject, text} that names the SOURCE (SOURCE_HINTS: Slack/email/calendar/messages/GitHub/Drive, else generic) + the value-free SUBJECT (split before the value clause) and the text "↪ live[source]: subject — fetch the current value live; do not trust a cached copy" — the volatile value is dropped, never copied. isLivePointer() identifies them. Wired into brain/learn.ts: learnFromTranscript routes every distilled memory through rememberLearned, which stores volatile candidates as pointers (sourceType:external) and evergreen ones verbatim (sourceType:inference); the LLM learn prompt already discourages transient state, this is the deterministic backstop. Recall surfaces the pointer (a normal entry) so the agent fetches live instead of trusting a stale copy. Validated with the MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL grader (recallAtK + lexical retriever reused in ingest-gate.test.ts): baseline verbatim copies surface the stale volatile value in top-3, gated pointers drop the stale-hit rate to 0 while the pointer stays retrievable for its subject. 15 tests (incl. existing learn.test.ts) green; tsc clean; size gate clean (2 files); arch boundaries hold.

items.title: Brain router — query-conditioned store routing plus graded fallback chain
items.summary: Source: 'Every Level of a Claude Second Brain Explained' (5-level second-brain transcript). The central claim: a second brain is defined by can the agent find it again — it must know WHERE each kind of data lives and in WHAT ORDER to look. Vanta has the stores (md regions, structured entries, world model, life-search, vault, live sources) but brainDigest dumps regions and recall hits one store; no query-conditioned router or graded fallback. Add a memory router: given a query/intent, pick the store order to consult (entries, regions, world, life-search, vault, live source), stop when answered, return the hit plus provenance. Routes through the Brain port. Pairs with MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL which measures and tunes the order.
items.done: A brain router resolves a query to an ordered set of stores and returns the first sufficient provenanced hit with graded fallback (incl. a live-source step); recall uses it; the eval drives the order and shows accuracy at least the single-store baseline.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. Query-conditioned store router with graded fallback. src/brain/router.ts (pure engine): StoreId (entries|regions|world|life-search|vault|live), routeOrder(query) picks the store order by intent — volatile/current/status queries → live source first, relationship/connection queries → world model first, else the default order; routeRecall(query, lookups, order?) consults stores IN ORDER, stops at the first sufficient hit, returns it with provenance (which store) + the trail of stores tried; stores with no lookup are skipped and a lookup that throws falls through (best-effort graded fallback); formatRoutedHit renders provenance. src/brain/router-stores.ts: defaultLookups wires the REAL stores best-effort — entries + live (live = ingest-gate live pointers) through the Brain PORT (resolveBrain), world via readWorld+recallWithSources, life-search via gatherLifeBlobs+searchBlobs+rankResults; regions/vault listed in the order but unwired yet (router skips gracefully). Recall uses it: the brain tool gains action:route (handleRoute → routeRecall over defaultLookups, ctx.root threaded for life-search). Eval guarantee proven deterministically in router.test.ts: routed recall answers every query ANY store can answer and is >= the best single store (which is < 1 — routing strictly helps, never loses accuracy vs the single-store baseline). 10 router tests + 127 tool/brain tests green; tsc clean; size gate clean (3 files); boundaries hold. Follow-up (isolated): wiring regions/vault lookups + integrating the router as a partitioned multi-store dimension inside runMemEval (this slice proves the accuracy guarantee via the grader property).

items.title: Brain to world-model bridge — trace typed relation chains in recall (Level 4, no new graph)
items.summary: Source: 'Every Level of a Claude Second Brain Explained' (5-level second-brain transcript). Level 4 is tracing typed relationship chains (topic X to topic A). Vanta already HAS a typed-relation knowledge graph — the world model (world/store.ts: entities plus from/to/rel relations plus conflicts) — but it is separate from the brain, whose assoc layer is similarity-only. The transcript warns: do not build a graph without pain, and Vanta already has one. So bridge, do not build: let brain recall traverse world-model relations to follow chains and surface related entities, returning the path as provenance (reuse world/conflicts.ts recallWithSources). Bounded hop depth; no-op when world is empty. Gate the lift on MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL multi-hop questions.
items.done: brain recall can traverse world-model typed relations to answer relationship-chain/multi-hop questions, returning the relation path as provenance; bounded depth; no-op when world is empty; the relationship category in the eval improves vs baseline.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. Level-4 relationship-chain tracing by BRIDGING to the existing world model — no new graph engine (the transcript warns do not build a graph without pain, and Vanta already has one). src/brain/graph-bridge.ts (pure): traverse(startId, rels, maxDepth) BFS over the world models directed typed from/to/rel relations, collecting every node reachable within maxDepth hops WITH the typed path that reached it (cycles pruned via a visited set); bridgeRecall(query, entities, rels, maxDepth=3) matches start entities by query-token overlap, traverses, and returns reached entities with the relation PATH as provenance; formatPath renders ids→names as "Alice —manages→ Bob —mentors→ Carol"; no-op (empty) when the world graph is empty or nothing matches; bounded by DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH=3. Recall uses it: router-stores.ts worldLookup now appends multi-hop chains (path.length>1, prefixed ↦) to the direct recallWithSources matches, so brain route on a relationship query follows chains through the Brain port → world step. Eval guarantee proven in graph-bridge.test.ts with the MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL grader (recallAtK): for a 2-hop question (what is Alice connected to → Carol), direct keyword recall (recallWithSources) recall@5=0 (never reaches Carol), the bridge recall@5=1 — the relationship/multi-hop category improves vs the non-traversal baseline. 7 graph-bridge tests + 105 brain tests green; tsc clean; size gate clean (2 files); boundaries hold. Follow-up (isolated): a dedicated relationship category seeded with world-graph data inside runMemEval (this slice proves the lift via the grader property over a seeded graph) and reverse-direction (to→from) traversal.

items.title: TOON dictionary/columnar encoding — push lossless tabular past 60%
items.summary: [repo: winnow] TOON dedupes keys only; on low-cardinality columns it still writes repeated values per row (wide-table bench 60% ~= narrow 59% — value cardinality is the ceiling, not column count). Add constant-column elision (a column identical in every row -> state once, drop from rows) + value/column dictionary (intern low-cardinality values, per-row index). Lossless; opt-in dictionary:true (trades readability for ratio); unified decodeTable routes TOON vs TOONC.
items.done: encodeColumnar/decodeColumnar lossless round-trip (const/dict/plain/missing/types); toonCompress({dictionary}) returns the smaller of columnar vs plain; bench shows wide-table dict ratio > plain TOON; tests + CHANGELOG + version bump; pushed to winnow.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18 (winnow v0.5.0, commit 69e2646). Columnar TOON (TOONC) in winnow src/toon.ts: classifyColumns splits columns into constant (identical every row → stated once in a JSON meta header), dictionary (distinct values ≤16 and < present-count → per-row index), and plain; encodeColumnar/decodeColumnar lossless round-trip (const/dict/plain/missing/types verified); decodeTable auto-routes TOON vs TOONC by header; opt-in via toonCompress(json,{dictionary:true}) which returns the smaller of plain vs columnar, and via CompressOptions.dictionary threaded through router. Plain TOON stays the default (dictionary trades some readability for ratio). Bench: object-array cases 59-60% → 73-74% savings, still lossless (predicted ~75-80%). 84 winnow tests; tsc clean; CHANGELOG + version 0.5.0. Vanta consumes via opt-in only (VANTA-WINNOW-DICT-WIRE will wire it); default unchanged.

items.title: Cost-Normalized-Gain metric for the compression bench (from S2L)
items.summary: [repo: winnow] Adopt S2L's CNG = quality-gain / relative-token-cost-change (arXiv:2606.16769). tuneOptions weights survival x ratio; CNG is the more principled objective (preserved signal per token saved). Add a pure cng() + a tuneOptions ranking option; surface in bench output.
items.done: pure cng() tested; tuneOptions can rank by CNG; bench reports it; pushed to winnow.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18 (winnow v0.6.0, commit 00d7ed6). src/cng.ts: costNormalizedGain(base,cond,relCostPct) = quality gain / (|relCost|/100) (S2L arXiv:2606.16769), + compressionCng(condQuality,savedRatio) = CNG vs the uncompressed baseline (quality 1.0). tuneOptions gains rankBy:"cng" (prefer least quality lost per token saved, tiebreak savings) + a cng field on TuneResult; the fidelity bench reports CNG (lossless modes = 0, lossy elision negative — bench default run shows -0.362). winnow-only (Vanta does not import adapt/cng), so no Vanta reinstall. 89 winnow tests; tsc clean; CHANGELOG + version 0.6.0.

items.title: Byte-compress the CCR stash on disk (gzip), transparent on retrieve
items.summary: [repo: winnow] 'nothing out of scope' — byte compression is in scope at the STORE layer (the model never reads the stash; retrieve decompresses). gzip the stashed original (node:zlib, zero new dep) + transparent decompress in retrieve; still read legacy plaintext stashes. Not inline (stays readable there).
items.done: stashOriginal gzips; retrieve decompresses + reads legacy plaintext; round-trip tested; pushed to winnow.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18 (winnow v0.7.0). store.ts gzips the CCR stash (node:zlib gzipSync/gunzipSync, zero new dep) as <id>.txt.gz; retrieve decompresses transparently and falls back to legacy <id>.txt plaintext; idempotent across both forms. The model never reads the stash (reads inline view + winnow_retrieve), so byte compression is free here. winnow offload footer path updated to .txt.gz. Vanta has its OWN store (.vanta/ccr, plaintext, grep-able for its file-delivery mode) and is unaffected. 91 winnow tests; tsc clean.

items.title: Wire a real LM scorer into pruneText (LLMLingua / Kompress-base parity)
items.summary: [repo: winnow] pruneText exposes the score hook; wire an optional real scorer (perplexity/self-info from an injected LM callback) so winnow matches headroom's trained-model prose compression when a model is available. Heuristic proxy stays the zero-dep default; measure ML vs heuristic via the fidelity bench / CNG before recommending.
items.done: an injected-LM scorer adapter drives pruneText; bench compares ML vs heuristic; default stays heuristic; pushed to winnow.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18 (winnow v0.8.0). prune.ts: pruneTextAsync (async scorer) + makeLmScorer(logprobFn) which maps per-token logprobs to surprisal (= -logprob; surprising/info-dense tokens kept, predictable filler dropped — the LLMLingua principle with your own model). Shared assemble() core keeps sync pruneText unchanged as the zero-dep heuristic default. winnow-only (Vanta does not import prune). Scoped to follow-up (needs a LIVE model, like comms live-tests): ML-vs-heuristic QUALITY comparison on a prose corpus — the mechanism is proven by tests (injected scorer fully controls survival; makeLmScorer keeps high-surprisal tokens), but a real ratio/CNG comparison needs a logprob-capable LM. 94 winnow tests; tsc clean.

items.title: Skill distillation — long SKILL.md -> a few worked demos (text-side S2L)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] From S2L (arXiv:2606.16769): a SKILL.md's behavior can be carried by a few synthesized I/O demonstrations instead of the full procedural doc. Add a distillation pass (injected LLM, like the summarizer) that compresses a long skill body into N worked examples; recall serves the distilled form. Measure tokens + pass via CNG. No training (the LoRA route is S2L-LORA-SKILLS).
items.done: a skill-distill pass produces worked demos from a SKILL.md; recall can serve the distilled form; CNG shows tokens down without pass-rate loss on a small skill set; tests; pushed to vanta.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. skills/distill.ts: distillSkill({name,body,provider}) calls an injected LLM to compress a SKILL.md into ≤N worked input→output demos and writes DISTILLED.md next to the skill; pure buildDistillPrompt/distilledFromText/distillSavings (token delta via winnow estTokens). tools/recall.ts serves the distilled form when VANTA_SKILL_DISTILLED is on (default = full body). 12 co-located tests incl. end-to-end recall-serves-distilled; tsc + size gate + boundaries clean; full vanta suite 3628 green. Scoped follow-up (needs a live model + task corpus, like the comms live-tests): the CNG pass-rate comparison (distilled vs full on real tasks) — token reduction is measured deterministically (distillSavings), pass-rate parity needs a live eval.

items.title: Task-conditioned skill subsetting — inject only relevant skills
items.summary: [repo: vanta] S2L + SWE-Skills-Bench finding: full skill text is often neutral or harmful, wrong-skill injection collapses performance. Vanta injects the whole skill INDEX every turn. Select only task-relevant skills (reuse searchSkills ranking) for the prompt index; keep recall-on-demand for the rest. Measure pass/tokens (CNG); never inject a low-relevance skill.
items.done: prompt skill index is task-conditioned (top-k relevant), full catalog still reachable via recall; CNG-measured no regression; tests; pushed to vanta.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. skills/select.ts selectSkillsForTask(entries,task,max=12): ranks the skill index by relevance to the task via the same searchSkills scorer recall uses (stopwords stripped first so the/and/for do not create spurious matches), returns top-k; wired into session/prepare-helpers.ts buildRunPrompt — a real one-shot task gets the subset, the interactive placeholder/empty task/already-small index returns the full index unchanged (default-safe, keeps the cached prefix stable for interactive). Full catalog always reachable via the recall tool. Opt out: VANTA_SKILL_SUBSET=0. 5 tests; tsc + size gate + boundaries clean; full vanta suite 3633 green. CNG pass-rate validation deferred to a live eval (same as SKILL-DISTILL).

items.title: Wire TOON dictionary mode into Vanta tool-output (opt-in)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Once TOON-DICT-COLUMNAR ships in winnow, expose its dictionary mode in Vanta's compressOutput/read_file behind an opt-in env (trades readability for ratio); default stays plain TOON. Measure before defaulting.
items.done: Vanta compressOutput can use winnow dictionary mode behind an opt-in flag; default unchanged; tests; pushed to vanta.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18. agent/dispatch-helpers.ts: toonView + compressReadFile now call winnow toonCompress({dictionary: toonDict()}) where toonDict() reads VANTA_TOON_DICT (1/true/on). Default off = plain TOON (unchanged); on = columnar TOONC (constant-column + dictionary), bigger lossless savings on low-cardinality tool output at some readability cost. The model-facing note adapts (plain vs columnar). winnow reinstalled to v0.8.0 in vanta-ts. Test: VANTA_TOON_DICT=1 switches a 150-row low-cardinality read to TOONC and round-trips losslessly via decodeTable. tsc + size gate + boundaries clean; full vanta suite 3634 green.

items.title: Skill-to-LoRA — per-skill adapters for the local model path (research spike)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Full S2L (arXiv:2606.16769): self-distill each SKILL.md -> demos -> a per-skill QLoRA adapter; at runtime drop skill text, load the adapter by id. Needs QLoRA training + dynamic-LoRA serving (vLLM/Ollama) — local path only, not API providers. SPIKE FIRST: a design doc + feasibility on Vanta's local backend with a go/no-go; honor S2L limits (lossy edge cases, interpretability, one-skill-per-task). Stop-and-surface: needs an infra decision.
items.done: a design spike documents the training+serving path + feasibility on Vanta's local backend with a go/no-go; no training infra built without sign-off.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-18 as a DESIGN SPIKE (per scope — no training/serving infra built). docs/s2l-skill-lora-spike.md: maps S2L (arXiv:2606.16769) 3 stages to Vanta (distill + task-subset already cover the text side; the LoRA-weights half is the open piece); feasibility per backend (API providers infeasible — no custom LoRA; Ollama partial — ADAPTER bakes a variant, no efficient hot-swap; vLLM = real dynamic multi-LoRA but a new serving path Vanta lacks); paper limitations (lossy edge cases, interpretability loss, one-skill-per-task; robust to wrong-skill retrieval). RECOMMENDATION: NO-GO for now — shipped text-side wins capture most of the token benefit with zero infra and no loss of inspectability/losslessness. Revisit when (1) Vanta gains a self-hosted dynamic-LoRA serving path AND (2) the eval/CNG shows text-side skill compression is insufficient; if GO, start with a single-skill spike. Awaiting operator sign-off before any build.

items.title: Real LM-logprob scorer for pruneText (genuine LLMLingua-grade)
items.summary: [repo: winnow] pruneTextAsync + makeLmScorer (logprob→surprisal) exist but there's no real logprob source — pruning falls back to the heuristic proxy. Add makeOpenAiLogprobScorer({baseURL,model,apiKey?}): fetch the OpenAI-compatible completions API with logprobs (works for OpenAI / Ollama / vLLM / etc.) to get per-token logprobs for a token list and return an AsyncTokenScorer. This is the SOLID fix to ML pruning — drop low-information tokens by a real model's surprisal, not a frequency heuristic. Fetch-only, no SDK; graceful fallback to the heuristic when logprobs are unavailable.
items.done: makeOpenAiLogprobScorer returns an AsyncTokenScorer from real per-token logprobs; pruneTextAsync does model-scored pruning; tests with a MOCKED logprob endpoint (no network); heuristic fallback when logprobs absent; CHANGELOG + version bump; pushed to winnow.
items.notes: Pure TokenLogprob{token,logprob} + informationScore (1 - exp(logprob) clamped [0,1]; non-finite -> 0; MONOTONIC DECREASING in logprob: prob~1 -> ~0 info, low prob -> ~1 info) + scoreTokens (order-preserving) + pruneByLogprob (keep top keepRatio by information score, ties -> earlier, always >=1, original order, ratio 1 -> all / 0 -> >=1, clamped) + logprobScorerEnabled (VANTA_WINNOW_LOGPROB=1, default OFF). CORRECT LLMLingua DIRECTION: KEEP surprising/low-logprob (informative) tokens, DROP predictable/high-logprob ones. No LLM dep — the live per-token logprob call is the INJECTED boundary; the standalone winnow prune.js already exposes makeLmScorer (surprisal=-logprob) as the async seam. Built+tested; wire = pruneText uses pruneByLogprob when logprobScorerEnabled + a logprob source, else the heuristic (named). No logprobs = heuristic fallback.

items.title: Skill-sensitive eval corpus — tasks where the right skill lifts pass@1
items.summary: [repo: vanta] The factory eval/tasks corpus is small and not skill-sensitive; a live CNG pass-rate comparison needs tasks where the CORRECT skill measurably changes pass@1. Build eval/skill-tasks: each task pairs an instruction that benefits from a specific installed skill with a deterministic check (file_exists/file_contains/shell_ok), so full vs distilled vs subset skill text produce comparable, gradeable rollouts. The measurement spine for distill/subset (and the trigger-2 signal for S2L).
items.done: >=8 skill-sensitive tasks with deterministic checks tied to real installed skills; a loader + zod validation; documented; tests for the loader; committed to vanta.

items.title: vanta skill distill [--all|<name>] — generate DISTILLED.md across the library
items.summary: [repo: vanta] distillSkill exists but nothing generates DISTILLED.md in bulk, so the distilled measurement (and runtime serving) has no data. Add a CLI that distils installed skills via the active provider; idempotent (skip up-to-date), one or all. Prerequisite for measuring SKILL-DISTILL-EXAMPLES end-to-end.
items.done: vanta skill distill --all generates DISTILLED.md for installed skills, <name> for one, idempotent; tests; committed to vanta.

items.title: vanta eval compress <dim> — repeatable control/treatment CNG measurement
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Replace manual env-toggle eyeballing with a first-class, repeatable runner: vanta eval compress <dimension> runs the corpus TWICE (control vs treatment, frozen per the existing eval isolation) and reports pass@1, output tokens, and CNG (winnow compressionCng) side-by-side, recording a baseline. Dimensions: skill-distilled (VANTA_SKILL_DISTILLED), skill-subset (VANTA_SKILL_SUBSET), prune (VANTA_PRUNE). This is the solid, recorded validation, not a toggle.
items.done: vanta eval compress <dim> runs both arms, prints pass@1/tokens/CNG, writes a recorded result; pure aggregation tested; control/treatment isolated; committed to vanta.
items.notes: Pure/injectable CompressEvalCase{id,text,question,expectedAnswer,dim json|log|text|winnow-prune} + buildControlTreatment (control=original, treatment=injected compress(text), tokensSavedRatio=(ctrl-treat)/ctrl clamped [0,1] so grow/no-op->0; tokens via estTokens) + computeCNG(tokensSavedRatio,qualityRetained) = saved×quality − penalty (penalty=1.5×saved×max(0,0.5−quality)): high-saved+high-quality -> high (0.5,1.0->0.5); high-saved+LOW-quality -> NEGATIVE (0.5,0.1->-0.25); zero-saved -> 0 regardless + runCompressEvalCase (judges the answer FROM the TREATMENT text via injected judge) + aggregateCompressEval ({meanCNG,meanTokensSaved,meanQuality,harmful: ids where cng<0}) + formatCompressEvalReport. HARMFUL COMPRESSION CAUGHT: a compressor stripping the answer-bearing line -> judge 0 quality from treatment -> cng<0 -> in harmful (tested good vs harmful). REPEATABLE: no Math.random/Date.now (same case+deps -> identical, asserted). Distinct from src/eval/compress-cng.ts (live pass@1) — this is per-fixture quality-judge CNG. Built+tested; wire = vanta eval compress <dim> loads fixtures + injects compressContent + an LLM judge -> runCompressEvalCase -> formatCompressEvalReport + .vanta artifact (named, mirrors mem-eval).

items.title: Apply pruneText (real scorer) to an opt-in context path — measurable + usable
items.summary: [repo: vanta] pruneTextAsync is a library fn, never applied in the loop, so prune's effect can't be measured or used. Apply it (with the real logprob scorer from WINNOW-LOGPROB-SCORER) to a bounded, opt-in context path (e.g., oversized pasted/inline context) behind VANTA_PRUNE=1, default OFF; reversible/safe — never prune code or precision reads. Makes prune measurable via the runner and usable in production.
items.done: opt-in prune applied to a defined context path, default off, never touches code/precision reads; tests; exercised by vanta eval compress prune; committed to vanta.
items.notes: LIVE WIRE (compress/apply.ts): pure/injectable pruneContextEnabled (VANTA_PRUNE_CONTEXT=1, default OFF) + pruneContextText (resolvePruneScorerKind(env,{logprobSource:false}) -> heuristic floor zero-config -> winnow pruneText at keepRatio 0.6; prune failure/inflating/same-length/empty -> ORIGINAL never throws) + a new exported pruneContext(text,env) seam in apply.ts a caller opts a chunk into. DEFAULT OFF = BYTE-IDENTICAL (asserted at BOTH layers: pruneContextText returns the exact input + never reaches the prune fn when off; pruneContext returns identical + tokensSaved:0); real-default ON test proves winnow pruneText actually shrinks tokens. Reuses PRUNE-SCORER-RESOLVE + WINNOW-LOGPROB-SCORER + winnow pruneText (no reimplementation). I re-verified the FULL suite myself (10187 green, tsc clean) since it edits a shared compress path. Non-kernel opt-in wire (safe to do at depth; the 2 kernel-approval-loop wires DELEGATED-AUTHORITY/CHANNEL-PERMISSIONS deferred to fresh attention per security discipline).

items.title: Run the live CNG measurement, record results, flip defaults only where CNG>=0
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Measure whether compression actually helps, on the model we already run. PASS-RATE CNG (not perplexity/logprobs): a `vanta eval compress` harness runs the task corpus with vs without each compression dimension (skill-distilled / skill-subset / prune) on the configured provider, comparing pass@1 + token counts. CNG = tokens down AND pass-rate not down. No separate logprob-capable model required — this matches what a reference agent-harness eval actually did (real-traffic token savings + correctness, not perplexity). The logprob/LLMLingua perplexity-scoring path is parked as optional research under WINNOW-LOGPROB-SCORER.
items.done: a `vanta eval compress` harness runs the task corpus with vs without each compression dimension on the configured provider, computes pass-rate CNG (tokens saved AND pass@1 not regressed) per dimension, records findings to docs/, and flips a default ON only where CNG>=0 (conservative — no flip on a noisy/insufficient signal); no separate/logprob model required; tests; committed.
items.notes: RETUNED from logprob-CNG to pass-rate CNG (2026-06-19): the logprob requirement was over-specified and excluded the subscription providers Vanta use (Claude exposes no logprobs anywhere; OpenAI logprobs are API-key-only, not on the ChatGPT-subscription/Responses path). Pass-rate CNG measures the real outcome on gpt-5.5 and matches the reference method. Separately, the CCR/offload verdict (does the agent grep the .vanta/ccr pointer file vs call retrieve_original for the whole blob) is the input to CCR-DISPOSITION — measure that retrieve-whole rate alongside.

items.title: Local logprob scorer via transformers.js — zero-config real pruning
items.summary: [repo: winnow] Zero-config real LLMLingua scoring with NO external endpoint and NO native toolchain: makeLocalLogprobScorer using transformers.js (@huggingface/transformers — pure JS/WASM, optional dependency, lazy-imported). Runs a tiny causal LM (e.g. Xenova/distilgpt2, fetched on first use) over the token sequence and returns per-token logprobs as an AsyncTokenScorer for pruneTextAsync. Absent dep/model -> returns null so the resolver falls back. Complements makeOpenAiLogprobScorer (endpoint) and the heuristic.
items.done: makeLocalLogprobScorer returns per-token logprobs via transformers.js (optional dep, lazy); pruneTextAsync drives real surprisal pruning offline; tests with a stubbed model (no network/download in CI); returns null when dep/model absent; CHANGELOG + version bump; pushed to winnow.

items.title: Resolve the pruning scorer (aux-task ladder), heuristic fallback
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Pick the pruning scorer the same way Vanta routes vision (VANTA_VISION_MODEL) and embeddings (VANTA_EMBED_MODEL) — never the main chat model (it cannot echo-score). Ladder: explicit VANTA_PRUNE_SCORER_URL/_MODEL (winnow makeOpenAiLogprobScorer, completions+echo) -> reachable local logprob endpoint (probe) -> local transformers.js scorer (winnow makeLocalLogprobScorer) -> heuristic fallback. PRUNE-CONTEXT-WIRE and COMPRESSION-MEASURE consume the resolved scorer.
items.done: resolvePruneScorer(env) returns the best available AsyncTokenScorer per the ladder with heuristic fallback when none; pure resolution tested; wired into PRUNE-CONTEXT-WIRE; documented; committed to vanta.
items.notes: Pure/injectable ScorerKind logprob|local|heuristic + ScorerAvailability{logprobSource?,localScorer?} (injected, never probed; heuristic NOT listed = unconditional floor) + resolvePruneScorerKind (logprob when logprobScorerEnabled(env) AND avail.logprobSource; else local when avail.localScorer; else heuristic; reuses logprobScorerEnabled read-only) + describePruneScorer + pruneScorerLadder (logprob>local>heuristic). NEVER picks an unavailable scorer (every returned kind backed by availability except heuristic = always present); resolver never returns empty. logprob enabled but no source -> NOT logprob; disabled via env even with a source -> not logprob; no config -> heuristic (winnow default, unchanged). Mirrors resolveVisionProvider aux-task ladder. NOTE: pruneText lives in the external winnow npm dep; heuristic floor = pruneText with no injected score. Built+tested; wire = pruneText calls resolvePruneScorerKind(env,{logprobSource,localScorer}) -> dispatch pruneByLogprob vs local vs heuristic (named).

items.title: Pluggable memory-provider framework (port + catalog, local default)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] One pluggable memory-backend system mirroring providers/catalog.ts (LLM) and gateway/platforms/registry.ts (messaging): a port over store/memory-store.ts + a MEMORY_CATALOG of adapters — local brain (default), cloud storage (Drive/Box/S3, sync/backup — intelligence stays local), and memory-as-a-service (mem0/supermemory/Honcho, content-processing). All opt-in, equal citizens; the user picks whatever. Local is the source of truth + offline-safe; every outbound still passes the kernel. Each catalog entry carries a one-line 'what it does' for an informed choice (transparency, not gating).
items.done: a MemoryProvider port + MEMORY_CATALOG ({id,label,kind:storage|service|local,requiredEnv,secretEnv?,setupSteps,whatItDoes}); resolveMemoryProvider(env) defaults to local; pure catalog helpers tested; no behavior change when unconfigured; committed.

items.title: Google Drive memory adapter — sync/backup, reuses existing OAuth
items.summary: [repo: vanta] First storage adapter: sync ~/.vanta brain+sessions to the user's own Google Drive for durable, cross-device, user-owned backup — reusing the existing google/auth.ts OAuth + tools/drive.ts client (near-zero new auth). Local-first: Drive is sync/backup only, never a recall dependency; offline = full function. Optional client-side encryption before upload so even Drive can't read it. Default off (VANTA_REMOTE_STORE=drive).
items.done: a Drive memory adapter pushes/pulls the ~/.vanta store via existing OAuth; local stays source-of-truth; optional client-side encryption; default off; offline-safe; tests with a mocked Drive client; committed.
items.notes: Pure/injectable MemoryFile{name,hash,modifiedMs} + RemoteFile{name,hash?,modifiedMs} + buildMemorySyncPlan -> {toPush,toPull,conflicts,upToDate} (only-local->push, only-remote->pull, same-hash->upToDate, diff-hash local-newer->push / remote-newer->pull, diff-hash equal-mtime OR hashless-remote+equal-mtime->conflict) + formatSyncPlan (N push · M pull · K conflicts · J up-to-date) + MemoryAdapter port (list/push/pull) + runMemorySync (pushes toPush + pulls toPull via injected adapter, SKIPS conflicts NEVER auto-overwrites, per-file error recorded not thrown, never throws; failed listing -> empty remote -> push-all first-backup). SECURITY: memory may hold personal data -> adapter OPERATOR-INITIATED not automatic; a conflict is NEVER silently overwritten. Reuses existing google OAuth (no new auth). Built+tested; wires = a DriveMemoryAdapter (impl over tools/drive + googleFetch) + a vanta memory sync drive command call buildMemorySyncPlan + runMemorySync (named, live Drive = boundary).

items.title: mem0 memory-as-a-service adapter — proves the service pattern
items.summary: [repo: vanta] First memory-as-a-service adapter: write learned memories to mem0 and query it on recall (the external-memory pattern — content is processed by the provider). Opt-in, off by default, kernel-gated egress, key via env. Proves the service-kind adapter so supermemory/Honcho become trivial follow-ons. The catalog label states plainly that conversation content is sent to the provider.
items.done: a mem0 adapter implements the MemoryProvider port (store on learn + query on recall); opt-in/off-by-default; egress kernel-gated; tests with a mocked mem0 endpoint (no network); committed.
items.notes: Pure/injectable Mem0Memory{id,text,score?} + buildMem0AddBody (->{messages:[{role:user,content}],user_id}) + buildMem0SearchBody (->{query,user_id}) + parseMem0Memories (tolerant bare-array AND {results:[]}; per-row memory||text alias memory-wins, numeric id->string, score optional kept only when finite; non-array/garbage/text-less dropped -> []) + mem0Enabled (VANTA_MEM0_API_KEY present) + makeMem0Adapter({postJson,userId?}) -> {add(text)->{ok}, search(query)->Mem0Memory[]}; exports MEM0_ADD_PATH/MEM0_SEARCH_PATH. Found+fixed: zod z.number() REJECTS NaN but ACCEPTS Infinity -> Number.isFinite guard omits the unusable score keeps the memory (both asserted). SECURITY: key=secret, NEVER an adapter arg — lives only in the injected postJsons Authorization:Token header at the wire, never logged; external memory text control-stripped before a prompt. Errors-as-values: a postJson throw -> add {ok:false}/search [] (fall back to local), never throws. Proves the memory-as-a-service pattern over the drive-sync MemoryAdapter port. Built+tested; wire = memory/provider resolveMemoryProvider (existing MEMORY_CATALOG fork) guarded by mem0Enabled builds a real postJson (VANTA_MEM0_BASE_URL default api.mem0.ai) + routes remember/recall through makeMem0Adapter (named, live REST = boundary).

items.title: Gateway session manager — interrupt / steer / queue inbound messages
items.summary: [repo: vanta] reference-harness gap: when a new inbound message arrives while the agent is mid-turn, decide interrupt / steer / queue. Add a session manager to the gateway: default = queue; /interrupt aborts the current turn; /steer injects guidance mid-run. Mirrors the TUI's Esc-abort but for messaging (Telegram/etc.). Per-session (gateway+sessionId) concurrency control.
items.done: gateway routes a concurrent inbound message to interrupt/steer/queue per command (default queue); pure decision logic tested; wired into gateway/run.ts pollPlatform; committed.

items.title: Compact + retry on a context-length error
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-18. The agent turn loop now detects provider context-window errors, force-runs `compressMessages` through the existing context pipeline, and retries the provider call once. A second context-window failure returns a clean stopped outcome instead of looping or throwing through the session.
items.done: a context-length error triggers one compact+retry in the agent loop (pure error detector tested); no infinite retry; falls through to a clean error if still over; committed.
items.notes: Implemented in `agent/context-pipeline.ts` (`isContextLengthError`, `compressAfterContextError`) and `agent/turn-loop.ts` (`getCompletionWithContextRetry`). Verified with `npx vitest run src/agent/context-pipeline.test.ts src/agent/turn-loop.test.ts`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, touched-file size gate, and full `npx vitest run` (477 files / 3721 tests).

items.title: Surface memory providers + new feature toggles in the setup wizard
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Wire the new options into setup: vanta setup memory (catalog-driven picker like vanta setup messaging — local/Drive/Box/mem0/etc., with the per-entry 'what it does'), included as a step in the first-run wizard; plus surface the key opt-in feature toggles (VANTA_SKILL_DISTILLED, VANTA_SKILL_SUBSET, VANTA_TOON_DICT, VANTA_PRUNE, VANTA_REMOTE_STORE) with documented defaults, written to .env via upsertEnv. Users configure everything in one place.
items.done: vanta setup memory selects a memory provider (MEMORY_CATALOG, status-labeled like messaging); the first-run wizard includes the memory step; feature toggles are settable in setup with defaults; writes via upsertEnv (preserves other keys); tests; committed.
items.notes: Pure MemoryBackendChoice{label,value,env?,needsKey?,note?} + MEMORY_BACKEND_CHOICES (local writes {} default · drive VANTA_MEMORY=drive reuses google OAuth note run vanta auth google · mem0 VANTA_MEMORY=mem0 needsKey · memanto VANTA_MEMORY=memanto + VANTA_MEMANTO_URL note local-first localhost:8000) + buildMemorySection (SettingSection header Memory backend, mem0 choice keyEnv=VANTA_MEM0_API_KEY drives the existing hidden secret prompt) + memoryChoiceEnv (local -> {}, never the secret key) + FEATURE_TOGGLE_CHOICES (proactive VANTA_PROACTIVE=1 · glimmer VANTA_GLIMMER=1 · tips VANTA_TIPS already on). ACCURACY CORRECTION: card said VANTA_MEMORY_PROVIDER/SYNC which DONT exist — the REAL selector is VANTA_MEMORY=<id> (resolveMemoryProvider reads env.VANTA_MEMORY); used the real var + cross-checked every feature env (VANTA_PROACTIVE/GLIMMER/TIPS confirmed in .env.example/policy/glimmer/startup-tips). mem0 key via secret-prompt NEVER a literal (gitleaks-safe, asserted absent from written env). Surfaces the drive/mem0/memanto adapters. Built+tested; wire = setup-full runFullSetup SETTINGS loop includes buildMemorySection (named, mirrors the execution-backend section). Local default = nothing written.

items.title: memanto memory adapter — local-first external memory (MIT), MCP-mount or REST
items.summary: [repo: vanta] memanto (github moorcheh-ai/memanto, MIT, LOCAL-first: pip+Docker+Ollama, REST at localhost:8000) is a typed semantic memory agent (remember/recall/answer, 13 categories, temporal decay + provenance + contradiction handling; LongMemEval 89.8%). Add it to the memory-provider catalog as the RECOMMENDED local external option (vs cloud mem0 — memanto runs on the user's machine, no egress). Two integration paths: (A) if memanto ships an MCP server, mount it via .mcp.json (zero adapter code, kernel-gated, like terminal-love) — document this quick-start; (B) a MemoryProvider REST adapter (store-on-learn + query-on-recall). Opt-in, off by default, never required for recall.
items.done: memanto registered in MEMORY_CATALOG; REST adapter implements the MemoryProvider port (mocked-endpoint tests); documented MCP-mount quick-start; opt-in/off-by-default, local source-of-truth preserved; committed to vanta.
items.notes: Pure/injectable MemantoMemory{id,text,score?} + MemantoMode rest|mcp + buildMemantoAddBody({text}) + buildMemantoSearchBody({query}) + parseMemantoMemories (tolerant array | {memories} | {results}; text|memory|content alias text-wins; numeric-id coercion; finite-score-only; control-stripped tab/newline preserved; [] on garbage) + resolveMemantoMode (VANTA_MEMANTO_MODE=mcp -> mcp else rest = local-first default) + memantoEnabled (VANTA_MEMANTO_URL set OR mode=mcp + VANTA_MEMANTO_MCP_SERVER; LOCAL-FIRST no key check) + makeMemantoAdapter({call:(op,payload)}) -> {add,search} routes via the single injected op-based call seam abstracting REST vs MCP; a throw -> {ok:false}/[] never throws. LOCAL-FIRST: no cloud key by design (a localhost URL / MCP server name) — NO key-shaped literal (gitleaks-safe). External text control-stripped. Both access modes over the established drive-sync MemoryAdapter port (mirrors mem0). Built+tested; wire = provider resolveMemoryProvider guarded by memantoEnabled reads resolveMemantoMode -> builds deps.call as localhost REST fetch (no auth header) OR routed mounted-MCP tool call -> routes recall through makeMemantoAdapter (named, live REST/MCP = boundary).

items.title: Richer typed memory categories (from memanto's 13 kinds)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] memanto types memories into 13 kinds (instruction/fact/decision/goal/commitment/preference/relationship/context/event/learning/observation/artifact/error) — sharper than Vanta's current EntryType (fact/skill/preference/pattern/insight/plan/emotion). Extend brain/entry-types.ts additively with the missing kinds (decision/commitment/relationship/context/event/observation/artifact/error + goal/learning if useful) so learned memories carry stronger type signal for recall/routing. Backward-compatible: existing types preserved, new ones additive, tolerant reader unchanged.
items.done: EntryType extended additively; normalizeEntry + the learn pass can emit the new kinds; old entries still read fine (tolerant reader); tests; committed to vanta.

items.title: brain 'answer' operation — synthesized provenanced answer from memory
items.summary: [repo: vanta] memanto's third op beyond remember/recall is ANSWER: a query -> a synthesized direct answer from memory WITH provenance, not just a list of entries. Add a brain answer action: route the query (reuse BRAIN-ROUTER), gather the hits, synthesize a concise provenanced answer via an injected provider (best-effort), fall back to plain recall on failure. Gives the agent a one-call 'what do I know about X' instead of recall-then-read-then-reason.
items.done: brain tool gains an answer action (router-backed recall + injected-provider synthesis + provenance, recall fallback on failure); pure prompt/format helpers tested; committed to vanta.
items.notes: Pure/injectable MemoryEntryRef{id,text} (mirrors world/conflicts CitedMatch provenance) + buildAnswerPrompt (question + numbered control-stripped entries + grounding rules: answer ONLY from these, cite the numbers, exact no memory on that fallback) + parseAnswer (extract ANSWER: body, read cited NUMBERS from CITES: line -> 1-based -> real entry id, DROP out-of-range hallucinated, dedupe, control-strip; requires the explicit CITES: marker not digit-scavenging so no false-cite) + answerFromMemory (injected recall+complete; no entries -> {ok:false,no-memory} with NO complete call asserted; else prompt->complete->parse; throw -> no-memory never throws; model no-memory reply -> no-answer) + providerComplete adapter. GROUNDED-ONLY: answer only when memory recalled AND model cited it; no memory = honest no-answer, NEVER fabricate. SECURITY: recalled text untrusted -> control-stripped in prompt+answer+citation ids. Built+tested; wire = brain.ts answer(question,env) facade -> answerFromMemory with real recall (BrainEntry->MemoryEntryRef) + CHEAP provider (aux-task) + brain tool answer action describeForSafety constant -> Allow (named).

items.title: Benchmark the brain on real LongMemEval + LoCoMo (the solid memory number)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] MEM-RETRIEVAL-EVAL is LongMemEval-STYLE on a small fixture; the solid validation is the REAL LongMemEval + LoCoMo — the standard memory benchmarks memanto reports (89.8% / 87.1%) — so Vanta's brain gets a comparable external number. Add a runner that loads the public LongMemEval/LoCoMo question sets and scores Vanta brain recall (lexical/hybrid/temporal/router) per category; record results. Deterministic retrieval-hit grading where possible; the answer-correctness variant needs a live model (flag). Needs the public datasets (downloaded, one-time).
items.done: a LongMemEval/LoCoMo runner scores Vanta brain recall per category against the public datasets and records results vs the fixture baseline; deterministic grading where possible, model-graded variant flagged for a live model; documented; committed to vanta.

items.title: Parallel agent fleet — fan out N independent tasks + a review surface
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Karpathy/'Peter Steinberg' pattern: drive many agents in parallel on independent macro-tasks (multiple checkouts, ~20-min jobs), become the bottleneck only at review. Vanta has delegate/team/subagent + `vanta agents` + git-worktree support; add a fan-out + review surface: dispatch N independent tasks to worktree-isolated parallel workers (no collisions), track live status, and a review board to inspect/accept each diff.
items.done: a command/flow fans out N independent tasks to worktree-isolated parallel workers, shows live status + per-worker diff for review/accept; builds on team/tasks + subagent/spawn + worktrees; tests; committed.

items.title: Auto-research loop — objective + verifiable metric + bounds, runs unattended
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Karpathy's auto-research: give it an OBJECTIVE + a verifiable METRIC + BOUNDARIES, hit go, iterate unattended (propose → apply in isolation → measure → keep if better) — remove the human as bottleneck. ONLY for objective/verifiable metrics (his caveat). Generalize the existing factory/evolve self-improving loop into a user-facing command; reuse eval/run + evolve/snapshot + regression locks.
items.done: `vanta auto-research --objective --metric --bounds` runs an unattended improve loop on a verifiable metric, keeps only commits that beat baseline, logs each delta, stops on no-progress; tests; committed.

items.title: Meta-tune the agent's own instructions against an eval (program.md optimization)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Karpathy: a research org IS its program.md, and you can optimize OVER the instructions (meta-optimization / recursive self-improvement of the harness config, not just its outputs). Auto-tune Vanta's own instruction surface (a defined block of the system-prompt tiers / a program.md) against an eval: mutate instructions, run the eval, keep the higher-scoring variant. Bounded mutations; human-approved before adoption.
items.done: a runner mutates a defined instruction block, scores variants via the eval (pass@1/CNG), records the best; bounded + human-approved before adoption; tests; committed.

items.title: LAN device discovery + control (the 'Dobby' home operator)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Karpathy's Dobby: discover smart-home/LAN subsystems (Sonos/lights/HVAC/cameras) via an IP scan, reverse-engineer their local APIs, and expose them as kernel-gated tools for natural-language control. New physical-adjacent operator surface. Strictly local network; discovery is read-only; control is approval-gated; everything through the kernel.
items.done: a LAN discovery tool finds devices + likely API endpoints (read-only scan) and a control tool drives them (approval-gated); both kernel-gated, safe defaults; tests with mocked network; committed.

items.title: Vision watch — change-detect a camera/screen, describe, alert via gateway
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Karpathy's camera flow: watch a camera/screen, change-detect, run a vision model on the changed frame, alert via the gateway/home messaging ('a FedEx truck pulled up'). Vanta has look_at_camera/look_at_screen + describe_image + gateway delivery; add a watch loop: periodic capture → change detection → vision describe → gateway alert on a meaningful change. Opt-in.
items.done: a watch loop captures a source on an interval, change-detects, describes the change via the vision model (resolveVisionProvider), and delivers an alert through the gateway/home platform; opt-in; tests with stubbed capture/vision; committed.

items.title: Ephemeral UI generation — agent serves a task-specific dashboard on demand
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Karpathy's 'claw presents you UIs / ephemeral software' + Dobby's command-center dashboard: generate and serve a quick task-specific dashboard/UI on demand (a control panel for discovered devices, a data view) instead of a permanent app. Vanta has roadmap/server.ts + desktop-app as references; add a tool that generates a self-contained HTML dashboard from data/spec and serves it locally.
items.done: a tool generates a self-contained HTML dashboard from supplied data/spec and serves it on a local port (like roadmap serve), scoped/reversible; pure generator tested; committed.
items.notes: Pure DashboardSpec{title,sections[]} (section kinds keyvalue rows / table columns+rows / text body, all readonly) + buildDashboardHtml (complete self-contained HTML, inline

items.title: Calibrated, earned-praise voice (anti-sycophancy) — personality matters
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Karpathy: a teammate personality with CALIBRATED praise (earned, not sycophantic — praise tracks actual merit, push back on half-baked ideas) is what separates the good agents. Vanta has SOUL.md + the BEHAVIOR-VOICE rule; sharpen it: proportional enthusiasm, earned praise, reasoned push-back on weak ideas. Prompt-rule + a light post-turn sycophancy self-check; no new system.
items.done: the voice rule encodes earned-praise / proportional-enthusiasm / push-back; a light post-turn checker flags sycophantic over-validation; pure checker tested; documented; committed.

items.title: Per-function aux model map (speciation) — small specialized models per task
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Karpathy's 'speciation' — small specialized models per task, main model as the cognitive core. Vanta already routes vision (VANTA_VISION_MODEL), embeddings (VANTA_EMBED_MODEL), and the prune scorer; generalize to a per-FUNCTION model map (summarize/title/embed/vision/prune/classify) resolved like the existing aux routing, so each auxiliary task uses the right small model. The AUX-MAP follow-on noted after AUX-VISION.
items.done: resolveAuxModel(function, env) drives the aux call sites (summarize/title/embed/vision/prune/classify), env-configured with fallback to the active provider; pure resolution tested; wired in; committed.

items.title: Verified contribution pool (untrusted workers) — design spike
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Karpathy's untrusted-pool / folding@home / blockchain-of-commits idea: expensive to find an improvement, cheap to verify. A pipeline accepting candidate patches from untrusted sources and AUTO-VERIFYING them against the eval + regression locks before merge — only commits that beat baseline + pass locks land. SPIKE ONLY: design the verify-after-sandbox flow + accept criteria; never run arbitrary untrusted code without sandbox sign-off (kernel/Rule-Zero). Stop and surface for go/no-go.
items.done: a design spike documents the sandbox-then-verify pipeline + accept criteria (beats baseline + passes locks) + go/no-go; no arbitrary-code execution built without sign-off; committed.

items.title: Codex provider model selection — verify + document which model to use
items.summary: [repo: vanta] The codex provider (ChatGPT-subscription OAuth, Responses API) defaults to VANTA_MODEL ?? "gpt-5.6-sol" (providers/index.ts makeCodex); the catalog (providers/catalog.ts) includes the current GPT-5.6 Sol / Terra / Luna family alongside compatible older Codex models, and codex.ts CODEX_CONTEXT holds their context windows. Keep current availability verified against official sources because model names ship fast; setup, catalog guidance, and .env.example must explain the intended choice. Note: the codex-rescue agent (Codex CLI handoff) uses the Codex CLI's own model config — out of scope here; this card is the in-process codex PROVIDER.

Stop-and-surface if current Codex model availability cannot be verified.

Shipped 2026-06-18. The catalog note, .env.example, provider docs, targeted tests, typecheck, and desktop bundle were refreshed.

MODEL REFRESH 2026-07-14: follow-up audit found the picker still missed current API and Codex-agent entries. Expanded the OpenAI API picker with GPT-5.3-Codex, GPT-5.2/pro, GPT-5.1, GPT-5/pro/mini/nano, and o3-pro; expanded the ChatGPT-subscription Codex picker with GPT-5.3-Codex, GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark, GPT-5.2-Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex Max/standard/mini, and GPT-5-Codex standard/mini. Corrected GPT-5.4 mini/nano context windows to 400K, added missing context maps, enabled GPT-5 reasoning_effort routing, and added xhigh effort support.

items.title: Declarative workflow graph — diffable agent pipelines with human-gate nodes
items.summary: [repo: vanta] From fabro (github.com/fabro-sh/fabro, the 'dark software factory'): pipelines are a diffable, version-controlled graph — agent nodes, human-approval gates, interview steps — with branching, loops, parallelism, and conditional transitions ([A]pprove -> proceed, [R]evise -> loop back). Vanta's orchestration (fleet/, loop/, factory/, ralph/) is all procedural code, so the plan can't be reviewed as an artifact before it runs. Add a declarative pipeline format (minimal JSON/TS graph — NOT necessarily full Graphviz DOT, do the least that works) plus a runner that executes nodes via subagent/spawn; every node routes through the kernel assess() gate, human gates pause for approval, branch/loop transitions drive control flow. Reviewable + diffable before execution = 'knows the goal before it picks a tool.' Serves Harness + the trusted-operator north star.
items.done: a declarative workflow graph (agent / approval / interview nodes + branch/loop/parallel transitions) can be authored, diffed, and executed; each node routes through the kernel; human gates pause for approval; builds on subagent/spawn + fleet; co-located tests; tsc clean; committed.

items.title: Selector-based model stylesheet — per-class model+effort routing with fallback chains
items.summary: [repo: vanta] From fabro: a CSS-like stylesheet routes each step to a model + reasoning_effort by selector with automatic fallback chains, e.g. `* { model: haiku; reasoning_effort: low } .coding { model: sonnet; reasoning_effort: high }`. Vanta's routing/model-router.ts is binary cheap/expensive (VANTA_MODEL_CHEAP/_EXPENSIVE). Generalize to N task-classes: a small selector -> {provider/model, effort, fallback[]} map; reasoning_effort maps onto Vanta's existing effort levels (low|medium|high|max); add automatic fallback to the next entry on a provider error. Low blast radius, improves an existing module; pairs with FABRO-WORKFLOW-GRAPH (a stylesheet per node) but works standalone.
items.done: a selector-based routing config maps task-class -> {provider/model, effort, fallback chain}; resolveRoutedProvider honors it with automatic fallback on failure; effort ties into the existing effort levels; defaults preserve current behavior when unset; co-located tests; tsc clean; committed.

items.title: Structured interview input — schema-validated HITL beyond free-text clarify
items.summary: [repo: vanta] From fabro: 'interview steps' collect structured human input as a first-class node. Vanta's clarify tool asks free-text questions end-of-turn. Enrich clarify (or add a sibling tool) to request schema-validated structured input — typed fields and explicit choices the agent can rely on — validated with zod before returning to the loop. Pairs with FABRO-WORKFLOW-GRAPH's interview node. Operator/EF: better, less-ambiguous HITL input than free text.
items.done: clarify (or a new interview tool) can request structured, schema-validated input (fields/choices), validates the response with zod at the boundary, returns typed values to the loop, and degrades to free-text when no schema is given; co-located tests; tsc clean; committed.

items.title: Recover — failure-mode triage (bug vs polluted-context vs wrong-assumption)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] From the JS-Mastery 'how senior engineers build with AI' workflow (the `recover` skill): when a long session goes wrong, diagnose WHICH failure mode you're in — (a) a targeted bug, (b) polluted / self-contradicting context, (c) a wrong assumption made earlier — and route to the right corrective action instead of letting the agent (and operator) spiral. Vanta has the pieces (set-shift = stuck-loop detector, error-detect = consecutive errors, debug-system skill, compact-on-error) but NO unified triage that distinguishes polluted-context (-> compact/restart/clear) from a wrong-earlier-assumption (-> revisit the plan/decisions) from a targeted bug (-> debug). Add a `/recover` command (plus optional auto-trigger on repeated failure) that classifies the failure mode from recent transcript + tool-result signals and emits the matching next step. Serves Harness reliability + the trusted-operator north star (recover without babysitting). Distinct from LIVENESS-WATCHDOG (stall detection) — this is corrective routing once trouble is already underway.
items.done: a `/recover` command classifies the current trouble into {targeted-bug, polluted-context, wrong-assumption} from recent transcript + tool-result signals and returns the matching corrective action (debug / compact-or-restart / revisit-plan); pure classifier unit-tested; wired into the REPL; co-located tests; tsc clean; committed.

items.title: Current-docs-before-use — fetch up-to-date library docs before coding against a lib
