items.summary: [repo: vanta] From the same workflow (context7 usage + the per-project `library docs` context file): before writing code against a third-party library, fetch its CURRENT docs rather than trusting training data. Vanta has the source-grounded skill (the principle) + MCP mounting + web-fetch, but no built-in 'pull up-to-date docs for library X before using it' step. Add a small capability that resolves current docs for a named library (via a context7-style MCP when mounted, else a web-fetch fallback) and returns a short usage digest before the agent codes against it. Reinforces source-grounded; pairs with the per-project context scaffold (VANTA-BLUEPRINTS / VANTA-MAGIC-DOCS). Sand-tier: small, low-risk, foldable into an existing tool if cleaner.
items.done: a tool/step resolves current docs for a named library (context7-style MCP when available, web-fetch fallback) and returns a short usage digest; degrades cleanly when offline/unmounted; co-located tests with fixtures (no live network); tsc clean; committed.
items.notes: Pure LibRef{name,source:import|mention} + detectLibraries (import/require/from-import + Python from lib import -> import; bare known-lib mentions -> mention, import wins on dedupe; EXCLUDES relative ./../ + absolute + node builtins plain/node: prefix; subpaths stripped to package root; @scope/pkg kept whole) + DOCS_URL_PATTERNS (20 known libs -> docs site) + buildDocsFetchPlan ({name,docsUrl}, npm fallback) + libDocsEnabled (VANTA_LIB_DOCS=1, default OFF) + formatDocsPlan. Mechanical assist for the source-grounded discipline. SECURITY: a name only reaches a URL if it matches SAFE_PACKAGE charset (evil/../../etc/passwd + https://evil.test dropped, tested); npm fallback URL-encodes each segment. No arbitrary URL injection. Built+tested; wire = pre-code path when libDocsEnabled detectLibraries(task) -> buildDocsFetchPlan -> formatDocsPlan via onText -> fetch via web_fetch (named, kernel-gated boundary). No lib = no fetch.

items.title: Path-grouped lossless densification of grep/search tool output
items.summary: [repo: vanta] From a reference agent-harness eval (a headroom-compression eval): the one proven win was lossless path-grouped densification of search output — group consecutive same-path matches under one header with indented `line: content` pairs => ~58% fewer tokens, zero-dep, lossless, fires only on >=5 matches. Vanta's grep/search tools (grep_files, code-search, glob-files, life-search) emit the same repeated path strings + JSON keys. Add a pure path-grouped densifier to the compress/ system (compress/apply COMPRESS_TOOLS) so these results densify before entering context. Lossless with exhaustive edge-case tests (unicode/emoji/colons/quotes/empty content/trailing whitespace); backward-compatible; NO new dependency. Beats CCR on this shape (see COMPRESSION-MEASURE).
items.done: a pure LOSSLESS path-grouped densifier reformats grep/search tool output on >=5 matches (consecutive same-path matches under one header + indented `line: content`), preserving every line number and byte exactly; applied as a SEPARATE lossless step (NOT added to the lossy COMPRESS_TOOLS allow-list) and run BEFORE result-offload so densified output may stay under the 50K offload threshold; exhaustive round-trip losslessness tests (unicode/colons/quotes/empty/trailing-ws); no new dep; tsc clean; committed.
items.notes: Lossless lane is the whole point: Vanta's lossy COMPRESS_TOOLS allow-list deliberately excludes grep/search (line-shift corrupts file:line). Path-grouping is lossless so it is the ONE compressor safe on precision search output. Source: a reference agent-harness eval.

items.title: CCR disposition — keep / scope-to-history / retire, from the measurement
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Gated on COMPRESSION-MEASURE. Vanta's CCR/offload (compress/result-offload.ts + store.ts) hands STRONG backbones a grep-able `.vanta/ccr/<id>.txt` pointer plus a `retrieve_original` whole-blob path, defaulting inline for weak backbones (delivery-policy.ts, cites the agent-harness paper). a reference agent-harness eval found headroom's retrieve-whole CCR net-negative for coding agents (agent pulls content right back -> double context, 0.34% net) — but Vanta's grep-pointer design may already avoid that, so the verdict is OPEN until measured. Once COMPRESSION-MEASURE reports the retrieve_original WHOLE-BLOB rate vs grep-slice rate + net corpus tokens: if whole-retrieve is high (CCR net-negative here) -> scope CCR to history-compaction only / retire it from the live tool-output path (delete > add, net simplification of compress/); if strong models grep slices (net-positive) -> keep offload as-is and only tighten the weak-backbone window. Evidence-led minimal change.
items.done: using COMPRESSION-MEASURE's whole-retrieve-vs-grep + net-token data, CCR is either kept (with the net-positive number documented), scoped to history-compaction, or retired from the live tool-output path; the chosen change is implemented + tested; no compression path that measures net-negative stays on by default; committed.

items.title: Currency/recency rule in the system prompt — verify-before-stating, no cutoff hedging
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Extracted from the Claude Fable 5 system prompt. Vanta's prompt already had strong verify/honesty/epistemic-label discipline (rules 2-4,7,10); the one gap was CURRENCY. Added rule 7a to prompt.ts stableTier: verify current versions/API shapes/model names/CLI flags before stating them, unfamiliar capitalized/version-like names probably postdate training (search first; partial recognition != current knowledge), and never hedge with 'as of my cutoff'. Also extended the voice rule (10) with own-mistakes-without-self-abasement. Keeps the 3-tier architecture; prompt.test green.
items.done: system prompt stableTier carries a currency rule (verify versions/APIs/models before stating; unrecognized name -> check; no cutoff hedging) + an own-mistakes-without-abasement clause on the voice rule; prompt.test passes; committed.

items.title: Interactive /config dialog — view + change settings in a TUI menu (CC parity)
items.summary: [repo: vanta] Claude Code's /config opens an interactive settings dialog; Vanta's /config is only a read-only CLI passthrough (show resolved config). Upgrade /config to open an interactive TUI overlay that shows current settings grouped + lets you change the high-value ones — effort level, output-style verbosity, composer anchor, permission/auto mode, sandbox toggles, ND gates, theme/spinner — persisting to settings.local.json. Mirror the shipped sandbox-panel pattern (pure view-shaping + actions modules + Ink overlay + settings store). Keep `vanta config show/get/set` CLI for headless.
items.done: /config opens an interactive overlay listing current settings by group; the high-value settings (effort, verbosity, composer anchor, permission/auto mode, sandbox, ND gates, theme/spinner) can be toggled/cycled and persist to settings.local.json; dangerous raw fields (allowed/blockedTools, env) are read-only or omitted; pure view+actions unit-tested + panel render-tested; tsc + suite green; committed.

items.title: Lighten the trust gate — auto-trust option + non-intrusive prompt
items.summary: [repo: vanta] VANTA-TRUST-DIALOG (shipped) persists once but the prompt is a wall-of-text modal dumping full context-file previews, and it fires for the operator's OWN repos — friction without benefit for a single-trusted-operator tool. Two fixes: (1) an auto-trust lever — settings.trust.auto / VANTA_TRUST_ALL env → skip the project gate entirely (auto-trust + persist), no prompt; (2) a LIGHTER prompt when it does fire — show context-file names + line counts + a one-line ask (trust / don't / view-to-expand), not the full file dumps. Keep MCP-server trust as-is (more security-relevant) and keep persistence. Honors Rule Zero: still ADDS a gate, never bypasses the kernel.
items.done: settings.trust.auto (or VANTA_TRUST_ALL) auto-trusts projects without prompting + persists; when the gate does prompt, it shows file names + counts + a one-line trust/deny/view choice (no full-file wall of text); MCP trust + persistence unchanged; pure logic unit-tested + dialog render-tested; tsc + suite green; committed.

items.title: Run anywhere you control — laptop · VPS · server · Docker · SSH · serverless
items.summary: Identity anchor + umbrella for run-anywhere (DECISIONS 2026-06-19). Vanta is not laptop-bound: it runs on any host the user controls and the kernel travels with it, gating every action wherever it runs. User-controlled-infrastructure-first — no vendor lock-in, the user's data residency. Children carry the backend impls: BACKEND-DOCKER, BACKEND-SERVERLESS, VANTA-SSH-SESSION, SETUP-TERMINAL-BACKEND (wizard surface). Pure-infra (Docker/SSH/VPS) first; serverless-SaaS sequenced after behind a rule-zero data-residency sub-decision.
items.done: Vanta runs an agent session on a remote host the user controls (VPS/server/container/SSH) with the kernel enforcing scope there exactly as locally; the setup wizard picks the execution backend; README documents the $5-VPS path. Serverless is a later child.

items.title: Docker execution backend — shell + code run in a container
items.summary: Containerized execution backend for shell_cmd + run_code: tool execution runs inside a Docker container instead of the host, kernel-gated as today. The first concrete run-anywhere backend (pure user-infra, zero SaaS tension). Gives SETUP-TERMINAL-BACKEND's local/sandbox/docker/ssh selector a real docker adapter.
items.done: With the docker backend selected, shell_cmd and run_code execute in a container; the kernel assess() gate still runs; out-of-container writes refused; tests cover the adapter; falls back cleanly when Docker is absent.

items.title: Serverless backend — Modal/Daytona hibernate-when-idle persistence
items.summary: The opt-in Modal/Daytona execution adapter is wired behind the shared backend port with structured command transport, idle policy, CLI/authentication readiness checks, setup choices, and fail-closed location intent. Modal now targets the real Sandbox API rather than an invalid generic `modal run` shape. Real hibernate/wake execution is owned by BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE.
items.done: Modal and Daytona config/argv adapters are selectable through the execution-backend resolver; the kernel remains upstream; unavailable or invalid remote selection refuses instead of running locally; focused tests cover the adapter. Live cloud execution is a separate gate.
items.notes:
UPDATED 2026-07-10: the pure Modal/Daytona config and discrete-argv builder is now registered through ExecBackendAdapter alongside local and Docker. exec/adapters/serverless.ts owns CLI probing and transport argv; exec/backend.ts is the sole resolver/registration point. Explicit VANTA_EXEC_BACKEND=serverless with invalid config or missing CLI returns an actionable refusal and never silently changes location. Setup offers opt-in Modal and Daytona choices for the operator's own account. No SDK or cloud secret is embedded. Executed evidence: 43 backend/serverless/setup tests, 24 shell/run-code consumer tests, TypeScript, 7 architecture tests, and the size gate pass.

CORRECTED 2026-07-10: current Modal 1.5.1 documentation and CLI parsing proved the original `modal run -- <command>` assumption invalid because `modal run` requires a Python function/local-entrypoint reference. Replaced it with a bundled Modal local entrypoint that decodes one base64url JSON payload, copies the actual command workdir to `/workspace` while excluding `.git`, `.vanta`, dependencies, caches, and `.env` files, creates a real `modal.Sandbox`, applies block_network and timeout policy, executes structured argv, streams stdout/stderr, propagates exit status, and terminates the sandbox.

AUTH PREFLIGHT 2026-07-10: the adapter now distinguishes a missing CLI from an installed but unauthenticated Modal CLI by checking `modal token info`. Executed the real `wrapExec` path against Modal 1.5.1 with a `SHOULD_NOT_RUN` sentinel; it returned the exact `modal token new --verify` recovery and refused local fallback before the command ran. Executed `vanta doctor --verbose` with Modal selected and observed the same recovery under the visible notices section. The browser account/token authorization is still pending; no live cloud command, hibernate, or wake behavior is claimed.

items.title: Script injection + tools-via-RPC — zero-context-cost pipelines
items.summary: The one real architecture gap vs a reference agent harness (gap analysis 2026-06-19). Two halves: (1) cron/gateway --script pre-processor — a script runs BEFORE the agent turn and its stdout is injected as context (mechanical fetch/diff/compute in code, agent reasons over the result); (2) tools-via-RPC — a script calls Vanta tools over a local RPC so a multi-step pipeline collapses into one zero-context-cost turn. Closest existing analog is FABRO/compose_workflow (declarative graph), but neither the --script pre-processor nor the RPC-from-script pattern exists. Kernel still gates every tool call the script makes.
items.done: A cron/gateway task can run a --script whose stdout becomes the agent's context; a user script can call N Vanta tools via local RPC in one turn with each call kernel-gated; both unit-tested; docs show a watch-site pipeline example.
items.notes: Pure/injectable zod PipelineStep{tool,args,assignTo?} (assignTo [A-Za-z0-9_-]) + Pipeline{steps 1..50} + parsePipeline (zod + unique-assignTo guard, errors-as-values never throws) + substituteRefs ($name + {{name}} bare+inline; unbound ref -> <unbound:name> marker surfaced not silent) + runPipeline (injected callTool; run in order, bind assignTo, later steps reference prior bindings, a tool error/throw/unbound STOPS the chain + returns failedStep, returns ONLY the final steps result — intermediates stay in bindings, NEVER surfaced to the model = zero-context-cost) + pipelineContextCost. Linear sibling of the FABRO graph. SECURITY: orchestration NOT a permission escape — each step routes through the injected kernel-assess-gated callTool, gated EXACTLY as a direct call. Built+tested; wire = a run_pipeline tool parsePipeline -> runPipeline with registry.get(tool).execute as callTool, returns only the final output (named). Bad step stops the chain.

items.title: Fix: Cmd+V image paste into the TUI doesn't attach (only /image + drag-drop work)
items.summary: Reported 2026-06-19 (Jason): pasting a screenshot into the TUI composer leaves the model blind — Vanta replies it can't see the image unless you use /image <path> or drag it in. But TUI-IMAGE-CLICKABLE and VANTA-S-IMAGE are both marked SHIPPED claiming paste works, so this is a shipped-but-broken gap on the core flow. Likely fix: bind the composer paste chord (Cmd+V / bracketed-paste image data) to the SAME clipboard-image grab /paste already does on macOS (and wire the existing clipboard adapter for non-/paste capture), so paste Just Works without a command. Pairs with the shipped VANTA-CLIPBOARD-IMAGE-HINT flow.
items.done: Cmd+V in the TUI composer with an image on the clipboard attaches it to the next message and the model receives it — verified live by pasting a screenshot, no /image path or drag-drop needed. The shipped paste cards (TUI-IMAGE-CLICKABLE, VANTA-S-IMAGE) are reconciled with the real flow.

items.title: Cmd+Backspace clears the line (kitty keyboard protocol)
items.summary: Enable Ink's kitty keyboard protocol (render mode:auto — no-op where unsupported) so the Cmd (super) modifier reaches the composer; bind super+Backspace to kill-to-line-start (same as ^U). Ink already parsed `super` but the composer's hand-rolled Key type dropped it; isPrintable guarded against super so Cmd+letter never types the letter. Works on kitty-protocol terminals (Ghostty/Kitty/WezTerm/recent iTerm2); Terminal.app stays legacy with ^U as the universal fallback.
items.done: Cmd+Backspace clears to line start in the TUI composer on a kitty-protocol terminal; ^U remains the fallback everywhere; tests cover the binding + the super-not-printable guard; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Cmd+Backspace on Terminal.app — known limitation + ^U hint
items.summary: Known limitation (2026-06-19): Cmd+Backspace → clear-line (TUI-CMD-BACKSPACE-CLEAR) only fires on kitty-protocol terminals (Ghostty/Kitty/WezTerm/recent iTerm2). macOS Terminal.app doesn't speak the kitty/CSI-u protocol that carries the Cmd (super) modifier and reserves Cmd for its own menus, so the keystroke can't reach the app there — no reliable in-app fix. ^U is the universal clear-line fallback in every terminal today. Concrete future action: on first run detect Terminal.app (TERM_PROGRAM=Apple_Terminal) and surface a one-time hint to use ^U or switch to a kitty-capable terminal.
items.done: On Terminal.app, Vanta surfaces a one-time hint that Cmd+Backspace needs a kitty-capable terminal and that ^U clears the line; zero behavior change on capable terminals.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. term/cmd-backspace-hint.ts (pure + tolerant one-time flag): isTerminalApp (TERM_PROGRAM=Apple_Terminal), isKittyCapable (KITTY_WINDOW_ID/TERM kitty/ghostty/wezterm/iterm — carries the Cmd/super modifier), cmdBackspaceHint (points to ^U + a kitty-capable terminal), shouldShowCmdBackspaceHint (Terminal.app AND not-capable AND not-already-shown), maybeShowCmdBackspaceHint (emits once, persists the id to ~/.vanta/shown-hints.json, best-effort — a persist failure just re-shows next time). Wired into interactive.ts announceSessionStart after the banner/resume notice; ZERO behavior change on capable terminals (early false). 7 tests (Terminal.app + kitty detection, should-show matrix, emit-once-then-persisted, never-on-capable). Full suite 1067 files/11935 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Agent org chart — roles, titles, reporting lines, permissions
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Org Chart & Agents). Give the team roster (team/*) a real hierarchy: each agent has a role, title, manager edge, permission set, and budget. Manager agents own reports; the chart drives delegation + escalation routing. This is the company layer pillar 5 assumed THEFT would own — no THEFT, so Vanta carries it.
items.done: An agent roster carries role/title/manager edges; /team renders the hierarchy and delegation/escalation route along it.

items.title: Hire flow — add a budgeted, role-tagged agent (any adapter)
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Identity & Access / Org Chart). A "hire" path that registers an agent — any adapter/provider (Vanta itself, codex, claude-code, an HTTP bot) — with a role, job description, and monthly budget into the org, selectable for task assignment. Extends team dispatch + provider catalog.
items.done: vanta hire <role> --adapter <x> adds a budgeted, role-tagged agent to the roster that can be assigned tasks.

items.title: Delegation up/down the org chart
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Heartbeat / Org Chart). A manager agent assigns a subtask to a report; a report escalates a blocker upward. Builds on delegate/team/subagent + the task ledger so work flows along the hierarchy instead of flat fan-out.
items.done: A manager can assign a subtask to a report and a report can escalate a blocker to its manager, both recorded in the task ledger.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. Work flows along the org hierarchy, not flat fan-out. team/delegation.ts (pure, over the existing org-chart edges + team/tasks ledger): planDelegateDown validates reportId is a DIRECT report of managerId (via resolveDelegateTarget — delegation follows the chart, can't jump to a grand-report) → an assignTask spec for the report; planEscalateUp resolves the worker's manager (resolveEscalateTarget) → a manager-assigned task titled '[escalated from <id>] <blocker>', refused when the worker is a root (no manager). Both RECORDED IN THE TASK LEDGER (assignTask + appendTask). Wired as `team` tool actions delegate_down (managerId, reportId, taskId, title) + escalate_up (id, taskId, blocker). 5 pure planner tests (assign to report, refuse non-report/grand-report, unknown manager, escalate-to-manager with origin title, refuse root escalation) + LIVE real-path via ALL_TOOLS (lead→ana delegate + ana→lead escalate both landed as ledger tasks, verified with action:tasks). Full suite 1051 files/11798 green; tsc + size gate clean. Reuses the org-chart resolution + task ledger; no new store.

items.title: Leadership chat that resolves to real work objects
items.summary: Paperclip extract (CEO Chat, roadmap ⚪). A lightweight conversation with the lead agent that RESOLVES TO tracked work objects — plans, issues, approvals, decisions — instead of a freeform chat window. Improves interaction without turning Vanta into a chatbot.
items.done: Talking to the lead agent produces tracked issues/plans/approvals, not just replies.

items.title: Self-organization — agents propose org changes within governance
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Self-Organization, roadmap ⚪). Agents propose structural changes — new recurring routines, role adjustments, delegation edits — that enter the approval queue before taking effect. Adaptive org that stays inside governance/approval boundaries.
items.done: An agent can propose an org change (routine/role/delegation) that enters the approval queue before it takes effect.

items.title: Organizational learning — completed work → reusable playbooks
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Automatic Organizational Learning, roadmap ⚪). Completed work distills into reusable playbooks, recurring-fix patterns, and decision patterns at team scope — brain, but shared across the roster and re-injected on similar future tasks.
items.done: A recurring task pattern is captured as a shared playbook and re-injected into future similar tasks.

items.title: Export/import an operator workspace (scrubbed bundle)
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Company Portability / companies.sh). Export and import a whole operator workspace — agents, skills, goals, routines, tasks — with secret scrubbing and id-collision handling. Reframed from Paperclip's multi-company export to single-workspace; aligns with Vanta's local-first / your-data-residency values.
items.done: vanta export writes a scrubbed bundle; vanta import re-creates agents/skills/goals/routines with collision handling.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. Distinct from `vanta backup` (raw whole-store tarball, unscrubbed): a SCRUBBED, SELECTIVE, collision-handled workspace bundle. workspace/portability.ts (pure, collection-generic — a collection = a named list of {id,...} records): parseBundle (zod, version:1, drops id-less records), scrubBundle (recurses every string field through redactForLog — vendor-key + positional URL/header/conn-string credential masking; new bundle, input untouched), mergeCollection (id-collision handling: skip keeps existing / overwrite lets incoming win / rename adds incoming under a fresh '<id>-imported[-n]' id; order-stable, returns added/skipped/renamed/overwritten counts). cli/workspace-cmd.ts: `vanta workspace export [file]` gathers via collection adapters → scrub → JSON; `import <file> [--on-collision skip|rename|overwrite]` merges back per-collection. Adapters wired for skills (writeSkill) + routines (durable-cron) — both with clean list/write APIs; the engine is collection-generic so goals/agents/tasks are additive one-adapter-each. 8 engine tests (parse+drop, scrub incl. nested + no-mutation, all 4 collision behaviors incl. -imported suffix bump). LIVE real-path: exported a skill workspace (scrub verified — a real 48-char sk- key masked; a sub-threshold 14-char token correctly kept, near-zero-false-positive by design) then imported into a fresh home (+1 added). Full suite 1047 files/11759 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Atomic task checkout + execution locks (no double-work)
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Why Paperclip is special — atomic execution; withAgentStartLock). Task checkout and budget enforcement are atomic so two parallel agents never double-work one task. Reliability for Vanta's worktree fleet — aligned regardless of the THEFT pivot.
items.done: Concurrent fleet workers cannot both claim the same task; the second is refused or queued, proven by a test.

items.title: Scoped budget hard-stops — overspend auto-pauses + cancels queued
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Budget & Cost Control; budgets.ts pauseScopeForBudget). Token/cost budgets per goal/agent/session with warning thresholds + HARD STOPS: on overspend the scope flips to paused (pauseReason:budget) and queued work is cancelled. Vanta has COST-VISIBLE display only — this is the "walk away safely" rail (the run-unwatched trust gap).
items.done: A goal/agent with a budget auto-pauses and cancels its queued work on overspend, not just displays the cost.

items.title: Heartbeat run pipeline — coalesced queue, secret/skill injection, orphan recovery
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Heartbeat Execution). The full run pipeline beyond WAKE-CONTEXT's context-shaping: a durable wakeup queue with coalescing, budget check, workspace resolution, scoped-secret injection, skill loading, and adapter invocation as one recoverable run; orphaned runs auto-recover on restart (recoveryService → also closes LIVENESS-WATCHDOG).
items.done: A single wakeup runs the coalesced budget→workspace→secret→skill→adapter pipeline and auto-recovers an orphaned run on restart.

items.title: Multi-dimensional cost attribution (by goal/agent/provider/model)
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Budget & Cost Control). Token/$ rollups attributed by goal, agent, project, provider, and model over a window — Vanta's pricing.ts is per-turn only. Feeds budgets + operator visibility.
items.done: /usage breaks spend down by goal/agent/provider/model over a chosen window.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04 — the done criterion's scope: goal/agent/provider/model (the summary also mentions 'project', but Vanta is single-project-per-repo today; project-level attribution would need the VANTA_PROJECTS_DIR rooms concept threaded through, out of this slice's bar). cost/ledger.ts (zod-validated .vanta/spend-ledger.jsonl, tolerant reads) + cost/attribution.ts (pure summarizeSpend/filterSpendSince/formatSpendBreakdown) is the persisted complement to pricing.ts's in-memory session view. Wired at BOTH real cost-computing call sites: interactive-post-turn.ts (extracted + exported recordTurnCost, held runPostTurnPipeline under the cx gate) tags agent:'interactive'; cli/ops.ts buildCronRunTask tags agent:'gateway' — both attribute to the active kernel goal id when one exists, both no-op on null/zero cost (unpriced model, local provider). New `/usage breakdown [--since <ISO>]` in repl/context-cmds.ts; bare `/usage` stays the unchanged session-scoped view. 15 cost/ tests + 5 context-cmds tests + 4 recordTurnCost tests; full suite 1012 files/11435 tests green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Staged review/approval as first-class workflow steps
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Governance & Approvals / Agent Reviews). Execution policies with named review + approval STAGES — reviewer routing, change-requests, approval gates — beyond Vanta's per-action y/n. Sits on the kernel approval queue.
items.done: A task can require a named review stage that routes to a reviewer and blocks completion until approved.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-06. team/review-stage.ts (pure): ReviewStage {name, reviewerId, status pending|approved|rejected, decidedBy/reason/decidedAt}; requireStage (dedup by name, refused on done/removed tasks), decideStage (provenance; a rejected stage can be re-approved after rework), blockingStages, checkReviewGate (done is refused while ANY stage is not APPROVED — pending and rejected both block: completion requires an explicit approval, not the absence of a decision), reviewQueue (the routes-to-a-reviewer surface: pending stages per reviewer across the ledger). Same additive contract as COFOUNDER-ENFORCED-OUTCOME: WorkerTask gains optional reviewStages; advanceTask consults the review gate right after the outcome gate; stage-free tasks byte-identical. Tool surface: `team` gains require_review / review / reviews actions (schema + zod + dispatch). 13 new pure tests (gate via the REAL advanceTask, multi-stage all-must-approve, reject-then-redeem, queue routing) + REAL tool-path verified end-to-end via ALL_TOOLS (dispatch -> require_review -> done REFUSED naming stage+reviewer -> reviews queue -> approve -> done closes). Full suite 1022 files/11536 green; tsc + size gate clean. NOTE (pre-existing, not this slice): importing tools/team.js directly (not via all-tools/registry) hits a module-init cycle team-run -> tools/index -> all-tools — reproduced with this slice stashed; registry entry unaffected.

items.title: Config/agent/skill revisioning with safe rollback
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Governance with rollback). Config, agent, and skill changes are versioned and safely roll-back-able; a bad change reverts cleanly. Pairs with SELFHARNESS-SANDBOX (test a change before it lands).
items.done: A config change is versioned and `vanta config rollback` restores the prior revision.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04 — the done criterion's scope: config revisioning + rollback (agent/skill revisioning is broader than the done bar; skills already have their own git-backed versioning + reversible archiveSkill, so left out of this slice). cli-dx/config-revisions.ts (pure, zod-validated): appendRevision/listRevisions/getRevision/latestRevision over .vanta/config-revisions.jsonl, tolerant reads (a corrupt line never wedges rollback). config.ts's setConfig + migrateConfig now snapshot the PRE-write .env content before every write, so the jsonl accumulates exactly the prior states. New `vanta config rollback [REV]` restores a specific numbered revision, or the latest (undo-last-change) when omitted — and snapshots the current state FIRST, so a rollback is itself a recorded, reversible revision (proven: rolling back twice restores the more-recent state). New `vanta config revisions` lists history. ops-app.ts's runConfigCommand subcommand dispatch refactored from an if-chain to a CONFIG_SUBCOMMANDS data table (held the cx gate as 2 subcommands were added). 24 config.ts + config-revisions.ts tests green; full suite 1008 files/11411 tests green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Enforced outcomes — tasks resolve to a typed result, not a status
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Enforced Outcomes, roadmap ⚪). Tasks/approvals resolve to a concrete outcome — merged code, published artifact, shipped doc, explicit decision — not a vague status update. Stricter than /verify: 'done' requires the outcome object exist.
items.done: A task cannot close without a typed outcome object; status 'done' requires the outcome be present.
items.notes: Shipped-by-reference 2026-07-04 — a DUPLICATE of already-shipped work, not a new build. This done criterion, word-for-word, is already satisfied by COFOUNDER-ENFORCED-OUTCOME (cofounder/outcome-contract.ts: OutcomeContract + canCloseTask — a task closes only when an artifact of the expected type exists or an explicit persisted no-artifact reason is set) + ENFORCED-OUTCOME-WIRE (team/tasks.ts advanceTask → checkOutcomeGate: the live running→done transition is refused without a satisfied contract; a task with no contract is unaffected, byte-identical to before the gate). Verified via existing tests (team/tasks.test.ts: present-artifact allows close, absent-artifact refuses, explicit no-artifact-reason force-closes, gate is inert on non-done transitions) — 24/24 green, re-run to confirm before marking this shipped. No new code: writing a redundant module for an already-fully-tested, already-wired guarantee would be manufactured busywork, not a real slice.

items.title: Maximizer mode — higher-autonomy execution under hard governance
items.summary: Paperclip extract (MAXIMIZER MODE, roadmap ⚪). A higher-autonomy profile: aggressive delegation, deeper follow-through, stronger operating loops with explicit budgets, visibility, and governance — "more output per supervisor", not hidden autonomy. Builds on Ralph loop + fleet + budget hard-stop.
items.done: A maximizer run delegates + follows through across multiple tasks under a hard budget with a visible activity trail, ending in verified outcomes.

items.title: Deep planning — revisionable plan docs with a pre-execution review gate
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Deep Planning, roadmap ⚪). Strategy-heavy work gets a revisionable plan/issue document and a review loop BEFORE execution starts. Extends /planmode + VANTA-PLAN-MODE-V2 with durable, revisable plan artifacts.
items.done: A strategy task produces a revisionable plan doc that must pass a review gate before execution starts.

items.title: Per-run runtime services — dev servers + preview URLs on worktrees
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Workspaces & Runtime). On top of the existing worktree fleet: resolve per-run runtime services — dev servers, preview URLs — so an agent's work is previewable and bound to its execution workspace.
items.done: A run can start a dev server / preview URL bound to its worktree and surface the URL on the task.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added fleet runtime preview services. `vanta runtime start --fleet <id> --worker <id> --port <n> --command <cmd>` spawns a detached preview process in the worker worktree, records pid/url/command under the worker's fleet report, and `vanta runtime list` plus fleet status/review/digest surface the preview URL. Live evidence: created a temporary fleet worker, started a real HTTP preview through the CLI, fetched the recorded URL successfully, then killed the spawned pid and removed the fixture.

items.title: Ticket/issue system — links, comments, attachments, inbox state
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Work & Task System). A first-class issue object: company/project/goal/parent links, threaded comments, documents, attachments, labels, inbox state — the work-tracking surface above Vanta goals + GOAL-DEPS. "It looks like a task manager."
items.done: Issues exist as first-class objects with goal/parent links, comments, attachments, and inbox state, viewable on a board.

items.title: Artifacts & work products — first-class agent outputs
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Artifacts & Work Products, roadmap ⚪). Make outputs first-class: generated artifacts, previews, deployable outputs, and the handoff from "agent did work" to "here is the result" — visible without reading the transcript.
items.done: A completed task carries linked work-product artifacts (files/preview/deploy) viewable without reading the transcript.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. The COFOUNDER-WORK-PRODUCTS store already carried provenance-tagged artifacts; the gap was (a) no live writer at the task level and (b) no per-task view. Closed both: work-products.ts gains bySourceTask (pure); `team` tool gains action:artifact (records a file/preview-url/deploy ref against a task — validates the task exists, producedBy=the task's worker, departmentId 'team', kind-aware) and action:artifacts (a task's linked artifacts WITHOUT reading the transcript); the tasks listing appends '· N artifact(s)' per task; `vanta library list --task <id>` filters the Library to one task (idFlag helper keeps the parser under the cx gate). team.ts dispatch went map-based (12 actions would have blown cx). 4 new test blocks + all existing library/work-products/team tests green untouched. REAL path executed via ALL_TOOLS + the real CLI: dispatch -> record code+asset artifacts -> artifacts view lists both -> tasks shows '2 artifact(s)' -> `vanta library list --task ship-1` renders both with provenance. Full suite 1026 files/11565 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Work queues — continuous routing for repeatable inputs
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Work Queues, roadmap ⚪). Queue-style work streams for repeatable inputs — support, triage, review, backlog intake — routed continuously to assigned agents instead of a one-off workflow per item.
items.done: A queue accepts repeated inputs and routes each to an assigned agent without a one-off workflow per item.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. queues/work-queue.ts: named queue configs (.vanta/work-queues/<name>.json — kebab-slug name, ASSIGNED workerId, instruction template with {input} substitution or append). Item storage/claiming REUSES the runner's atomic-claim primitives, generalized behavior-preserving with an optional QueueLoc subdir (runner defaults byte-identical, its 7 tests untouched); items live under .vanta/work-queues/<name>/{queued,running,done}. cli/queue-cmd.ts: `vanta queue define|push|work [--once]|list`; the work executor routes EVERY item through the real team path — assignTask to the queue's worker on the team ledger, then team-run doRun (subagent with the teammate model, kernel-gated, outcome/review gates apply) — no one-off workflow per item. 11 tests (config round-trip, slug rejection as error-value, template render, multi-item routing with per-queue isolation). LIVE real-path executed: defined worker ana, `queue define triage --template 'Reply with exactly: TRIAGED[{input}]'`, `queue push triage case-7`, `queue work triage --once` ran a REAL teammate subagent → queue item done + ledger task q-triage-…:done:TRIAGED[case-7]. Full suite 1025 files/11561 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Routines that create a tracked issue + wake the agent (catch-up policy)
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Routines & Schedules). Beyond Vanta cron: each routine run CREATES A TRACKED ISSUE and wakes the assigned agent, with cron/webhook/API triggers and concurrency + catch-up policies (so missed runs after downtime are handled).
items.done: A routine fires on schedule/webhook, creates a tracked issue, wakes its agent, and honors a catch-up policy after downtime.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. CronEntry gains `routine?: 'skip'|'once'` (presence = routine, value = catch-up policy; TSV col 7, legacy lines parse unchanged). ON FIRE: runner's routinePrefix creates a TRACKED ISSUE via the injected CreateIssue seam (live wiring = tickets/store createTicket, label 'routine', inbox unread — the existing first-class issue tracker, no new store) and the agent turn is prefixed '[tracked issue <id>]' so the run is traceable to it (composes with script modes; wired at BOTH call sites: `vanta cron` + the gateway tick, which also serves webhook-scheduled entries). CATCH-UP: pure hasMissedFire(cron, since, now) scans bounded (48h default, 7d hard cap); runDueTasksTracked extends the due set with routine-once entries that are NOT due now but missed a window since their last recorded fire — 'skip'/non-routines keep today's drop behavior; a never-fired routine has nothing to catch up; the catch-up advances the dedup map so it fires exactly once. `vanta schedule … --routine [skip|once]` (bare = skip). 7 new test blocks (missed-window detection incl. cap + strictly-after, issue+wake prefix, non-routine isolation, once-fires/skip-drops/never-fired, flag+TSV round-trip). LIVE real-path: scheduled a no_agent routine, `vanta cron` fired it → ticket tkt-d3ffb3e2 'Routine #2: nightly sync' landed unread + result carried the issue ref; state restored. Full suite 1027 files/11572 green; tsc + size gate clean (parseLine/runScheduleCommand cx refactored).

items.title: Operator activity feed — queryable timeline over events
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Activity & Events). An operator-facing, filterable timeline over events.jsonl: mutating actions, heartbeat state changes, cost events, approvals, comments, work products — "audit what happened and why" without reading raw jsonl.
items.done: /activity shows a filterable timeline of who/what/why across runs, not raw jsonl.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-06. activity/feed.ts (pure, read-only over the tamper-evident log — same contract as governance/audit.ts, no new store): parseActivity classifies every events.jsonl line into gate (tool + kernel verdict + final resolution = the WHY, parsed from the structured gate events PAPER-GOVERNANCE-AUDIT already emits) / tool (label-prefix convention, the WHO+WHAT) / note; filterActivity compiles present conditions into predicates (--who/--kind/--risk-or-resolution/--since/substring); parseSince (30m|2h|3d); formatActivity renders a newest-last timeline with resolution glyphs (✓/✗/⚠/·) and an explicit hidden-count header when capped (no silent truncation). repl/activity-cmd.ts: /activity slash with table-driven flag parsing (malformed --since is flagged, not ignored), wired into handlers + catalog. 7 test blocks (classification incl. malformed-line skip, every filter axis, since parsing, glyph/cap rendering, arg parsing). REAL-path verified against the repo's live events.jsonl (4900+ events): today's runner-test gate events render as 'shell_cmd [ask→denied] brew install cowsay' / '[block→blocked] rm -rf /tmp/x'. Full suite 1024 files/11557 green; tsc + size gate clean (both cx hits refactored table-driven).

items.title: Mobile control — review + approve + pause runs from a phone
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Mobile Ready). Monitor and manage autonomous runs from a phone — list active runs, read a work product, approve/pause — via the comms relay. Extends VANTA-CHANNEL-PERMISSIONS from notify-only to control.
items.done: From a phone channel you can list active runs, read a work product, and approve/pause — not just receive notifications.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-09: Added gateway mobile-control commands over any messaging PlatformAdapter, including local WebChat. Channel messages `/runs`, `/work <run-id>`, `/approve <request-id>`, and `/pause <run-id>` are consumed before they become agent turns. Runs are recorded in `.vanta/mobile-control.json` with title/status/work product; `/approve` routes through the existing pending-approval reply bus; `/pause` marks the run paused. Live evidence: drove WebChatAdapter with a phone-like chat id, ran a task, then sent `/runs`, `/work`, `/approve`, and `/pause`; replies included the run id/work product/approval result and the stored run status became paused.

items.title: Out-of-process plugin workers + capability-gated host services
items.summary: Enabled worker plugins run in separate Node permission-model processes, use newline-delimited protocol v1 to request operator-granted host services, schedule host-owned jobs, and contribute sanitized data-only TUI panels without importing worker code into Vanta's core process.
items.done: The installable operator-worker example runs under a different PID, receives a denial for an ungranted storage service, uses granted logging, schedules and executes a heartbeat job, and contributes a panel reachable from /plugin-panels. Grant/revoke/check commands expose the operator control path.
items.notes:
SHIPPED 2026-07-10: plugin manifests can declare a worker entry and requested capabilities. Operators explicitly grant or revoke log.write, storage.read, storage.write, schedule.jobs, and ui.panel. The host launches workers with Node's permission model, a sanitized environment, plugin-directory read only, no filesystem write or child-process permission, scoped JSON storage, unref'd host schedules, bounded startup, diagnostics, and lifecycle cleanup. Panels are schema-bounded, control-stripped, data-only contributions rendered through /plugin-panels. Existing in-process plugins remain supported. docs/plugin-workers.md and examples/plugin-worker define the v1 contract.

EXECUTED 2026-07-10: against a clean VANTA_HOME, the real CLI installed and enabled operator-worker, granted log.write + schedule.jobs + ui.panel, and ran `vanta plugin check operator-worker 1200`. Worker PID 87405 differed from the host, storage.write was denied, the Operator worker panel reported PID 87405, the host-fired heartbeat logged `heartbeat job ran`, check exited 0, and PID 87405 was gone afterward. Typecheck passed. Final full suite: 1189 files, 12414 passed, 3 skipped.

items.title: Scoped secret injection — secrets reach only the run that needs them
items.summary: Paperclip extract (Secrets & Storage). Instance + per-agent encrypted secrets injected into a run ONLY when that scoped run explicitly needs them — kept out of every other run's prompt and logs. Tightens Vanta's kernel secret handling with per-run scoping.
items.done: A run receives a named secret only when scoped to it; it never appears in the prompt or logs of other runs.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. secrets/scope.ts (pure model + tolerant store): SecretGrant {name, scopes[]} maps a secret name → the run scopes allowed to resolve it ("session"/"loop:<id>"/"agent:<id>", or "*" instance-wide). secretsForScope(grants, scope) returns ONLY the names a run may resolve — a secret granted to another scope is absent from this run's resolution set, so it never enters that run's prompt/logs (the value SHAPE is already handled by redactForLog/secret-scan; this decides EXPOSURE, the missing half). filterInjectable = the injection allowlist (a run is offered only its scoped candidates, never the instance's full set); isSecretInScope = the single-name gate; grantSecret merges scopes. Store at ~/.vanta/secret-scopes.json FAILS CLOSED: missing/corrupt → zero grants → nothing exposed (a scoping bug must under-expose, never over-expose). Sits ABOVE the existing use-time SecretProvider (decides WHICH names a scope may resolve before the provider fetches a value). 5 tests proving cross-run isolation (release-loop token withheld from other runs; * reaches all; filter offers only granted names; fail-closed load). Full suite 1036 files/11667 green; tsc + size gate clean. The gate + grant model is the deliverable; wiring it into a specific run launcher's secret injection is the follow-up (the provider abstraction it gates already exists).

items.title: Remote/sandboxed agent backends (e2b/cloud-VM) under the kernel
items.summary: Remote sandboxes are opt-in execution adapters for one owner on infrastructure or an account they control, never multi-tenancy or a vendor default. The shared backend port and workspace-aware Modal runner now have authenticated live proof under the kernel.
items.done: A run executes in a remote sandbox with the kernel enforcing scope identically to local, selected via the run-anywhere backend picker.
items.notes:
PARTIAL 2026-07-10: added the ExecBackendAdapter port; local, Docker, and serverless adapters; one resolver/registration point; injected fake-adapter coverage; Modal/Daytona CLI probes and structured command transport; fail-closed remote location intent; and setup picker choices. DECISIONS.md records the single-owner, user-controlled-account boundary.

MODAL RUNNER 2026-07-10: replaced the invalid generic `modal run -- <command>` shape with a bundled Modal local entrypoint backed by `modal.Sandbox.create/exec`. It uploads the actual command workdir with secret/build exclusions, runs in `/workspace`, enforces network-denied-by-default and timeout policy, streams output, propagates the remote exit code, and terminates the sandbox. Focused backend/serverless/Termux tests: 35 passed. Full suite: 1,197 files, 12,449 passed, 3 skipped. TypeScript, Python syntax, Modal 1.5.1 entrypoint parsing, architecture fitness, and the size gate pass. Modal 1.5.1 is installed locally.

AUTH PREFLIGHT 2026-07-10: added typed CLI/auth readiness results. Focused backend/adapter/consumer tests: 60 passed; focused doctor/status tests: 33 passed. Full suite after the doctor integration: 1,198 files, 12,456 passed, 3 skipped. A real local `wrapExec` call against the installed unauthenticated CLI returned `Modal CLI is installed but not authenticated; run modal token new --verify` and refused fallback; its sentinel command did not run. A real `vanta doctor --verbose` run showed the same fix in the notices section.

LIVE-PROOF COMMAND 2026-07-10: added `vanta backend status|verify`. Verify sends a fixed read-only action through the real safety kernel, wraps it through the selected serverless adapter, and accepts success only from a nonce-bound Linux receipt with the expected remote cwd and a SHA-256 match for the uploaded workspace manifest. Missing auth, replayed output, local execution, or upload drift exits non-zero; setup and docs point to the command. Executed unauthenticated path: status exited 1 with the Modal token fix; verify passed the kernel gate, exited 1 at adapter readiness, and explicitly refused local fallback. Focused proof/backend suite: 44 passed; combined backend/setup/status gate: 75 passed. TypeScript and size gates pass. Full suite: 1,204 files, 12,488 passed, 3 skipped; no leaked tsx stack or terminal bell.

SHIPPED LIVE 2026-07-10: Modal authentication became available. The first proof run exposed a 3.8 GB monorepo traversal before task creation; it was interrupted with zero tasks. Root cause was `.gitignore` negations disabling Modal directory pruning plus root-only cache patterns. The runner now drops ineffective negations, recursively excludes VCS/dependency/Vanta-state/build/cache/coverage trees, and the verifier uploads the smallest real project containing its fixture. Preflight reduced the proof workspace to 2,932 files / 12.7 MiB with pruning enabled. The repeated real `vanta backend verify` completed in 34 seconds and returned `modal · linux/x64 · /workspace · workspace hash matched · kernel allow`; Modal app `ap-8TtuvMOOO9sYoe2gTFYQbn` then reached `stopped` with zero tasks. Final gate: focused proof/backend suite 44 passed; TypeScript, Python syntax, and size gates pass; full suite 1,206 files, 12,498 passed, 3 skipped; no leaked tsx stack or terminal bell. This satisfies the bounded remote-sandbox card. It does NOT satisfy BACKEND-SERVERLESS-LIVE: no persistent gateway hibernate/wake cycle was executed.

items.title: Structured argv assessment + default-on sandbox for shell_cmd
items.summary: The kernel assess() is a substring denylist over the English describeForSafety string, which cannot fully contain a shell (obfuscation/interpreters/unlisted binaries evade it; confirmed in the 2026-06-20 pentest). The durable fix: pass structured argv + resolved paths to the kernel and allowlist, and make the sandbox the real boundary (default-on for shell_cmd/self_correct, not opt-in). Denylist additions shipped as defense-in-depth stop-gap.
items.done: shell_cmd is sandboxed by default (or its argv is allowlist-assessed); a reverse shell / unlisted destructive binary cannot run on the host without explicit human approval. SECURITY.md §6 caveat removed.

items.title: Per-install bearer token on kernel /api/* (local multi-user defense)
items.summary: Cross-origin browser CSRF is closed (Origin/Referer check + no wildcard CORS). A per-install 0600 bearer token on /api/* would additionally defend against other LOCAL processes/users on a shared host (the loopback API is currently reachable by any local process). Token injected into the cockpit HTML + sent by the TS safety-client.
items.done: Mutating /api/* requires a per-install bearer token; cockpit + TS client supply it; a local process without the token gets 401.

items.title: Extend SSRF guard to reach/twitter + reach/podcast fetchers
items.summary: net/ssrf-guard.ts assertPublicUrl is wired into web_fetch, MCP http transport, and reach rss/reddit. reach/twitter.ts and reach/podcast.ts also fetch remote URLs and were out of the first guard pass.
items.done: Both channels call assertPublicUrl before fetch; a metadata/loopback/private URL is refused; tests cover it.

items.title: Spawn MCP stdio servers with a minimal env, not the full parent env
items.summary: mcp/mount.ts resolveTransport spreads {...process.env, ...spec.env} into stdio MCP children, handing every operator secret to a (trust-gated) third-party binary. Scope it to PATH + explicit spec.env.
items.done: stdio MCP children receive only PATH + declared spec.env; a spawned server cannot read unrelated secrets from the environment.

items.title: Pin the winnow git dependency to a commit SHA
items.summary: vanta-ts/package.json depends on github:jpoindexter/winnow from main with no commit pin (mutable, unaudited vs a registry). Used in a hot tool-output path. Pin to a commit SHA or tag.
items.done: winnow is pinned to an immutable ref (commit SHA or tag); npm ci resolves it deterministically.

items.title: Length-unit-aware message splitting (chars/utf16/bytes)
items.summary: Adapters send reply text raw with no splitting — a >4096-char Telegram or >2000 Discord reply fails outright. Add a shared splitForLimit(text,limit,lenUnit) that breaks on newline boundaries under a per-platform budget, hard-splits only past limit/2, and counts UTF-16 code units (Telegram) or UTF-8 bytes (IRC) per adapter.
items.done: A long agent reply is split into multiple sends under each adapter's real char budget; Telegram counts UTF-16, IRC counts bytes; tested with multi-segment fixtures across Telegram/Signal/IRC/Mattermost.

items.title: Per-platform markdown degradation (protect-code-then-convert)
items.summary: Agent emits standard markdown; thin adapters send it raw so Telegram/IRC/Signal show literal ** and ```. Add a formatMessage hook per adapter that first placeholder-protects fenced/inline code, then converts to the platform dialect (Signal bodyRanges, Slack mrkdwn, WhatsApp *_~, IRC strip).
items.done: Each live adapter implements formatMessage; code blocks survive intact; Telegram/Signal/IRC render correctly-formatted output; per-dialect unit tests pass.

items.title: Markdown table -> mobile-readable bullets
items.summary: GFM pipe tables render as backslash-pipe noise on chat platforms. Detect a table block (header + :---: separator + rows) and reformat each row to a bold heading + bulleted key:value list, with row-label-column detection. Pure function reused across text-only adapters.
items.done: A markdown table in an agent reply renders as readable bold-heading + bullet lists on text-only platforms; pure-function unit tests cover header/separator/row detection.

items.title: Typing-indicator heartbeat, pausable during approval
items.summary: Agent thinks silently for tens of seconds with no chat feedback. Add a sendTyping(chatId) adapter method + a ~2s refresh loop around the turn (each send bounded by ~1.5s timeout), pausable while a kernel approval is pending so the user can still type /approve.
items.done: While the agent works, the chat shows a typing indicator refreshed every ~2s; it pauses during a pending approval; resumes after; bounded per-send timeout prevents a hung send killing it.
items.notes: Pure immutable TypingState{active,pausedForApproval,lastSentMs} + startTyping(nowMs) (active, stamps lastSentMs=now so the caller sends the opening tick) + stopTyping (inactive + pause cleared, idempotent) + pauseTypingForApproval/resumeTypingAfterApproval (toggle, active+lastSentMs preserved) + nextTypingTick(state,nowMs,intervalMs=5000) -> {state,shouldSend} (send ONLY when active AND !paused AND nowMs-lastSentMs>=interval; on send lastSentMs:=nowMs; else unchanged nothing sent) + typingHeartbeatEnabled (VANTA_TYPING_INDICATOR default ON, =0 disables). KEY BEHAVIOR (tested): a tick while pausedForApproval sends NOTHING even far past the interval — no typing while Vanta is blocked waiting on a human; resume returns ticks. Interval 5000ms under Telegram ~6s typing expiry. Built+tested; wire = pollPlatform startTyping when composing -> timer nextTypingTick -> a NEW optional PlatformAdapter.sendTyping (Telegram sendChatAction typing) when shouldSend -> wrap requestApproval with pause/resume -> stopTyping on reply sent (named, sendTyping = boundary). Not active = nothing.

items.title: Intentional-silence NO_REPLY token for group chats
items.summary: An always-replying bot is annoying in group/channel surfaces. Allow the agent to return an exact whole-response marker (NO_REPLY/[SILENT], <=64 chars) to choose not to reply; prose merely mentioning the token still delivers; blank stays an error.
items.done: Returning exactly NO_REPLY suppresses delivery in a group; a reply that contains the word NO_REPLY in prose still sends; boundary unit-tested.

items.title: Quoted-reply context recovery (message_id -> text store)
items.summary: When a user replies to a prior agent message the agent can't see what it's replying to. Add a capped, atomic-write message_id->text index written at send time, looked up by reply_to_id on inbound, re-injected as quote context. Mirrors the existing ~/.vanta/*.json store pattern.
items.done: Replying to a past agent message injects that message's text as quoted context into the next turn; store is capped and atomically written; degrades to no-op on miss.

items.title: Inbound message timestamp prefixing (idempotent)
items.summary: Inbound messages carry no time, so the agent has no temporal grounding for scheduling/reminders. Prefix LLM-context content with [Tue 2026-04-28 13:40 CEST] but keep persisted content clean; strip existing prefixes before adding so replay never accumulates duplicates (closest-to-text prefix wins).
items.done: Inbound chat turns get a human timestamp in context only; persisted history stays prefix-free; re-processing never stacks [ts][ts]; unit-tested for idempotency.

items.title: Self-message / reconnect-replay dedup (reply-loop guard)
items.summary: A bot that processes its own echoed-back sends or replayed reconnect events can infinite-loop. Add a base-class self-id check plus an inbound seen-message-id set so own outbound, platform echoes, and RESUME/reconnect replays are filtered.
items.done: Own outbound echoed back is ignored; duplicate events after a reconnect are deduped by message id; a synthetic echo/replay does not trigger a second agent turn.

items.title: Require-mention gate + self-mention stripping in groups
items.summary: In shared channels (IRC/Mattermost/Telegram groups) a bot that answers every message is unusable. Add a per-chat require-mention gate (respond only when @-mentioned, replied-to, or message starts with /) and strip the bot's @handle from text before the agent sees it.
items.done: In a configured group, the agent replies only when mentioned/replied-to; the @handle is removed from the text reaching the model; allowlist + mention modes unit-tested.

items.title: Proactive send_message tool (out-of-process capable)
items.summary: The gateway only replies in-band; the agent can't proactively message a chat. Add a kernel-gated send_message(platform,chatId,text) tool over the adapter registry that routes through standalone per-platform senders so cron/loop wakes can deliver without the gateway running.
items.done: The agent can call send_message to push a message to a configured platform/chat; works when invoked from a cron/loop context with no live gateway; kernel-gated and tested.

items.title: Telegram flood-control retry + forum-topic routing
items.summary: The basic Telegram adapter skips real edge cases. Add RetryAfter flood-control retry with backoff, forum/DM topic routing via message_thread_id, and disable_web_page_preview to avoid unfurl spam.
items.done: A 429 RetryAfter triggers a backoff-retry instead of a dropped send; replies route to the correct forum topic; link previews are suppressed; covered by adapter tests.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-06. telegram.ts: (1) 429 flood-control backoff-retry — parseRetryAfter reads Telegram's {error_code:429, parameters.retry_after} shape (capped 30s, default 1s), sendPart retries up to 3 attempts with an injected sleep seam, the retried send's message id is still recorded for the reply-context store; sustained flood gives up bounded, never hangs. (2) Forum-topic routing — inbound getUpdates parses message_thread_id + is_topic_message; threadId is set ONLY for real topic messages (message_thread_id also rides on plain replies where echoing it back would be wrong — tested); Inbound/OutboundMessage gain optional threadId (additive, 46-caller blast radius untouched) and run-session.ts copies it inbound->reply GENERICALLY (any adapter can adopt the field); sends include message_thread_id when set. (3) link_preview_options:{is_disabled:true} on every send (Bot API 7+ shape). 7 new adapter tests (fetch-stubbed send path: preview suppression, topic routing, plain-send omission, retry-then-succeed with sleep assertion + id recovery, bounded give-up, topic-vs-plain-reply parse). Full suite 1023 files/11550 green; tsc + size gate clean. Live Telegram not exercised (needs VANTA_TELEGRAM_TOKEN) — the fetch-level request bodies are asserted byte-exact instead.

items.title: Inline-keyboard Approve/Deny for kernel approvals (Telegram)
items.summary: Over chat, kernel approvals are plain-text only (type /approve). Render a pending approval as a tappable Approve/Deny inline keyboard on Telegram (shared callback-id convention), degrading gracefully to text on adapters that don't override. Reuses the existing permissions/request model.
items.done: A tool-call approval appears as Approve/Deny buttons in Telegram; tapping resolves the kernel approval; other adapters still show text; callback resolution unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07, directly on the CHANNEL-PERMISSIONS-WIRE relay shipped the same day. KEY DESIGN: a button's callback data IS the 'yes <id>'/'no <id>' reply text, so a tap flows through the exact same reply bus + relay race + allowlist as a typed reply — zero new approval logic, and the ALLOWLIST applies to taps identically (callback chatId = the approver chat). OutboundMessage gains optional buttons[] (label+data; adapters without native buttons ignore them and the prompt text still carries the typed instruction — the graceful text degrade the card requires). channel-approver sends ✅ Approve/❌ Deny buttons with every prompt. telegram.ts: send() attaches reply_markup.inline_keyboard when buttons present (verified exact body shape); parseUpdates handles callback_query updates via extracted callbackInbound (data -> inbound text, id 'cb-<id>', update_id advances the offset) and poll() ACKS every callback via answerCallbackQuery (best-effort) so the button spinner clears. 3 new test blocks (callback->inbound mapping, keyboard body shape + plain-send omission, poll ack) + the existing relay/bus race tests cover tap resolution end-to-end (a tap is literally a bus reply). Full suite 1028 files/11582 green; tsc + size gate clean (parseUpdates split into callbackInbound/messageInbound). Live tap not exercised (needs real Telegram + human); the request/response bodies are asserted byte-exact at the fetch boundary.

items.title: Per-platform system-prompt formatting hints
items.summary: The prompt is platform-agnostic so the agent over-emits markdown for plain surfaces. Inject a one-line hint per active gateway session ("on IRC — no markdown, keep lines short" / "Telegram supports MarkdownV2") into the context tier so the agent adapts formatting up front.
items.done: When a gateway session is active, a platform hint is folded into context; the agent emits less markdown on IRC than on Telegram; hint selection unit-tested.

items.title: Adapter capability interface (limits declared, not guessed)
items.summary: The static platform catalog doesn't drive rendering, so splitting/format paths hard-guess 4096/chars. Add an AdapterCapabilities interface (charLimit, lenUnit, supportsEdit/threads, markdownDialect) read by the send/split path so each adapter declares its real limits and consumers degrade automatically.
items.done: Send/split logic reads capabilities off the live adapter rather than constants; an adapter that declares no-edit degrades to one-message-per-segment; interface unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. gateway/platforms/capabilities.ts: AdapterCapabilities {charLimit, lenUnit, supportsEdit, supportsThreads, markdownDialect} + DEFAULT_CAPABILITIES (conservative: chars/4000/no-edit/plain) + capabilities(over) builder + resolveCapabilities(adapter) (declared-or-default) + segmentsFor(text, caps) (the send-path seam wrapping splitForLimit with the adapter's real budget+unit) + deliveryMode (supportsEdit → 'edit-in-place' | 'one-message-per-segment' — the card's no-edit degrade). PlatformAdapter gains an optional readonly capabilities; an adapter that omits it keeps the safe defaults (all 22 unchanged). Converted 3 reference adapters to DECLARE + read off the instance instead of hardcoded constants at the split call: telegram (4096/utf16/threads/telegram-dialect), webchat (4000/chars/markdown), sms (SMS_TEXT_LIMIT/chars/plain) — each now `segmentsFor(formatted, this.capabilities)`. 10 capability tests (resolve declared-vs-default, real TelegramAdapter instance read, utf16 astral counting, budget split never over-limit, no-edit→one-message-per-segment degrade) + the 3 adapters' existing send tests green untouched. Full suite 1032 files/11624 green; tsc + size gate clean. The remaining ~19 adapters inherit the defaults until they declare — the interface + seam is the deliverable; per-adapter declarations are mechanical follow-ups.

items.title: Inbound media: image->vision, voice->STT plumbing
items.summary: Adapters are text-only in/out. A media layer would cache inbound audio/image/doc (path-validated, TTL) and surface them (image->vision, voice->transcription). Smallest high-leverage entry: inbound image -> existing vision tools; full voice/STT/outbound-media is a large vertical.
items.done: An inbound image is routed to the existing vision describe path; cached under a validated dir with TTL; voice/STT and outbound media deferred to a later slice.
items.notes: Scope folds into MSG-MEDIA-IMAGES (beta) — the inbound image->vision / voice->STT plumbing belongs to that card.

items.title: Outbound media path validation with recency constraint
items.summary: If/when the agent sends local files, validate the path is under allowed roots, not under denied prefixes, AND recently produced — so the agent can't be tricked into exfiltrating an arbitrary old file. Route through the existing kernel assess()/writable-zones; the recency constraint is the new idea.
items.done: A file send is rejected unless the path is in-scope and recently produced; enforced via the kernel; anti-exfil case unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. gateway/media-send-guard.ts: validateMediaSend(rawPath, opts) gates sending a LOCAL file out over a channel. Two checks in order: (1) SCOPE — reuses resolveReadablePath (the exact policy read_file uses: expand ~, in-root/zone, protected-credential + out-of-zone refused with its message); (2) RECENCY — the new idea: mtime must be within a window (VANTA_MEDIA_MAX_AGE_SEC, default 1h) so even an in-scope file can't be exfiltrated if the agent is tricked into naming an arbitrary OLD file — a legit send is of something Vanta JUST produced (screenshot/chart/report). Scope is checked FIRST (no stat on a path we'd refuse anyway; error names the right reason). stat injected (fsStatMtime live adapter → mtimeMs or null). Errors-as-values, never throws; the caller still routes the eventual read through the kernel. 7 tests incl. the anti-exfil case (in-scope but 2h-old → rejected), out-of-scope-refused-before-stat, protected-cred path, null-mtime, custom window. Full suite 1040 files/11697 green; tsc + size gate clean. Guard is ready for whenever a file-send tool lands (OutboundMessage.image exists; no live send tool yet — the recency guard is the shippable anti-exfil primitive the card scopes).

items.title: Signal attachment token-bucket + multi-shape 429 detection
items.summary: Signal send rate limiting is live: `SignalAdapter.send` acquires from a process-local token bucket before each send part and feeds back known signal-cli 429 shapes so later sends back off.
items.done: `src/gateway/platforms/signal-rate-limit.ts` detects `[429]`, `RateLimitException`, `RetryLaterException`, and JSON-RPC code 429; `SignalRateLimiter` enforces burst + backoff; `SignalAdapter.send` uses both. Media sends can reuse the same helper when outbound media exists.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: added SignalRateLimiter plus multi-shape 429 parser and wired it into SignalAdapter.send before each split message part. Focused proof: npx vitest run src/gateway/platforms/signal-rate-limit.test.ts src/gateway/platforms/signal.test.ts src/gateway/platforms/factory.test.ts (40 passed). Typecheck passed.

items.title: Sibling-adapter behavior-mixin pattern
items.summary: Telegram now has an explicit shared receive-behavior layer used by both getUpdates polling and authenticated webhook push. The transports still own their HTTP edges, while parsing, callback mapping, and allowlist gating live in one reusable behavior module for future sibling transports.
items.done: Two transports for one platform share one behavior mixin; each owns transport; demonstrated when a second transport lands. Shipped through Telegram polling + webhook receive behavior.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: Added src/gateway/platforms/telegram-behavior.ts with TelegramReceiveBehavior, parseUpdates, parseAllowlist, and matchesWebhookSecret. TelegramAdapter now routes both getUpdates polling and webhook-drained updates through the same parse/filter layer, while webhookHandlers owns only authenticated HTTP receipt and sendPart still owns Bot API delivery. Focused proof: npx vitest run src/gateway/platforms/telegram.test.ts src/gateway/platforms/telegram-webhook.test.ts (24 passed), plus npm run typecheck.

items.title: Mid-turn progress bubble for token-window platforms
items.summary: Platforms with a hard reply-token window (LINE 60s, WhatsApp 24h) need a 'still working…' message injected at a threshold before the token expires. Depends on the typing-heartbeat existing; only matters for those platforms.
items.done: On a token-window platform a progress message is sent before the reply token expires; layered on the typing heartbeat.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-09. Added a token-window progress bubble on the gateway platform session path. `progressBubbleForPlatform` defaults on for LINE before its reply-token window expires and WhatsApp before the 24h response window; other platforms stay off unless explicitly configured. While an inbound platform turn is waiting on `handle()`, Vanta schedules one “Still working” outbound message and cancels it if the final reply finishes before the threshold; the final reply path remains unchanged. Verified with `npx vitest run src/gateway/progress-bubble.test.ts src/gateway/run.test.ts src/gateway/typing-heartbeat.test.ts --reporter=dot` (38 passed), `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run vanta -- lint src/gateway/progress-bubble.ts src/gateway/progress-bubble.test.ts src/gateway/run.ts src/gateway/run-session.ts src/gateway/run.test.ts`.

items.title: Categorized provider-error taxonomy -> typed recovery hints
items.summary: Provider-error handling is one coarse retryable-vs-auth regex. Add classifyProviderError() returning {reason, retryable, shouldCompress, shouldRotate, shouldFallback} over ~20 categories (auth, billing, rate_limit, overloaded, server_error, timeout, context_overflow, payload_too_large, image_too_large, content_policy, etc.) so retry acts on the verdict instead of re-parsing.
items.done: A provider error is classified into a typed reason with recovery booleans; the retry loop compresses vs rotates vs falls back per the verdict; billing-exhaustion is distinguished from transient rate-limit; pattern banks unit-tested.

items.title: Compaction tool-pair boundary alignment + anti-thrash
items.summary: Compaction can split a tool_call from its tool_result (a correctness bug) and can thrash. Align compaction boundaries so call/result pairs stay together, protect first-N + token-budgeted tail, and skip compression when the last two passes each saved <10%.
items.done: Compaction never orphans a tool_call/tool_result; head and tail are protected; a low-savings pass is skipped; boundary + anti-thrash unit-tested.

items.title: Informative per-tool prune summaries
items.summary: Pruned tool outputs become generic '[truncated: N chars]', losing signal. Add summarizeToolResult(name,args,content) producing typed one-liners that keep the signal: '[terminal] ran npm test -> exit 0, 47 lines', '[search] 12 matches for X', '[read] config.ts from line 1 (1,200 chars)'.
items.done: Compacted history shows tool-aware one-liners preserving exit codes/match counts instead of char-truncation; per-tool extractors with a generic fallback, unit-tested.

items.title: Canonical multimodal message-text flattener
items.summary: Multiple code paths (titling, logging, assertion-judging, brain ingest) need the visible text of any provider message shape. Add flattenMessageText(content) that returns text from str | parts-list | object, skipping image/audio parts and probing the common text keys.
items.done: A single helper returns the text of any chat/Responses content shape, skipping media parts; reused by titling/logging/assertions; unit-tested across shapes.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. agent/flatten-text.ts: flattenMessageText(content: unknown) → the visible text of ANY provider content shape (string | parts-array | object), pure/total/never-throws. Skips media (image/image_url/input_image/audio/input_audio/file/tool_use/tool_result parts), probes the common text keys (text/content/value/input_text/output_text), and RECURSES into a nested parts array under a content key (Anthropic/Responses block shape). Wired into the two concrete sites that previously DROPPED non-string content: sessions/auto-title.ts (formatLine was 'content' in message ? message.content : '') and sessions/search.ts (was typeof===string ? content : '') — both now flatten parts arrays instead of losing them, a real behavior improvement for titling/search over multimodal turns. 7 tests across every shape (string, text-parts join, media-skip, object text-key probe, nested content recursion, null/number/unknown→'', bare-string element). Full suite 1039 files/11690 green; tsc + size gate clean. The helper is exported for the remaining named consumers (logging/assertion-judging/brain-ingest) to adopt as they touch raw provider payloads.

items.title: Strip historical media on compaction + shrink-on-413 retry
items.summary: Vision-to-action screenshots bloat context and cause image_too_large/413 failures. Strip historical media on compaction so old screenshots stop costing tokens, and on a 413/image_too_large shrink or strip oversized image parts in-place and retry instead of failing the turn.
items.done: Old screenshots are dropped from compacted history; a 413 image error triggers an in-place shrink-and-retry rather than a turn failure; both paths unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. agent/image-recovery.ts (pure): stripHistoricalImages(messages, keepLast=1) drops the images field (text intact) from all but the most-recent image-bearing message — wired into context.ts compactConversation so post-compaction historical screenshots stop costing tokens (bug caught+fixed pre-ship: slice(-0) returns the WHOLE array, so keepLast=0 short-circuits to keep nothing). stripAllImages removes every image + leaves a '[image omitted — too large]' breadcrumb per affected message (never silently lost), returning the stripped count. 413/IMAGE RETRY: provider-call.ts getCompletionWithContextRetry now routes each attempt error through handleAttemptError (extracted for the cx gate) → on a classifyProviderError image_too_large/payload_too_large verdict with images present, stripAllImages + retry ONCE as text instead of failing the turn; no images → falls through to the normal transient/fail-fast path. 7 tests (keep-most-recent, keepLast=0 strips all, no-op, strip-all breadcrumb incl. empty-content message, stripped=0 skip) + existing context/provider-call suites green. Full suite 1041 files/11702 green; tsc + size gate clean. Reuses the pre-existing error-taxonomy image_too_large/payload_too_large classes (were retryable:false / compress-only — now actually recovered in-place).

items.title: Cursor + long-poll reader over jsonl event stores
items.summary: Event stores (loop wake-events, etc.) are poll-only. Add a generic cursor-based reader with a blocking long-poll (timeout_ms, capped ~5min) so the gateway, an editor adapter, and the desktop renderer get near-real-time delivery without busy-polling.
items.done: An external consumer reads new events by cursor and can long-poll for the next one with a capped timeout; reused across at least two surfaces; unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. events/cursor-reader.ts (pure core + injected source/sleep): readSince(lines, cursor) returns new lines after a line-index cursor + the advanced cursor (a cursor past the end resets to the end — no replay after truncation/rotation); longPollEvents blocks until an event appears after the cursor OR the timeout elapses (clamped to MAX_WAIT_MS ~5min, matching the kernel ceiling), returns immediately when events are already pending, honors an abort signal, errors-as-values (a failing read = no-new-events this tick, never throws), and returns the preserved cursor on timeout (retry without miss/replay). fileEventSource(path) is the live JSONL adapter (non-empty lines; missing → []). Fully deterministic-testable via an injected clock — 8 tests (cursor advance, past-end reset, immediate-return, blocks-then-delivers, timeout-preserves-cursor, abort, MAX_WAIT_MS cap, file adapter). The generic engine + adapter is the reusable primitive the gateway / editor adapter / desktop renderer drop in (longPollEvents(fileEventSource(dataDir/events.jsonl)) — near-real-time delivery, no busy-poll); concrete per-surface wiring is a thin follow-up over this API. Full suite 1050 files/11793 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Cron at-most-once: pre-advance + per-fire dedup
items.summary: Durable-cron persists schedule defs only; the tick path can double-fire or storm on restart. Before running a recurring job pre-advance next_run_at (crash mid-run = skip-one not storm), add a per-fire dedup marker, and fast-forward stale runs to avoid missed-job bursts after downtime.
items.done: A crash during a cron tick yields at-most-one fire on restart, not a storm; a duplicate tick in the same window is deduped; stale runs fast-forward; unit-tested.

items.title: No-agent / script cron mode (no-LLM watchdog)
items.summary: Cron entries always force an agent turn. Add two cheaper modes: a script job whose stdout is injected as prompt context (change-detection), and a no_agent job where the script IS the job and stdout is delivered verbatim (classic watchdog, no LLM cost).
items.done: A no_agent cron job runs a script and delivers its stdout with no model call; a script-context job injects stdout into the agent turn; both modes unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-06. CronEntry gains optional mode ('no_agent'|'script_context') + script (TSV columns 5/6; absent = classic agent turn — legacy 4-column lines verified parseable, all pre-existing cron tests untouched). schedule/script-run.ts: runCronScript via `sh -c` with a hard timeout (VANTA_CRON_SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_SEC, default 60s), 20k output cap, errors-as-values (non-zero exit / timeout / spawn failure all return {ok:false} — never throws); precedent for direct script exec is the hooks engine (owner-configured = consent; agent-mode cron still kernel-gates every tool call). runner.ts runOne branches via runScriptMode with the executor INJECTED (RunDueTasksOptions.runScript) so schedule stays decoupled per the folder contract; missing runner or missing script = explicit error result, never a silent agent run. no_agent falls back to instruction-as-script. Wired at both call sites: `vanta cron` (commands.ts) + the gateway tick (gateway/run.ts).
