CLI: `vanta schedule "…" --cron "…" --mode no_agent|script_context --script "cmd"` with invalid-mode and script_context-needs-script rejections. 17 new tests (round-trip + back-compat, both modes through the runner incl. no-model-call assertions, flag parsing, real-exec timeout kill). LIVE real-path verified: scheduled a no_agent df-h entry, `vanta cron` delivered real stdout with zero model involvement, state restored. Full suite 1021 files/11526 green; tsc + size gate clean (runOne cx fixed by extracting runScriptMode).

items.title: Self-scheduling skills (cron in skill frontmatter)
items.summary: Skills, cron, and hooks are separate. Let a skill's frontmatter optionally declare a cron expression so loading the skill registers a recurring job via the existing scheduler — skills become self-scheduling automations with no new subsystem.
items.done: A skill with a schedule field in frontmatter registers a cron job on load and unregisters on unload; wired to the existing cron; unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. A skill's frontmatter `schedule: <cron>` self-registers a recurring job on the EXISTING durable scheduler — no new subsystem. cron.ts gains a real isValidCron (uses its own field parser — first probe attempt via time-sweep was buggy: hour-step 6 missed 09:00 and couldn't cover dom/dow, so validity moved to the parser where it belongs). frontmatter.ts gains readSkillFrontmatter (raw key→string record; SkillMeta is closed + drops `schedule`, same reason conditional-activate mines raw). skills/scheduled.ts: parseSkillSchedule, skillCronInstruction/skillNameFromInstruction (SKILL_CRON_PREFIX tags skill-owned crons), reconcileSkillCrons (PURE: add new/changed, remove skill-owned crons whose skill unloaded or re-timed, NEVER touch hand-added jobs), loadScheduledSkills (reads raw SKILL.md files), syncSkillCrons (applies the reconcile to the durable-cron store). Wired at interactive session start (loadSkillsWithCronSync, best-effort) so load = register, unload = unregister on the next sync. 13 tests incl. a LIVE temp-skills-dir round-trip (write a scheduled skill → sync registers it → rm the skill → sync unregisters it) + reconcile keeps hand-added crons. Full suite 1042 files/11710 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Mount-time warn for shell-interpreter MCP egress
items.summary: User-added MCP servers aren't vetted at mount. Add an advisory warning (not a block) when a mounted MCP server is a shell interpreter (bash/pwsh) whose inline args invoke network egress (curl/wget/nc//dev/tcp/Invoke-WebRequest), upgraded if exfil-shaped (--data-binary, .env, POST). Complements the sandbox.
items.done: Mounting an MCP server that pipes a shell interpreter to egress tooling prints a clear advisory warning; benign servers are silent; heuristic unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04. mcp/egress-warn.ts (pure) detectMcpEgressRisk(command,args): risky when a download pipes into a shell (curl … | bash), a shell interpreter (sh/bash/zsh/dash/ash/ksh/csh) reaches the network (curl/wget/nc/ncat/telnet/scp/sftp/ftp/ssh), or the server command IS a bare egress binary. Benign servers (node server.js / python -m mcp / npx @vendor/mcp) are silent; word-boundary matching so 'curly-server.js' doesn't trip 'curl'. formatEgressWarning notes the kernel still gates each tool call. Wired as an advisory (never a block) in mount.ts mountOneServer before spawn. Verified: 10 unit tests (4 risky shapes, 5 benign, 1 formatter) + mount tests (17) regression-clean; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Typed gateway stream-event vocabulary (transport vs context)
items.summary: Gateway delivery uses typed platform-agnostic MessageChunk, Commentary, and MessageStop events. One sink keeps chunks/commentary transient and makes the canonical stop the only delivered and reply-context-recorded text, preventing transport/history races.
items.done: The real gateway runner emits typed MessageChunk and Commentary events into one sink; MessageStop is delivered exactly once and recorded identically. Divergent streamed drafts fail closed to the canonical stop, with automated and live provider/kernel proof.
items.notes:
SHIPPED 2026-07-10: buildCronRunTask now exposes per-run typed callbacks; buildGatewayHandle maps provider deltas and agent activity to MessageChunk/Commentary; run-session routes both through createGatewayStreamSink and delivers only MessageStop. Commentary never enters delivery or reply context. If accumulated chunks differ from the canonical final reply, the sink logs drift and sends/stores only the canonical stop.

EXECUTED 2026-07-10: a configured provider/kernel gateway turn emitted 5 MessageChunk events, delivered GATEWAY_STREAM_OK exactly once, and stored the identical text under the outbound reply id. Focused sink tests cover exact, commentary, and divergent-draft cases. Final full suite: 1184 files, 12401 passed, 3 skipped.

items.title: One-shot-per-job scale-to-zero cron + reconcile
items.summary: Beyond the OS-scheduler tick, the strong pattern is arming exactly one external one-shot per job at its real next-fire time, waking the (stopped) agent via an authed webhook, then re-arming — with at-most-once via store compare-and-set and a desired-vs-armed reconcile on boot. Keep the CAS+reconcile idea; the hosted machinery is out of scope for local-first.
items.done: Captured as a hardening reference for vanta cron; the store-CAS + reconcile pattern noted for the cron ledger.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04. The store-CAS + reconcile pattern made real on the cron ledger (hosted one-shot machinery correctly out of scope). schedule/cron-cas.ts adds claimFire() — an atomic O_EXCL per-(taskId,windowKey) claim (same primitive as team/checkout.ts) giving cross-PROCESS at-most-once, closing the double-run race the in-process at-most-once.ts map can't (gateway tick + manual `vanta cron run`, or a launchd double-invoke, both read cron-fired.json as not-fired and both fire); fails soft toward firing on non-EEXIST io error so a broken FS never silently drops a due fire. sweepClaims() prunes old-window claim files. at-most-once.ts gains pure reconcileWindow() (desired-vs-armed split on boot). runner.ts takes an optional claim dep (fire iff shouldFire AND claim wins; omitted = byte-identical), wired live into schedule/commands.ts (`vanta cron`) + gateway/run.ts. Verified: 24-way concurrent claim -> exactly 1 winner; 40 schedule tests + 738 gateway/schedule-command tests green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Scheduler-provider seam (additive-only ABC)
items.summary: Scheduler-provider seam is live: due-task selection is separated from execution through an optional provider, and provider failure falls back to the built-in cron ticker so Vanta is never left without a trigger source.
items.done: `src/schedule/provider.ts` defines the SchedulerProvider seam, builtin cron selector, additive growth hook names, and fallback resolver; `runDueTasksTracked` uses the resolver before executing tasks. Full alternative schedulers remain deferred until a second trigger needs one.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: added src/schedule/provider.ts and provider tests; wired runDueTasksTracked through resolveSchedulerDue with fallback to builtin-cron on provider failure. Focused proof: npx vitest run src/schedule/provider.test.ts src/schedule/runner.test.ts src/schedule/commands.test.ts (29 passed). Typecheck passed.

items.title: Skill prereq/env gating + injection-scan on bodies
items.summary: Mine two ideas if absent: offer-time gating on OS + runtime env + declared prerequisites (env_vars/commands) with setup.collect_secrets, and a prompt-injection pattern scan on skill/plugin content before load (security-relevant given the trusted-operator posture).
items.done: Skills declare prerequisites and are gated by platform/env at offer time; skill bodies are injection-scanned before load; both unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07; tightened 2026-07-09. skills/gating.ts (pure): (1) OFFER-TIME GATING — parseSkillPrereqs reads platforms/env_vars/commands from a skill's raw frontmatter (list or comma-string; accepts platform/requiresEnv/requiresCommands aliases); gateSkill(prereqs, {platform, env, hasCommand}) offers a skill only where its OS matches + required env vars are set + required commands are on PATH, first-unmet-is-the-reason. (2) INJECTION SCAN — scanForInjection(text) matches 6 prompt-injection pattern banks (override-instructions, reveal-system-prompt, role-hijack, exfil-secrets, hidden zero-width/bidi control chars, fake-tool-call incl. curl|sh & rm -rf) returning {clean, hits}, tuned to flag intent not incidental prose. WIRED: store.ts scans every SKILL.md before parse. CORRECTED: trusted local skills now load quietly by default because the operator's own security-topic skills legitimately quote hostile phrases; VANTA_SKILL_STRICT=1 still hard-skips flagged skills. NEW 2026-07-09: /skills audit explicitly reports injection-scan hits on demand, and the store/read path typecheck regression was fixed. Verified with focused skill + slash tests, full TS suite (1070 files / 11979 pass / 3 skipped), tsc, cargo test, size gate, and git diff check.

items.title: Harvest jailbreak signatures as kernel detection inputs
items.summary: Defensive-only: the obfuscation/refusal-suppression signatures used by jailbreak tooling are useful as DETECTION inputs for the kernel safety denylist (recognize these attacks against Vanta itself), never as capability. Low priority.
items.done: A set of jailbreak/obfuscation signatures is available to the kernel safety layer as detection patterns; capability use explicitly excluded.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. DEFENSIVE-ONLY. prompt/jailbreak-signatures.ts (pure, zero capability — only regexes that FLAG attack shapes, no bypass/how-to): detectJailbreak(text) → {flagged, signatures[]} over 6 signature banks — persona-unlock (DAN/dev-mode/god-mode/jailbreak), refusal-suppression (no restrictions/bypass safety/disable filter), unbounded-claim (you have no limits/are free), policy-void (pretend no policy applies), encoded-payload (base64/rot13 decode-and-run smuggling), sim-launder (fictional/roleplay-with-no-rules framing). Tuned to flag intent, not ordinary prose ('the game has rules and a hard mode' stays clean). WIRED as a detection input: folded into skills/gating.ts scanForInjection (jailbreak hits prefixed 'jailbreak:'), so a jailbreak-tainted skill/plugin body is flagged on load through the same path — available to the safety layer as detection, never a capability. 11 tests (each signature, benign negatives, id set, folded-scan flags a tainted body). Full suite 1049 files/11785 green; tsc + size gate clean. Capability use explicitly excluded, per the card.

items.title: Structural secret redaction + logging formatter
items.summary: The redaction rule-list misses structural secrets in logged tool I/O. Add patterns for URL query/userinfo, Authorization headers, JWTs, DB connection strings, and a logger-level redacting formatter that scrubs every emitted line (each regex gated behind a cheap substring pre-check).
items.done: A logged URL with ?token=, an auth header, and a connection string are all redacted at emit time by a logging formatter; structural patterns unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04. store/redact-structural.ts (pure) redacts secrets by POSITION (complements secret-scan.ts's vendor-VALUE redaction): URL query credentials (?token=/&api_key=/?access_token=/?password=… -> key kept, value masked to the next delimiter), auth headers (Authorization/X-Api-Key/Cookie/… -> value masked to line end), and connection-string passwords (scheme://user:pass@host -> user+host kept, pass masked). redactForLog(text)=redactSecrets(redactStructural(text)) is the logging formatter, wired at emit time in safety-client.ts logEvent() so a secret in tool output/an event never persists to events.jsonl. Verified: 12 unit tests covering all three done cases + composition + a bare-vendor-secret + no-false-positive on plain prose/non-secret query params; 19 logEvent-touching test files (88 tests) regression-clean; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: SSL/CA-bundle preflight guard in doctor
items.summary: A bad CA bundle (corporate proxy, partial update) surfaces as an opaque TLS failure deep in the HTTP client. Add a preflight that validates CA-bundle env vars + bundled certs (exists, non-trivial, parses) and raises one actionable error with a repair command; expose it in doctor. Escape hatch env var.
items.done: run.sh doctor validates the CA bundle and, on failure, prints the exact broken path + a copy-paste fix instead of an opaque error; happy and broken paths tested.
items.notes: Pure/injectable SslIssue (missing-ca-file|self-signed|expired|unknown-tls) + SslGuardResult ({ok:true}|{ok:false,issue,hint}, errors-as-values never throws) + classifyTlsError (SELF_SIGNED_CERT_IN_CHAIN/DEPTH_ZERO_SELF_SIGNED_CERT/UNABLE_TO_GET_ISSUER_CERT_LOCALLY -> self-signed; CERT_HAS_EXPIRED -> expired; else unknown-tls) + sslHint (per-issue actionable, CA-relevant ones name NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, missing-ca echoes the path, corporate-proxy interception called out) + checkSslGuard (caCertsPath set + !fileExists -> missing-ca-file pre-probe; else probeTls -> classify cert error / ok / a probe throw -> unknown-tls never crash) + formatSslGuardLine (✓ TLS ok / ⚠ TLS: issue — hint). Injected probe/fs = boundary. ADVISORY only — doctor health signal, does NOT change security posture, live fetch path unchanged. Built+tested; wire = status gatherStatus checkSslGuard(caCertsPath=NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, fileExists, probeTls=HTTPS HEAD) + formatStatus pushes the line (named). Healthy = ok.

items.title: Install fast-path: provisioned -> skip wizard
items.summary: Launching re-runs per-concern setup checks. Add a single 'fully provisioned' gate (keys present + provider chosen + kernel built) so run.sh short-circuits straight to launch, distinct from 'kernel built'. Improves launcher idempotence.
items.done: On a fully-provisioned install run.sh launches directly without the wizard; an incomplete install still runs setup; gate unit-tested.
items.notes: Pure/injectable ProvisionState{hasProvider,hasKey,storeReady} + detectProvisionState (hasProvider from VANTA_PROVIDER case-insensitive vs PROVIDER_CATALOG.providerById; hasKey from the entrys envVar set OR a keyless/local entry envVar:null = ollama/lmstudio/claude-code/codex/custom; storeReady from injected deps.storeExists; no fs/provider construction) + isFullyProvisioned (all three) + shouldSkipWizard (isFullyProvisioned UNLESS VANTA_FORCE_SETUP=1) + fastpathReason (skipping setup: provider+key+store ready / running setup: <missing comma-joined>). Local/subscription providers need no key. Partial/ambiguous (no provider/key/store, unknown provider) -> false -> run wizard (SAFE DEFAULT, current behavior). UX convenience ONLY — kernel still gates; a missing key surfaces as a clear provider-call failure later, not a safety hole. Built+tested; wire = startInteractive/ensureConfiguredOrSetup checks shouldSkipWizard(env,{storeExists}) before runFullSetup, skip when true (named).

items.title: Setup-wizard field validation + wrong-key detection
items.summary: Setup accepts pasted secrets without shape checks. Add per-field shape validation, 'you pasted the wrong key type' detection (e.g. xoxb vs xapp), and contextual 'where to find this value' help in the wizard.
items.done: Pasting a malformed or wrong-type key in setup is caught with a specific message and a where-to-find hint; validation unit-tested per field.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04. setup/validate-key.ts (pure) validateProviderKey(providerId,key,hint): high-confidence cross-vendor detection (xoxb-/sk-ant-/AIza/telegram-bot/... most-specific-first, so sk-ant- beats sk-) → 'This looks like a <Slack/Anthropic/…> key, but <Provider> keys start with "<prefix>"'; malformed → 'should start with "<prefix>"'; empty → caught; hint = the provider's catalog signupUrl. Conservative: only enforces providers with a reliable prefix (openai sk-/anthropic sk-ant-/openrouter sk-or-/gemini AIza) and passes everything else (ollama/keyless/router/oauth) so a valid-but-unusual key is never blocked. Wired into setup.ts runSetup key prompt (re-prompts on a caught key). Verified: 13 per-field unit tests (accept-right-shape ×4, wrong-vendor ×4, malformed/empty ×2, unenforced-passthrough ×3); setup tests (21) regression-clean; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Random feature-discovery tip at REPL start
items.summary: With 110 slash commands and 105 tools, discoverability is poor. Show one random one-line tip (a command, @-ref, keybinding, or flag) at session start, gated behind a 'seen enough' counter. Cheapest discoverability win available.
items.done: A random one-line feature tip prints at REPL start from a curated corpus; it stops after a configurable number of sessions; corpus + gating unit-tested.
items.notes: Pure STARTUP_TIPS (14 readonly tips, each <=80 chars 💡 Tip:) + pickStartupTip (deterministic STARTUP_TIPS[abs(seed)%len]; seed 0 -> first, large wraps, negative/non-finite clamped, never crash/empty — NO Math.random) + startupTipsEnabled (VANTA_TIPS, default ON, =0/false/off/no disables) + formatStartupTip (tip or ). ACCURACY: every tip cross-checked REAL — 11 cite a /command verified present in the imported SLASH_COMMANDS (/handoff /verify /skeptic /next /where /reach /skillify /recover /boundary /auto /deep-research), 3 cite documented env vars (VANTA_VERIFY/PROACTIVE/TUI); a test asserts each tip token + cross-checks every cited command/var against the real set. Deliberately EXCLUDED VANTA_GLIMMER (not in the documented env list — would be fabricated). Built+tested; wire = interactive renderBanner appends formatStartupTip(sessionSeed,env) as the last line when non-empty (seed = session counter / day-of-year so it rotates per session, stable within one; named). Disabled = no tip.

items.title: Surface 'compacting now' state in footer
items.summary: When auto-compaction runs mid-turn the scrollback can look like a reset. Set a per-session compacting flag and show 'compacting…' in the interactive footer/status line so the operator knows what's happening.
items.done: During auto-compaction the footer shows a compacting indicator scoped to the active session; clears when done; state unit-tested.

items.title: Web-Audio completion cue (desktop renderer)
items.summary: No 'long task finished' audio affordance. Add 2-3 synthesized completion chimes (Web Audio, no asset files) fired on turn-complete in the desktop renderer, behind a mute toggle.
items.done: The desktop renderer plays a synthesized chime on turn completion, selectable and mutable in settings; no bundled audio; toggle persisted.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-10. The built desktop renderer now primes Web Audio from the submit gesture and plays a low-gain synthesized cue only after a successful `/api/chat` turn. Three asset-free cues (Soft, Bright, Resonant) are selectable in a native modal settings dialog; the enabled toggle and cue choice persist in localStorage, storage/audio failures degrade silently, and the status strip shows the current cue or Muted. The modal uses `showModal()` for native focus trapping and Escape handling. Verified with 16 focused Vitest cases, a strict DOM-aware TypeScript check over `desktop-app/src`, the Vanta size lint, `npm run desktop:build`, and `npm run desktop:sound:smoke`. The Electron smoke drove the built UI through submit, observed 2 native oscillator starts for Soft, 3 for Bright preview, persisted `{enabled:false,sound:"bright"}`, and rendered `Sound: Muted`; screenshot inspection confirmed centered, non-overlapping controls.

items.title: Native OS notification on turn-complete when unfocused
items.summary: Long runs finish unnoticed when the window is in the background. Fire a native OS notification when a turn completes while the desktop window is unfocused.
items.done: Completing a turn with the window unfocused raises a native notification; focused completion stays silent; gated by a setting.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-09. Added the live TUI turn-complete notification path, gated by `ui.notifyWhenUnfocused` / `VANTA_NOTIFY_UNFOCUSED`. `useAgent.send()` now calls `maybeNotifyTurnComplete` after a foreground `turnEnd`; focused completions stay silent, detached background responses do not double-fire, and enabled unfocused completions reuse `term/notify.ts` with `notificationType: turn_complete` while forcing the desktop notification env for that notification. Focus detection is safe by default: `VANTA_WINDOW_FOCUSED=0|1` can provide an explicit renderer signal, injected probes are supported for tests, and macOS falls back to frontmost-app detection for known terminal apps; unsupported/failed detection reports focused so it never spams. Verified with `npx vitest run src/term/turn-complete-notify.test.ts src/settings/ux-settings.test.ts src/settings/store.test.ts src/ui/use-agent.test.ts --reporter=dot` (62 passed), `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run vanta -- lint src/term/turn-complete-notify.ts src/ui/use-agent.ts src/settings/ux-settings.ts`.

SCOPE NOTE: Vanta has no separate 'fast mode' toggle (the card's 'fast' is CC-specific; effort IS the dial here — zero fastMode hits in src) — schema leaves room for future per-model keys. 6 test blocks, incl. items.title: Fail-closed profile-scoped secret resolution
items.summary: For the future multi-profile/fleet-worker case: a per-task secret scope so worker A never reads worker B's keys; get_secret reads the active scope and RAISES (fail-closed) rather than falling back to process env when multiplexing, with a tight global-var allowlist. Platform-thinking before multi-profile users — park.
items.done: Captured for the fleet/multi-profile slice; the fail-closed-on-missing-scope discipline noted.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-09. Existing secret grant storage already failed closed on missing/corrupt `secret-scopes.json`; this adds scoped lookup enforcement to `getScopedSecret`. When `VANTA_SECRET_SCOPE` or an explicit scope is active, Vanta now checks the grant list before reading process env or the configured backend, throws `SecretScopeError` if the secret is not granted to that scope, supports instance-wide `*` grants, and keeps a tight global env allowlist for non-secret runtime variables. Legacy `getSecret` behavior remains unchanged when no active scope is set. Verified with `npx vitest run src/secrets/provider.test.ts src/secrets/scope.test.ts --reporter=dot` (25 passed), `npm run typecheck`, `npm run vanta -- lint src/secrets/provider.ts src/secrets/provider.test.ts src/secrets/scope.ts src/secrets/scope.test.ts`, and direct smoke `node --import tsx -e ...` returning `fail-closed`.

items.title: Verify turn-granular shadow-git rollback coverage
items.summary: A shadow-git store snapshots working dirs before file-mutating ops (once per turn) with rollback independent of the user's real git. Vanta already has checkpoint/rewind + ~/.vanta git-init; verify the existing surface covers turn-granular rollback before building — likely covered.
items.done: Confirmed whether existing checkpoint/rewind offers turn-granular rollback via a store that never touches user git; build only if a gap is found.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07 — confirm-or-build found a REAL gap and built the delta. Confirmed present: FileCheckpointStore snapshots pre-mutation content on every write_file/edit_file and restores independent of the user's real git (writeFile/rm, never touches user VCS) — `/rewind <id>` restores one file. GAP: it was per-MUTATION with no turn grouping, so there was no turn-granular rollback (the card's done criterion). Built the minimal delta: FileCheckpoint gains `turn`; the store owns a turn counter advanced by beginTurn() called from turn-loop.ts at each turn start (beside beginTurnContext); save() is now ONCE-PER-TURN-per-path (a later mutation of the same file in the same turn does NOT re-snapshot → a turn rollback restores the PRE-TURN state, not a mid-turn one) and returns the existing id on dedup; restoreTurn(turn?) rolls back every file mutation of a turn (default latest) in reverse, still git-independent; latestTurn()/currentTurn() helpers. `/rewind turn [n]` rolls back the last (or nth) turn's file mutations; bare `/rewind <id>` unchanged; the list now shows the turn + a hint. 5 new store tests (once-per-turn dedup, cross-file turn rollback incl. a created-file removal, empty/unknown-turn no-op) — existing 20-cap + restore-by-id tests green untouched. Full suite 1031 files/11617 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Live X / social search channel

items.title: Docker network egress isolation (SEC backlog)
items.summary: A deployment-layer defense: run the agent on an internal Docker network with no internet, allowlisting only required services — a second layer against prompt-injection exfiltration beyond the shell sandbox. Reference for a future Docker/deploy story.
items.done: Captured for the SEC backlog / a future Docker deployment story as an egress-isolation reference.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-09. Docker egress isolation is now operator-visible through `vanta egress [status|plan]`: it reports whether the Docker backend is selected, whether Docker will run with hard no-egress (`--network none`) or opened network, and the app-level HTTP egress allow/deny policy (`VANTA_EGRESS_ALLOW` / `VANTA_EGRESS_DENY`). The command prints the safe launch form `VANTA_EXEC_BACKEND=docker VANTA_SANDBOX_NET=0 vanta run "<task>"` and warns when domain allowlists are only Vanta HTTP-guard enforced because Docker itself is open. Existing enforcement verified: exec Docker backend builds `docker run --rm ... --network none` by default, autonomous Docker agent wrapper also defaults to `--network none`, and app-level egress policy is default-deny when an allowlist exists. Verified with `npx vitest run src/cli/egress-cmd.test.ts src/exec/backend.test.ts src/agents/autonomous-docker.test.ts src/net/egress-policy.test.ts --reporter=dot` (24 passed), `npm run typecheck`, `npm run vanta -- lint src/cli/egress-cmd.ts src/cli/egress-cmd.test.ts src/cli/commands-table.ts src/cli/usage.ts`, and live CLI smoke `npm run vanta -- egress status`.

items.title: Real spec-compliant ACP server (editor/IDE integration)

Build a real Agent Client Protocol stdio JSON-RPC server (initialize/new/load/fork/resume/list sessions, set model/mode, prompt, cancel) with streaming thought/message/tool chunks, so Zed/any ACP editor can drive the agent in-IDE. The kernel assess()->approval model maps onto ACP request_permission.

items.title: Pre-exec edit-diff approval + sensitive-path policy
items.summary: Before a file mutation, surface an old/new diff to the client for approve/deny, with session-scoped auto-approve policies (ask/session/always) that still force-prompt on sensitive paths (.env/.git/.ssh/id_rsa). The always-ask-on-secrets auto-approve policy is worth stealing into the permission rules even without ACP.
items.done: A file edit surfaces a diff for approval; an auto-approve-for-session policy still prompts on sensitive paths; policy unit-tested; integrates with EXT-ACP-ADAPTER.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. acp/edit-policy.ts (pure): isSensitivePath (.env/.env.*/.git/.ssh/id_rsa|ed25519/*.pem|key|p12/credentials/.aws/.npmrc/secrets.*), decideEditPrompt (a session 'Allow edits this session' grant auto-approves NON-sensitive file edits; a sensitive path ALWAYS re-prompts even under the grant — the steal-worthy floor the card names), buildLineDiff (prefix/suffix-trimmed old/new preview, capped), EDIT_PERMISSION_OPTIONS (allow / allow_always / reject). Tool gained describeDiff (computed BEFORE approval; write_file diffs proposed content vs on-disk, edit_file mirrors its own replace/replace_all semantics — both never throw/mutate); applySafetyGate awaits it and passes {diff} through the widened requestApproval(...,detail). ACP session: file-tool asks carry the diff as tool-call content + the allow_always option which arms session.autoApproveEdits; the permission channel now round-trips the selected optionId end-to-end (dispatch permissionOptionId, sink Promise<string>) so allow_always is distinguishable from allow. 19 policy tests + a SessionManager grant test (allow_always → later non-sensitive edits skip the prompt, .env re-prompts, diff rides the ask) + updated round-trip (file tool → 3-option menu). Full suite 1030 files/11606 green; tsc + size gate clean. Fixed a pre-ship dynamic-import flake (edit-policy made a static import — see ERRORS.md 2026-07-07). Live editor tap not exercised (needs a real ACP client); the SessionManager + dispatch paths are driven by tests with an injected sink, per the card's own done criterion.

items.title: Vetted MCP catalog + vanta mcp install
items.summary: Mounting MCPs is raw-config today. Add a declarative manifest standard (transport, auth shape, install bootstrap, a read-mostly default tool subset) and a vanta mcp install <name> over a small approved catalog, so mutating tools are opt-in per threat model. Pairs with per-tool kernel gating.
items.done: vanta mcp install <name> installs and configures a vetted server from a catalog with a read-mostly default tool subset; manifest schema + install path unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. mcp/catalog.ts: a vetted MCP_CATALOG (filesystem/github/fetch — official servers) where each McpCatalogEntry declares transport (command+args or url), authEnv, and a READ-MOSTLY defaultTools subset with mutating tools separated into optInTools. buildInstallSpec produces the .mcp.json ServerSpec with tools = defaultTools + explicitly-requested opt-ins (an opt-in not in the entry's declared set is REJECTED, never silently mounted); authEnv becomes ${VAR} placeholders. installIntoConfig merges (name wins on re-install). THREAT MODEL MADE REAL, not cosmetic: added an optional `tools` allowlist to the MCP ServerSchema and wired the mount loop (filterAllowedTools) to mount ONLY allowlisted tools — so a catalog install's read-mostly subset actually excludes mutating tools at mount time (kernel still gates every call on top). `vanta mcp catalog` lists the vetted set; `vanta mcp install <name> [--with-tool <t>]…` writes ~/.vanta/mcp.json. 12 tests (read-mostly invariant across the catalog, default excludes mutating, opt-in adds it, unknown-tool rejected, http vs stdio spec, config merge/overwrite). LIVE real-path executed: `mcp install github` wrote 6 read-only tools with create_issue ABSENT + the auth-env reminder; `--with-tool create_issue` added it. Full suite 1038 files/11683 green; tsc + size gate clean (mountOneServer/runMcpCommand refactored under cx).

items.title: Remote model-catalog manifest with fallback chain
items.summary: The model catalog is a static array, so adding models needs a release. Fetch a JSON manifest with TTL + atomic disk cache and a fallback chain (primary URL -> raw-GitHub -> stale cache -> bundled), plus seed-from-checkout after update. Point it at the docs site + GitHub raw.
items.done: The model picker refreshes from a remote manifest with the full fallback chain and works offline from stale/bundled cache; refresh + fallback unit-tested.
items.notes:
Shipped 2026-07-07. providers/catalog-manifest.ts: refresh the model catalog from a remote JSON manifest so adding models no longer needs a release. resolveCatalog runs the FULL fallback chain in order — fresh disk cache (within TTL, short-circuits the network) → primary URL (docs.vanta.theft.studio/model-catalog.json) → raw-GitHub mirror (raw.githubusercontent.com/…/main/docs/model-catalog.json) → STALE cache → bundled PROVIDER_CATALOG (the always-available floor) — and NEVER throws, always returning a usable catalog + its source tag. A successful fetch atomically rewrites the cache. diskCacheDeps is the live adapter: atomic write (temp file + rename so a reader never sees a half-write), tolerant read (missing/corrupt → null). parseManifest zod-validates (version:1 + ≥1 provider, passthrough on extra fields) so a junk/wrong-version payload is ignored and the chain keeps falling through. fetch + fs fully injected → 9 offline tests cover every rung: fresh-cache short-circuit, stale→primary refresh+persist, primary-fail→github, all-fail→stale-cache, offline+no-cache→bundled, invalid-payload passthrough, atomic round-trip + no .tmp residue, corrupt-read→null. Full suite 1037 files/11676 green; tsc + size gate clean. Publishing the manifest at the two URLs + calling resolveCatalog from the /model picker's refresh is the deploy-time follow-up; the resolver + cache is the shippable core.

REPAIRED 2026-07-13: the desktop `/api/models` endpoint now resolves the manifest through the existing six-hour cache and fallback chain, instead of always reading the stale bundled array. `docs/model-catalog.json` is generated from `PROVIDER_CATALOG`, the documentation build copies it to `vanta-website/static/model-catalog.json`, and both configured remote URLs now have a committed source artifact. The OpenAI picker fallback and published manifest include current agent-capable GPT-5.6, GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, and GPT-5.3 Codex entries; context windows are mapped for the new IDs. Verified focused desktop/catalog/context tests, TypeScript, and the production documentation build. A picker entry is catalog visibility, not account entitlement.

CI REPAIR 2026-07-13: the docs workflow installs website dependencies only, so its prebuild no longer depends on the TypeScript workspace `tsx` binary. The generated manifest is committed as the published source; the docs build copies it to static assets. A new Vanta test reads `docs/model-catalog.json` and requires byte-equivalent structured content with `PROVIDER_CATALOG`, preventing future catalog edits from publishing a stale manifest. Focused manifest/desktop tests, TypeScript, and the production docs build pass locally.

DEPLOYMENT ROUTE REPAIR 2026-07-13: direct Pages deployment verified `https://vanta-docs.pages.dev/model-catalog.json` returns the published manifest with 27 providers and current GPT-5 models. The configured custom domain serves the site but returned a stale 404 for this new static asset, so the resolver primary is now the verified stable Pages production URL; GitHub raw remains the mirror. Targeted endpoint, manifest, and desktop picker tests plus TypeScript pass.

CI/PRODUCTION RECEIPT 2026-07-14: GitHub Actions run 29273736326 passed for the endpoint correction; the two preceding docs build/deploy workflows also passed. Direct production retrieval from `https://vanta-docs.pages.dev/model-catalog.json` returned manifest v1 with 27 providers, including the current OpenAI agent family. `main` and `origin/main` match at 94815cab.

CATALOG REFRESH 2026-07-14: regenerated the published model manifest after the official-model follow-up so the remote picker source includes GPT-5.2/pro, GPT-5.1, GPT-5/pro/mini/nano, o3-pro, GPT-5.3-Codex, GPT-5.2-Codex, GPT-5.1 Codex variants, and GPT-5 Codex variants. Website docs and static catalog were rebuilt from the regenerated source.

items.title: Declarative config field-descriptor -> generic setup
items.summary: Adding a configurable backend means bespoke setup/endpoint code. Add a pure-data field descriptor (kind=text/select/secret, env_key, aliases, env_fallbacks) so one generic renderer + one generic config path handle any pluggable surface; secrets route to env-store and are never read back (only is_set).
items.done: A new configurable surface is added by declaring fields only, with no bespoke setup UI; secrets are write-only; the generic renderer is unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. config/field-schema.ts (pure data + generic behavior): FieldDescriptor {key, label, kind text|select|secret, envKey, aliases, envFallbacks, options, placeholder}. resolveField reads text/select across envKey→aliases→fallbacks (first hit wins) and a SECRET yields ONLY {isSet} — the value is never returned (write-only). renderField/renderSurface = the one generic renderer (secret masked '•••• (set)'/'not set', text shows value-or-placeholder). fieldUpdate = the one generic config path (select validates against options; text/secret write verbatim to envKey — write-only means never READ back, not never written; empty rejected). A new configurable surface is now added by declaring a FieldDescriptor[] with zero bespoke setup UI — the test defines a whole pluggable memory-backend surface (provider select / endpoint text / api key secret) as data alone and drives it through the generic renderer + config path. 7 tests, incl. explicit assertions that a secret's value never appears in resolve/render output (including via alias). Full suite 1034 files/11654 green; tsc + size gate clean. Existing setup surfaces keep their bespoke code; this is the primitive future pluggable surfaces declare against (env writes route through the existing setup-env upsert).

items.title: normalizeModelForProvider() id canonicalizer
items.summary: Routing to OpenRouter/Ollama hits 'right model, wrong id-shape' 400s. Add a small normalizeModelForProvider() that strips/prepends vendor prefix, detects vendor from a bare id, and applies the few provider-specific id quirks the providers actually hit. Keep it tiny.
items.done: A model id is canonicalized for the target provider before the API call, preventing wrong-shape 400s; covered for each routed provider.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. providers/normalize-model.ts (tiny, pure): normalizeModelForProvider(providerId, model) canonicalizes the id for the TARGET provider before the factory reads VANTA_MODEL, killing wrong-shape 400s. Two real quirk classes: OpenRouter wants vendor/model — a bare id gets the detected vendor prepended (detectVendor: gpt/o1-4/chatgpt→openai, claude→anthropic, gemini→google, llama→meta-llama, mistral/mixtral→mistralai, qwen/deepseek/grok→…), an already-slashed id untouched; native openai/anthropic/gemini/codex/claude-code want a BARE id — a KNOWN-vendor prefix is stripped. SAFETY: an unknown vendor is never fabricated (bare→bare) and an unrecognized namespace on a native provider is NOT stripped (a user's 'team/internal-7b' survives); freeform backends (ollama/custom/openai-compat) pass through untouched (their ids legitimately carry tags + hf.co/… paths). Wired at the single resolveProvider seam — the factory gets a normalized-VANTA_MODEL env only when the id actually changed (else the original env, so unset-model factory defaults are byte-identical). 19 tests incl. 4 end-to-end through resolveProvider (bare→OpenRouter vendor/model, OpenRouter-shaped→native bare, correct/unset left alone). Full suite 1033 files/11643 green; tsc + size gate clean.

Verified with `npx vitest run src/mcp/server.test.ts --reporter=dot` (19 passed), `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run vanta -- lint src/mcp/server.ts src/mcp/server.test.ts`.

items.title: Routed creative-ideation method catalog
items.summary: Mine a routed library of named ideation methods (first-principles, biomimicry, oblique-strategies, JTBD, TRIZ, SCAMPER, leverage-points, lateral-provocations, premortem-inversion, analogy/blending) — each with when/when-not, procedure, worked example, anti-slop notes — as a Vanta skill with a signal-routing table. Feeds the Cofounder/solutioning pillar. Reimplement in Vanta's voice, don't copy text.
items.done: A solutioning skill routes a problem (phase/domain/specificity signals) to one ideation method and applies its procedure; method catalog + router unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-20 (10 methods); EXPANDED 2026-06-24 to the full 22 creative methods + a feasibility<->creativity 'balance' axis the Hermes (SHL0MS creative-ideation, MIT) prose skill lacks. solutioning/ideation.ts split into ideation-catalog.ts (analytic 10) + ideation-creative.ts (12 artist/thinker methods: Oulipo/Queneau-Perec, Story Skeletons/Vonnegut, Chance-Remix/Cage, Pataphysics/Jarry, Pattern Languages/Alexander, Derive/Debord, Compression-Progress/Schmidhuber, Polya, Defamiliarization/Shklovsky, Creative Discipline/Tharp, Affinity Diagrams/KJ, Volume Generation), each with origin attribution + a 0..1 creativity weight. New balance signal (feasible|balanced|novel): novel escalates to the phase's divergent method, feasible grounds it, unset/balanced keeps the backward-compatible base route. SKILL.md rewritten for 22 grouped on the axis + an UserPromptSubmit auto-fire trigger. NOT a verbatim port (native procedures, our tested deterministic router beats the prose one). 962 files/11018 tests green.

items.title: User-extensible provider registry (~/.vanta/providers.json)
items.summary: Add ANY OpenAI-compatible provider without a code change, mirroring the agents.json / mcp.json pattern. Came out of a TokenRouter setup session: a router token reaches many models but each provider was hand-coded in the OPENAI_COMPAT map. A user registry removes that friction and lets users self-fix a built-in's URL/model without waiting on a release.
items.done: ~/.vanta/providers.json declares {baseURL, keyEnv?, model?, router?} per id; VANTA_PROVIDER=<id> resolves it; the secret stays in .env (only the env-var NAME is in the file); keyless local endpoints work; a user entry overrides a built-in of the same id; resolveProvider checks the registry before the built-in table; unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-24 (commit 6fdca3c). providers/user-providers.ts: loadUserProviders (tolerant read/validate, bad entry dropped) + makeUserProvider (requireKey from keyEnv, '-' placeholder for keyless, model from VANTA_MODEL>entry, clear errors). Wired: resolveProvider checks loadUserProviders[id] first (user wins -> add OR fix any backend). providers.json.example + .env.example document it. 11 unit tests + full suite 11012 green.

items.title: Router providers free-type any model in the setup wizard
items.summary: Fix the 'I select TokenRouter but it only shows MiniMax' confusion: a router's one token reaches every model it proxies, so the wizard shouldn't pin a fixed suggestion list. VANTA_MODEL already accepted any id; this makes the setup UX match the capability.
items.done: ProviderEntry gains router?:boolean; tokenrouter + openrouter flagged; setup wizard chooseModelStep goes straight to free-typed 'enter any model id' for routers instead of a pinned pick; tests green.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-24 (commit b908aa2). Also fixed the TokenRouter built-in: base URL is api.tokenrouter.com/v1 (not .io) and model ids are bare (MiniMax-M3), verified live against a real token (commit b5955f0). 11001 tests green.

items.title: Auto-fire ideation routing across Vanta / Claude / Codex
items.summary: Make the 22-method ideation routing fire automatically when an ideation-intent prompt arrives, in every agent the operator uses. Vanta side is done (UserPromptSubmit trigger in the skill frontmatter, auto-synced to ~/.vanta/hooks.json each session). The cross-agent half writes global agent config, so it stays confirm-gated.
items.done: vanta skills sync-triggers --claude installs the UserPromptSubmit hook into ~/.claude/settings.json; an AGENTS.md snippet carries the routing for Codex (no event hooks); both verified to fire on an ideation-intent prompt.
items.notes: Vanta auto-fire SHIPPED in the ideation-methods SKILL.md trigger (2026-06-24). Claude (~/.claude/settings.json via sync-triggers --claude) + Codex (AGENTS.md) PENDING — both touch global agent config, confirm-first per Rule Zero. Codex half SHIPPED 2026-06-28: compileTriggersForCodex + mergeAgentsMd + `vanta skills sync-triggers --codex` write the prompt-routing into ~/.codex/AGENTS.md (live-verified: ideation routing synced). All 3 agents now have a routing-sync mechanism (Vanta auto, Claude --claude, Codex --codex). Remaining: a live cross-agent FIRE test (Codex/Claude applying ideation on a prompt with the synced config).

items.title: Internal sandbox blocks tsx when Vanta shells out to its own CLI (EPERM on IPC pipe)
items.summary: When Vanta (the agent) runs a shell command that invokes its OWN CLI via the tsx launcher, the shell sandbox denies the tsx IPC pipe with EPERM, so the command fails. Found 2026-06-24: an internal `vanta skills install --force` + `sync-triggers --claude` failed under the sandbox; the agent worked around it by pointing VANTA_HOME at a throwaway .vanta-state dir + `node --import tsx`, which silently installed the new skill + synced triggers to the WRONG home — the real ~/.vanta was left stale and activation no-op'd. The bug is the sandbox/tsx-IPC interaction; the wrong-home write is a downstream symptom of the fallback.
items.done: a Vanta-initiated `vanta skills …` (or any self-CLI) call runs under the shell sandbox without EPERM on the tsx IPC pipe — OR the launcher detects the sandbox and runs the CLI a way that works AND respects the real VANTA_HOME (never silently a throwaway dir). Repro + regression test.
items.notes:
Found 2026-06-24 during ideation-skill activation. Symptom: `vanta skills install --force` from inside Vanta hit 'sandbox EPERM on tsx IPC pipe'; the agent fell back to VANTA_HOME=.vanta-state + node --import tsx, installing the 22-method skill + syncing triggers to a throwaway repo-local dir instead of ~/.vanta — activation silently no-op'd against the real store. Fixed manually by re-running both against ~/.vanta; the stray .vanta-state is now gitignored + removed. Root fix: allow the tsx IPC pipe under the shell sandbox for self-calls, OR have the launcher fall back without overriding VANTA_HOME. Separately exposed VANTA-CLAUDE-USERPROMPT-GATE (fixed same day): Claude ignores `matcher` for UserPromptSubmit, so the synced ideation trigger fired on every prompt until the emitter was taught to gate on the prompt regex (promptMatchesTrigger).

HARNESS CLEANUP 2026-07-10: the real macOS seatbelt regression still proves `node --import tsx` succeeds and the `tsx` CLI fails with EPERM, but the expected failing child now captures stderr instead of leaking a full `node:net` stack into every otherwise-green Vitest run. Focused execution: 8/8 tests passed and the captured output contained no `listen EPERM` or tsx pipe stack.

items.title: Width-responsive TUI menus — kill fixed truncation across every palette/list
items.summary: The slash/skills/overlay menus clipped descriptions at hardcoded widths (48/52/72), so they cramped on a wide terminal and misaligned on a narrow one — long skill names rammed straight into their descriptions. Operator readability fix surfaced 2026-06-25 from a screenshot of the cramped /skills menu; user asked to fix every menu, not just skills.
items.done: a shared width helper sizes the label column to the longest entry and gives the description the rest of the real terminal width; applied to the slash palette, the overlay menu (model/sessions/skills/themes picker), quick-open, the /skills text list, and the loops panel; descriptions align into one scannable column; tests green.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-25 (commit 9893473). New term/width.ts (planColumns + clipTo + termWidth, pure + tested) drives every menu: ui/slash-palette, ui/overlay-list (high-leverage — model/sessions/skills/themes all inherit), ui/quick-open, repl/handlers /skills (name col 24→40 so long design-skill names fit + responsive desc), ui/loops-panel. Descriptions dimmed (command name = scannable anchor), active row inverse. Reasoned from dec-cognitive-load (fixed clip = extraneous load), dec-krug (one scannable column), dec-ux-laws/Von Restorff. Left as-is: @/# palettes (name-only), config/settings panels (aligned key:value), task-rows (pure unit-tested shaper). 963 files/11028 tests green.

items.title: Gate UserPromptSubmit skill triggers on the prompt regex for Claude Code
items.summary: Claude Code ignores the `matcher` field for UserPromptSubmit hooks (it's tool-name-only), so a synced SKILL-TRIGGERS match-trigger fired on EVERY Claude prompt — the ideation-methods recall note spammed every turn. Found 2026-06-25 while activating the ideation auto-fire across agents (sibling of VANTA-SANDBOX-TSX-SELFCALL).
items.done: the trigger-emit emitter reads the prompt from the hook payload and only injects the recall note when it matches the trigger's regex; a non-matching prompt emits nothing; verified live + unit-tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-25 (commit 6c5e7a4). Pure promptMatchesTrigger (skills/triggers.ts) handles the (?i) inline flag JS RegExp lacks; cli/skills-cmd.ts userPromptGatedOut reads stdin payload.prompt and suppresses the note on a non-match. Vanta's own hook engine already gated upstream (toolNamePattern) — only the Claude path leaked. 3 unit tests; full suite green.

items.title: Fold 5-test slop diagnostic into anti-slop
items.summary: Strengthen the existing anti-slop with a slop-signature blocklist, a 5-test diagnostic, and suppression techniques (refuse-first-three, force-specificity-via-proper-nouns, weirdness budget). 'Name-dropping a tech stack is NOT specificity' is the sharp reusable rule.
items.done: Idea-generation output is run through the 5-test diagnostic and specificity rule; the existing anti-slop gains the blocklist + suppression techniques; diagnostics unit-tested.
items.notes: Pure 5-test substance slop diagnostic (verify/, alongside the existing repl/anti-slop.ts VOICE-drift axis): hedgeDensity (weasel words/100w) + fillerDensity (boilerplate phrases) + buzzwordDensity (synergy/leverage/holistic..) + lacksSpecifics (no numbers/proper-nouns/code-tokens -> sloppy) + restatesPrompt (answer-vs-prompt overlap without new tokens) -> runSlopDiagnostic (tests:SlopTest[] {id,label,failed,detail?} ids [hedge,filler,no-specifics,restates-prompt,buzzword]; slopScore weighted avg; isSlop = slopScore>0.34 OR >=3 fail) + formatSlopReport (slop N% — failed: ids / — clean). Substantive text -> low score isSlop false; slop -> high + failed ids; empty -> 1.0 all-failed. Thresholds heuristic, tuned to clear cases not over-fit. Built+tested; wire = the Cofounder work-product reviewer / anti-slop caller runs runSlopDiagnostic(text,prompt) + rejects/flags when isSlop (named).

items.title: Layered multi-LLM answer synthesis (MoA)
items.summary: Layered answer-synthesis pattern is captured by Vanta’s bounded council primitive: a hard prompt fans out across injected role/model runners, then one synthesis role consolidates the answers into a single recommendation.
items.done: Existing `src/council/council.ts` provides the buildable pattern: bounded roster, injected role runner for model/subagent routing, one deliberation pass, and one synthesis/reflection step. A dedicated multi-layer MoA runtime remains deferred until repeated concrete need justifies it.
items.notes: SHIPPED/CAPTURED 2026-07-10: no new runtime primitive added. Existing Agent Council orchestration covers the reusable fan-out + synthesis pattern without unbounded recursion. Focused proof: npx vitest run src/council/council.test.ts (9 passed).

items.title: 1-3-1 decision-output rigor
items.summary: 1-3-1 decision-output rigor is captured as a pure solutioning template: one problem sentence, exactly three distinct options with pros/cons, one recommendation, and an attached Definition of Done.
items.done: `ONE_THREE_ONE_TEMPLATE`, `isOneThreeOneDecision`, and `formatOneThreeOneDecision` exist in `src/solutioning/one-three-one.ts`; tests pin exactly-three distinct options, pros/cons, recommendation, and Definition of Done rendering.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: added src/solutioning/one-three-one.ts and src/solutioning/one-three-one.test.ts. Focused proof: npx vitest run src/solutioning/one-three-one.test.ts (5 passed). Typecheck passed.

items.title: Memory lifecycle-hook taxonomy for the brain interface
items.summary: Memory lifecycle-hook taxonomy is now captured on the Brain port without adding a backend plugin framework: prefetch, queue_prefetch, sync_turn, on_session_end, on_session_switch, on_pre_compress, and on_delegation.
items.done: `MEMORY_LIFECYCLE_HOOKS`, `MemoryLifecycleHookName`, and `isMemoryLifecycleHookName` live in `src/brain/interface.ts`; the live Brain adapter exposes the taxonomy; tests pin the hook names and provider mock compatibility. Full hook bus/backend ABC remains deferred.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: added a typed lifecycle taxonomy to the existing Brain port instead of a runtime hook bus or pluggable-backend ABC. Focused proof: npx vitest run src/brain/interface.test.ts src/memory/provider.test.ts (19 passed). Typecheck passed.

items.title: Wire anti-thrash gate into per-turn compaction (recentSavings)
items.summary: HARNESS-COMPACT-BOUNDARY shipped shouldCompact()/passSavings() (pure, tested) but they have no call site — graduatedCompaction is a within-call layer descent, not the cross-turn repeated-pass loop the gate targets. Track recentSavings across compaction invocations in the agent compaction path (agent/context-pipeline.ts) and skip a pass when the last 2 each saved <10%.
items.done: The per-turn compaction path records each pass’s savings and skips a compaction pass when the last two passes each saved <10% (shouldCompact); wired + tested; default behavior unchanged when savings are healthy.

items.title: Enforce settings.blockedTools at registry build (buildRegistry exclude)
items.summary: SETUP-TOOLS-CONFIG persists tool toggles to settings.blockedTools, but the field is not yet consumed by buildRegistry() — the registry is built in session.ts before settings load, and loadRuntimeExtensions registers MCP/plugin tools into that same registry (chicken-and-egg). Reorder bootstrap so settings load first, then buildRegistry({exclude: settings.blockedTools}). Env kill-switches (browser) + provider env are already live; this closes general tool-disable enforcement.
items.done: A tool in settings.blockedTools is absent from the live registry for the session; bootstrap loads settings before buildRegistry; MCP/plugin registration still works; tested.

items.title: Consume ND output-density/sensory/time prefs in prompt + nudges
items.summary: ND-PROFILE added persisted NdPreferences (output-density, sensory-load, time-support-style) + getOutputDensity accessor, but nothing consumes them yet (EF gate thresholds already drive the engine). Wire getOutputDensity into prompt.ts verbosity / nd-sensory-load directive, and sensory/time prefs into the nd gate nudge formatters (session/nd-gates.ts).
items.done: Output-density pref scales prompt verbosity; sensory-load pref tunes nudge decoration; time-support pref drives the time-blindness gate output style; tested; default profile preserves current behavior.

items.title: Populate inbound id/isGroup/replyToId in the remaining adapters
items.summary: The inbound pipeline (MSG-SELF-ECHO-DEDUP / MSG-REQUIRE-MENTION / MSG-REPLY-CONTEXT) consumes optional InboundMessage fields id/isGroup/replyToId/fromMe + OutboundMessage.id. Telegram now populates them; the other live adapters (signal, irc, mattermost, imessage, ntfy) do not yet, so the pipeline is inert for them. Parse those fields from each platform payload + capture the sent id, mirroring telegram.ts.
items.done: signal/irc/mattermost/imessage/ntfy populate inbound id/isGroup/replyToId (best-effort fromMe) and capture the sent message id; the inbound pipeline fires on each; per-adapter tests.

items.title: Wire GlobalSearchDialog open trigger (app.tsx slot + keybinding)
items.summary: VANTA-GLOBAL-SEARCH-UI shipped searchSessions + GlobalSearchDialog (built+tested) but no open trigger. Add a /searchall slash + handler and an overlay slot in ui/app.tsx LiveBody (state flag + a Ctrl-key in useGlobalKeys, onSelect -> /resume <id>), mirroring the existing QuickOpen wiring. The dialog uses the QuickOpen prop shape so it drops in.
items.done: A keybinding/slash opens GlobalSearchDialog over all sessions; selecting a result resumes that session; the overlay mounts/unmounts cleanly in app.tsx; render-tested.

items.title: Wire channel permission relay into the live approval loop
items.summary: VANTA-CHANNEL-PERMISSIONS shipped the pure relay/race/parse core (permissions/channel-relay.ts). Wire it live: at the requestApproval site (agent/dispatch-tool.ts -> applySafetyGate -> AgentDeps.requestApproval), generate a stable requestId, call relayApproval with send=send_chat (per configured approver platform/chat), replies=the gateway inbound stream filtered to this requestId, localResolve=the existing host approver, allowlist=the operator-profile approver chat-ids. Persist the pending request. Touches the hot approval path + gateway + operator-profile, so it is its own careful slice.
items.done: An approval prompt is sent to the configured channel; replying yes/no <id> from an allowlisted chat resolves it, raced against the local prompt (first wins); only allowlisted chats can approve; tested with an injected gateway stream.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. The pure relay core (permissions/channel-relay.ts) wired LIVE, opt-in via VANTA_APPROVER_CHATS (unset -> byte-identical deny behavior). permissions/reply-bus.ts: in-process bridge between the gateway inbound loop and pending relays — consumes an inbound ONLY when its text references a pending request id (normal messages pass through); subscribers get an abortable async stream; plus a bypass stash (below). permissions/channel-approver.ts: buildChannelApprover -> requestApproval that relays the prompt (buildPermissionRequest, id embedded), races the allowlisted channel reply vs a DENY-AT-TIMEOUT local resolver (VANTA_CHANNEL_APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_SEC, 120s default) — headless 'instant deny' becomes 'human can approve from chat within the window'. DEADLOCK FOUND & FIXED BEFORE SHIP: the gateway loop is BLOCKED while a turn awaits approval, so the reply would never be polled and every race would time out — the relay now pumps platform.poll() itself while pending (safe: the blocked single-threaded loop means no concurrent getUpdates) and parks non-approval messages on the bus; pollPlatformSession drains parked messages first, so none are lost. Wired: buildCronRunTask gains opts.requestApproval; runGatewayCommand assembles bus+approver (buildGatewayApprover); GatewayDeps.replyBus consume in the poll loop. 7 test blocks incl. the pump deadlock-breaker (bypassed message preserved) and a pollPlatformSession integration (reply consumed, parked message runs as a turn, relay stream receives the reply); stranger yes ignored, ambiguous never approves (core relay tests pre-existing). Full suite 1028 files/11579 green; tsc + size gate clean. Live channel not exercised (needs a real Telegram token + human) — the injected-stream integration is the executed path, per the card's own done criterion.

items.title: Departments as first-class org units (roles + standing budget + standing goals + default skills)
items.summary: THEFT/PARITY-DELTA seeds 8 departments each with a default agent, default skills, and default starter issues, and DECISIONS locks departments as structural — but Vanta's team layer only has flat role-tagged workers (team/hire.ts, team/store.ts) and a derived org-chart (team/org-chart.ts, reporting lines only). There is no named org unit that owns a standing goal set, a scoped budget, a skill bundle, and a worker roster as one durable entity. Build a Department primitive over team/* that binds workers, a goal-subset (goals.tsv), a budget scope (budget/store.ts already keys by scope), and a default skill set, so the engine can fan work out per-department rather than per-loose-worker.
items.done: `vanta dept add <name>` creates a department that owns >=1 worker, a budget scope, and a standing goal, and `vanta dept status` shows per-department roster, spend-vs-budget, and open goals.

items.title: Enforced-outcome contract: a task is not done without its declared artifact
items.summary: COFOUNDER-HAPPY-PATH-MVP's central rule is 'every generated task must declare an expected output type' and 'a task cannot read as complete without a visible artifact or an explicit no-artifact reason'; Paperclip's roadmap calls this Enforced Outcomes. Vanta's team/tasks.ts lets a task transition running->done on any result string with no artifact gate, and tickets/store.ts has no required-output field. Add an outcome contract to the task/ticket model — declared expected output + a verifier that blocks the done transition until a matching artifact exists — reusing the /verify deterministic close-out machinery Vanta already ships.
items.done: A task created with an expected output of type X is refused the done transition until an artifact of type X is recorded; a forced close requires an explicit 'no artifact because…' reason that persists on the task.
items.notes: Pure OutcomeContract + canCloseTask gate wired into team/tasks advanceTask (running->done refused unless an artifact of the expected type exists OR a no-artifact reason is set; fail-closed by default; no-contract task unchanged). The hasArtifact predicate is injected; connecting it to the work-products store at the live `team advance` call site is ENFORCED-OUTCOME-WIRE.

items.title: Work-products / artifact library as the durable company-memory surface
items.summary: THEFT's happy path ends every department run in a Library artifact with provenance (source task, owning department, approved/pinned state), and Paperclip lists Artifacts & Work Products plus company Memory/Knowledge as core. Vanta persists transcripts, learnings (learnings/store.ts, per-project insights) and a brain, but has no first-class company artifact store keyed by producing worker + source task + department + approval state. Build a work-products store + tool so a verified outcome lands as a durable, provenance-tagged, queryable artifact, distinct from agent memory.
items.done: A completed department task writes a work-product record carrying {artifact, sourceTaskId, departmentId, producedBy, approved} and `vanta library` lists/filters artifacts by department and approval state.

items.title: Department hand-off: a locked artifact becomes another department's input context
items.summary: COFOUNDER-FRAME-FLOW step 7 is explicit: locked artifacts become file-backed context for downstream department work (e.g. DESIGN.md feeds the Design department). Vanta has subagent spawn and an A2A bus (a2a/local.ts) for message passing, but no mechanism that promotes a department's approved work-product into the standing prompt/context of a downstream department's workers. Build a hand-off edge that injects a named upstream artifact as durable context for a target department's tasks, on top of work-products and the department unit.
items.done: Approving an artifact in department A and declaring a hand-off to department B causes B's next task run to receive that artifact as injected context, verifiable in the run transcript.

items.title: Artifact-review approval gate distinct from kernel risk approvals
items.summary: THEFT's flow requires founder review of a produced artifact with approve / request-revision actions, and revision must update the artifact (not just add a comment); Paperclip models this as first-class review/approval stages with reviewer routing. Vanta's kernel approvals (src/approvals, Ask verdicts) gate risky ACTIONS before they run — there is no review queue for finished OUTPUTS where the owner approves/revises a work-product and a revision re-runs the producing task. Add an outcome-review queue layered on work-products + tickets, separate from the kernel's pre-action gate.
items.done: An owner can list pending artifacts, request a revision that re-runs the producing task to update the same artifact, and approve — and approval flips the work-product's state and unblocks any hand-off depending on it.

items.title: Company operating-cadence loop that advances all departments each tick
items.summary: Paperclip's heartbeat model wakes the whole org on a schedule and delegation flows up and down the org chart so the company keeps operating with no manual kick-off. Vanta has a per-loop proactive tick (proactive/tick.ts) and a single heartbeat pipeline (heartbeat/runtime.ts) that drain queued wakes, but no company-level cadence that, on each beat, surveys every department's standing goals and dispatches the next task per department under their budgets. Build a cadence orchestrator over the department unit that runs one company-wide operating beat — survey departments -> pick next task each -> dispatch within budget.
items.done: `vanta company tick` advances at most one task in each department that has open standing goals and remaining budget, records a per-department beat summary, and is cron/heartbeat-invocable.
items.notes: Pure runCompanyTick orchestration (per-dept beat: dispatch one task when dept has open goals + remaining budget; one-per-dept; failures isolated) + tested. Live `vanta company tick` deps assembly (departments + kernel goals + budget + team-tasks dispatch) deferred to CADENCE-WIRE.

items.title: Company OKRs / metrics the engine drives work against
items.summary: Paperclip's value prop is 'manage business goals, not pull requests' with every task tracing to a measured company mission; THEFT's Tech Tree frames build-order as evidence-required nodes toward company outcomes. Vanta has a goal ledger with dependencies (goals.tsv, goal-deps.json) and an auto-research metric loop (auto-research/, single-metric on a worktree), but no company-level objective->key-result structure with measured current values that the cadence loop prioritizes against. Add a company-metrics/OKR store where objectives carry measurable key results, and have the cadence loop rank department dispatch by the least-met key result.
items.done: An objective with key results and current/target values exists; `vanta okr` shows progress, and the company tick prioritizes the department whose key result is furthest from target.

items.title: Cross-department skills/asset exchange
items.summary: THEFT imports a curated skill library and maps default skills per department, and Paperclip treats company skills as a shared, sync-able library across agents. Vanta ships a 44-skill bundle (skills-library/) loaded into prompts, but skills are a global static bundle — there is no notion of one department owning/producing a skill or asset and another department requesting and adopting it at runtime. Build an exchange so a department can publish a skill/asset and another can subscribe to it, scoping which workers load which skills rather than all-or-nothing global injection.
items.done: Department A publishes a skill/asset to the exchange and department B adopts it, after which only B's workers load it on their next run, verifiable in the run's loaded-skill list.

items.title: Delegated approval authority with company-scope audit
items.summary: Paperclip models execution policies with review/approval stages, budget hard-stops, and per-actor audit so the board can delegate sign-off; THEFT routes approvals to a founder attention queue. Vanta's kernel approvals are single-owner and flat (Ask->queue->human), with no concept of delegating a class of approvals to a manager-worker up to a bound, nor a company-scoped audit of who approved what. Build a delegated-authority layer over the kernel approval queue: the owner grants a manager worker authority to approve a bounded class of actions (e.g. spend <= $X, file writes in scope Y), with every delegated approval landing in the tamper-evident audit chain.
items.done: An owner grants a manager authority over a bounded approval class; an Ask action in that class is auto-approved by the manager up to the bound and the decision (delegator, delegate, action) is recorded in the audit chain.
items.notes: Pure grant/checkDelegated/audit shipped + `vanta authority` CLI live. checkDelegated auto-approves only an ASK-tier request within an active grant's bound (spend<=maxUsd or write in scope via resolveInScope); Block-floor NEVER delegated. Live kernel wire (call checkDelegated at the ask tier + append audit) deferred to DELEGATED-AUTHORITY-WIRE.

items.title: Company export/import templates (portable org)
items.summary: Paperclip's company portability — export/import an entire org (agents, skills, projects, routines, departments) with secret scrubbing and collision handling — is shipped, and Clipmart (one-click company templates) is the headline 'coming soon'. Vanta has per-project goals/learnings/tickets and a roadmap, but no operation that serializes a whole department-org + budgets + standing goals + skill bindings into a portable template and rehydrates it into a fresh root. Build company export/import over the department, OKR, and skill-binding stores with secret scrubbing.
items.done: `vanta company export` writes a secret-scrubbed template of departments + budgets + standing goals + skill bindings, and `vanta company import` rehydrates it into a fresh data dir producing the same org structure.

items.title: Automatic organizational learning: completed work becomes reusable playbooks
items.summary: Paperclip's roadmap names Automatic Organizational Learning — turning completed work into reusable playbooks, recurring fixes, and decision patterns so future work starts from what the company already learned. Vanta has a per-project learnings index (learnings/store.ts) and a memory brain, but nothing that mines completed department outcomes into department-scoped, reusable playbooks that get injected when a similar task starts. Build a consolidation pass over work-products + outcomes that extracts a playbook per recurring task-type and surfaces it as context for the next matching department task.
items.done: After >=3 completed tasks of a recurring type in a department, a playbook is generated and the next matching task run receives it as injected context, verifiable in the transcript.

items.title: Self-organization proposals within governance bounds
items.summary: Paperclip's roadmap names Self-Organization: as the company grows, agents propose structural changes — role adjustments, delegation changes, new recurring routines — that still pass through governance/approval. Vanta has a fixed org-chart (team/org-chart.ts) and manual hiring (team/hire.ts) with no path for the engine to PROPOSE a structural change (hire a role, re-assign a manager edge, add a standing routine) for owner approval. Build a self-organization proposer that, off the cadence loop and OKR gaps, emits structural-change proposals into the approval queue — the owner ratifies, never the engine unilaterally.
items.done: When a department is over-budget or an OKR key result is stalled, the engine emits a structural proposal (e.g. 'hire role R' or 'add routine X') into the approval queue, and ratifying it applies the org change.

items.title: Wire the outcome gate to the work-products store at the live task-advance site
items.summary: COFOUNDER-ENFORCED-OUTCOME built the pure outcome gate (canCloseTask with an injected hasArtifact predicate) in team/tasks advanceTask, and COFOUNDER-WORK-PRODUCTS built the artifact store. Connect them: at the live `team` tool advance action, pass hasArtifact = (type) => listWorkProducts filtered by this task's sourceTaskId + kind has a match, so a contract-bearing task actually closes once its artifact is recorded (instead of fail-closed).
items.done: Recording a work-product of the expected kind for a task lets that task's running->done advance succeed at the live team-tool call site; without it the advance is refused; tested with an injected store.
items.notes: LIVE WIRE that also FIXED A LATENT BUG: the pure outcome gate (cofounder/outcome-contract canCloseTask) was already wired into advanceTask via checkOutcomeGate, BUT the live advance sites passed NO hasArtifact predicate -> the default ()=>false would WRONGLY REFUSE any contract-bearing task (never read the store). Fix: inject a work-products-store-backed probe at the 2 live advance-to-done sites (tools/team.ts doAdvance + tools/team-run.ts settle), resolved ONLY when targeting done. Two new helpers bridge gate<->store without changing either: cofounder/outcome-gate.ts (PURE taskProducedArtifact + artifactProbeForTask: a declared outcome satisfied iff the store holds an artifact with sourceTaskId===task.id) + tools/task-outcome-probe.ts (thin adapter resolveTaskArtifactProbe reuses readWorkProducts, read failure -> empty probe never throws). DEFAULT-PERMISSIVE (asserted): no outcome contract -> advances to done unchanged (gate skipped, predicate never consulted; no live path sets outcome today so byte-identical). REQUIRED+UNMET -> refused with reason (errors-as-value, task stays running, never throws). REQUIRED+MET -> allowed. store failure -> permissive. DONE-ONLY (non-done transitions unaffected). KERNEL STILL GATES every tool a tasks work invokes — this is a COMPLETION-EVIDENCE gate NOT a permission gate. fleet.ts sets done directly (not via advanceTask) so left untouched. I re-verified the FULL suite myself (10220 green, tsc clean). Non-kernel hot-path wire.

items.title: Wire `vanta company tick` live deps (departments+goals+budget+dispatch)
items.summary: COFOUNDER-CADENCE-LOOP shipped the pure runCompanyTick. Assemble live CompanyDeps in cli/company-cmd: listDepartments=readDepartments, openGoalsFor=kernel goals filtered to dept.goalIds active, remainingBudgetFor=getBudget(dept.budgetScope), nextTaskFor=next open team/tasks WorkerTask for the dept roster, dispatch=team run path. Add the `company` COMMANDS entry. Cron/heartbeat-invocable.
items.done: `vanta company tick` advances one real task per eligible department against live departments/goals/budget/tasks and prints the beat summary.
items.notes: LIVE WIRE: vanta company tick was a NO-OP (the command wasnt even in the cli.ts COMMANDS table + no live-deps builder existed). Now registered (company: runCompanyCommand) + wired with REAL deps via buildCompanyTickDeps(liveCompanySources(root)) into the UNCHANGED pure runCompanyTick: departments = cofounder/department readDepartments (DEPARTMENT-UNIT store ~/.vanta/departments.json); goals = kernel ledger getGoals + openGoalIdsFor (dept-owned still-active); budget = budget/store getBudget(dataDir, dept.budgetScope) -> remainingUsd (null=unbounded); dispatch = team/tasks nextAssignedTask (oldest assigned owned by a dept worker) -> advanceTask assigned->running. SAFE empty-deps fallback at 2 levels: listDepartments failure -> empty company -> clean no-op tick (never throws); each per-dept read .catch-degraded to a safe skip. New pure mappers in cofounder/cadence-deps.ts (injectable, unit-tested: dispatch/no-open-goals/no-budget/unbounded/no-task/dep-load-failure/per-read-failure). KERNEL STILL GATES: the dispatch seam only mutates the non-tool team task ledger; any tool a later worker run invokes still passes assess() unchanged (documented inline). No logic change to the pure tick. I re-verified the FULL suite myself (10205 green, tsc clean). Non-kernel hot-path wire.

items.title: Wire checkDelegated into the live kernel ask-tier approval
items.summary: COFOUNDER-DELEGATED-AUTHORITY shipped the pure checkDelegated + audit. At the requestApproval site, on an ASK verdict, consult checkDelegated(request, activeGrants); if it auto-approves, resolve the approval and appendAuditRecord. The kernel Block floor stays immovable (never delegated).
items.done: An ask-tier action within an active grant's bound is auto-approved without a human prompt and the decision is in the audit log; out-of-bound/block-floor still prompts.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-04. agent/delegated-gate.ts wires the pure checkDelegated at the ASK branch of dispatch-helpers.ts applySafetyGate: delegatedRequestForCall maps a write_file/edit_file call -> a writeScope DelegatedRequest (path from args); tryDelegatedAutoApprove reads active grants, and on an in-bound match auto-approves WITHOUT the human prompt + appends the audit record (auditDelegatedDecision -> authority-audit.jsonl). SAFETY: runs ONLY in the ask branch (kernel BLOCK is handled above, never reaches here); checkDelegated is default-deny + never auto-approves a non-ask; NO active grants -> readGrants returns [] -> byte-identical to before (zero behavior change for anyone without a grant); a grant-read/audit-write failure degrades to the prompt, never an auto-approve. Verified: 7 delegated-gate unit tests (write-path extraction, no-grants->prompt, in-scope->auto-approve+audit, out-of-scope->prompt, revoked->prompt, fs-throw->prompt) + 360 agent/permissions regression tests green; tsc + size gate clean (applySafetyGate kept <=50 via a delegatedGateResult helper).

items.title: Open-beta gate — live-prove the top operator task paths on a clean machine
items.summary: Many capabilities are unit-tested but live-gated. Pick the top N operator task paths (multi-step build task; reach-me-on-Telegram; run a scheduled job; desktop/voice action) and prove each end-to-end on a NON-dev machine with a real model + real creds. This is the open-beta readiness gate, sibling to INSTALL-PARITY.
items.done: Each named task path runs end-to-end on a clean machine (not the dev box) with captured evidence; the list of live-proven vs still-gated paths is recorded in the repo.
items.notes: verify/beta-proof.ts: the open-beta gate harness — BETA_PATHS (install/does-a-task/multi-step/safe/reach/media/scheduled), runBetaProof (run non-gated live, record gated honestly), formatBetaReport. vanta beta-proof CLI runs the real headless checks (kernel blocks rm -rf, run_pipeline collapses 3 tools to 1 turn, provider configured) -> writes docs/beta-readiness.md. 3 paths proven live on this machine; 4 gated (clean machine / channel tokens / whisper) recorded honestly. Live clean-machine run of the gated paths is the operators.
items.source: a reference agent harness-a reference agent harness-audit-2026-06-21

items.title: Multi-channel LIVE — 5+ platforms from one gateway (Telegram · WhatsApp · Signal · Discord · Slack)
items.summary: Adapters exist (many marked shipped) but only Telegram is proven live. Bring 5+ channels actually live end-to-end from the single gateway — Telegram (live) + WhatsApp + Signal + Discord + Slack — with real tokens, inbound→agent→reply proven, matching a reference agent harness' 'lives where you do'.
items.done: From one running gateway, a message on each of Telegram, WhatsApp, Signal, Discord, and Slack reaches the agent and gets a reply, verified live; the gateway survives one channel failing.
items.notes:
gateway/platforms/multi-channel.ts: MultiChannelAdapter composite — polls ALL configured channels (per-channel error isolation: one down != all down), tags each inbound chatId platform:<id>, routes replies back to the originating channel. resolveMessagingChannel(env): 0->undefined, 1->adapter, 2+->composite. Discord wired into factory (5th channel). Gateway (cli/ops.ts) now runs all configured channels. Telegram+WhatsApp+Signal+Slack+Discord resolve together; live needs each channels real token.

RELIABILITY FIX 2026-07-13: `vanta gateway status [--json]` is now a finite read-only command backed by the existing mode-0600 readiness snapshot. It reports live/stale/idle state, age, and redacted per-channel health, and unknown gateway subcommands print usage instead of silently starting another daemon. Focused utility tests pass; a live CLI check returned the existing stale receipt and exited without creating a second process.
items.source: a reference agent harness-a reference agent harness-audit-2026-06-21

items.title: Images + voice memos in/out across channels
items.summary: Multimodal messaging: receive an image or voice memo on a channel → into the agent's context (vision/STT); send images back (screenshots, generated images, charts).
