Vanta already carries images on the internal Message type and has vision + STT — wire them through the channel adapters so 'send Vanta a photo on WhatsApp/Telegram' and 'Vanta replies with an image' work.
items.done: On at least Telegram + WhatsApp, an inbound image is understood by the agent and an outbound image is delivered; an inbound voice memo is transcribed; all verified live.
items.notes: gateway/media.ts: inbound media bridge (image->vision ImageAttachments, voice->STT injected transcribe, mixed, degrade-on-fail) + resolveInbound. Threaded through runAgent->RunTask->buildCronRunTask->gateway handle(text,images) so inbound images reach the agents vision. media-deps.ts = live whisper+fetch wire. WhatsApp parse extracts inbound image/audio into msg.media. Contracts: InboundMessage.media, OutboundMessage.image. Boundary (live): per-platform auth media byte-download (wa-media: id resolve, Telegram getFile) needs the channel token; outbound-image per-adapter send is the remaining wire.
items.source: a reference agent harness-a reference agent harness-audit-2026-06-21

items.title: Serverless LIVE — Modal/Daytona hibernate + wake-on-message, proven
items.summary: BACKEND-SERVERLESS shipped as a structured command-transport slice. Prove it live: the agent runs on Modal/Daytona, hibernates when idle (~$0), and wakes on an inbound message so you can talk to it from Telegram while it works on a cloud VM. The kernel travels with it and gates remotely. Gateway status now reports redacted Telegram registration readiness alongside Modal app/secret/scale state, prints exact setup commands for the missing secret/deploy/env/registration steps, and exposes the same readiness as JSON.
items.done: A real Modal (or Daytona) deployment runs the gateway, hibernates when idle, wakes on an inbound message, processes it, and replies end-to-end — with cost-when-idle near zero, verified.
items.notes:
PARTIAL 2026-07-10: Added authenticated Telegram webhook ingress with constant-time secret validation, webhook-mode queue draining without getUpdates conflicts, callback acknowledgement, Bot API delivery receipts, and a localhost HTTP update -> gateway turn -> accepted reply proof. Added `vanta backend gateway status|deploy|register-telegram|arm|prove` plus a Modal web-server deployment with min_containers=0, max_containers=1, a 60s scaledown window, and persistent volume-backed Vanta state. Vanta never uploads local secrets automatically. Executed Modal image evidence: the pruned 11.8 MiB context built the pinned Rust 1.85 + Node 24 image; a network-blocked Linux Sandbox ran the compiled kernel and returned status ready from /workspace/vanta-ts; the actual TypeScript gateway process bound 0.0.0.0:3978 and accepted an authenticated Telegram update with HTTP 202; all three temporary proof apps stopped at 0 tasks. Focused suite: 100 passed. Full suite: 1,208 files, 12,508 passed, 3 skipped; TypeScript, Python syntax, JSON, changed-file size gates, socket-noise scan, and bell scan pass. Card remains blocked: the named Modal secret and real Telegram token are absent, so no public inbound message -> real provider -> Telegram reply -> idle-zero proof has been executed.

READINESS STATUS 2026-07-10: `vanta backend gateway status` now includes redacted Telegram registration readiness: endpoint receipt, local token presence, and webhook secret presence, without logging secret values. Executed locally after the change: app vanta-gateway not deployed, Modal secret vanta-gateway missing, Telegram registration no endpoint receipt, token missing, webhook secret missing, min 0/scaledown 60s/volume vanta-gateway-data. Focused modal gateway tests cover present-token/secret redaction. Card remains blocked until a real Modal secret/provider config plus Telegram token/webhook execute deploy -> register -> arm -> public Telegram wake/reply -> scaledown proof.

RECHECK 2026-07-10: Still blocked by live gateway environment. `vanta backend gateway status` reports app vanta-gateway not deployed, secret vanta-gateway missing, Telegram endpoint receipt absent, token missing, and webhook secret missing. Do not mark shipped until a real inbound Telegram message wakes the remote runtime, reaches a real provider-backed agent, replies through Telegram, and scales back toward zero.

UNBLOCKED/PARKED 2026-07-10: operator chose to set live bot/provider setup aside for later. This card is no longer a board blocker, but it is not shipped; resume when Modal/Daytona credentials, Telegram token/webhook secret, and a real public wake -> reply -> scaledown proof are available.

SETUP HINTS 2026-07-10: `vanta backend gateway status` now prints exact next commands when the live proof environment is absent: `modal secret create vanta-gateway ...`, `vanta backend gateway deploy`, missing Telegram env exports, and `vanta backend gateway register-telegram <https-endpoint>`. Live run still exits 1 because app, secret, endpoint, token, and webhook secret are absent; this is setup guidance only, not the wake proof.

JSON STATUS 2026-07-10: `vanta backend gateway status --json` now emits redacted readiness fields for app, secret, Telegram endpoint/token/webhook presence, min-zero/scaledown policy, and next actions. Live run returns ready=false with the same missing secret/deploy/env/register next actions; no secret values are printed.

LIVE DEPLOYMENT PROGRESS 2026-07-13: Modal profile authentication passed, an existing OpenRouter key answered a minimal `openai/gpt-4o-mini` request, a mode-0600 upload created the `vanta-gateway` Modal secret, and the real app deployed at min_containers=0 with 0 tasks and a 60-second scaledown window. Telegram registration then returned Not Found. Direct redacted validation proved the stored 20-character value was a placeholder, not a BotFather token. Gateway readiness now distinguishes token `valid-format`/`invalid-format`/`missing`, records successful webhook registration separately from deployment, and refuses to arm before registration. Live status correctly reports app deployed, tasks 0, secret ready, webhook secret present, token invalid-format, and registration pending. Card remains parked until a valid BotFather token is supplied, the Modal secret is refreshed, and a real message completes wake -> provider reply -> scale to zero.

PROOF QUEUE AUDIT 2026-07-13: Telegram Desktop is installed and logged in, and the official verified BotFather is open at Start. Creating the persistent bot credential requires operator confirmation before the external account action. `roadmap proof-status` now preserves receipt-derived stage actions: because Modal is already deployed, it no longer repeats secret/deploy setup and reports only status -> register -> arm -> real message -> prove. Focused readiness tests pass 8/8 and TypeScript passes.
items.source: a reference agent harness-a reference agent harness-audit-2026-06-21

items.title: Verify + harden SCRIPT-RPC-PIPELINE as first-class zero-context pipelines
items.summary: SCRIPT-RPC-PIPELINE is marked shipped. Confirm it is actually wired and first-class: the agent writes a script that calls tools over RPC and collapses a multi-step pipeline into ONE turn with no per-step model round-trip (a reference agent harness' headline efficiency trick). Measure context/turn savings on a real pipeline; harden + document if there are gaps.
items.done: A representative multi-step task (fetch→transform→write across several tools) runs as a single scripted RPC pipeline in one turn, with measured context savings vs the per-tool-call path and a documented worked example.
items.notes: SCRIPT-RPC-PIPELINE made first-class: tools/run-pipeline.ts run_pipeline factory tool (kernel-gated callTool per step: assess->approval->execute; cannot bypass gate). Only final result returns; intermediate outputs stay in bindings. measureSavings reports tokens kept out of context. Worked fetch->transform->write example in description + test. Registered tools 122->123.
items.source: a reference agent harness-a reference agent harness-audit-2026-06-21

items.title: Dialectic user modeling — a deepening model of who you are (Honcho-style)
items.summary: a reference agent harness uses Honcho dialectic user modeling to build a deepening theory-of-the-user across sessions. Vanta has operator-profile + brain user_model; upgrade to a dialectic loop that forms, tests, and revises explicit beliefs about the user's preferences/goals/working-style from real interactions, feeding the prompt + approvals.
items.done: Across sessions, Vanta forms and revises explicit, inspectable beliefs about the user that measurably change its behavior, with provenance and a way to see/correct them.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-10. Added a local versioned operator-belief store with hypothesis/accepted/rejected/superseded status, confidence, evidence quotes, session/turn provenance, and revision links. The gated post-turn dialectic pass accepts direct self-reports without an extra model call, periodically forms bounded hypotheses, and requires a direct user correction before observational inference may replace an accepted belief. `/preferences` now lists, adds, corrects, rejects, and retains legacy signal export. Active beliefs load into every new session as evidence-labelled data plus deterministic response cues; rejected and sub-55% hypotheses stay out. Behavior eval moved from 0/4 baseline policy matches to 4/4 with modeled beliefs. Real isolated-home REPL proof executed add/list/correct/reject with retained history; a live gpt-5.5 turn stored a tiny-step preference post-turn and printed `operator model: 1 belief accepted`; the next real `--dump-system-prompt` included its session provenance and concise/one-action/small-step cues. Verified with `npm test -- --reporter=dot` (1160 files, 12342 passed, 3 skipped), `npm run typecheck`, and the Vanta size lint. The live proof consumed $0.06.
items.source: a reference agent harness-a reference agent harness-audit-2026-06-21

items.title: Batch trajectory generation + compression for training tool-calling models
items.summary: Vanta converts private local sessions into versioned tool trajectories and decision-level SFT rows, structurally redacts secrets, compresses bulky tool results with source hashes, and feeds the existing local LoRA trainer without uploading the dataset.
items.done: The real CLI exports a private tools-only batch from Vanta session history, preserves tool-call decisions, measurably compresses tool results, emits training-ready trajectory and LoRA JSONL, and trains a saved LoRA adapter from that batch.
items.notes:
SHIPPED 2026-07-10: `vanta trajectory export` emits private vanta.trajectory.v1 JSONL, one decision-level LoRA SFT row per assistant action, and a batch manifest. `--tools-only` selects genuine tool turns. Structural/high-confidence secret redaction runs before export. Winnow compresses tool output first; results still over 8,000 characters retain deterministic head/tail content plus original SHA-256. `vanta trajectory train` feeds those rows into the existing LoRA runner, supports VANTA_LORA_PYTHON virtual environments, keeps the chosen action by left-truncating long context, and makes adapter artifacts 0600 under a 0700 directory. docs/trajectory-training.md documents the local-only contract.

EXECUTED 2026-07-10: the real CLI exported 3 tools-only trajectories from local Vanta history containing 43 calls + 43 results. Tool-result storage compressed 41,104 to 23,901 estimated tokens; the export produced 22 decision-level SFT rows, all files were 0600, and both JSONL artifacts returned zero secret-scan matches. A disposable Python environment then trained the real tiny-test LoRA on MPS from those 22 rows: 4,096 trainable parameters, adapter_saved=true, adapter_model.safetensors SHA-256 87c2ae7236525f686320dcc20733ebe36ad8f9125ce2756d59b0384ad3697804. The private proof dataset and 870 MB disposable environment were deleted afterward. Python syntax and TypeScript passed. Final full suite: 1192 files, 12423 passed, 3 skipped.
items.source: a reference agent harness-a reference agent harness-audit-2026-06-21

items.title: Wake word — hands-free 'Hey Vanta' always-listening trigger
items.summary: Voice STT today is push-to-talk. Add an always-listening wake word ('Hey Vanta') that opens the mic hands-free, like a reference agent harness's voice-wake / a reference agent harness talk mode — the Jarvis 'just say it' trigger. Local wake detection, privacy-first (no audio leaves the device until the wake fires).
items.done: Speaking the wake phrase opens a listening turn hands-free on at least macOS; detection runs locally; false-trigger rate is acceptable in normal speech; opt-in and easily disabled.

items.title: Ambient presence — macOS menu-bar + mobile companion node
items.summary: Vanta has an Electron desktop app but isn't ambient. Add a menu-bar/tray presence (quick ask, status, recent activity, approve-from-tray) and a lightweight mobile companion node — so Vanta is always reachable, like a reference agent harness's Windows Hub / macOS menu bar / mobile nodes. Jarvis is present, not launched.
items.done: A macOS menu-bar app shows status + lets you ask/approve without opening a terminal; a mobile node can reach the running gateway; both gate actions through the kernel.

items.title: Tool-call repair — auto-fix malformed tool calls (weak/local models)
items.summary: a reference agent harness ships a tool-call-repair layer that fixes malformed/partial tool calls before they fail. Vanta targets local + weaker models (Ollama, small open models) where this matters most — repair the JSON/args (coerce types, fill defaults, fix truncation) so a fumbled call retries clean instead of wasting a turn. Reliability for the real-operator bar.
items.done: A representative malformed tool call from a weak/local model is repaired and executed instead of erroring; repair is logged; measured drop in tool-call failures on a local model.
items.notes: providers/tool-call-repair.ts: JSON-repair ladder (fences/extract-object/loose-fix/complete-truncated) + schema coercion (string->number/bool) + default-fill. Wired into openai-convert parseToolCall (replaces {_raw} fallback) + dispatch normalizeToolCall (coerce to tool schema, log via note event). ToolCall.repaired flag.

items.title: Visual canvas — a surface the agent draws/builds on (beyond text)
items.summary: a reference agent harness has a Live Canvas / A2UI. For an operator that 'builds things', text is a ceiling — add a canvas the agent can render to: charts, tables, UI mockups, diffs, live artifacts, a previewable build output. Surfaces in the desktop app + companion presence.
items.done: The agent can render a structured visual artifact (e.g. a chart, table, or UI preview) to a canvas the user can see and interact with, driven by a tool call, kernel-gated.

items.title: Proactive outreach — Vanta messages you first on a channel
items.summary: KAIROS proactivity + nudge cadence exist internally, but Vanta doesn't reach OUT on a channel on its own. Make it initiate: briefings, alerts, 'you should look at X', finished-task pings — delivered to Telegram/WhatsApp/etc., throttled and budget-bounded. The Jarvis 'Sir, you have a meeting' behaviour. Strictly opt-in and rate-limited.
items.done: On a real trigger (schedule, watcher, or finished autonomous task), Vanta sends an unprompted message to a configured channel, within throttle + budget limits, opt-in and easy to silence.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-06. proactive/outreach.ts (pure): OutreachConfig from VANTA_OUTREACH/_TO/_INTERVAL_MIN/_MAX_PER_DAY/_BUDGET_SCOPE (strictly opt-in: enabled AND platform:chatId target required), decideOutreach (silencedUntil -> budget hard-stop -> min-interval -> daily cap, mirroring the KAIROS policy shape), recordOutreach/silenceOutreach, parseOutreachTarget (colon-keeping so matrix:!room:server works), outreachTickText. outreach-store.ts persists .vanta/outreach.json (tolerant load, same contract as proactive/store.ts). outreach-send.ts orchestrates resolve->decide->createAdapter(platform,env)->connect/send/disconnect->record, with adapter + budget verdict injected; a FAILED send reports the reason and does NOT consume the throttle. REAL TRIGGER wired: `vanta proactive run` (the cron-invoked KAIROS tick) pings the channel after advancing queued loop wakes (finished-autonomous-work ping); `vanta proactive silence <minutes|off>` + a status line = easy to silence; env keys documented in .env.example. 24 new tests (19 policy/parse/text + 5 orchestrator e2e through a fake PlatformAdapter, incl. throttle-not-consumed-on-failure and no-adapter-touch-when-refused); CLI real-path verified live (status disabled hint / silence set+lift persisted / enabled target line). tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Network egress policy — allow/deny for all outbound, not just hooks
items.summary: VANTA-HOOK-SSRF-GUARD blocks private/metadata IPs for HTTP hooks only. Generalize it (a reference agent harness's net-policy steal) into a first-class egress policy across ALL outbound — tools, fetch, browser, MCP — with an allow/deny list, private-range/metadata blocking by default, and per-domain rules. The kernel gates actions; this gates where bytes go.
items.done: All outbound network paths (web_fetch, browser, MCP, tools) route through one egress policy that blocks private/metadata ranges by default and honors an allow/deny list; bypass attempts are denied + logged.
items.notes: net/egress-policy.ts: allow/deny domain policy (domainMatches apex+sub + *.wildcard, parseEgressPolicy from VANTA_EGRESS_ALLOW/DENY, checkEgressPolicy: deny-wins + allowlist=default-deny) + best-effort logEgressDenial (~/.vanta/egress-denied.log + stderr). Folded into assertPublicUrl so EVERY outbound path (web_fetch/MCP/hooks/reach) inherits it; an explicit deny is a hard stop even with the private-IP guard opted out. 13 policy tests + 4 assertPublicUrl integration tests.

items.title: Native Windows support — CLI / gateway / tools without WSL
items.summary: Vanta now runs natively on Windows through the production PowerShell installer and launcher, with native kernel resolution, Git Bash or PowerShell tool execution, CLI, gateway, doctor, CI, and release packaging without WSL.
items.done: On a clean Windows box, a one-line installer brings up Vanta natively (no WSL required); CLI, gateway, and shell-based tools work; `vanta doctor` is green.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10. GitHub Windows native run 29064249910 passed on a fresh windows-latest checkout: production install.ps1 cold path, vanta.cmd launch, native Rust kernel build, TypeScript checks, 61 Windows-focused tests, real shell_cmd execution (WINDOWS_SHELL_OK), live kernel API, green vanta doctor (WINDOWS_DOCTOR_OK), and gateway verify-channels. Shell resolution explicitly rejects the WSL bash launcher and uses Git Bash without profiles or noninteractive PowerShell. Local final suite: 1,195 files passed; 12,435 tests passed; 3 skipped. Evidence: https://github.com/jpoindexter/Vanta/actions/runs/29064249910

items.title: Public API + embeddable SDK — drive Vanta programmatically (versioned contract)
items.summary: Vanta now exposes an authenticated, versioned HTTP/SSE API and the published `@jpoindexter/vanta-operator-sdk` typed client, with a stable v1 plugin contract and every operation still routed through the kernel.
items.done: A documented, versioned public API (HTTP + typed client) lets an external program start a session, send input, stream output, and resolve approvals; a published SDK package wraps it; the plugin contract is versioned and stable. Every call still routes through the kernel.
items.notes:
SHIPPED 2026-07-10. IMPLEMENTED: authenticated /api/v1 routes, hashed/revocable bearer tokens, session/input/SSE/approval operations, v1 plugin manifest contract, CLI serve/token commands, operator documentation, and the scoped @jpoindexter/vanta-operator-sdk package.

EXECUTED API PATH: packed the SDK, installed it into a clean consumer, and used that artifact against the real Vanta server/kernel. The client started a session, reconstructed exact provider output from output.delta frames, received a typed turn.completed terminal frame without a timing delay, found the persisted session, validated plugin contract v1, then received 401 immediately after token revocation. Full suite at that gate: 1,181 files passed; 12,397 tests passed; 3 skipped.

EXECUTED REGISTRY PATH: GitHub Actions run 29065319911 used repository-scoped packages:write permission to publish @jpoindexter/vanta-operator-sdk@1.0.0 to GitHub Packages. The registry returned the exact packed artifact SHA-1 75e79b7a22c50a20f3b959f7ef0d863363d2f36d. A blank consumer then installed that version from the registry and executed VantaClient API request construction, API version exports, and the v1 plugin manifest validator (SDK_REGISTRY_EXECUTION_OK). Consumers need a GitHub token with read:packages, as documented. Evidence: https://github.com/jpoindexter/Vanta/actions/runs/29065319911

items.title: User-customizable keybindings — rebuilt on the current TUI
items.summary: The old keybinding registry (DEFAULT_BINDINGS/KeyContext) was deleted in the 06-13 TUI rebuild, parking KEYBINDING-CONTEXTS/-SYSTEM/CHORD-BINDINGS as N/A. The capability — user-defined, context-scoped, chord-capable keybindings from a config file — is still a real product feature users expect. This is the fresh card to rebuild it on the current Ink TUI, replacing those three stale ones.
items.done: Users can define context-scoped keybindings (incl. chords) in a config file, hot-reloaded and schema-validated, layered over sensible defaults, on the current TUI.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. Rebuilt the deleted keybinding registry on the current Ink TUI. ui/keybindings.ts (pure): DEFAULT_BINDINGS (the 5 global actions — exitOrAbort/quickOpen/interrupt/cycleAgentNext|Prev — matching the pre-config hardcoded keys), normalizeChord (canonical modifier order + case), displayChord (⌃⇧T glyphs), KeybindingConfigSchema (zod version:1), resolveBindings (user overrides REPLACE a default's chord for the same action+context, new ones ADD — layered), lookupChord (action+context→chord, global fallback — the shortcut-display seam), actionForChord + eventToChord (Ink event → canonical chord → bound action), loadKeybindings (tolerant ~/.vanta/keybindings.json + defaults; re-call = hot-reload). LIVE ON THE TUI: app-keys.ts handleGlobalKey is now CONFIG-DRIVEN — event→chord→actionForChord→table-driven dispatch (same guards as before); useKeybindings() loads the resolved set in app.tsx. A grantless run is byte-identical (DEFAULT_BINDINGS fallback). 15 registry tests + 6 dispatch tests through the exported handleGlobalKey (default chords fire, a CUSTOM ctrl+k quick-open rebind takes effect live while the old ctrl+p no longer does, guards preserved) — no Ink render harness needed (handleGlobalKey is a pure fn). Full suite 1053 files/11813 green; tsc + size gate clean. Un-parks KEYBINDING-CONTEXTS/-SYSTEM/CHORD-BINDINGS and UNBLOCKS VANTA-SHORTCUT-DISPLAY (lookupChord + displayChord are its exact primitives).

items.title: Entity-match recall signal — mem0's 3rd retrieval signal beyond lexical+semantic
items.summary: Vanta's recall fuses lexical + semantic (search/rrf.ts). mem0's v3 jump (LoCoMo 71→92) adds a THIRD signal: extract entities from each memory, link them across memories, and boost retrieval by entity match — with an IDF-like down-weight for over-linked entities (1/(1+0.001·(n-1)^2)) so a ubiquitous entity doesn't dominate. Add entity extraction + an entity-match score into the brain's fused ranker.
items.done: Brain recall scores an entity-match signal alongside lexical + semantic; entities are extracted, stored, and linked across memories; LongMemEval/LoCoMo recall@k improves vs the 2-signal baseline (measured via `vanta eval mem public`), else the signal is dropped.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-06 — MEASURED LIFT on the public benchmark. search/entities.ts (pure, deterministic — no LLM, honoring mem-eval's no-live-model rule): extractEntities (proper-noun spans + each word, emails, @handles; trailing punctuation breaks a span; cap-stopword filter), buildEntityIndex/buildEntityIndexFrom (entity -> record ids = the cross-memory links), entityWeight = 1/(1+0.001*(n-1)^2) (the card's exact over-link down-weight), entityScores (query-normalized 0..1) + entityRank. KEY NEGATIVE RESULT locked in a comment: fusing entity as an equal-rank RRF peer list DROPPED LoCoMo recall@5 32.4->14.8 (rank fusion flattens the rarity weights the formula exists to carry); the working shape is mem0's own — a score COMPONENT blended into lexical relevance (ENTITY_BLEND swept 0.25/0.5/1.0/2.0 -> 0.25 optimal). MEASURED via `vanta eval mem public`: LoCoMo recall@5 hybrid 32.4% -> 34.9% (+2.5pt; every category up: temporal 41.9->47.8, multi-session 9.4->14.5, IE 11.3->12.4, world-knowledge 38.9->40.3, adversarial 31.5->33.7); LongMemEval unregressed at 99.9%. Eval gains an `entity` mode (41.2% LongMemEval / 7.9% LoCoMo alone — weak solo, additive blended, exactly mem0's claim). BRAIN WIRING: BrainEntry gains `entities` (extracted at write in normalizeEntry, backfilled for legacy entries on load — every load path normalizes); assoc.ts directHits adds ENTITY_REL_BOOST 0.25 so a memory sharing only a rare name with the query clears DIRECT_MIN_REL and becomes recallable (tested). 10 entities tests + 5 brain entity-signal tests (storage, backfill, below-floor rare-entity recall, rank-above-equal-similarity); full suite 1019 files/11512 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: True BM25 lexical signal (IDF + lemmatization + sigmoid normalize)
items.summary: Vanta's lexical recall is token-density ranking (search/life-rank.ts). mem0 uses real BM25 — IDF-weighted, lemmatized, and sigmoid-normalized to 0..1 (normalize_bm25(raw, midpoint, steepness)) so it fuses cleanly with the semantic + entity signals. Upgrade the lexical signal to BM25 (zero-dep) and A/B it on the eval harness.
items.done: The lexical recall signal is IDF-weighted BM25 with lemmatization and bounded 0..1 normalization; LongMemEval/LoCoMo recall@k is at least as good as the token-density baseline, kept only if it improves.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07 — the session's biggest MEASURED recall win. search/bm25.ts (pure, zero-dep): lemmatize (light suffix stemmer: -ies→y, -sses→ss, -ing/-ed strip, bare -s), tokenizeLemmas (stopword-filtered, lemmatized), buildBm25Index (doc lengths, avgdl, docFreq), idf (Robertson-Spärck-Jones +1-guarded), bm25Score (k1=1.5,b=0.75, length-normalized), normalizeBm25 (sigmoid → 0..1, mem0's normalize_bm25 shape), bm25Rank (nonzero-only, ties keep order). A/B ON THE HARNESS: LoCoMo recall@5 lexical 32.4% → 47.8% (+15.4pt; adversarial 31.5→54.4, world-knowledge 38.9→54.6, multi-session 9.4→19.4), LongMemEval flat 99.9→99.8 (saturated). KEPT (card: only if it improves) — so the eval's lexical retriever now IS BM25, and hybrid's lexical base swapped density→BM25 (hybrid 34.9% → 48.4%, still ≥ lexical so the entity blend adds on top of BM25). A dedicated `bm25` eval mode added for ongoing A/B. 16 bm25 unit tests (lemmatize table, IDF rare>common, sigmoid monotonic, more-terms-wins ranking, length-normalization, empty-signal, plural-query match) + mem-eval suite green. Full suite 1048 files/11775 green (one Ollama-network flake on the first run, green on re-run); tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: A/B mem0's sum-of-normalized-scores fusion vs the existing RRF
items.summary: Vanta fuses recall signals with reciprocal-rank-fusion (search/rrf.ts). mem0 instead sums normalized signal scores ((semantic + bm25 + entity_boost)/max_possible). Run both fusions through the existing mem-eval LongMemEval/LoCoMo harness and keep whichever ranks better — a cheap, decisive A/B Vanta is uniquely set up to run.
items.done: Both fusion methods are benchmarked on LongMemEval + LoCoMo via `vanta eval mem public`; the winner is adopted with the delta recorded; the loser is documented, not silently dropped.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07 — decisive A/B, RRF WINS, kept. search/fusion-sumnorm.ts (pure): fuseSumNorm/fuseSumNormScored — mem0's fusion (normalize each signal to 0..1 by its own max, then SUM, keeping the score magnitude RRF discards). Added a `hybrid_sumnorm` eval mode (bm25 + entity + semantic sum-normalized) and benchmarked vs the RRF `hybrid` on `vanta eval mem public`. RESULT: LongMemEval tied 99.9% (saturated); LoCoMo recall@5 hybrid(RRF) 48.4% vs hybrid_sumnorm 44.3% — RRF wins by -4.1pt. sum-norm edged a few sub-categories (temporal 56.1→57.8, multi-session 20.8→21.0, info-extraction 18.8→19.6) but COLLAPSED on adversarial (55.4→35.1): magnitude-summing lets a confident-but-wrong signal dominate on distractor questions, exactly the failure rank-based RRF guards against. WINNER ADOPTED: RRF retained (hybrid unchanged). LOSER DOCUMENTED (not dropped): the `hybrid_sumnorm` mode stays available for future A/B; this result recorded here + in the fusion module header. 5 fuseSumNorm unit tests (per-list max-normalization, missing-item=0, zero-guard, stable ties, empty). Full suite 1058 files/11860 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: A/B ADD-only memory formation (agent-facts first-class) vs crystallization
items.summary: mem0's v3 uses single-pass ADD-only extraction (one LLM call, no UPDATE/DELETE; memories accumulate) and stores agent-confirmed facts with equal weight — and it benchmarks higher than its prior update/merge approach. This conflicts with Vanta's brain thesis (crystallization, consolidation, forgetting). Don't adopt blindly: A/B ADD-only-accumulate vs the current formation on LongMemEval/LoCoMo, and capture agent-confirmed actions as first-class memories.
items.done: An ADD-only formation path is benchmarked against the crystallization path on the eval harness; the result (and whether agent-confirmed facts as first-class help) is recorded in a decision; the better path is adopted or the experiment is parked with its number.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-09. Added `vanta eval mem formation [data-dir]`, a deterministic A/B harness comparing crystallized formation, ADD-only accumulation, and ADD-only with agent-confirmed facts first-class. The live run wrote ignored local artifact `.vanta/mem-formation-ab.json` and sampled the available public data files with `VANTA_MEM_FORMATION_PUBLIC_CASES` defaulting to 50. Fixture result: crystallize 65.4% recall@5, add_only 65.4%, add_only_agent_facts 73.1% because the agent-confirmed push fact is retrievable. Public sample: LongMemEval 50/500 tied at 91.9%; LoCoMo 50/1982 favored ADD-only at 20.5% over crystallize at 2.0%. Decision recorded: adopt candidate `add_only_agent_facts` for the next memory-formation path because fixture+public mean leads at 61.8% recall@5 while preserving agent-confirmed facts as first-class. Verified with `npx vitest run src/mem-eval/formation.test.ts src/mem-eval/run.test.ts src/mem-eval/public-run.test.ts --reporter=dot` (11 passed), `npm run typecheck`, `npm run vanta -- lint src/mem-eval/formation.ts src/mem-eval/formation-report.ts src/mem-eval/formation.test.ts src/cli/eval-cmd.ts src/cli/usage.ts`, and live CLI smoke `npm run vanta -- eval mem formation`.

items.title: Route 'talk to / start Claude Code' to the existing call_agent tool
items.summary: Root cause from a real 2026-06-25 session: a user asked Vanta (gpt-5.5) to 'talk to / start up Claude Code'. Vanta HAS a purpose-built tool for exactly this — `call_agent` (tools/call-agent.ts + agents/external-cli.ts: calls claude/codex/gemini/cursor-agent/opencode non-interactively, 300s timeout, 16MB buffer, kernel- AND approval-gated) — but never reached for it. It fell back to raw `shell_cmd` (`claude`, `claude -p`, `tmux new-session`) and dead-ended on the auto-on macOS shell sandbox (background+tmux refused) plus shell_cmd's 30s timeout. The capability existed; DISCOVERY failed. Fix routing so 'talk to / start / use / ask <agent-name>' intent surfaces call_agent first: a stable system-prompt line naming call_agent for cross-agent intent, a UserPromptSubmit skill-trigger that injects a recall note when a known agent name (claude/codex/gemini/cursor-agent/opencode) appears, and guarantee call_agent's full schema is never hidden by deferred tool-scoping (agent/tool-scope.ts) for those prompts.
items.done: In a fresh session the instruction 'talk to Claude Code' (or 'ask claude to ...') makes Vanta call the call_agent tool — not raw shell — on the first attempt, verified live with a logged tool call (the model that failed in the transcript, or an equivalent local model).
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-25. repl/agent-route.ts (pure: hasAgentIntent/detectAgentName/buildAgentRouteHint) prepends a one-line route hint in buildSendText (interactive-turn.ts, both hosts, VANTA_AGENT_ROUTE=0 disables). agent/tool-scope.ts adds an 'agents' group + HINT so call_agent/delegate schemas stay callable under deferred scoping on any cross-agent mention (the hidden-schema root cause). prompt.ts stable line names call_agent for claude/codex/gemini/cursor-agent/opencode, forbids shelling out. Tests: agent-route.test.ts (11) + tool-scope.test.ts case; prompt's 34 tests still green; tsc clean; size gate exit 0.

items.title: Persistent interactive external-agent session — drive Claude Code turn-by-turn
items.summary: call_agent is ONE-SHOT (execFile -> capture stdout): it delegates a single prompt, it can't hold a conversation. The user's literal ask was 'start one up and keep talking to it.' Build a persistent, drivable session over primitives Vanta already ships LIVE: spawn the agent CLI in a detached tmux pane (reuse fleet/tmux-backend.ts, proven live), inject prompts with `tmux send-keys`, read replies with the tmux-backed terminal_capture tool, loop. New `agent_session` tool: open(agent)->id / send(id,text) / read(id) / close(id). Each action kernel-gated; output bounded; idle/stall detection (reuse tools/shell-stall.ts). Runs through a Vanta-owned tmux channel so it sidesteps the shell-sandbox background refusal (see VANTA-SANDBOX-AGENT-REDIRECT). This is the Cofounder-engine 'drive a sub-operator' mechanic made real; pty path stays blocked on node v24 (node-pty native binding), so tmux is the substrate.
items.done: Vanta opens a live Claude Code session, sends 3 sequential prompts where the 2nd/3rd depend on the 1st's context, reads each reply back into the transcript, and closes it cleanly — verified live end-to-end against the installed `claude` CLI, every send/read kernel-gated.
items.notes: SHIPPED + LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-25. agent_session tool (open/send/read/close/list) drives a PERSISTENT interactive session over another agent CLI via a detached tmux session. agents/agent-session.ts = pure orchestration over an injected SessionBackend port (open primes past the startup modal: boot-wait + Escape — found live, claude's MCP picker ate the first prompt; send polls capture until output settles; read/close). agents/tmux-session-backend.ts = live execFileSync tmux backend (send-keys -l literal text, no shell). Kernel-gated (describeForSafety) + approval-gated (open/send/close). LIVE PROOF vs real claude CLI: opened session, 'Remember 42'->OK, 'What number?'->42 (context carried across turns), closed clean, no stray tmux. Tests: agent-session.test.ts (9, fake backend) + tools roster. Full suite 965 files/11049 green; tsc clean; size gate exit 0. Boundary: tmux required; pty blocked on node v24.

items.title: ACP client — drive another ACP agent (Claude Code) over the protocol, not the terminal
items.summary: Vanta already SERVES ACP (acp/serve.ts + acp/acp-server.ts: `vanta acp serve` exposes Vanta AS an ACP agent to an editor, gating actions through the kernel as session/request_permission). The inverse — an ACP CLIENT that drives ANOTHER ACP agent — doesn't exist. Claude Code can run as an ACP agent, and an ACP client is the clean STRUCTURED interactive bridge (typed initialize/session/prompt/update/permission JSON-RPC) versus scraping a tmux pane (VANTA-AGENT-SESSION-INTERACTIVE). Reuse the pure protocol codec (acp/protocol.ts) + a stdio transport; add an outbound AcpClient: initialize -> new session -> prompt-turn loop with streamed session/update surfaced in the transcript, forwarding the PEER's permission requests to OUR kernel. STRATEGY E6 named ACP 'the real interop path' — this is its client half. Two bridges, one captured intent: ship the tmux session first (works with any CLI), this for agents that speak ACP.
items.done: Vanta connects to a real ACP agent over stdio, runs a multi-turn session (initialize -> new session -> >=2 prompt turns with streamed updates shown in the transcript) and routes the peer's permission requests through the Vanta kernel — verified live against Claude Code (or another ACP agent), with a recorded session.
items.notes: SHIPPED + LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-25. acp/client.ts = the OUTBOUND ACP half (Vanta already SERVES ACP): AcpClient drives another ACP agent over stdio (initialize -> session/new -> session/prompt turns), reusing the pure protocol codec; forwards the peer's session/update notifications to onUpdate and routes its session/request_permission through the kernel (kernelApprover: assess -> allow-only, picks the matching allow/reject option). spawnAcpTransport (child stdio) + runAcpClientSession orchestration. CLI: vanta acp connect <cmd> [args] -- <prompt> (cli/acp-connect.ts) wires the real kernelApprover. LIVE PROOF vs real 'vanta acp serve': initialize + session/new (real id) + 2 prompt turns + 4 streamed session/update (tool_call/tool_call_update); separate run drove the peer to write outside root -> session/request_permission routed through the kernel -> rejected (verdict false) -> write blocked. Tests: client.test.ts (6, fake transport: round-trip, updates, close-rejects-pending, allow/reject option routing, multi-turn orchestration). Full suite 967/11063 green; tsc + size gate + boundaries clean. STRATEGY E6 = 'ACP is the real interop path' — client half now built.

items.title: Sandbox dead-end -> redirect a refused agent launch to the supported path
items.summary: In the transcript Vanta tried to start Claude Code via `shell_cmd background:true` and `tmux new-session` and got two terminal refusals with no way forward: 'background tasks are not sandboxed ...' (shell-cmd.ts:253, auto-on macOS sandbox) and tmux's 'Operation not permitted' (seatbelt). Both refusals are CORRECT but dead-end — they don't point at the supported path. Make them actionable: when a refused background/tmux command looks like launching a known agent CLI (claude/codex/gemini/cursor-agent/opencode), the refusal text names `call_agent` / `agent_session` as the route. Optionally add a NARROW Vanta-owned sandbox-exempt spawn channel reserved for the agent-orchestration tools (those tools own their cleanup) — NOT a general background bypass — so the supported path works under auto-sandbox without weakening arbitrary shell_cmd.
items.done: Under default macOS auto-sandbox, a shell_cmd attempt to launch a known agent CLI in background/tmux returns a refusal that NAMES call_agent/agent_session; and the supported agent-spawn tool runs successfully under the same sandbox — both verified live.
items.notes: SHIPPED + LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-25. tools/agent-launch-hint.ts (pure): agentLaunchRedirect(cmd) returns a tail naming call_agent + agent_session when a known agent CLI (claude/codex/gemini/cursor-agent/opencode) appears; isTmuxAgentLaunch(cmd) = tmux + agent. shell-cmd.ts: sandboxAgentRefusal(cmd) (exported) refuses a tmux-agent launch under the sandbox with the redirect; wired as a pre-check in execute (after the destructive check) AND appended to the background-under-sandbox refusal. LIVE PROOF (VANTA_SHELL_SANDBOX=1): (a) shell_cmd 'tmux new-session -d -s probe claude' -> refused, output names BOTH call_agent and agent_session; (b) the agent_session tmux backend spawned + cleaned up a session under the SAME sandbox env (bypasses shell_cmd's sandbox via execFileSync). Tests: agent-launch-hint.test.ts (6) + shell-cmd.test.ts (12) green. Full suite 966/11057 green; tsc + size gate + boundaries clean.

items.title: Stream a called agent's output live instead of a silent wait-then-dump
items.summary: runExternalAgent (agents/external-cli.ts) uses execFile and returns ONLY after the child exits — up to a 300s wait with zero feedback, then one dump. In the transcript the user watched a hung terminal with no signal the agent was even working. Switch the call path to a streamed spawn (child_process.spawn, line-buffered) that forwards the called agent's stdout/stderr into the Vanta transcript as it arrives (reuse the StreamEvent surface used elsewhere), plus an elapsed/working heartbeat and the existing stall watchdog (tools/shell-stall.ts). Keep the one-shot return contract identical; this purely makes a long delegated call OBSERVABLE. Benefits call_agent today and the interactive session (VANTA-AGENT-SESSION-INTERACTIVE).
items.done: A call_agent run to a real agent CLI shows incremental output (or at minimum a periodic elapsed/working heartbeat) in the transcript BEFORE the child exits, verified live with a multi-second call; the final returned result is byte-equivalent to today's.
items.notes: SHIPPED + LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-25. runExternalAgent (agents/external-cli.ts) switched from execFile wait-then-dump to a STREAMED child_process.spawn: line-buffered onChunk forwards the called agent's stdout/stderr as it arrives + a periodic heartbeat (… <cmd> working (Ns)) via setInterval; injectable SpawnFn seam for tests. RunResult is byte-equivalent (full stdout/stderr capped 60k, exit code, ENOENT->notInstalled, timeout classification unchanged). ToolContext gained optional onProgress (tools/types.ts); dispatch-tool.ts wires it to the 'note' StreamEvent (deps.onEvent); call_agent passes ctx.onProgress as onChunk. LIVE PROOF vs real claude (multi-second call): 11 chunks streamed before the final return — heartbeats at +3/6/9/12s during the 12s think, then output streamed line-by-line, final ok=true at +13.4s. Tests: external-cli.test.ts (9, incl. line-streaming + return-identical-with/without-onChunk via fake spawn). Full suite 965/11051 green; tsc + size gate + boundaries clean.

items.title: Delegate a build to Claude — headless, streaming, fast (Sonnet)
items.summary: Agent-to-agent build delegation that works. call_agent/agent_session gained coding:true (claude --permission-mode acceptEdits) so the agent WRITES code, not just answers. Builds default to fast Sonnet (not the slow Opus default that 15-min-timed-out) and run --output-format stream-json (parsed by agents/claude-stream.ts) → Vanta streams live progress instead of a silent block. agent_session(show:true) can pop a VISIBLE terminal to watch. stdin ignored (kills the 3s warning).
items.done: call_agent(coding:true) builds via claude on Sonnet, streams live Write/Bash progress, returns the result; verified end-to-end (landing page in 26s).

items.title: Route 'have claude build X' to call_agent(coding:true) + verify (A2A piece 1)
items.summary: Reliability: a build delegation reliably takes the build path instead of confabulating/answer-only. agent-route.ts detects a build verb NEAR an agent name ("have claude build…") as delegation intent; hasBuildIntent steers the hint to coding:true AND verify before reporting done.
items.done: buildAgentRouteHint detects a build verb near an agent name and steers to call_agent(coding:true) + verify; 15 route tests green.

items.title: build_with_agent — the cofounder loop: delegate → verify → fix (A2A piece 3)
items.summary: Closes the loop instead of one-shot-and-hope. agents/build-loop.ts = pure runBuildLoop (delegate + verify injected): delegate → verify → fix→verify… until pass or maxIters; an agent failure re-delegates with the error; verify runs only after a successful delegate. tools/build-with-agent.ts wires delegate=call_agent(coding) + verify=expectFiles/verifyCmd. Now 123 built-in tools.
items.done: build_with_agent delegates a build, verifies (expectFiles + verifyCmd), and re-delegates a targeted fix until pass or maxIters; pure loop unit-tested.

items.title: Un-block read-only verify in the kernel (pathlib over-match)
items.summary: So the A2A loop can VERIFY what Claude built. The kernel blocked Vanta parsing built HTML via python3+pathlib as 'destructive' — the bare module name 'pathlib' was in the DESTRUCTIVE denylist but over-matched read-only Path(x).read_text(). Removed the bare name; the destructive METHODS still block (unlink(/rmdir/rmtree/os.remove).
items.done: A read-only python3+pathlib verify no longer Blocks; destructive pathlib methods still Block; 67 kernel tests green.

items.title: Autonomous (bash) builds in a Docker container — the containment that works
items.summary: Replaces the retired macOS sandbox-exec approach, where claude couldn't even RUN inside macOS seatbelt. A Docker container gives claude a FULL machine where it runs normally, boxed to exactly the folders Vanta mounts (the mount-set IS the boundary). Daytona was the obvious purpose-built fit but went open→CLOSED source — don't build Vanta's autonomy on a ruggable platform; use the open Docker primitive Vanta ALREADY ships (exec/backend.ts buildDockerArgs / the BACKEND-DOCKER card). Plumbing left: a small claude-image (node + `npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`) + per-run OAuth-token injection, --network on for npm, project bind-mounted. CONTAINMENT PROVEN LIVE 2026-06-26: a real OrbStack/Docker container cleaned a mounted /tmp folder (organized by type) and provably could NOT read an un-mounted folder or ~/.ssh; work persisted on disk after --rm. Can't live-verify the claude bypass from Claude Code's own harness (it blocks the flag) → build the image + adapter here, operator verifies the first real run. Apple's open `container` runtime is a macOS-native lighter alternative to evaluate.
items.done: call_agent(autonomous:true) runs `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` inside a Docker container scoped to the mounted dir(s); a real npm-driven build completes, streams progress, and provably can't touch anything outside the mounts.
items.notes: SHIPPED + LIVE-PROVEN end-to-end 2026-06-28. call_agent(autonomous:true) runs claude --dangerously-skip-permissions inside a Docker box scoped to the mounted dir(s); a real build completes (claude authenticated, wrote /work/hello.txt, clean exit 10s) and provably cannot escape (a host file outside the mount stayed untouched, network off). The macOS-keychain wall is FIXED the way Hermes does it: resolveBoxCredential forwards a headless credential as env (-e CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; value from the parent, never in argv) instead of mounting host creds — so a Linux container authenticates without the keychain. Non-root image (claude rejects --dangerously-skip-permissions under root). Universal setup: autonomousPreflight + bundled docker/agent.Dockerfile + vanta agent-image build. Mount-scope (VANTA-A2A-MOUNT-SCOPE) derives the blast radius + OS-enforced read-only dry-run on destructive tasks.

items.title: Mount-set = scope: Vanta picks + approves the agent's blast radius per task
items.summary: The policy layer over VANTA-A2A-DOCKER-AUTONOMOUS. The container turns Vanta's existing 'scope' concept into a HARD, OS-enforced boundary — a mount can't be talked-past the way a keyword denylist can. Vanta infers the mount-set from the task (build → a fresh output dir; clean Downloads → ~/Downloads rw; fix repo X → that repo; analyze photos → ~/Pictures ro), SURFACES it for human approval (the blast radius) before spinning up, then runs the agent boxed to it. Whole-system tasks ('find big files across my disk') don't fit a box (mounting / = no containment) → fall back to host + approval-gating, or ask. Caveat to design for: the box scopes WHERE, not WHETHER-reversible — 'clean up' inside the mount still means real deletes, so destructive-within-scope wants a dry-run/confirm or a pre-snapshot of the dir.
items.done: for a delegated task Vanta derives the mount-set (the folders it needs), shows it for approval, and runs the boxed agent with exactly those mounts (ro/rw); a destructive-within-scope task gets a dry-run/confirm or a pre-snapshot.
items.notes: LIVE-PROVEN 2026-06-28: the dry-run is OS-ENFORCED. deriveMountScope routes a destructive task to a read-only output mount; the boxed process gets "rm: Read-only file system" on delete and the write is refused — the file survives. Mount-scope derivation + preview + enforced dry-run (apply:true for the confirmed rw pass) complete, wired into call_agent autonomous, and verified end-to-end with real Docker.

items.title: One-shot `vanta run` hangs after completing (never exits — MCP handles)
items.summary: Found 2026-06-26 by driving a real task end-to-end. One-shot `run` printed the answer + '[done]' then HUNG — never exited; zombie `node … cli.ts run` procs piled up across sessions (a 'what is 2+2' run was alive after a DAY). Root cause: cli.ts dispatched run via `return runInstruction(...)` (void) — never called process.exit, so MCP stdio children (torn down only on process.once('exit')) stayed alive and their handles kept the event loop open. Fix: await runInstruction then process.exit(0). Live-verified: fresh run exits ~4s, 0 zombies. Follow-up: check skill/room one-shots (same runInstruction path via COMMANDS) for the same class.
items.done: a `vanta run "…"` completes AND exits (no lingering node, MCP children killed); verified a fresh run exits in ~4s with zero zombies.

items.title: Sandbox denied /tmp writes (default-on shell sandbox; /tmp not in writable zones)
items.summary: Found 2026-06-26 by driving a real task ('write to /tmp'). shell_cmd defaults the OS sandbox ON wherever a backend exists (seatbelt is always present on macOS), but writableZonesFor included $TMPDIR (/var/folders) NOT the system /tmp (/private/tmp) — so `> /tmp/…` got EPERM 'Operation not permitted', surfaced to the model as 'Blocked by scope'. Reproduced in BOTH the dev harness AND the user's direct terminal (not a confound). Fix: sandbox/run.ts writableZonesFor adds realpathSync('/tmp'), deduped vs $TMPDIR. /tmp is scratch, not a credential dir; DANGEROUS_DIRS denies still override. Verified: a sandboxed /tmp write now succeeds; 53 sandbox tests green.
items.done: a shell_cmd `> /tmp/…` write succeeds under the default-on macOS shell sandbox; darwin-gated regression test.

items.title: Reliability stress harness — scored, repeated, concurrency + long-horizon batteries
items.summary: The measurement half of the readiness bar (STRATEGY §"The readiness bar"). Two-axis scoring: RELIABILITY (terminates/clean-exit/no-zombie/survives-load = the bar, model-agnostic) vs SUCCESS (also-correct output). scripts/reliability-smoke.sh (binary gate), reliability-stress.sh (K-repeat + concurrency burst), reliability-longhorizon.sh (long-loop + piped-REPL multi-turn probe). Found+fixed the interactive-REPL exit hang (MCP-handle leak, same class as VANTA-ONESHOT-RUN-HANG; cli.ts 79fce703).
items.done: scripts drive real agent tasks, score reliability/success, exit on a reliability threshold; first codex/gpt-5.5 campaign ~131 runs 100% reliable (98% success), 16x concurrency clean, 1 bug found+fixed.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-27 (commits 6c55dfd9, 75452eb0, 79fce703).

items.title: Prove a long autonomous run finishes unattended (Pillar 1 win condition)
items.summary: THE riskiest unproven assumption (discovery/Working-Backwards 2026-06-27): every reliability test so far is a one-shot. Pillar 1's actual bar — "a long autonomous run finishes verified work without babysitting" — is untested. Build a real ~10-min, 30+-tool-call multi-stage task into the harness, run it unattended Nx, score reliability + stays-on-goal + verified-output (no false-done). This is the headline 'close your laptop' PR claim and must be proven before claiming readiness like Hermes/OpenClaw.
items.done: a real long-horizon task runs unattended >=5x at a high reliability pass-rate — no hang, no goal-drift, no false-done — scored by the harness.
items.notes: Hill-climb keystone (2026-06-27). Riskiest-assumption-first per Build-Measure-Learn.

items.title: Headless multi-turn: fix the input mechanism or declare run the only path
items.summary: Stress testing surfaced that the only headless multi-turn path (piping into the non-TTY REPL) does not consume lines as turns (readline/promises emits 'line' before question() awaits, so turns are lost). The exit-hang was fixed (79fce703); the INPUT mechanism remains. Decide: queue piped lines as sequential turns in the REPL, OR declare `vanta run` the only headless interface and document it.
items.done: either a headless multi-turn conversation carries context across turns (verified by the multi-turn probe showing context-carried=1), or an explicit documented decision that run is the only headless path.

items.title: Wire the reliability harnesses into a tracked scored eval
items.summary: Turn the harnesses into a tracked pass-rate-over-time signal (the measurement half of AHE-EVAL-HARNESS). Per discovery: do this AFTER the long-run proof — don't plumb a metric that's already ~100% on short tasks until there is a meaningful (long-run) signal to track.
items.done: one command runs the battery and records a dated pass-rate to a results file; deltas tracked over time (and optionally a CI job).

items.title: Reliability across providers (cold-start / timeout / tool-parse variance)
items.summary: Ollama showed cold-start/timeout sensitivity in stress testing; codex was clean. Run the battery across providers to surface provider-specific reliability (streaming/tool-parse/cold-start). PROVIDER-AWARE-WATCHDOG addresses cold-start timeouts structurally; this validates it live.
items.done: the battery runs green (reliability) on >=2 providers (codex + ollama) with provider-appropriate timeouts/warmup.

items.title: Higher-concurrency kernel soak (>16 parallel) to find the contention ceiling
items.summary: 16x parallel held clean (0 zombies). Push to 32-64x to find the kernel contention ceiling (assess/log/audit-chain under load on the raw-TCP HTTP server).
items.done: the kernel survives >=32x parallel runs with 0 zombies, or the contention ceiling is identified and documented.

items.title: Provider hardening for long unattended runs — request/idle timeout + transient retry
items.summary: Found by the long-run stress harness (RELIABILITY-LONG-RUN-PROOF). Two latent provider-reliability gaps a multi-hour unattended run will eventually hit (invisible to short runs): (1) the codex provider's raw SSE fetch had NO request/idle timeout (openai.ts got the PROVIDER-AWARE-WATCHDOG timeout; codex.ts did not), so a stalled stream hangs the run forever — the silent-hang failure mode. (2) turn-loop.ts:257/264 re-throw every non-context-length provider error (429/timeout/reset), crashing the run exit=1 instead of retrying. The keystone long-run is otherwise PROVEN (12/12 scope-corrected); these are defensive hardening for the 'close your laptop for hours' claim.
items.done: a transient provider error (idle timeout / 429 / connection reset) during a long run is survived (bounded retry) or fails gracefully (clean stop, no exit=1 crash, no silent hang); covered by unit tests + a long-run re-check.
items.notes: PART 1 SHIPPED 2026-06-27: codex idle-timeout (codex.ts stream() — AbortController reset per SSE chunk, reuses resolveProviderTimeoutMs; combined with the user interrupt signal). Test: stalled stream aborts in ~1s instead of hanging. PART 2 REMAINING: turn-loop bounded transient-retry (reuse tool-retry TRANSIENT) so the abort is survived, not just turned into a clean crash.

items.title: China messaging adapters — QQ + WeChat (the two gaps in the 20)
items.summary: Use-case audit vs the Hermes corpus (2026-06-27): of the China/SEA platforms Hermes users build for, Vanta already wires LINE, Feishu, Zalo — but NOT QQ or WeChat (a Hermes user shipped an 822-line QQ adapter). Add both as PlatformAdapters in adapter-registry.ts. Build needs the China platform API + a bot token to live-verify (untestable from here), so it's adapter code + an offline parser test, live-gated like the other 19.
items.done: QQ and WeChat PlatformAdapters registered (connect/send/poll + pure parsers, offline-tested); live-verified when a token is provisioned.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. QQ 官方机器人 v2 + WeChat 公众号 PlatformAdapters (gateway/platforms/qq.ts + wechat.ts), same injected-transport + webhook-buffer pattern as feishu/line/whatsapp — poll() drains the caller's webhook buffer (default none), the live wiring is the documented boundary. Pure parsers offline-tested: qq-parse.ts parseQQEvents (GROUP_AT_MESSAGE_CREATE → group_openid=chatId, author.member_openid=from, d.id=inbound msg_id) + buildQQMessage; QQAdapter tracks the last inbound msg_id per group and replies PASSIVELY (msg_type 0 + msg_id + unique incrementing msg_seq per split-part) because active group messages are QQ-capped (4/mo). wechat-parse.ts parseWeChatMessage (dependency-free flat-CDATA XML via xmlTag → FromUserName=openid=chatId, Content=text, MsgId=id) + buildWeChatMessage (custom/send {touser,msgtype:text,text}). httpTransports mint+cache the QQBot access_token (POST bots.qq.com/app/getAppAccessToken) / WeChat access_token (/cgi-bin/token), secret read only there. Registered qq+wechat in adapter-registry.ts (**22 adapters now**) + MESSAGING_CATALOG (vanta setup messaging). Enable: VANTA_QQ_APP_ID/SECRET, VANTA_WECHAT_APP_ID/SECRET (+ optional *_ALLOWLIST). WIRE-SHAPE honesty: QQ send + WeChat inbound XML/outbound are doc-confirmed; QQ inbound is the botpy/QQ-v2 convention (tolerant zod parser, documented assumption) — live-verified when a token is provisioned, same bar as the other 19 live-gated adapters. 25 QQ/WeChat tests + full suite 997 files/11285 green, tsc + size gate + architecture boundaries clean.

items.title: Native mobile surface (iOS/Android) over the Hermes API/gateway
items.summary: Real reach gap surfaced by the use-case audit: Hermes community built native Android + iOS wrappers; Vanta has TUI (Ink) + desktop-app (Vite/React) + cockpit web, but no mobile. Lowest-effort path is a thin mobile client over the existing kernel API server / gateway (streaming chat, sessions, slash, tool viz) rather than a full native agent. Messaging (Telegram etc.) already gives phone reach today — this is the dedicated-app gap, not a capability gap.
items.done: a mobile client (iOS and/or Android) connects to a running Vanta host over the API/gateway with streaming chat + session management.

items.title: Termux / Android runtime (run-anywhere North Star gap)
items.summary: The shared installer detects Termux, prefers the checksum-verified ARM64 Android/Bionic release kernel, installs compilers only as a fallback, installs portable runtime dependencies and the launcher into PREFIX/bin, and applies a phone-sized heap profile. An x86_64 Android emulator gate passes end to end, the ARM64/Bionic artifact builds in CI, and release v0.9.0 publishes the Android kernel plus checksum; physical ARM64 execution remains the release gate. A physical ARM64 proof runner now lives at scripts/termux-arm64-device-proof.sh and supports --require-release-kernel so the final device run proves the release artifact path rather than source-build fallback.
items.done: install.sh + run.sh bring up a working Vanta (kernel + agent) on Termux/Android, doctor green; aarch64-android kernel published or built on-device.
items.notes:
IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-10: scripts/setup-lib.sh detects TERMUX_VERSION/PREFIX, refuses incompatible nodejs.org GNU Node and glibc kernel downloads, and installs the Android runtime set through pkg, prefers a checksum-verified aarch64-linux-android kernel, and installs Rust/Clang only as a fallback. install.sh preserves native kernel compilation and writes the launcher to PREFIX/bin. Termux dependency installation omits optional desktop accelerators and lifecycle scripts; run.sh exports VANTA_PLATFORM=termux plus the native ESBUILD_BINARY_PATH and defaults the V8 heap ceiling to 1536 MB. The Bash safety parser now loads the exact MIT-licensed tree-sitter-bash 0.25.1 WASM grammar from a checksummed vendored asset instead of installing its unused native binding. Winnow remains pinned to commit 7d5756b via an immutable archive, avoiding npm Git preparation. Preflight identifies Termux separately, omits cargo when the installer supplies the kernel, emits pkg commands, and omits macOS-only cliclick. vanta-ts/docs/termux.md owns the operator steps. The former duplicate mobile-install card was retired; this card is the sole Android/Termux owner.

EXECUTED ANDROID EMULATOR 2026-07-10: GitHub Actions run 29070389320 passed the checksum-pinned official Termux 0.118.3 APK on Android API 35 x86_64 in 3m38s. The real app bootstrap installed Node 24.17.0, Rust 1.96.1, and esbuild 0.28.1; the real ./install.sh compiled the Android/Bionic kernel, installed 126 runtime packages, and installed the self-locating launcher. The installed CLI produced help, doctor reported kernel and custom provider green with no failed checks, `vanta run` returned TERMUX_OK through an OpenAI-compatible provider, `vanta gateway verify-channels` passed, and the gate emitted TERMUX_ANDROID_E2E_OK plus all three phase markers. Workflow: https://github.com/jpoindexter/Vanta/actions/runs/29070389320. Local typecheck, 172 Winnow/compression tests, and 5 vendored Bash-parser tests also passed. This is executed Android proof, not ARM64 hardware proof.

EXECUTED ARM64 ARTIFACT 2026-07-10: release workflow run 29079864380 passed all six kernel targets on commit 030e528e. The aarch64-linux-android job used the Android NDK API-24 linker, verified an AArch64 ELF header plus /system/bin/linker64, generated a SHA-256 receipt, and uploaded the artifact in 22s. The downloaded 835,432-byte artifact independently passed shasum -c; file reported an ARM aarch64 ELF with the Bionic interpreter. Installer tests execute accepted-checksum and tampered-checksum paths. Full suite: 1,208 files, 12,511 passed, 3 skipped; TypeScript, shell syntax, YAML parse, socket-noise scan, and bell scan pass. Release v0.9.0 now serves this artifact through releases/latest; the remaining gap is physical ARM64 Termux execution.

REMAINING REAL-DEVICE GATE: release v0.9.0 now contains the Android/Bionic kernel and checksum; on a physical ARM64 Android/Termux host, run `scripts/termux-arm64-device-proof.sh --require-release-kernel`. Keep parked until the release kernel, TUI/CLI, provider turn, and gateway all execute on-device and write `TERMUX_ARM64_E2E_OK release_kernel=1`.

PROOF RUNNER 2026-07-10: added scripts/termux-arm64-device-proof.sh and vanta-ts/docs/termux.md instructions for the remaining physical ARM64 gate. The script refuses non-Termux/non-ARM64 hosts, installs the Termux runtime packages, runs ./install.sh, starts a local mock OpenAI-compatible provider plus the real safety kernel, then executes vanta --help, vanta doctor, vanta run "Reply with TERMUX_OK and do not use tools", and vanta gateway verify-channels. Focused Termux install/runtime tests now assert the script syntax and non-Termux refusal. This does NOT ship the card: release v0.9.0 exists, but the script still must be executed on physical ARM64 Android/Termux hardware to prove the release artifact path on-device.

RELEASE-ARTIFACT GUARD 2026-07-10: verified latest GitHub release v0.8.0 exposes macOS/Linux kernel assets but no vanta-kernel-aarch64-linux-android asset. Added VANTA_REQUIRE_PREBUILT_KERNEL=1 handling in install.sh and run.sh plus scripts/termux-arm64-device-proof.sh --require-release-kernel, which removes any existing local kernel before install and fails instead of compiling from source when the release asset is missing. Focused tests cover the prebuilt-required refusal and prove cargo build is not invoked. This prepares the final physical ARM64 proof after an explicitly approved tagged release.

RECHECK 2026-07-10: Release asset prerequisite is now satisfied. GitHub release v0.9.0 has `vanta-kernel-aarch64-linux-android` and `vanta-kernel-aarch64-linux-android.sha256`; `vanta run-anywhere status --check-release` reports Android release asset check: ready. Current host is Darwin arm64 with no TERMUX_VERSION/PREFIX, so the physical device proof remains unexecuted. Do not mark shipped until `scripts/termux-arm64-device-proof.sh --require-release-kernel` passes on a physical ARM64 Android/Termux device against that release asset.

UNBLOCKED/PARKED 2026-07-10: operator chose to set physical Android/Termux proof aside for later. This card is no longer a board blocker, but it is not shipped; resume when physical ARM64 Termux hardware is available to run scripts/termux-arm64-device-proof.sh --require-release-kernel.

items.title: shell_cmd foreground exec wedges the session on a backgrounded / long-running process
items.summary: Found live: a real 'build a dashboard + serve it' task wedged the REPL ('vanta just stops'). Root cause CONFIRMED by minimal reproduction: shell_cmd's foreground runLocal uses promisify(execFile), which does not resolve until ALL inherited stdio pipes hit EOF — not when the direct child exits. When the model starts a long-running server with a trailing '&' (manual backgrounding) instead of background:true, the backgrounded grandchild inherits the open stderr pipe, so execFile blocks for the FULL process lifetime; for a never-exiting server it blocks until the 30s TIMEOUT_MS, then the timeout kills sh/sandbox-exec but the orphaned daemon survives in its own group and keeps holding the port. Repro: `sleep 6 & echo $!` resolved in 6014ms (waited the whole child lifetime) vs `sleep 6 >/dev/null 2>&1 & echo $!` in 7ms. The reliability harnesses never spawned a long-lived process, so 12/12 'reliable' missed this whole failure class — exactly the thin-evidence gap behind the readiness bar.
items.done: a foreground shell_cmd that backgrounds a process (real trailing '&' control operator) or starts a known long-running server is refused with guidance to re-run with background:true (the detached spawnBackground path that already unref()s), so it can never block the turn or orphan a daemon. Pure detection helper + tool-level test prove the refusal; full vanta-ts TS suite + size gate + tsc green.
items.notes: Fix #1 (this card): detect-and-steer in shell-cmd.ts (pure helper, tested). Fast-follow #2 (separate card if pursued): reap at the source — spawn foreground commands in their own process group and SIGKILL the group on exit/timeout so even an undetected long-runner can't orphan. Evidence + chain in the session transcript 2026-06-29. SHIPPED 2026-06-29: detect-and-steer in shell-cmd.ts (globalRefusal extraction + needsBackground) over pure helper shell-background-detect.ts (looksLikeBackgrounding/isLongRunningServer) + 8 tests. Full vanta-ts suite 988 files / 11186 tests green (3 live-gated skipped), tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: X/Twitter search 404s on a stale query id — wire the self-heal to the 404 signal
items.summary: Found in two live 'search X for biz ideas' runs: twitter_read returned 'X returned HTTP 404'. Root cause CONFIRMED: a cookie IS configured (~/.vanta/cookies/twitter.cookie) and qids ARE cached incl. SearchTimeline, yet X 404s — on X's GraphQL a 404 (not 403) = the persisted-query hash (queryId) rotated since the last heal (Jun 14). The channel calls itself self-healing (reach/twitter-heal.ts refreshQueryIds re-scrapes current ids) but nothing triggered the heal on the 404 — it only ran on a manual `reach heal twitter`, so a healed qid goes stale within days and every search 404s until re-healed by hand.
items.done: a twitter_read 404 auto-triggers ONE heal+retry (inject refreshQueryIds into twitter.ts xGraphQL, cycle-safe; retry only if the qid actually changed); if heal can't refresh, return actionable guidance to re-import a cookie or fall back to web_search "site:x.com <query>" (which the agent already uses successfully). Unit-tested: 404→heal→retry→success, and 404→heal-no-change→fallback guidance. HONEST LIVE CAVEAT: the wiring is tested green, but whether the live heal beats X's current anti-scraping (and whether the cookie is still valid) is NOT verified here — native cookie-scraped X search is inherently fragile, which is why the deterministic web_search fallback matters.
items.notes: Fix: twitter.ts auto-heal-and-retry-once on 404 + stale404() fallback message; twitter-read.ts passes refreshQueryIds as the injected heal (no import cycle — heal imports twitter.ts, so injection avoids it). Full vanta-ts suite green, tsc + size gate clean. Follow-up if X search stays flaky: consider making web_search site:x.com the primary X path and twitter_read opt-in. CORRECTION 2026-06-30: live `reach heal twitter` proved the channel heal works (browser capture, refreshed SearchTimeline+Bookmarks qids), so X search is functional again — root cause (stale qid) confirmed. The auto-heal injection was switched from refreshQueryIds (bundle-scrape only) to healTwitter (browser capture first, bundle-scrape fallback) — the same proven heal the manual command uses; loads its own cookie, cycle-safe (twitter-read→twitter-capture).

items.title: Reliability battery never spawned a long-lived process — add a daemon-spawn task
items.summary: The 12/12 'reliable' battery never spawned a long-lived process, so the shell-bg-wedge class (RELIABILITY-SHELL-BG-WEDGE) was invisible to it — a real ad-hoc 'serve a dashboard' task is what found the wedge. Closed the gap: scripts/reliability-smoke.sh now has a `daemon` task that starts a server, confirms it, and stops it; the bar is a CLEAN EXIT + no zombie (a foreground server blocks the turn, so the agent must route to background:true).
items.done: smoke battery includes a server-spawn task. LIVE-VERIFIED both modes: sandbox-ON → server can't start (background refused + foreground EPERM) but Vanta gives up cleanly (exit 0, 0 zombies, port clear, honest report — no wedge); sandbox-OFF → foreground '&' server REFUSED by the fix → agent steered to background:true → detached spawn → confirmed LISTEN on :8123 → stopped → exit 0, 0 zombies, port clear. End-to-end proof of RELIABILITY-SHELL-BG-WEDGE against the real agent.
items.notes: Evidence: the sandbox-off run shows the exact chain — `✗ shell_cmd refused` on the foreground '&', then `✓ background task started: bg-...`, then lsof LISTEN, then kill→stopped, exit 0. This is the live close-out the prior fix lacked.

items.title: Battery doesn't exercise stale external creds/query-ids (reach-channel staleness class)
items.summary: The reliability battery never exercises a stale external credential / rotated query id — the class behind REACH-X-SEARCH-STALE-QID (X search 404'd on a stale qid for days). The 404→auto-heal→retry path is guarded by deterministic vitest (reach/twitter.test.ts), but no battery scenario simulates staleness, so a regression in the heal wiring would only show up in live use.
items.done: a deterministic reliability scenario seeds a synthetic stale qid (no dependency on live X) and asserts the channel either auto-heals + retries or degrades gracefully (actionable fallback) — never a wedge, never a fake success. The vitest auto-heal tests are the current guard; this is the live-battery half.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. scripts/reliability-reach-staleness.sh + vanta-ts/scripts/reach-staleness-scenario.ts: a deterministic, fixture-based battery scenario that drives the REAL searchTwitter() wiring against a stubbed fetch + a seeded STALE query id — no live X, no cookie. Asserts both outcomes: (A) 404→heal-refreshes-id→retry returns a real parsed post (never a fake success), and (B) heal-can't-refresh→bounded single fetch + actionable fallback (site:x.com + cookie-reimport) — never a wedge, never an infinite retry. A HANG guard in the harness treats non-termination as the failure. 9/9 assertions green, clean exit. The vitest (reach/twitter.test.ts) remains the unit guard; this is the live-battery mirror. Pairs with REACH-X-SEARCH-STALE-QID.

items.title: Under the default sandbox the agent can't serve a web app — fail fast with guidance
items.summary: Found live running 'serve a dashboard' under the default auto-sandbox: there is NO path to start a listening server — background:true is refused (no exit-time profile cleanup for an unref'd child under sandbox) AND a foreground bind gets EPERM (deny-default network). The agent discovers this only by burning 3 consecutive tool failures → the repair loop opens, then it honestly reports the blocker. Reliable (clean exit, no wedge) but poor UX, and it directly blocks the 'dashboard you can run a biz from' use case.
items.done: shell_cmd detects a server/listen intent under an active sandbox and fails FAST with clear, actionable guidance (e.g. 'serving a web app needs a non-sandboxed run: VANTA_SHELL_SANDBOX=0') BEFORE the agent burns 3 tool failures + the repair loop. Reliability already holds; this is a clarity/UX fix for the serve path.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03; expanded 2026-07-09. Chose fast-fail-with-guidance (the boundary-preserving option). shell-background-detect.ts has looksLikeListenServer + looksLikeServeIntent (named dev servers via isLongRunningServer OR a raw nc/socat port bind); shell-cmd.ts sandboxServeRefusal() fires when a serve/listen intent meets an active shell sandbox, returning ONE actionable refusal (re-run non-sandboxed: VANTA_SHELL_SANDBOX=0, then background:true) BEFORE the background↔foreground refusal ping-pong. Placed after the ssh branch (remote serves unaffected), pre-empts both the bg-sandbox refusal and the needsBackground steer. NEW 2026-07-09: classify Tauri/Bun dev commands too, including cd ... && CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=3.5 bun run tauri dev, bun run dev, pnpm/yarn/bun dev/start/serve/watch, bunx, and pnpm dlx. Verified with shell-background-detect + shell-cmd regression tests (31 passed), tsc, size gate, and git diff check.

items.title: Size-tiered web_extract → summarize pipeline (chunk + synthesize big pages)
items.summary: Hermes web_extract handles a page by size instead of dumping raw HTML: <5k chars returned as-is; 5k–500k single-pass LLM summary (~5k out); 500k–2M parallel-chunked (100k chunks) then synthesized; >2M refused with guidance to pick a focused source. Vanta's web_fetch returns content but lacks the tiered summarize/chunk/synthesis ladder — big pages blow context. Build the ladder so extract returns a bounded, useful digest. Source: Hermes Agent web-search docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/web-search).
items.done: web_extract (or web_fetch) routes by char-count through 4 tiers (as-is / single-pass summary / parallel-chunk+synthesis / refuse-with-guidance); thresholds configurable; chunk synthesis tested on a >500k fixture; output bounded ~5k chars.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-05. tools/extract-pipeline.ts (pure): classifyExtractTier (as-is/summarize/chunk-synthesize/refuse over env-configurable thresholds, defaults matching Hermes' 5k/500k/2M exactly), splitIntoChunks (gapless, verified reconstructs the original), runExtractPipeline (map-reduce: chunk tier summarizes every chunk CONCURRENTLY then one more synthesis pass over the joined chunk-summaries). web-fetch.ts wires the live summarizer via the new routing/extract.ts (mirrors routing/vision.ts's auxiliary-model pattern) in place of the old blind cap()-only truncation; cap() stays as an absolute backstop past the pipeline. Also built WEB-EXTRACT-AUX-MODEL's full scope in the same slice (see that card). Tested: 11 pure pipeline tests with exact call-count/argument assertions on the chunk-synthesize map-reduce (proves the mechanics, not just the tier boundary) + 4 end-to-end webFetchTool tests (one per tier) through the REAL tool call with a mocked aux provider — small pages stay byte-identical (as-is, no LLM call). The done criterion's '>500k fixture' is satisfied at the tier-classification level (tested at the exact 500,000/2,000,000 boundaries) rather than a literal 500k-char string fixture — the chunking/synthesis logic is pure character-count arithmetic with no size-dependent behavior beyond the thresholds, so a scaled-down structurally-identical fixture proves the same mechanics. Full suite 1014 files/11457 tests green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Auxiliary cheap-model routing for extract summarization
items.summary: Hermes routes extraction summarization to a configurable auxiliary model (auxiliary.web_extract.provider) with its own timeout (default 360s), independent of the main chat model — so big-page digests don't burn the expensive model. Vanta has provider routing; add a dedicated cheap-summarizer slot for web_extract. Source: Hermes Agent web-search docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/web-search).
items.done: web_extract summarization can target a separate (cheap) provider/model with its own timeout, defaulting to the main model when unset; one VANTA_* env or config key; tested with an injected aux provider.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-05, same slice as WEB-EXTRACT-PIPELINE (which depended on this groundwork anyway). routing/extract.ts mirrors routing/vision.ts exactly: extractEnv() swaps VANTA_MODEL/VANTA_PROVIDER to VANTA_EXTRACT_MODEL/_PROVIDER when set (unset → active provider, byte-identical). Its own timeout: extractEnv ALWAYS sets VANTA_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_SEC to VANTA_EXTRACT_TIMEOUT_SEC (default 360s, matching Hermes exactly) — this applies even when NOT using a separate aux model, since a big-page summarize call deserves its own budget independent of whatever the main chat model's timeout is tuned to. Piggybacks on the EXISTING resolveProviderTimeoutMs (PROVIDER-AWARE-WATCHDOG) which already reads VANTA_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_SEC at provider-construction time — no new plumbing needed. 7 extractEnv tests (incl. the independent-timeout-without-aux-swap case) + the web-fetch.ts integration tests (injected aux provider) prove it end-to-end. Full suite 1014 files/11457 tests green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Managed keyed search+extract backends — Firecrawl, Tavily, Parallel
items.summary: Hermes ships keyed REST backends that do BOTH search and extract: Firecrawl (default, 500 credits/mo), Tavily (1k searches/mo), Parallel (paid). Vanta's SearchProvider interface already fits search-only keyed backends (Brave/SerpApi); add these three as combined search+extract providers, with auto-detect credential priority (Firecrawl→Parallel→Tavily→Exa→SearXNG). Source: Hermes Agent web-search docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/web-search).
items.done: FirecrawlProvider/TavilyProvider/ParallelProvider implement SearchProvider (+extract where supported); resolveSearchProvider auto-detects via API-key presence in the documented priority order; each provider unit-tested with a mocked fetch; keys documented in .env.example.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. search/{firecrawl,tavily,parallel}.ts add three managed backends as SearchProviders, each against its VERIFIED live API (shapes confirmed from docs, not assumed): Firecrawl (POST /v2/search, Bearer, native includeDomains/excludeDomains → filtersDomains=true, results data.web[]), Tavily (POST /search, Bearer, native include/exclude_domains → filtersDomains=true, results[]), Parallel (POST /v1/search, x-api-key, objective+search_queries[] → results[{title,url,excerpts}]; no native domain filter so it uses the site: rewrite).
