Each has a pure build*Body + map*Json (skip title/url-less, cap to max, tolerant) unit-tested with fixtures. resolveSearchProviders auto-detects by API-key presence in the documented priority Firecrawl→Parallel→Tavily→Exa→…; resolveNamedProvider refactored to a KEYED data table (holds the growing switch under the cx gate). Keys documented in .env.example. 137 search/ tests green; tsc + size gate clean. The '+extract' half awaits WEB-BACKEND-SPLIT (no extract-backend concept yet). Skipped Noun Portal/Tool Gateway per prior note.

items.title: Exa neural/semantic search+extract backend
items.summary: Exa (1k searches/mo) does embeddings/neural retrieval rather than keyword match — a genuinely different recall profile from the existing keyword scrapers, useful for 'find pages about X' queries. Add as a SearchProvider with extract support; folds into the RRF fusion chain. Source: Hermes Agent web-search docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/web-search).
items.done: ExaProvider implements SearchProvider (+extract); selectable via VANTA_SEARCH_PROVIDER=exa and eligible in the auto chain; mocked-fetch unit tests; key in .env.example.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. search/exa.ts: ExaProvider implements SearchProvider against the VERIFIED live Exa API (POST api.exa.ai/search, x-api-key; shape confirmed via docs, not assumed). Pure buildExaBody (maps our SearchConfig domain scope → Exa's NATIVE includeDomains/excludeDomains; requests a text snippet) + mapExaJson (highlights→text→summary snippet, skips title/url-less, caps to max, tolerant). Because Exa filters domains natively, ExaProvider sets filtersDomains=true — making it the FIRST real consumer of the WEB-DOMAIN-SCOPING seam (the tool passes the scope through instead of a site: rewrite). Wired: VANTA_SEARCH_PROVIDER=exa (named, key-gated) + leads the auto chain when EXA_API_KEY is set; EXA_API_KEY documented in .env.example. 9 exa + 8 resolver tests; 121 search/ tests green; tsc + size gate clean. The '+extract' backend half is deferred with WEB-BACKEND-SPLIT (no extract-backend concept exists yet); this is the search side.

items.title: xAI Grok native grounded-search backend (+ domain filters)
items.summary: Hermes can use xAI/Grok's native live search: a reasoning model (default grok-build-0.1) returns grounded results, with allowed_domains/excluded_domains (max 5 each, mutually exclusive), and a 90s timeout. Different shape from REST search — the model does the searching. Source: Hermes Agent web-search docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/web-search).
items.done: XaiSearchProvider implements SearchProvider via the xAI live-search API; supports model override, allowed/excluded_domains (capped 5, mutually exclusive, validated), and timeout; auth via XAI_API_KEY; mocked unit tests; key in .env.example.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-05. Verified the REAL API before coding (docs.x.ai + a real integration bug — github.com/openclaw/openclaw#13171 — confirmed the response has NO top-level output_text/citations; the actual path is output[].content[].annotations on the message's output_text block, each annotation carrying {title,url,start_index,end_index}). search/xai.ts: XaiSearchProvider calls POST /v1/responses with a web_search tool (domain filters nest under tools[0].filters.allowed_domains|excluded_domains, verified shape); mapXaiResponse maps each citation annotation to a SearchResult (title/url + the cited text span as snippet) — a fundamentally different shape from every other backend (one reasoning-model answer with citations, not a REST result index), but a genuinely honest, non-lossy mapping since annotations already carry structured {title,url} pairs. filtersDomains=true (native, capped at 5 each, mutually exclusive — validateXaiDomains rejects both the cap and combining allowed+excluded, defense-in-depth since web_search's shared validateDomainScope already enforces this upstream). Model override via VANTA_XAI_SEARCH_MODEL (default grok-4.3 — the roadmap card's 'grok-build-0.1' was stale; verified the current docs example). 90s timeout (matches the card; xAI's own live-search call is itself agentic/multi-step). Wired into the resolver (named 'xai' + auto-chain right after Exa). 13 xai.ts tests (incl. the exact real-shape fixture proving the openclaw bug class doesn't recur) + 3 resolver tests; full suite 1015 files/11473 tests green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Per-capability search/extract backend split
items.summary: Hermes lets you pick different backends per capability (search_backend: searxng, extract_backend: firecrawl) instead of one backend for both — e.g. cheap/keyless search + high-quality extract. Vanta resolves one provider; split the resolver so search and extract can target different backends. Source: Hermes Agent web-search docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/web-search).
items.done: resolveSearchProvider supports independent VANTA_SEARCH_BACKEND vs VANTA_EXTRACT_BACKEND (falling back to the single VANTA_SEARCH_PROVIDER); web_search and web_extract route independently; tested for both-split and single-value configs.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-07. search/index.ts: resolveSearchProviders/resolveSearchProvider gained a WebCapability param ('search'|'extract', default 'search'). backendIdFor(env, capability) picks the id: the per-capability override (VANTA_SEARCH_BACKEND for search, VANTA_EXTRACT_BACKEND for extract) if set, else the shared VANTA_SEARCH_PROVIDER, else 'auto' — so cheap/keyless search + high-quality extract can run at once (Hermes' search_backend/extract_backend split) while a single VANTA_SEARCH_PROVIDER still drives both, BACK-COMPATIBLE (existing configs byte-identical: no per-cap key → falls back exactly as before). web_search now calls resolveSearchProviders(env, 'search') explicitly; the extract-capable path resolves via 'extract'. .env.example documents both keys. 6 new tests (backendIdFor override/shared/auto precedence, search≠extract when split, single-value drives both, default-capability) — the existing resolution tests pass untouched. Full suite 1044 files/11733 green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: allowed_domains / excluded_domains scoping on web_search
items.summary: Hermes supports domain allow/exclude lists (mutually exclusive, capped) so a search stays inside or avoids specific sites. Vanta agents already hand-roll this via 'site:x.com' query hacks (see REACH-X-SEARCH-STALE-QID). Add a first-class allowed/excluded_domains param on web_search, applied per-provider (native where supported, query-rewrite fallback otherwise). Source: Hermes Agent web-search docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/web-search).
items.done: web_search accepts allowed_domains|excluded_domains (validated mutually-exclusive, capped); providers that support it natively pass it through, others get a site:/-site: query rewrite; tested for both paths.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. web_search gained allowed_domains|excluded_domains params. search/scope.ts (pure): validateDomainScope (mutually-exclusive + capped at MAX_SCOPE_DOMAINS=10 + non-blank), hasDomainScope, and scopeQuery (allowed → `q (site:a OR site:b)`, excluded → `q -site:a -site:b`, normalizing scheme/path/case). SearchProvider gained a filtersDomains capability flag; the extracted testable searchAcross(providers, query, config) router passes domains through to a native-filtering provider (raw query) and query-rewrites for every other provider (dropping the domain config) — proving both paths. No built-in provider sets filtersDomains yet (all use the rewrite), but the seam is ready for xAI Grok (WEB-BACKEND-XAI-GROK). Replaces the ad-hoc 'site:' hacks. 10 web-search + 9 scope tests; 112 search/ tests green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Search category filtering + pagination
items.summary: Hermes' searxng-search skill teaches category filtering (news/images/etc.) and pagination handling over the search API. Vanta returns a flat first page; add optional category + page/offset params so agents can scope by type and walk deeper result sets. Source: Hermes Agent web-search docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/web-search).
items.done: web_search accepts optional category and page/offset params, honored by providers that support them (SearXNG natively) and ignored gracefully elsewhere; tested.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-03. web_search gained optional category + 1-based page params, carried through SearchConfig. SearXNG honors them natively: buildSearxngUrl (extracted pure fn) adds &categories= and &pageno= when present, plain first-page search otherwise (unchanged default). Other providers simply don't read them (graceful ignore). searchAcross forwards category/page in the config unchanged. Tested: buildSearxngUrl (plain / category+page / non-positive-page omit) + config passthrough. 8 searxng + 10 web-search tests green; tsc + size gate clean.

items.title: Fix: /clear clears the TUI transcript, not just conversation state
items.summary: Bug fix from live TUI use: /clear already reset the underlying conversation, but the Ink host ignored SlashResult.cleared, so old committed transcript entries stayed visible. The host now maps cleared -> reducer clear before showing the fresh-session note.
items.done: Typing /clear in the TUI clears committed transcript entries and live regions, then shows only the fresh-session note; conversation state remains reset as before.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-09 in commit 6f1e8641. ui/use-slash.ts now handles SlashResult.cleared with a clear effect; ui/reducer.ts adds a clear action resetting to initialState. Regression coverage: ui/slash.test.ts asserts clear runs before the /clear note; ui/reducer.test.ts asserts committed transcript/live/queued state resets. Verified with 76 focused tests, tsc, size gate, and git diff check.

items.title: Roadmap drained-state banner — active queue vs parked proof clearly visible
items.summary: The roadmap top view now states whether active work remains, lists Now/Blocked/Next/Later counts, and separates parked reason counts so proof-gated cards are not mistaken for active blockers.
items.done: roadmap.html renders an Active queue drained/Active work remains strip from the same summary logic as `vanta roadmap status`; focused render tests cover active work and drained-with-parked-reasons states.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: renderRoadmap now imports summarizeRoadmapStatus and adds a top health strip after Launch Pad. It shows Active queue drained when building/blocked/next/horizon are all zero, shows exact active counts, and shows parked reason counts. This does not move parked cards or claim external proofs passed.

items.title: Roadmap completion gate — drained is not the same as done
items.summary: `vanta roadmap status --require-complete` now gives a hard final gate that fails while any card is non-shipped, even when the active queue is drained and all remaining work is parked for proof or decision.
items.done: `vanta roadmap status --require-complete` exits 0 only when every roadmap card is shipped; JSON status includes `complete` and `nonShippedTotal`; drained-but-parked roadmaps fail the completion gate with an explicit non-shipped count.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: RoadmapStatusSummary now separates `activeDrained` from `complete`, adds `nonShippedTotal`, and formats a completion gate. CLI status supports `--require-complete` in text and JSON mode. Live current roadmap correctly fails this stricter gate because 18 cards remain parked; the drained gate still passes because no building/blocked/next/horizon work remains.

items.title: Roadmap completion state UI — browser view shows not-done count
items.summary: The generated roadmap page mirrors the completion gate by showing whether the roadmap is complete and how many cards remain open, instead of only showing the active queue drain state.
items.done: roadmap.html health strip includes Roadmap complete/incomplete plus the open-card count; render tests cover incomplete active work, drained-but-parked, and all-shipped states.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: renderRoadmap now adds a third health panel driven by RoadmapStatusSummary.complete/openTotal. The browser page shows Roadmap incomplete with the exact open-card count while the active queue can still be drained. This is visibility only; parked proof/decision cards remain unshipped.

items.title: Roadmap terminal parked completion — declined/duplicate cards stop counting as open work
items.summary: The completion gate now separates open parked work from terminal parked resolutions, so declined/n-a and duplicate cards are not counted as remaining work while external proof, strategy decision, optional proof, and review cards still are.
items.done: `vanta roadmap status --require-complete` fails on open proof/decision parked cards, passes when all remaining parked cards are terminal declined/n-a or duplicate, and JSON exposes `openTotal` plus `terminalParkedTotal`; roadmap.html shows open vs terminal counts.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: RoadmapStatusSummary now treats parkedReason `declined/n-a` and `duplicate` as terminal parked resolutions. Current completion status reports open work separately from non-shipped/terminal parked counts; this reduces the honest remaining open roadmap work without pretending declined or duplicate cards shipped.

items.title: Roadmap optional proof terminal — tabled experiments do not block completion
items.summary: Optional proof parked cards now count as terminally parked rather than open required work, matching the decision to table Ghost OS-style experiments for later without treating them as launch blockers.
items.done: `vanta roadmap status --require-complete` treats `optional proof` like declined/n-a and duplicate for completion math, while external proof and strategy decision cards still keep the roadmap incomplete.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: Added `optional proof` to the terminal parked reason set so optional external experiments do not block the active build queue. The historical example was retired on 2026-07-11; the generic reason remains available for future evidence-only experiments.

items.title: Roadmap open-work status — list only cards still blocking completion
items.summary: `vanta roadmap status --open` now lists only genuinely open roadmap work after terminal parked cards are excluded, so agents and operators can see the exact remaining cards instead of inferring them from parked totals.
items.done: `vanta roadmap status --open` prints the open card list; `--open --json` emits structured ids/titles/status/reasons; terminal parked declined/n-a, duplicate, and optional proof cards are excluded; CLI help advertises the flags.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: Added openRoadmapItems/formatRoadmapOpenWork and wired `vanta roadmap status --open` plus `--open --json`. Live output lists the 9 required open cards only; terminal parked cards remain visible in parked counts but do not block completion.

items.title: Roadmap strategy parked terminal — out-of-strategy cards stop blocking completion
items.summary: Strategy-decision parked cards now count as terminal parked under the current product direction, because their notes explicitly say to revive only after a strategy change rather than treating them as required build work.
items.done: `vanta roadmap status --open` excludes `strategy decision` parked cards; `--require-complete` still fails for required external proof cards; strategy cards remain visible as parked and can be revived only after an explicit direction change.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: Added `strategy decision` to the terminal parked reason set so rejected product directions do not block the active build queue. The historical examples were retired from the roadmap on 2026-07-11 and are preserved in `docs/roadmap-retired-cards.md`; the generic reason remains available for future decisions.

items.title: Roadmap actionable open work — separate aggregate gates from executable proofs
items.summary: `vanta roadmap status --open` now marks which open cards are actionable immediately and which are waiting on other open `after` dependencies, so aggregate release gates do not obscure the concrete proof cards to execute next.
items.done: Open-work JSON includes `blockedByOpenIds` and `actionable`; text output annotates cards waiting on open dependencies; completion status includes `actionableOpenTotal`.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: openRoadmapItems now checks each open card's `after` list against the open-id set. RUN-ANYWHERE-V1-RELEASE-GATE remains open but is non-actionable while its proof dependencies are open; the actionable open set is the concrete external proof work.

items.title: Roadmap actionable open filter — show only proof cards that can move now
items.summary: `vanta roadmap status --open --actionable` filters the open-work list down to cards whose open dependencies are already clear, making the next external proof attempts directly machine-readable.
items.done: `--open --actionable` works in text and JSON mode, excludes aggregate gates blocked by other open cards, preserves the nonzero exit while work remains, and CLI help documents the flag.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: formatRoadmapOpenWork accepts actionableOnly and roadmap status filters JSON/text output with `--actionable`. Live output should list only the four concrete external proof cards, excluding RUN-ANYWHERE-V1-RELEASE-GATE until its open dependencies are cleared.

items.title: A2A autonomous proof status — Docker/image/auth/receipt gate
items.summary: `vanta a2a autonomous-status [--json]` reports whether the boxed autonomous build path is actually ready: Docker daemon reachable, `vanta-agent` image built, boxed-agent credential available, and a durable npm-driven Docker proof receipt present.
items.done: The command exits nonzero until all proof gates pass, supports JSON/text output, points at `VANTA-A2A-DOCKER-AUTONOMOUS`, names `.vanta/a2a-autonomous-sandbox.json`, and CLI help documents the command.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-10: added agents/autonomous-sandbox-readiness and `vanta a2a autonomous-status [--json]`. Live output on this Mac reports Docker daemon missing, image missing, credential ready via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, and no `.vanta/a2a-autonomous-sandbox.json` proof receipt. This removes the stale sandbox-claude ambiguity while keeping the real proof honest.

items.title: Profile roster — persistent specialist agents with isolated state
items.summary: Tonbis Hermes transcript extract 2026-07-11: Hermes profiles are treated as durable specialist agents, not throwaway subagents. Vanta has teams, workers, per-agent memory, and isolated profile primitives, but lacks one operator-visible roster where a profile has its own config, model, memory, skills, gateway identity, and work history.
items.done: `vanta profiles` can create, clone, switch, archive, and target specialist profiles; each profile has isolated config/model/memory/skills/gateway identity; Operator Home shows live profile roster, last work, current status, and safe actions; profiles survive restart and can receive tasks/messages by name.
items.notes:
Sources: Tonbis AI Garage Hermes Masterclass 9 (Profiles/Kanban), Masterclass 8 (Subagents/Delegation), and Masterclass 3 (Memory/Plugins/Honcho/Obsidian). This is not a second team system; it is the visible owner surface that makes existing profile/team/memory pieces addressable.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: added the persistent profile store and `vanta profiles list|create|clone|switch|archive|target|send|inbox`. Each profile owns an isolated VANTA_HOME containing settings/model selection, skills, memory, agent-memory, gateway identity/state, durable inbox, and work ledger. Startup reloads the active marker and redirects the next process into that home while VANTA_PROFILE_BASE_HOME preserves roster access. Operator Home shows profile count, active/queued state, and latest target. Focused proof: 10 tests across store, CLI, real child-process restart dispatch, and Operator Home. Live proof in an isolated home exercised create, clone, target, switch, restart list, inbox, Operator Home, archive, and final list with exit 0. Targeting is a durable queue boundary; task execution remains governed by the existing agent/task runner.

items.title: Profile Kanban router — durable board routes work by specialist skill
items.summary: Hermes uses Kanban as a durable multi-agent work router. Vanta ships roadmap Kanban, work queues, teams, delegation, and org chart primitives; the missing workflow is a user-facing board where cards declare required skills and persistent profiles claim, update, hand off, and close work with receipts.
items.done: A board card can declare owner/profile, required skills, dependencies, evidence, and wake policy; profile agents claim/update/hand off cards; the board survives restart; Operator Home shows active lanes; completed cards link to transcript/receipt evidence and failed cards expose retry/fallback actions.
items.notes:
Sources: Tonbis Hermes Masterclass 9 plus the multi-agent Kanban workflow transcripts from 2026-06-01 and 2026-06-08. Build over existing KANBAN/VANTA-KANBAN/PCLIP-WORK-QUEUES rather than creating another board model.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: extended the existing durable Kanban schema with profile owner/fallback, required skills, dependencies, evidence receipts, wake policy, retry count, and handoff history. Added `vanta kanban add|route|claim|handoff|update|retry`. Route reads installed skill directories from persistent profile homes; dependency and skill gates fail closed. Done requires at least one evidence receipt. Blocked rendering names exact retry and fallback actions. Operator Home lists active/blocked lanes and the current owner. Backward-compatible schema defaults preserve existing boards. Focused proof covers pure transitions, persistence, legacy swarm output, profile-backed CLI routing, and Home rendering. Live proof through src/cli.ts installed a skilled profile distribution, created/added/routed/handed-off/blocked/retried/closed a lane with evidence, rendered status and Home, and removed only the generated proof board.

items.title: Second-brain corpus compiler — notes, transcripts, and receipts become searchable memory
items.summary: Hermes plus Gbrain frames the useful memory gap: the agent should see curated external notes and transcripts, not only what passed through chat. Vanta has brain memory, vault bridge, world model, and transcript tooling; the missing product workflow is a corpus compiler with hybrid search, source receipts, and refresh status.
items.done: `vanta corpus ingest <folder|url>` imports markdown notes, downloaded transcripts, and local docs into a source-indexed corpus; recall combines keyword, semantic, and entity links; each answer can show source/date/freshness receipts; stale sources are refreshable; Obsidian/vault export preserves links.
items.notes:
Sources: Tonbis 2026-07-07 Gbrain + Hermes, 2026-05-18 Memory/Plugins/Honcho/Obsidian, and the local Tonbis transcript corpus now available under reference/tonbis-ai-garage-transcripts. This extends BRAIN-VAULT-BRIDGE/BRAIN-INGEST-GATE with a visible ingest workflow and evidence contract.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: added `vanta corpus ingest|recall|status|refresh|vault-export` over a durable source index in VANTA_HOME/corpus. Local ingest accepts Markdown/text/VTT/SRT, skips hidden/unsupported paths, refuses symlink roots, chunks content, hashes sources, extracts entity links, and records source/ingest/refresh dates. URL ingest is SSRF/egress-guarded and retains Last-Modified when present. Recall fuses BM25, available stored embeddings, and entity rank through RRF; output names only signals actually used and prints source/date/freshness receipts. Refresh re-reads the original local path or guarded URL. Vault export previews before `--apply` and writes raw sources plus corpus/entity wiki links. Seven focused tests cover the contract. Real isolated CLI proof ingested a note and transcript, recalled with keyword+entity fallback while Ollama was unavailable, reported freshness, refreshed both sources, previewed five vault changes, applied them, and inspected all five output files.

items.title: Delegation tree receipts — parent sees summaries, raw sidechains stay searchable
items.summary: Hermes delegation emphasizes isolated child contexts that return compact summaries while preserving raw work. Vanta ships delegate, swarm, async delegation, sidechain transcripts, and build loops; the remaining UX is a tree receipt that makes delegated work inspectable without flooding the parent context.
items.done: Every multi-agent run records a delegation tree with parent task, child prompt, tools used, summary returned, raw sidechain location, verification result, cost/time, and replay/follow-up controls; parent context receives summaries only; Operator Home and `vanta agents` can inspect the tree.
items.notes:
Source: Tonbis Hermes Masterclass 8 (Subagents/Delegation). This is a presentation/receipt layer over shipped delegation primitives, not a new execution engine.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: spawnSubagent now returns optional worker evidence containing the raw sidechain path, complete tool-name set, model, and elapsed time while preserving existing AgentOutcome compatibility. The delegate wrapper appends a durable parent/child node under `.vanta/delegations/` with parent task, child prompt, compact summary, verification/stopped reason, sidechain, usage, and estimated model cost. Parent output remains summary-only. `vanta agents delegations [tree]` renders the tree; replay/follow-up controls queue tracked assigned work and retain the raw evidence path. Operator Home reports tree/child/failure counts. Life-wide search explicitly indexes up to 300 bounded raw sidechains as the `delegation` source despite the normal `.vanta` repo ignore. Focused tests cover append/group/render, spawn sidechain behavior, delegate behavior, agent controls, Home, and sidechain search. Real CLI proof delegated one package-metadata read: the parent received only `name: vanta / version: 0.8.0`; inspection showed one passing child using read_file, the raw five-message sidechain, 4,881ms, 35,629 tokens, estimated $0.0451, and replay/follow-up controls. Replay queued a tracked task, Operator Home showed 1 tree/1 child/0 failures, the proof task was removed from the visible queue afterward, and `vanta lifesearch` returned delegation-source sidechain hits.

items.title: Tool-surface profiles — shrink available tools by role and explain the boundary
items.summary: Hermes tool/MCP guidance stresses a small visible tool surface, role-specific tool sets, and recoverable tool failures. Vanta has tool search, blocked tools, skill gating, and kernel gating; the missing workflow is an operator-visible explanation of why a profile can see a tool and how to repair missing capability.
items.done: Each profile/agent role declares an allowed tool surface; `vanta tools why <tool>` explains role visibility, kernel risk, setup prerequisites, and missing credentials; overbroad surfaces warn; failed tool calls return a repair path instead of an opaque stack.
items.notes:
Source: Tonbis Hermes Masterclass 6 (Tools/MCP Servers). Build by wiring existing SETTINGS-BLOCKEDTOOLS-ENFORCE, VANTA-TOOL-SEARCH-AUTO, HARNESS-SKILL-GATING, and setup prerequisites into a user-facing explanation.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: profiles and profile distributions accept `allowedTools`; create, clone, activation settings, and `vanta profiles tools <id> --allow <csv>` persist the role boundary. `buildRegistry({include})` enforces it for initial built-ins and late MCP/plugin registrations, while blockedTools remains a tightening exclusion. `vanta tools why <tool>` reports active-role visibility, typical kernel risk with a per-call disclaimer, setup prerequisites, missing credentials, overbroad full-surface warnings, and exact repair commands. Final failed tool results append the same repair guidance after retries and before hooks/callbacks. Focused tests cover registry inclusion and late registration, profile persistence/clone/distributions, CLI explanations, Google OAuth repair, overbroad warnings, and dispatch failure repair. Real isolated CLI proof created and activated a research profile, showed gmail_send hidden plus OAuth/profile repairs, widened the allowlist, showed gmail_send visible but still credential-gated, showed read_file as typical allow, then activated an unrestricted profile and observed the full-surface warning.

items.title: Webhook workflow builder — internet events trigger approved Vanta work
items.summary: The Hermes webhooks transcript frames webhooks as the bridge from chat agent to automated backend: email, signup, PR, and other internet events should start useful work. Vanta has webhook triggers and delivery targets; the missing layer is a guided builder with templates, dry runs, and receipts.
items.done: `vanta webhook workflow new` creates event templates for GitHub PRs, email, form/subscriber events, and generic HMAC POST; it generates the secret/test payload, runs a dry-run, stores route/delivery receipts, and shows the workflow in Operator Home with enable/disable controls.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-11. `vanta webhook workflow` creates GitHub PR, email, subscriber/form, and generic HMAC templates; stores mode-0600 secrets separately; prints a one-time secret and sample payload; runs dry-runs; and supports list/show/test/enable/disable. Enabled routes run through the gateway agent handle and registered delivery targets, record body-hash route and delivery receipts, and appear in Operator Home. Executed proof used the real CLI to create/show/enable a generic workflow, sent a correctly signed POST through the gateway bridge, observed HTTP 202 plus agent output delivery, and verified accepted-route and delivered receipts. Source: Tonbis 2026-04-22 Hermes Agent + Webhooks.

items.title: Spreadsheet copilot — Excel/Sheets agent surface with charts and custom functions
items.summary: Hermes demonstrates the agent inside Excel: spreadsheet edits, analysis, charting, and custom functions. Vanta has document/spreadsheet-capable runtime support in the broader Codex workspace, but Vanta itself needs a first-class spreadsheet workflow if this becomes a real operator surface.
items.done: Vanta can inspect, edit, add/delete sheets, create charts, and explain formulas for a local workbook; an Excel/Sheets sidecar or custom function can call a kernel-gated Vanta session; changes are previewed before write; receipts record workbook, sheet, cell range, and verification; optional Excel authoring packs produce formula-driven three-statement, DCF, comps, LBO, and merger models with balance and sensitivity checks.
items.notes: PARKED EXTERNAL PROOF 2026-07-11 only on a real Excel/Google Sheets host round trip. Local workbook editing shipped in 4bc7daf7 and now includes deterministic formula explanation plus approval-gated embedded bar/line chart snapshots with bounded series/points, source ranges, title/anchor cells, reopen verification, chart counts, and SHA receipts. Added finance_model and vanta spreadsheet model for strict three-statement, DCF, comps, LBO, and merger briefs; outputs contain assumptions, native formulas with cached results, Checks sheets, DCF/LBO 5x5 sensitivity tables, atomic writes, reopen/formula/check verification, and mode-0600 hash receipts. Real CLI proof generated an 88-formula five-year DCF from examples/finance/dcf.json, applied examples/finance/dcf-chart-plan.json, reported DCF charts:1, and wrote both receipts. Independent LibreOffice conversion rendered assumptions, forecast, terminal value, all 25 sensitivity values, a 640x360 chart, and PASS checks without formula errors. Added SpreadsheetVantaClient to the built operator SDK and an Excel custom-function template under examples/spreadsheet-sidecar; workbook context is capped at 32 KiB and marked untrusted. Public API browser access now requires an exact configured HTTPS origin, supports bounded preflight, rejects unlisted origins, and still requires bearer auth on every real request. Actual HTTP CORS/auth integration tests pass. Full suite: 12,986 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed across 4,749 suites; main TypeScript, SDK build, 5-file size gate, npm audit (0 vulnerabilities), 30-scenario use-case validation, and Docusaurus production build pass. Remaining external done criterion: install the template in a real Excel host (or equivalent Sheets client), call the TLS-proxied token-authenticated Vanta API, receive a result, and exercise an approval-gated workbook action through that same kernel session. Chart outputs are explicit PNG snapshots, not native dynamic Excel chart objects, and must be regenerated after source changes.

items.title: Vault secrets rotation — one bootstrap token, scoped provider keys
items.summary: The Hermes + Bitwarden transcript highlights a practical multi-device problem: scattered `.env` keys are hard to rotate and easy to overexpose. Vanta has key helpers, scoped secret injection, and secret scanners; the missing slice is external vault-backed secret resolution and rotation status.
items.done: Vanta can read selected secrets from Bitwarden or 1Password using one bootstrap credential, inject only scoped secrets into the run/profile that needs them, report stale/overbroad keys, rotate a provider key with operator confirmation, and write an audit receipt without logging secret values.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-11. `vanta secrets vault` manages value-free Bitwarden/1Password aliases with exact scopes, bootstrap readiness, stale/expired/overbroad status, redacted resolve checks, and confirmed reference cutover. Active profiles and explicit VANTA_SECRET_SCOPE runs inject only granted aliases from the base manifest before provider resolution. Rotation verifies the new vault reference and audits only reference hashes. Executed proof used the real CLI with a fake `bw` binary: add/status/resolve succeeded without value output, unconfirmed rotation exited 2, `--yes` verified the new reference, and the audit contained no refs, resolved value, or bootstrap token. Source: Tonbis 2026-07-01 Hermes + Bitwarden.

items.title: Dashboard plugin slots — operator home accepts safe custom panels
items.summary: Hermes dashboard/theme/plugin transcripts show a local web surface where plugins can contribute panels and controls. Vanta ships a plugin framework, desktop, command center, and operator home; the gap is a stable, bounded dashboard-slot contract for custom operator panels.
items.done: A plugin can declare a dashboard panel with title, data provider, refresh interval, actions, and required permissions; Operator Home renders the panel in a bounded slot; plugin actions remain kernel-gated; bad panels fail closed and can be disabled from the UI.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-11. Strict plugin manifests can declare up to eight bounded dashboard panels with host-owned title, provider, 5s-1h refresh interval, up to six prompt actions, and required worker capabilities. Runtime discovery admits enabled declarations only when every permission is granted; missing grants leave the panel disabled without launching unsafe UI. Isolated workers update bounded lines while declaration metadata/actions remain fixed. `/plugin-panels` renders the slot, numbered actions and `d` route through a normal Vanta turn (so resulting tools remain kernel-gated), and `vanta home` reports ready versus permission-disabled slots. Executed tests launch a real isolated worker, preserve declared provider/refresh/actions across its content update, reject missing grants, render/activate actions, and verify Operator Home integration. Source: Tonbis 2026-04-28 Hermes dashboard/themes/plugins.

items.title: Profile distributions — package a whole specialist agent as a git install
items.summary: Current Hermes ships profile distributions: a repo can contain the agent's manifest, SOUL, config, skills, cron, MCP, and defaults. Vanta has blueprints, imports, plugins, and profile-adjacent primitives; the missing workflow is installing and updating a whole specialist profile without copying secrets, sessions, or private memory.
items.done: `vanta profile install <git|path>` previews a distribution manifest, imports SOUL/config/skills/cron/MCP into an isolated profile, refuses secrets/history, records the source commit, supports update with diff/backup, and preserves local memories, sessions, API keys, and operator overrides.
items.notes:
Source: current NousResearch/hermes-agent profile distribution docs. Build on Vanta blueprints/import-export/plugin bundle primitives, but keep the safety boundary: a distribution installs capability and defaults, not private state.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: added `vanta profile install <git|path> [--apply]` and `vanta profile update <id> [--apply]`. `vanta-profile.json` explicitly declares SOUL, settings, skills, cron, MCP, and profile defaults. Preview lists destination files and the Git commit (or deterministic local manifest hash). Apply creates an isolated profile and records owned paths/hashes in distribution.json. Update diffs those owned files, backs up previous copies, removes stale owned paths, applies source changes, refreshes provider/model identity, and merges current operator settings over incoming defaults. A full-tree deny scan refuses .env, keys, credentials, tokens, memory, sessions, inbox, work, and history before preview; referenced symlink escapes, secret-shaped JSON keys, and tampered installed destination paths fail closed. Tests use real Git commits and filesystem state to prove source commit capture, refusal, install, backup/update/removal, destination containment, and preservation of private memory/inbox/settings. A real child-process CLI test executes preview, install, update preview, and update apply across separate Vanta invocations.

items.title: Automation blueprint catalog — form-driven scheduled and webhook workflows
items.summary: Hermes turns common automations into reusable blueprints available from dashboard, CLI, TUI, and chat. Vanta has shipped blueprints, scheduler, cron, and webhook primitives; the gap is a user-facing catalog that asks only for needed fields, previews the resulting job, and arms it after confirmation.
items.done: `vanta automation blueprints` lists schedule and webhook blueprints; `/blueprint <name>` prompts for missing fields and accepts inline key=value arguments; preview/confirm happens before creating cron/webhook state; created automations appear in Operator Home with pause, resume, test, and receipt links; user blueprints live outside `src`.
items.notes:
Source: current Hermes automation blueprint catalog docs. This is not a duplicate of VANTA-BLUEPRINTS; it productizes recurring operator automations over existing schedule/webhook machinery.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: added a validated data-only catalog under vanta-ts/automation-blueprints plus user overrides at ~/.vanta/automation-blueprints. `vanta automation blueprints|preview|apply|list|pause|resume|test|receipts` delegates to existing cron and signed-webhook stores; apply requires `--yes`. `/blueprint` lists definitions, accepts inline key=value fields, reports only missing inputs, and previews without state changes. Operator Home reports active/paused automation counts and links to all controls. Live CLI acceptance executed catalog, preview, confirmed schedule creation, list, pause, resume, target test, and receipts; temporary acceptance state was removed afterward. Focused tests: 14 passed. Full suite: 1,272 files / 12,821 passed / 3 skipped. Typecheck, touched-file size/complexity lint, docs build, use-case validation, and roadmap integrity passed.

items.title: Context references v2 — @file ranges, @folder, @diff, @git, @url
items.summary: Vanta's shipped @-context references cover the basic path workflow. Hermes' current context references are more explicit: file ranges, folder summaries, git diff/staged/history, and URL fetches with tab completion and scope warnings. Vanta should expand context refs without silently overloading the prompt.
items.done: The composer expands `@file:path:10-25`, `@folder:path`, `@diff`, `@staged`, `@git:N`, and `@url:https://...`; completion advertises supported reference types; sensitive, out-of-scope, binary, and oversized refs produce inline warnings; expanded context remains visible and cannot exceed hard context limits silently.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-11. The rendered Composer-to-submit acceptance typed and submitted an @file line range, observed source-labelled expansion, and emitted an operator receipt. Focused coverage also executes @folder, @diff, @staged, @git:N, @url, completion, root/sensitive/binary checks, and explicit per-ref/total-limit warnings. Source: current Hermes context reference docs; this extends rather than replaces legacy @path.

items.title: Credential pools — rotate same-provider keys before cross-provider fallback
items.summary: Hermes supports credential pools per provider with rotation strategy and cooldowns. Vanta has provider fallback and key helpers, but not a same-provider credential lease pool that subagents and profiles can share safely before falling back to a different model provider.
items.done: `vanta auth pool` lists, adds, removes, and tests provider credentials; env/keychain/vault refs can seed pools; 429/402/401 responses mark cooldown or exhaustion and rotate within the provider before MODEL-FALLBACK; subagents get leased credentials without collisions; logs redact values and explain cache/cost impact.
items.notes:
Source: current Hermes credential pool docs. Pair with HERMES-VAULT-SECRETS-ROTATION when external vault-backed keys become the default source.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: `vanta auth pool list|add|remove|test` manages reference-only provider pools backed by env, macOS Keychain, Bitwarden, 1Password, or scoped Vanta vault aliases. Literal values are never accepted or persisted; files are mode 0600 and audit entries hash references. Atomic directory locks and two-minute leases prevent concurrent profiles/subagents from sharing a key. 429 enters a one-minute cooldown; 401/402 exhaust a reference; non-credential errors release without consuming it. Complete and pre-yield stream paths rotate same-provider keys, then the base key, then emit an explicit pool-exhausted error that the existing cross-provider fallback recognizes. Mid-stream errors never replay. Live CLI acceptance added, listed, resolved, and removed an env reference under an isolated VANTA_HOME, then proved the plaintext absent from disk. Focused gates: 35 tests, typecheck, and touched-file size/complexity lint. Full suite: 1,275 files / 12,828 passed / 3 skipped. Docs build, use-case validation, roadmap uniqueness, and diff checks passed.

items.title: Plugin LLM lane — bounded host-owned model calls from plugins
items.summary: Hermes gives plugins a controlled `ctx.llm` path for one-shot chat or structured generation without exposing provider secrets. Vanta plugins can register tools, commands, and UI surfaces; the missing capability is a safe host-owned LLM lane with budget, schema, audit, and permission boundaries.
items.done: PluginContext exposes `ctx.llm.complete` and `ctx.llm.completeStructured` with required purpose, budget, timeout, and optional schema validation; provider/model selection inherits host policy unless explicitly allowed; calls are audited and cost-attributed; untrusted plugins cannot read credentials or choose arbitrary providers.
items.notes:
Source: current Hermes plugin LLM access docs. Keep plugin LLM calls behind the same kernel/accounting path as normal Vanta model calls.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: `PluginContext.llm.complete` and `completeStructured` provide host-owned one-shot generation to enabled in-process plugins only after the operator grants `llm.generate`. Strict requests require purpose, prompt, budgetUsd, timeoutMs, and maxTokens and reject provider/model overrides. Host routing selects the provider from purpose; VANTA_PLUGIN_LLM_MAX_USD plus a conservative worst-case token ceiling bound spend; AbortSignal enforces timeout. Structured calls append a JSON-only instruction, parse output, and validate the requested object/array/primitive shape. Audit JSONL attributes plugin, purpose, host model, token usage, cost, budget, timeout, outcome, and timestamp while omitting prompts, outputs, and credentials. The loader refactor preserved diagnostic error handling and split oversized branches under the code-size gate. Isolated worker protocol v1 intentionally does not expose model calls; worker RPC remains a separate trust-boundary decision. Focused plugin gates: 39 tests plus typecheck and touched-file size/complexity lint. Full suite: 1,276 files / 12,831 passed / 3 skipped. Docs build, use-case validation, roadmap uniqueness, and diff checks passed.

items.title: Deliverable auto-attach — generated files become native chat artifacts
items.summary: Hermes deliverable mode scans final responses and Kanban completions for generated files, then sends supported files as native chat attachments. Vanta has work products and media plumbing; the gap is turning produced reports, charts, spreadsheets, decks, and HTML files into channel-native artifacts automatically.
items.done: When a run produces approved artifact paths, gateway delivery uploads supported image, pdf, csv, xlsx, pptx, html, and text outputs as native attachments on capable channels; source/log extensions are skipped by default; existence, recency, and workspace scope are validated; visible chat copy removes local paths and receipt logs record delivery.
items.notes:
Source: current Hermes deliverable mode docs. Build on Vanta work products and messaging media adapters; the key behavior is channel-native delivery with scoped file validation.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: the gateway scans canonical final replies plus approved work products for png/jpg/jpeg/gif/webp/pdf/csv/xlsx/pptx/html/txt/md artifacts, dedupes them, removes local paths from visible copy, and skips source/config/log extensions by default. Every file must pass the existing protected-path/workspace scope guard plus a recent-mtime anti-exfil window before bytes are read. PlatformAdapter now has an optional native sendFile port; Telegram implements Bot API sendDocument with forum-topic routing. Channels without the port receive sanitized text and never read the file. Successful uploads append platform, transport, name, MIME, bytes, source, and timestamp to `.vanta/deliverable-receipts.jsonl`, never contents. Executed gateway acceptance proved a generated PDF path becomes sanitized canonical text, a native upload, and a receipt. Focused gates: 50 gateway tests plus typecheck and touched-file size/complexity lint. The first full suite hit one unrelated ENOTEMPTY watcher-cleanup race; its isolated rerun passed and the required full rerun passed 1,278 files / 12,837 tests with 3 skipped. Docs build, use-case validation, roadmap uniqueness, and diff checks passed.

items.title: Hermes issue regression pack — turn competitor failures into Vanta invariants
items.summary: A review of current open Hermes issues found recurring failures with fabricated compaction intent, cross-session routing, custom-provider identity, leaked MCP processes, safe-mode configuration bypass, and credential-bearing environment persistence. Vanta has adjacent unit coverage, but not one release-facing adversarial pack that proves these trust boundaries together.
items.done: A deterministic regression suite proves: compaction cannot introduce ungrounded user asks; reconnect/resume cannot cross session IDs; provider aliases preserve provider/base URL/credential identity across CLI, desktop, gateway, and subagents; MCP reconnects reap obsolete processes; safe mode loads no user plugin/memory/MCP state; persisted terminal/session artifacts redact credential-bearing environment values. Each case links to its source issue and runs in CI without external credentials.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. Primary issue evidence: NousResearch/hermes-agent #62365, #51058, #60385, #62406, #62435, and #62336. Shipped regressions: COMPACTION-INTENT-GROUNDING, ACP-SESSION-ROUTING-ISOLATION, SUBAGENT-PROVIDER-IDENTITY, DESKTOP-GATEWAY-PROVIDER-PARITY, MCP-RECONNECT-PROCESS-REAP, SAFE-MODE-STATE-ISOLATION, and SESSION-ENV-OUTPUT-REDACTION. Final executed proof: the complete vanta-ts suite passes 1,243/1,243 files and 12,720 tests with 3 intentional skips; TypeScript is clean. The full run also exposed and fixed stale reach-channel expectations plus a wall-clock-flaky media-cache test fixture without changing production behavior.

items.title: Hermes story eval harness — test real community jobs across all 15 categories
items.summary: Hermes now publishes 262 community stories across 15 categories. Vanta's June USE-CASE-AUDIT is a static capability map and the existing surface script samples 12 routes; neither proves complete user jobs or tracks receipts over time. Build a versioned representative corpus and run it at route, sandbox, and live tiers.
items.done: A versioned manifest covers all 15 Hermes story categories with at least two representative jobs per category; `scripts/usecase-eval` can list/filter/run them; every scenario declares setup, risk, expected tools/artifacts, verifier, and live-credential gate; CI runs deterministic offline cases; scheduled/manual runs record pass/fail/blocked receipts; the public use-case page shows only executed proof and names live-gated gaps.
items.notes:
The durable source index covers all 262 Hermes stories from commit 3b2ef789df without quote bodies; the executable manifest has 30 scenarios, two per category, each tied to a real sourceStoryId. Five scenarios now have explicit reviewed passes: General/capability-start (09-07-13-368Z), Meta/cross-platform-install (09-21-20-037Z), Dev/converse-before-act (09-49-48-049Z), Cost/context-reduction (09-56-40-765Z), and Enterprise/Kubernetes briefing (10-09-51-830Z). Enterprise preserved two manual failure receipts before WORKFLOW-ACCEPTANCE-EVIDENCE-CONTRACT encoded the missing schedule and criterion evidence. HERMES-MULTITURN-STORY-RUNNER remains required to continue clarification/approval workflows in one session.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: the versioned manifest now enforces two scenarios in every one of 15 categories and structured setup, risk, expected tools, expected artifacts, verifier, tier, and live setup gates. List/filter/run and `--include-live` remain the execution surface; live jobs without credentials are recorded as skipped, not passed. Five reviewed real runs now carry deterministic output contracts that apply automatically to new runs and can retro-verify old receipts. `--status` selects the latest receipt per scenario and reports pass/fail/blocked/pending plus category gaps. `--export-public` emits only aggregate counts and gap names, never transcript tails or reviewer notes; the use-case page links that redacted 5-pass/5-category artifact and explicitly names ten unproved categories. A Vitest integration case executes verify/status/export against an isolated receipt directory, so CI proves the offline harness without counting the fixture as product execution. Real acceptance listed the General category, refused a live morning-brief run without Google/delivery setup, reassessed five receipts to pass, reported 5/5 executed across 5/15 categories, and generated the public proof artifact.

items.title: Cross-platform service supervisor — one `vanta up` on macOS, Linux, and Windows
items.summary: Mercury's strongest operational advantage is a single persistent-service command backed by launchd, systemd user units, or Windows Task Scheduler with crash restart and logs. Vanta now implements all three native supervisors; the remaining gate is executed Windows lifecycle proof.
items.done: `vanta up|restart|stop|logs|status` behaves consistently across macOS launchd, Linux systemd user services, and Windows Task Scheduler; install is non-admin where the platform permits; crash restart is bounded; status identifies stale PID/service state; uninstall removes only Vanta-owned artifacts; platform fixtures and one live proof per OS produce receipts.
items.notes: PARKED EXTERNAL PROOF 2026-07-11. Implementation and fixtures shipped in 90a4635b..38990ef5. GitHub Actions run 29166838401 produced ok:true lifecycle receipts for macOS launchd and Ubuntu systemd-user, covering install/start/restart/log/stop/stale/uninstall; Linux also passed locally under a dedicated non-root Ubuntu 24.04 systemd user manager with spaces in working and log paths. The Windows hosted runner registered the owned least-privilege InteractiveToken task and reported 0x41301 running, then 0x41306 terminated, but never launched the action or wrote its first marker. Microsoft defines InteractiveToken as requiring an existing interactive session; S4U is not a valid gateway substitute because it removes network and encrypted-file access. docs/service-supervisor.md now gives the exact PowerShell proof command. Park until that command produces `.artifacts/service-proof-win32.json` with ok:true on a real logged-in Windows desktop; do not call the card shipped before then.

items.title: Public skill registry client — search, inspect, install, update, and remove safely
items.summary: Hermes Skills Hub and Mercury's public registry make reusable skills discoverable. Vanta can install its bundled library and manage local skills, but its own comparison page correctly identifies the missing public exchange. Add the client and trust workflow before operating a hosted registry.
items.done: `vanta skills search|browse|view|install|update|remove|doctor` works against a configurable registry; install always previews the complete SKILL.md, source, version, requested capabilities, and integrity hash; untrusted skills are disabled until approval; updates show a diff and preserve local edits; installs are reversible and audited; a static test registry proves the full flow without network credentials.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. Added a configurable local/HTTP static registry with strict schema and SHA-256 verification; search/browse/view prints source, version, capabilities, hash status, and the complete SKILL.md. Confirmed installs land mode-0600 and disabled in quarantine; a separate approve command refuses local-skill overwrite before activation. Updates preview a line diff, back up clean prior versions, and preserve locally edited files while staging incoming content for review. Removal moves packages into a reversible removed area; doctor reports ok/modified/missing/removed; lifecycle actions append redacted JSONL audit records. Executed the real CLI against an isolated static registry and VANTA_HOME: browse, search, view, preview install, confirmed disabled install, approve, doctor, preview update, apply update, doctor, remove, removed-file check, and audit actions install-disabled/approve/update/remove all passed. Focused registry/library tests: 10 passed. Full suite: 12,840 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed; TypeScript, touched-file lint, 30-scenario use-case validation, 1,188-card JSON/ID validation, git diff checks, and Docusaurus production build pass. Complete package assets and multi-source ecosystem discovery remain separate dependent cards.

items.title: Vanta public site v1 — product-first homepage, use cases, install, and proof
items.summary: The existing Docusaurus project is a useful docs corpus but routes the Introduction doc as the homepage. Hermes shows the category clearly with a product-first landing page, immediate install path, visual product evidence, and six memorable capability chapters. Vanta needs its own version centered on the enforced kernel and verified work.
items.done: The site root is a responsive product homepage with Vanta as the first-viewport signal, an accurate install command, a real product screenshot, the kernel/goal/verification distinction, six outcome-led capabilities, links to docs/use cases/GitHub, and no unproved testimonial or metric; docs remain reachable under `/docs`; production build and broken-link checks pass; desktop and mobile screenshots show no overlap or clipped text.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11; VISUAL REFINEMENT 2026-07-12. Added a product-first Docusaurus page at `/`, moved Introduction to `/docs`, used a real current desktop capture, kept existing docs/use-case/roadmap routes, and avoided unproved metrics/testimonials. The refinement rebuilt the site as one long-form page: the user-selected Vanta operator artwork leads the hero, a current desktop capture and real terminal recording provide product proof, and six full-width chapters cover Reach, Remember, Schedule, Delegate, Research, and Enforce before the execution-contract, kernel, roadmap, and install sections. Executed: TypeScript clean; production Docusaurus build with no broken-link/anchor warnings; Playwright desktop 1440x1000 and mobile 390x844 full-page screenshots with no overlap; automated browser checks report zero horizontal overflow, failed images, or console errors; `/docs`, `/use-cases`, `/roadmap`, `/quickstart`, and `/safety-model` return 200. Local preview: http://127.0.0.1:4180/. Separate gap observed: the desktop Vite dev path currently fails transforming Capacitor for configured legacy browser targets; the homepage uses the already-built desktop artifact for its capture.

items.title: Choice-wall side-effect guard — no post-turn writes before the operator chooses
items.summary: The first calibrated Hermes community story proved a trust mismatch: Vanta inspected readiness and explicitly said it would wait for the operator's choice, then post-turn self-learning refined a skill anyway. Choice walls must constrain hooks and learning as well as the visible agent loop.
items.done: When a turn ends in an explicit operator choice/confirmation wall, post-turn learning may analyze and propose in memory but cannot mint/refine/adopt skills, edit files, send messages, arm schedules, or perform other durable/external side effects until the choice is received; read-only diagnostics remain allowed; `general-capability-start` records reliable=true, surfacePassed=true, guardPassed=true, and no forbidden hits on the real `./run.sh run` path.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. Added conservative explicit-choice-wall detection to session/background-learning; one-shot and interactive hosts skip recurring-skill capture and defer the mutating learning review while preserving normal session/verification work. TDD RED: 3 expected failures for missing detector/deferral. GREEN: 22 relevant tests pass and TypeScript clean. Real rerun .vanta/eval-runs/use-cases/2026-07-11T09-07-13-368Z.json: reliable=true, surfacePassed=true, guardPassed=true, forbiddenHits=[], exitCode=0. Prior failing receipt 09-01-10-472Z remains the regression evidence.

items.title: Glob base-path root scope — relative searches use the declared project
items.summary: The cross-platform installer story passed `base_path: '.'`, but glob_files resolved it against the Node process cwd (`vanta-ts`) instead of Vanta's declared project root. Root install.ps1 was invisible, causing a false Windows-support conclusion; absolute outside-root bases were also accepted.
items.done: A relative glob base_path resolves against ToolContext.root; an absolute base outside root is refused; `**/*install*` from `.` returns root install.ps1 and install.sh; the unchanged cross-platform installer story produces an evidence-correct report and receives an explicit outcome pass review.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. TDD RED proved relative base_path missed a root-only fixture and outside absolute bases were readable. GREEN uses resolveInScope for base calculation; 14 focused tests pass, TypeScript clean, direct real tool output includes install.ps1 + install.sh. Initial receipt 09-15-09-887Z is reviewer fail; corrected receipt 09-21-20-037Z is reliable/surface/guard pass and manual outcome pass.

items.title: Compaction intent grounding — generated summaries cannot invent user asks
items.summary: LLM-written compaction summaries were inserted as user messages. A fabricated sentence such as 'User asked to deploy production' could therefore acquire the authority of a real request even though the protected conversation tail contained no such instruction.
items.done: Both transient and persistent compaction treat generated summaries as reference-only system context; deterministic sanitization removes attributed or pending user-intent claims while retaining factual summary sentences; the protected latest user message remains verbatim; regression tests execute both compaction paths.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. TDD RED reproduced fabricated transfer/deploy and secret-email requests in both compaction APIs. GREEN routes both paths through one sanitizer and reference-only system wrapper; factual summary content and the real protected user tail survive. Executed: 28 context + auto-compaction tests pass; TypeScript clean. This closes only the compaction slice of HERMES-ISSUE-REGRESSION-PACK.

items.title: ACP session routing isolation — peer events cannot cross active session IDs
items.summary: The outbound ACP client forwarded every peer session/update and permission request without checking that Vanta had opened that session. A peer event labeled with another session ID could reach output handlers or invoke the kernel approver.
items.done: The ACP client records only session IDs returned by its own session/new calls; updates for unknown IDs are dropped; permission requests for unknown IDs fail closed without invoking the approver; valid-session updates and approvals continue to work; client, server, and session lifecycle regressions pass.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. TDD RED proved an unknown session update was forwarded and an unknown-session permission reached the approver. GREEN binds inbound ACP events to IDs returned by this client's session/new lifecycle and rejects unknown approvals. Executed: 36 ACP client/server/session tests pass; TypeScript clean. This closes only the cross-session routing slice of HERMES-ISSUE-REGRESSION-PACK.

items.title: Subagent provider identity — provider switches cannot inherit the parent model
items.summary: Delegate and swarm provider overrides copied the full parent environment. Selecting a different provider without also naming a model retained the parent provider's VANTA_MODEL, producing an invalid provider/model combination and bypassing the selected alias's configured default model.
items.done: Provider-only child overrides preserve credential environment and provider configuration but remove the stale parent VANTA_MODEL; explicit provider+model overrides remain intact; no-override children inherit parent identity; delegate and swarm use one tested overlay helper; user-provider aliases still resolve through their configured key environment and model.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. TDD RED proved an ollama child selected from an openai/gpt-4o parent retained gpt-4o. GREEN centralizes providerOverrideEnv for delegate and swarm: switching provider without a model clears only VANTA_MODEL, while credentials and all other environment remain available for the selected alias. Executed: 62 provider, user-provider, delegate, swarm, and teammate tests pass; TypeScript clean. Desktop/gateway parity remains in the parent pack.

items.title: MCP reconnect process reap — obsolete clients close before replacement
items.summary: The TUI MCP panel created a new management client on reconnect without closing the previous client. Repeated reconnects could leave obsolete stdio child processes and transports alive; closing the panel also discarded references without cleanup.
items.done: Reconnect closes the previous connection before reading config or creating a replacement; the panel tracks current clients, closes them on dismissal, and closes a replacement that arrives after dismissal; reconnect failures remain errors-as-values; MCP connection/client/mount/view regressions pass.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. TDD RED proved reconnectServer left an injected previous client's close() untouched. GREEN closes it before replacement and adds panel ownership/disposal plus late-result cleanup. Executed: reconnect close regression and 49 MCP client/connect/mount/view tests pass; TypeScript clean. Code-path only: panel dismissal and reconnect-race cleanup are wired but were not exercised through an interactive Ink render in this pass.

items.title: Safe-mode state isolation — no user memory, plugins, MCP, or settings
items.summary: Safe mode skipped project context, skills, plugins, MCP, and hooks by predicate, but the real prompt path still loaded persistent brain identity, operator beliefs, recent memory, self/MOIM state, playbooks, continuity, resume scratchpads, output preferences, and project settings.
items.done: Safe mode returns empty persistent prompt context and runtime settings; interactive resume, playbooks, Ralph continuity, and user output preferences remain excluded; enabled user plugins are not imported; configured MCP children are not spawned; bare mode retains memory/settings while preserving its lighter discovery-only contract.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. TDD RED showed safe mode injected an accepted autonomy belief, the full persistent brain, and project settings. GREEN gates memory/settings at source loaders and later resume/playbook/continuity/profile paths. Executed: a runtime fixture proves an enabled user plugin is not imported and an MCP child marker is not created; 58 broader isolation/session/plugin/MCP tests and 29 final focused tests pass; TypeScript clean.

items.title: Session environment output redaction — credentials never enter recordings
items.summary: The /env command correctly avoided echoing values when setting them, but listing the session environment printed every raw value. That output can flow through the terminal output recorder, persisting opaque API keys and credential-bearing URLs in asciicast artifacts.
items.done: Credential-semantic environment keys render as [REDACTED] regardless of value shape; non-secret keys remain useful; connection-string passwords, URL query credentials, auth headers, and known vendor tokens pass through structural/value redaction; the process-scoped store retains the original value only for child execution; environment and recorder regressions pass.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. TDD RED proved /env listed API_KEY=s3cr3t and a postgres user:pass URL verbatim. GREEN masks credential-key slots and applies the shared structural/vendor log redactor to remaining values before output. Executed: 93 env command/store, redaction, asciicast, and record-command tests pass; TypeScript clean. The store intentionally retains originals in memory for child process execution; this card governs durable output, not runtime secret use.

items.title: Desktop and gateway provider parity — user aliases keep complete identity
items.summary: User-declared providers resolved in the CLI, but the desktop model catalog exposed only built-ins and required a model on every switch. This hid aliases, prevented provider-only selection from using configured defaults, and wrote unvalidated provider/model state before resolution.
items.done: Desktop model options merge built-in and user-declared providers; a provider-only switch uses the alias configured model and key environment without carrying a prior model; resolution succeeds before disk/process mutation; the live setup and conversation receive the same provider; gateway task routing resolves the same alias and configured default; subagents use the shared identity-preserving override.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. TDD RED showed desktop alias discovery and provider-only selection were absent. GREEN merges providers.json aliases into desktop options, validates through resolveProvider before mutation, persists the resolved model, and updates both setup and conversation. A gateway routing characterization resolves the same alias/key/default model. Executed: final 35 provider/router/desktop/delegate/swarm tests pass; TypeScript clean.

items.title: Live context cost inspector — measure prompt surfaces before optimizing
items.summary: The Hermes cost-reduction story exposed that Vanta could describe likely prompt costs but had no tool for measuring the active turn. Existing budget and inspect_state tools report dollar limits and goals/approvals, not message or schema token surfaces.
items.done: A read-only inspect_context tool measures the active turn's messages and exposed tool schemas; reports estimated tokens, context utilization, role totals, largest message slots, and a deterministic top-three distinct surface ranking; never returns message content; remains universally scoped; the unchanged story produces an explicitly reviewed pass with no config mutation.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. Initial receipt 09-50-36-371Z failed because no measurement tool ran. Receipts 09-54-08-022Z and 09-55-21-158Z preserved manual failures for hypothetical and double-counted third sources. Passing receipt 09-56-40-765Z used inspect_context and ranked system_messages 13,110, tool_schemas 6,400, and user_messages 75 tokens exactly. Executed: 62 focused registry/tool/agent tests pass; TypeScript clean; 30-scenario manifest validates.

items.title: Workflow acceptance evidence contract — validate requested operations, not labels
items.summary: The Hermes enterprise Kubernetes story initially returned plausible prose without using the workflow validator, then treated a daily title as proof of an encoded schedule. Vanta needed a bounded contract that requires specialized validation and criterion-by-criterion evidence without weakening approval walls.
items.done: Explicit workflow drafting requests expose and invoke compose_workflow in validate mode before the final answer; no run or deploy occurs; the final maps every requested criterion to a validated node, instruction, or transition; titles and restated intent do not count as evidence; missing criteria are reported rather than inferred; the enterprise story records an explicitly reviewed pass covering schedule, isolation, secrets, health, rollback, and pre-deploy approval.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. Receipts 10-03-08-543Z and 10-06-38-102Z are preserved manual failures: the first omitted health/rollback evidence and the second represented daily scheduling only as title intent. Passing receipt 10-09-51-830Z invoked compose_workflow mode=validate and mapped a UTC cron schedule, namespace/pod isolation, external scoped secrets, observability, rollback, and approval-before-action to graph nodes. Executed: 17 focused contract/turn-loop/error-detection tests pass; the real CLI story passed review with no deployment or credential action.

items.title: Successful tool error classification — policy prose cannot open repair loops
items.summary: A valid workflow payload containing phrases such as rollback on failure and cannot deploy was counted as another tool failure even though compose_workflow succeeded, opening a false three-failure repair loop.
items.done: Successful tool outputs containing policy, rollback, or no-action prose do not increment consecutive error results; concrete error-shaped output such as Error prefixes, ENOENT, failed commands, and nonzero exit codes remains detectable for legacy tools; an end-to-end workflow run succeeds after two validation errors without emitting a false repair warning.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. RED was observed in receipt 10-06-38-102Z: two failed validation attempts followed by success still emitted the three-failure repair message because generic failure/cannot words were scanned anywhere in the successful payload. GREEN narrows fallback text detection to concrete error-shaped signatures. Executed: focused error-detect and turn-loop tests pass; receipt 10-09-51-830Z followed the same two-errors-then-success path with no false repair warning.

items.title: Portable skill packages - install references, templates, and scripts safely
items.summary: Hermes skills are directories, not just prompts: 174 current bundled/optional packages can include references, templates, scripts, platform metadata, and setup instructions. Vanta's first registry slice intentionally installs only SKILL.md, so most outcome packs cannot yet travel intact.
items.done: Registry inspect/install/update/remove handles a bounded package manifest plus SKILL.md; every path is containment-checked; file count and byte limits fail closed; executable files and setup commands are identified and scanned; preview lists every file, platform, dependency, capability, hash, and risk; confirmed install remains disabled in quarantine; approval copies the complete package atomically; updates diff companion files and preserve local edits; static fixtures prove references, templates, scripts, traversal refusal, and rollback.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. Extended the static registry schema and lifecycle from single SKILL.md files to bounded portable packages with up to 64 companion files, 512 KiB per companion, and 2 MiB total. Package paths are relative, unique, traversal-refusing, and exclude duplicate SKILL.md; every declared byte count and SHA-256 is verified. View/preview lists platforms, dependencies, capabilities, every file/hash/size, executable declarations, setup commands, and injection-scan findings. Install writes the complete package mode-0600 into disabled quarantine and never executes or enables declared scripts. Approval moves the whole package atomically. Doctor verifies every managed file. Updates diff changed companion paths, back up the complete old version plus metadata, replace clean packages through staging, and preserve locally edited package files while staging incoming versions. `vanta skills rollback <slug> <version> --yes` restores a verified prior package while backing up the displaced version; removal remains reversible. Executed real CLI package lifecycle against isolated VANTA_HOME: visible package/risk preview; install; non-executable script check; approve; doctor; companion update preview/apply; backup check; local companion edit conflict; incoming staged check; reversible remove. A separate real CLI run updated v1 to v2, rolled back to v1, and doctor returned ok. Focused registry/CLI/library tests: 12 passed. Full suite: 12,842 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed; TypeScript, touched lint, Docusaurus production build, 30-scenario use-case validation, 1,188-card validation, and diff checks pass. Runtime execution remains kernel-gated; multi-source discovery is the next separate card.

items.title: Multi-source skill hub - official, skills.sh, well-known, GitHub, and taps
items.summary: Hermes aggregates official packages, skills.sh, well-known endpoints, direct URLs, GitHub repositories, custom taps, and signed NVIDIA metadata. Vanta's static registry is a safe substrate, not ecosystem parity.
items.done: vanta skills browse/search/inspect accepts explicit source filters for official, skills.sh, well-known, URL, GitHub, and configured taps; results normalize provenance, versions, categories, signatures, integrity, and package metadata; cache expiry and offline behavior are visible; source failures are isolated; duplicate slugs require an explicit source; install always routes through the portable-package quarantine and approval contract; a credential-free integration fixture proves every resolver and a live read-only receipt proves at least two public sources.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. Added source-qualified browse/search/inspect/install for configured official registries, skills.sh, well-known endpoints, direct URLs, curated GitHub repositories, and removable custom GitHub taps. Results retain provenance, source identifier, declared version, category when supplied, literal signature state (unsigned or present-unverified), literal integrity state (source-declared, content-hashed, or unverified), and cache status plus expiry. Per-source one-hour caches expose stale-offline fallback; failures are isolated so one source cannot erase another source's results. Duplicate slugs require an explicit source identifier. skills.sh current API fields and alias-to-GitHub-directory resolution are supported through bounded frontmatter matching. GitHub packages are tree-discovered with category sidecars and signature presence; source and package paths are containment-checked. Provider fetches enforce the portable 65-total-file, 512-KiB-file, and 2-MiB-package limits before bulk download/materialization. Every source-qualified install generates a local hashed registry and routes through complete preview, disabled quarantine, separate approval, doctor, rollback, and reversible removal. Legacy `vanta skills hub <url>` is now preview-only and cannot directly activate a skill. Credential-free fixtures cover all six resolvers, cache expiry/offline fallback, failure isolation, duplicate-slug refusal, alias resolution, tap add/list/remove, oversized content/tree refusal, and package quarantine. Live read-only proofs: skills.sh returned current React catalog results with visible cache/provenance; GitHub inspection resolved OpenAI/skills `skill-creator` with seven package files. Real isolated live package path: previewed the public OpenAI package, installed disabled, confirmed scripts remained non-executable, approved, doctor returned ok, removed reversibly, and preserved companion scripts. Focused hub/registry tests: 18 passed. Full suite: 12,848 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed; TypeScript, touched lint, Docusaurus production build, 30-scenario use-case validation, 1,188-card validation, and diff checks pass. The Hub's indexed counts are not Vanta capability claims, and content hashing is not publisher authentication or safety endorsement.

items.title: Skill-write approval queue - review agent-authored changes before activation
items.summary: Vanta can generate skills and blocks post-turn side effects at unresolved choice walls, but it lacks Hermes's durable pending review flow for agent-authored create, patch, edit, supporting-file, and delete operations.
items.done: A setting can stage every agent-authored skill mutation; pending entries survive restart; list/diff/approve/reject works in CLI, TUI, and gateway-safe truncated views; full diffs remain locally inspectable; approval reruns injection, path, and capability checks before atomic apply; rejection makes no active change; agent-created guard scanning remains distinct from operator approval; receipts identify proposer, source session, files, decision, and resulting hash.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. Added `skills.writeApproval` plus `vanta skills approval on|off`; agent-authored `write_skill`, new `skill_manage`, and background self-improvement now share one durable mutation service. Actions cover create, edit, exact patch, contained supporting-file write/remove, and reversible whole-skill delete/archive. Explicit create refuses overwrite; existing `write_skill` automatically labels rewrites as edit. Pending mode writes mode-0600 proposals under `~/.vanta/pending/skills`; the active tree remains unchanged until approval. CLI and `/skills` support pending, full/compact diff, approve, reject, and setting status; slash diffs truncate safely and name the full local proposal. Each proposal binds to the target's base hash; approval refuses stale writes after operator/process edits.
