Approval reruns path containment, injection scan, size/schema, and requested-capability syntax checks; scanner refusal remains independent of human approval. Rejection changes no active file. Supporting-file removal moves content into `skill-write-removed`; deletion uses the existing reversible skill archive. Resolved proposals survive under `_resolved`; redacted JSONL receipts retain id/action/skill/session/reason/decision/timestamps/result hash without skill content. Executed isolated real path: enabled approval; real write_skill staged create; CLI pending/diff/approve; active file appeared; real skill_manage staged patch; direct operator edit changed base; approval failed stale; reject succeeded; skill_manage staged supporting-file write; approve created file; queue emptied; audit contained approved:create, rejected:patch, approved:write_file and no body text. Focused queue/tool/background/CLI/TUI/settings tests: 126 passed. Full suite: 12,858 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed; TypeScript, touched lint, Docusaurus production build, 30-scenario use-case validation, 1,188-card validation, and diff checks pass. This proves local queue behavior; it does not make unreviewed third-party skill content safe.

items.title: Media studio skill pack - generate and assemble image, video, audio, and 3D work
items.summary: Hermes ships ComfyUI, Manim, HyperFrames, Blender, TouchDesigner, ASCII video, music-prompting, and Kanban video-production recipes. Vanta currently understands images and video but has no native video-generation tool or verified end-to-end production workflow.
items.done: An outcome-led media command accepts a brief, previews scenes/providers/costs, and produces a playable verified artifact; provider adapters cover at least one local image path and one video path; a profile/Kanban production template can assign script, visual, audio, render, and review stages; generated assets stay in the scoped project; external generation and publishing remain approval/cost gated; ffprobe and artifact checks verify duration, dimensions, streams, and nonblank frames; receipts retain prompts, providers, costs, source assets, and checks without secrets.
items.notes: Shipped in ab572dcd. Real proofs: ffmpeg-local color brief produced playable H.264 640x360 2.0s MP4; project-image adapter produced 640x360 1.0s MP4. Receipts passed artifact bytes, video stream, dimensions, duration, and nonblank-frame checks. media_studio approval preview includes scenes/providers/$0 cost; vanta media-studio board created routed script/visual/audio/render/review Kanban dependencies. Full suite: 12,878 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed.

items.title: Payment skill pack - Stripe Link, MPP, and SaaS provisioning under transaction gates
items.summary: Hermes offers Stripe Link checkout, Machine Payments Protocol, and Stripe Projects provisioning. Vanta's browser and MCP layers can reach payment surfaces, but model-token budgets do not authorize purchases and generic approvals do not protect card material or merchant totals.
items.done: Payment actions use a dedicated transaction contract with merchant, item, currency, exact total, per-purchase and period caps, credential type, and expiry; every spend requires a fresh operator approval plus the provider's external approval when available; payment credentials never enter model context, logs, receipts, or durable environment files; HTTP 402 challenges are validated; denials/timeouts stop cleanly; SaaS provisioning stores only vault references; receipts prove authorization, redacted provider result, amount, and cleanup; live sandbox/test-mode Stripe and MPP paths pass before any real-money enablement.
items.notes: PARKED EXTERNAL PROOF 2026-07-11 only on external payment acceptance. Implemented strict environment:test transaction contracts, exact minor-unit totals, per-purchase/day/month caps, expiry, replay refusal, concurrent ledger reservations, mode-0600 redacted receipts, provider-output allowlisting, scrubbed child environments, Stripe Link external-approval enforcement, bounded HTTP 402 parsing/validation, payment_transaction tool, and vanta payments preview|execute|receipts. Fresh approvals cannot be auto-approved or persisted; CLI approval must equal the transaction ID. Production payment CLIs are unreachable unless explicit test wrappers are configured. Stripe Projects now runs in a mode-0700 temporary workspace, requires the generated .env key set to exactly equal approved vault aliases, removes plaintext before success, pipes values into macOS Keychain through stdin, atomically registers only Keychain references, and rolls back writes on failure. A real child-process fixture executed init/add, generated two credentials, vaulted both without argv/manifest leakage, and removed the workspace. The real Vanta Link CLI fixture also passed exact-ID approval -> reserved -> spend_approved -> authorized receipt with replay refusal. Full suite: 12,967 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed across 4,737 suites; TypeScript, 4-file size gate, npm audit (0 vulnerabilities), 30-scenario use-case validation, and Docusaurus production build pass. This proves Vanta orchestration, vault-only provisioning, and test-adapter isolation, NOT live payment. Read-only host check: Stripe CLI 1.31.1 is installed, but its Projects plugin is absent; link-cli, Keychain opt-in, test wrappers, and an MPP test URL are unconfigured. Remaining done-criterion blockers: live Stripe Link sandbox/test receipt and live paid MPP endpoint receipt; a live Stripe Projects account is also unverified but no longer a local implementation blocker. Real money stays disabled.

items.title: Shopify operations - scoped catalog, orders, inventory, and verified mutations
items.summary: Extract the independently buildable Shopify surface from the combined commerce/telephony card. Reads and mutation previews do not require Vanta's payment release gate; checkout still does.
items.done: A strict store profile names the myshopify domain, API version, vault credential alias, and explicit read/write scopes; products, orders, and inventory reads are bounded and redact customer data by default; typed product and inventory mutations preview the exact GraphQL operation and variables, require fresh approval, include required idempotency keys, reject undeclared scopes, execute once, inspect userErrors, and read the resulting object back; receipts retain operation, object IDs, hashes, scopes, idempotency key, and verification without tokens or customer secrets; a local HTTP fixture and a real Shopify development-store path both pass before shipping.
items.notes: PARKED EXTERNAL PROOF 2026-07-11 on one external acceptance path. Implemented strict myshopify/version/vault/scope profiles; fixed bounded products/orders/inventory documents; order reads omit customer name/email/phone/address/payment fields; vault token resolution uses exact shopify:<store>:read|write scopes; transport fixes HTTPS production endpoints, caps responses at 1 MiB, and never returns raw headers/provider errors. Typed product_update and inventory_set plans enforce expiry, scopes, exact preview, fresh non-cacheable approval, UUID idempotency, replay lock, Shopify userErrors stop, separate readback verification, and mode-0600 hash/object-ID receipts without credentials or customer data. Inventory uses Shopify's @idempotent directive. Added shopify_operations plus vanta shopify read|preview|apply|receipts. Executed a real local HTTP server fixture proving token-only request headers, bounded order read, typed mutation, userErrors stop, and a second verification request. Full suite: 12,929 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed; TypeScript, 8-file size gate, npm audit (0 vulnerabilities), 30-scenario use-case validation, and Docusaurus build pass. Missing external proof: no SHOPIFY_DEV_TOKEN vault alias is configured, so a real development-store read plus approved verified mutation could not be executed. Checkout/returns that move money remain outside this child and depend on the payment card.

items.title: Telephony consent lifecycle - provision numbers, SMS, calls, callbacks, and retention
items.summary: Turn the existing Twilio SMS transport into an operator workflow and add consented call/number lifecycle receipts, with later Vapi/Bland adapters behind the same contract.
items.done: A provider-neutral telephony contract supports number search/provisioning, SMS/MMS, and outbound calls; every recipient, purpose, consent source, allowed time window, maximum duration, recording choice, and retention policy is explicit; number purchases and sends/calls require fresh approval and idempotency; Twilio status and recording callbacks are authenticated, sequence-tolerant, and correlated into delivery/call receipts; recording is off by default and retained artifacts/transcripts follow deletion policy; existing SMS behavior is reused without exposing credentials; local callback fixtures plus live Twilio test-number/SMS/call paths pass, and Vapi/Bland remain optional adapters rather than core assumptions.
items.notes: PARKED EXTERNAL PROOF 2026-07-11 only on live Twilio acceptance. Implemented strict environment:test Twilio profiles and provider-neutral number/SMS/call contracts with E.164 target, purpose, consent source/timestamps, allowed window, expiry, scope, UUID idempotency, bounded call duration, explicit recording/disclosure choice, callback URL, and retention. Reused the shipped SMS buildSmsForm path; added safe number search, exact number provisioning, generated XML-escaped TwiML, progress/recording callbacks, fresh non-cacheable approval, exact CLI action-ID confirmation, replay lock, and test API base restricted before credential resolution to localhost or Twilio-owned hosts. Authenticated callback service verifies the full URL/all fields using Twilio's HMAC-SHA1 algorithm and constant-time compare; test matches Twilio's published vector. Message/call/recording events correlate by account/provider ID and terminal states survive late out-of-order events. Mode-0600 receipts hash recipient/purpose/content. `vanta telephony ingress` now mounts a bounded 64 KiB form-only /twilio listener with fixed HTTPS public signature URL; serialized retention deletes due provider recordings through the scoped voice credential before pruning local receipts and preserves retry evidence on provider failure. Added telephony_workflow plus vanta telephony search|preview|execute|receipts|prune|ingress. Real local HTTP fixtures executed number search, SMS, call, number-provision, ingress, callback, recording-delete, and failed-retention paths while proving request-only Basic Auth and redacted outputs. Full suite: 12,961 passed, 3 skipped, 0 failed; TypeScript, 8-file size gate, npm audit (0 vulnerabilities), 30-scenario use-case validation, and Docusaurus build pass. Remaining external proof: no TWILIO_TEST_TOKEN vault alias or VANTA_TELEPHONY_TEST_API_BASE is configured, so the live Twilio test-number/SMS/call/callback/recording-retention receipt cannot run. Vapi/Bland remain optional follow-ups behind the shared contract.

items.title: Commerce and telephony skill pack - Shopify, shopping, returns, SMS, and calls
items.summary: Hermes packages shopping, Shopify administration, and Twilio/Vapi/Bland telephony as user-visible workflows. Vanta has an SMS adapter but not storefront operations, checkout/returns, number provisioning, or AI-call lifecycle receipts.

Resume this parent only after those three parked children ship. Reuse the existing Twilio SMS transport, but do not treat channel delivery as telephony workflow proof.

items.title: Hermes multi-turn story runner — continue choices and approvals in one session
items.summary: The current use-case runner executes one-shot commands. It can prove that Vanta stops at a clarification wall, but it cannot send a scripted operator choice back into the same conversation and verify the resulting plan, approval gate, artifact, or delivery across later turns.
items.done: Scenario manifests can declare ordered operator replies and expected turn boundaries; the runner drives one persistent Vanta session, waits for each completed turn, sends the next scripted reply, and exits cleanly; receipts preserve redacted per-turn outputs, tools, guards, and outcome checks; clarification, approval, resume, and reject branches have deterministic fixtures; CI runs offline multi-turn cases without a TTY race.
items.notes:
Captured from executed receipt 09-49-48-049Z. The first turn correctly asked one outcome-defining question and stopped with zero forbidden side effects, but a complete converse-before-act job requires the operator answer and plan-approval turn in the same session. Build this as a harness extension, not by weakening the agent's choice wall.

SHIPPED 2026-07-11: added a pure scripted-turn orchestrator, production persistent-conversation adapter, and `vanta story-eval --manifest --id --out` command. Manifests declare `firstTurn` plus ordered operator replies, boundary type, required semantic checks, and per-turn forbidden patterns. Each `send()` resolves before the next reply; no TTY/prompt parsing is used. Receipts retain redacted input/output, tools, stop reason, iterations, missing checks, guard hits, and boundary verdict per turn. Kernel approval is granted only when the active scripted reply begins with explicit approve; brain learning is frozen during the eval. The root `scripts/usecase-eval.mjs` delegates scripted scenarios to this command and merges the receipt into normal history. `--verify-receipt` can recompute multi-turn verdicts from saved outputs after contract calibration without rerunning the model. Deterministic tests cover clarification, approval, resume, reject, guard failure, CLI receipt writing, and retro-verification. Real path: `dev-converse-before-act` ran one persistent four-turn session (clarification -> scope clarification -> approval wall -> reject), exited cleanly, used no file/shell tools, changed no files, and the saved 12-37-41-278Z receipt re-verified with all four boundaries and guards passing.

items.title: External proof readiness - one release status for all parked gates
items.summary: Replace repeated manual audits with one machine-readable readiness report over every remaining external proof.
items.done: `vanta roadmap proof-status [--json]` evaluates all ten external cards from canonical receipts, returns nonzero while any gate is missing, rejects fixture-only provider ledgers without an external acceptance packet, prevents spreadsheet receipt path traversal, and reports exact next actions. `vanta roadmap proof-packet [--json]` emits the same ten-gate handoff packet but exits 0 while receipts are missing. `vanta roadmap proof-accept <card-id>` and `--all-ready` ship only ready canonical gates, require shipped dependencies, retain refusal behavior for missing receipts, record accepted evidence in card notes, and regenerate the roadmap. Serverless readiness refuses impossible arm/prove guidance when Telegram setup is invalid. `vanta roadmap status --open --actionable` excludes parked external-proof cards so local queues can drain without hiding release blockers. `vanta roadmap proof-template <card-id> [receipt-event-id...]` prints valid external acceptance packet skeletons for packet-based commerce/telephony gates.
items.notes:
SHIPPED 2026-07-11. Added a ten-gate aggregate over Modal/Telegram, Teams, physical Termux, Run Anywhere, Excel/Sheets, Windows service, Stripe Link/MPP, Shopify, Twilio, and the commerce parent. Provider candidates require a versioned external-test packet binding exact receipt event IDs and a redacted evidence hash. The real current-repo command exits 1 at 0/10 and correctly labels existing local Stripe Link events as candidates with the external packet missing. Focused evaluator/CLI tests, TypeScript, and size/complexity gates pass.

FOLLOW-UP 2026-07-13: added guarded `vanta roadmap proof-accept <card-id>` and `--all-ready`. Acceptance is limited to the same canonical readiness gates, requires parked reason `external proof`, enforces shipped `after` dependencies, writes the accepted receipt/evidence into card notes, and leaves roadmap state unchanged on missing proof. `roadmap move --force` remains unable to bypass parked proof acceptance. Focused proof covers missing-receipt refusal, accepted evidence metadata, aggregate dependency refusal, topological child-before-parent acceptance, noncanonical-card refusal, and CLI JSON/exit behavior.

PROOF PACKET 2026-07-14: added `vanta roadmap proof-packet [--json]` as the non-failing handoff companion to `proof-status`. It reads the same canonical ten-gate readiness report and prints receipt paths, evidence, and next actions, but exits 0 so agents can attach the packet to a task without weakening the hard release gate. `proof-status` still exits nonzero while receipts are absent, and parked proof cards remain unshipped.

FOLLOW-UP 2026-07-14: proof-packet/serverless readiness now mirrors gateway setup state before suggesting external proof. If the Telegram token is missing or malformed, the packet names that fix first and withholds arm/send/prove actions that cannot succeed. Live packet evidence now reports the deployed endpoint plus `Telegram token invalid-format; webhook secret present`, with next actions limited to status, token replacement, and webhook registration.

FOLLOW-UP 2026-07-14: roadmap status now separates local-actionable work from external-proof waiting work. Parked external-proof cards remain open and keep `--require-complete` failing, but `vanta roadmap status --open --actionable --json` returns an empty list when only credential/device/account proofs remain. Live current-repo output: activeTotal 0, openTotal 10, actionableOpenTotal 0.

FOLLOW-UP 2026-07-14: added `vanta roadmap proof-template <card-id> [receipt-event-id...] [--json]` for `HERMES-PAYMENT-SKILL-PACK`, `HERMES-SHOPIFY-OPERATIONS`, and `HERMES-TELEPHONY-CONSENT-LIFECYCLE`. It prints the exact `.vanta/external-proofs/<CARD>.json` acceptance-packet skeleton with version, ok, roadmapCardId, environment, executedAt, redacted evidence hash placeholder, and receiptEventIds so operators do not hand-write malformed proof packets.

items.title: FileChanged watcher drain-on-close - no hook writes after teardown
items.summary: Make session shutdown wait for already-dispatched FileChanged hooks after stopping new filesystem events.
items.done: The watcher close contract is awaitable; it closes the OS watcher, drains every in-flight hook with settled results, and interactive/one-shot callers await it; full-suite temporary workspace teardown no longer races a hook write.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. The current-HEAD full suite exposed ENOTEMPTY in FileChanged hook teardown. The watcher now tracks dispatched promises and close awaits Promise.allSettled after watcher.close. Interactive and one-shot lifecycles await shutdown; TUI cleanup starts the async drain. Hook suites (188 tests), focused file-watch/one-shot tests, TypeScript, and touched-file size gates pass. Full-suite rerun remains the release check.

items.title: Local store Git commits settle before teardown - disable invocation auto-maintenance
items.summary: Prevent Git auto-GC or maintenance from mutating a Vanta home after commitInHome has returned.
items.done: Vanta home commits disable auto-GC and auto-maintenance for that invocation; bundled library installs can be removed immediately after return without ENOTEMPTY; explicit/manual maintenance remains available.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-11. A current-HEAD full suite exposed Git background maintenance racing temporary VANTA_HOME removal after a 169-skill batch commit. commitInHome now invokes git with gc.auto=0 and maintenance.auto=false. The real bundled-library plus store suite passed three consecutive install/teardown runs; TypeScript and size gates pass. Full-suite rerun remains the release check.

items.title: Interrupted tool effects - preserve unknown mutations and inspect before retry
items.summary: Hermes current delta (a0a6cd80) distinguishes a tool that definitely did nothing from a side-effecting tool interrupted after dispatch whose outcome is unknown. Vanta avoids automatic retries for known mutators, but an exception, timeout, process death, or dangling tool tail is not durably classified; session recovery can therefore lose the fact that a mutation may already have landed. Add Vanta-native effect disposition to the canonical tool result and recovery path.
items.done: Every tool attempt records and persists an effect disposition of none, confirmed, or unknown; pre-execution denials and cancelled-not-started calls are none; completed results are confirmed; interrupted/timed-out/dangling effect-capable calls are unknown; unknown/plugin/MCP tools fail toward effect-capable. Session restore preserves the assistant call plus a synthetic result that tells the model to inspect state before retrying. A real crash/interrupt fixture around a mutating tool proves Vanta neither erases the attempt nor blindly repeats it.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-12. Tool calls persist pending immediately after the assistant call and started at the final pre-execution boundary. Canonical results carry none, confirmed, or unknown; restore repairs dangling effect-capable calls with inspect-before-retry guidance. The production turn-loop crash fixture executes a mutation once, observes post-effect failure, proves no blind retry, restores the unknown receipt, and verifies the metadata journal excludes arguments and output. Full suite: 1,325 files / 13,012 passed / 3 skipped; TypeScript clean.

items.title: Compaction real-headroom guard - stop when the prompt floor stays over threshold
items.summary: Hermes current delta (32f30d2a through 83000c72) fixed a compaction loop where message text shrank but the incompressible system prompt and tool schemas still left real provider usage over threshold. Vanta anti-thrash currently scores estimated before/after message savings, so it can judge a rewrite healthy while the actual prompt still cannot fit. Judge compaction effectiveness from the next real provider prompt count and treat no-op boundaries as failed progress.
items.done: After a compaction boundary, the next provider-reported prompt count decides whether headroom was restored. Two compactions that leave real prompt usage at or above the trigger suppress further automatic compaction for that episode; a no-op/too-short compaction counts as ineffective; only a real below-threshold reading or a fresh session resets the strike. Providers without usage retain the existing low-savings fallback. Tests reproduce a large system/tool-schema floor with shrinking messages and prove the third pass is skipped without blocking manual focused compaction.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-12. Changed automatic passes await the next real provider input count; two at/above-trigger readings suppress the third pass, no-op boundaries strike immediately, and only a positive below-trigger reading or fresh conversation resets the episode. Missing usage preserves the estimated low-savings fallback. Executed fixtures reproduce an invisible fixed prompt floor, zero-usage ambiguity, third-pass suppression, and focused manual /compact after suppression. Full suite: 1,325 files / 13,017 passed / 3 skipped; TypeScript clean.

items.title: Usage route ledger - attribute every model, fallback, and included call
items.summary: Hermes current delta (cb7f6bbb through 0d63c23f) persists usage by the model/provider/base route active for each API call, including mid-session switches, fallbacks, subscription-included calls, and zero-cost routes. Vanta has a pure CostDetail helper and a positive-cost spend ledger, but CostDetail is only imported by tests and recordTurnSpend drops null/zero-cost calls. Wire a durable all-call ledger so usage remains truthful across routing.
items.done: Every completed provider call writes a durable per-session route row with model, provider, normalized base route identity, billing mode/status, API call count, input/output/cache/reasoning tokens, and estimated/actual cost when known. Local and subscription-included calls are retained with zero cost instead of dropped. /usage breakdown and the dashboard show mid-session switches and fallback calls under the route that actually served them. Existing sessions migrate without double counting, and an executed fixture switches model, falls back once, and reconciles totals exactly.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-12. Versioned route rows are persisted at the completed agent-loop call boundary with actual served provider/model/base route, fallback depth, billing mode/status, call count, input/output/cache/reasoning tokens, and known/unknown/zero cost. /usage breakdown prefers call rows and falls back to legacy turn spend without summing both; /dashboard renders serving routes. The production fixture fails primary, serves an included fallback, switches model, and reconciles exactly to 2 calls / 150 in / 30 out / 10 cached / 5 reasoning. Full suite: 1,328 files / 13,025 passed / 3 skipped; TypeScript clean.

items.title: Session-scoped model switching - change this conversation without changing the default
items.summary: Hermes current delta (ce5c1f9f, aac77f16) keeps desktop/TUI picker changes session-scoped. Vanta hot-swaps correctly but every typed /model selection sets persistGlobal=true and writes .env, so experimenting in one concurrent session silently changes the next launch and other surfaces. Add explicit session/global scope with a session-safe picker default.
items.done: /model <provider> <model> --session changes only the current conversation and survives that session resume; --global updates the configured default; the TUI/desktop picker defaults to session scope and clearly offers Set as default. Concurrent sessions can use different providers without process.env or .env bleed. A production-path test opens two sessions, switches one, resumes both, and proves the global default is unchanged.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-07-12. Session JSON carries optional providerId/modelId; typed /model, TUI rows, and desktop picker default to session scope, while --global/Set as default is the only .env mutation path. Resume rebuilds the saved provider from a copied env, and old sessions adopt the configured default on next save. The production fixture opens two sessions, switches one, saves and resumes both on distinct models, and proves global env plus .env are unchanged. Desktop production build passed. Full suite: 1,329 files / 13,029 passed / 3 skipped; TypeScript clean.

items.title: Remote context references - safe @file, @diff, and @git expansion through the gateway
items.summary: Hermes current delta (4df6e628 through 4281151a) repaired @ context references on gateway messages and scoped expansion to the routed profile and effective model budget. Vanta HERMES-CONTEXT-REFS-V2 executes through the TUI composer only; inbound Telegram/Slack/etc. messages bypass ui/use-submit.ts and cannot use the same bounded references. Extend the feature to remote work without widening file scope.
items.done: An allowlisted inbound gateway message can use the same @file ranges, @folder, @diff, @staged, @git:N, and @url syntax as the local composer. Expansion runs under the message session profile/project root, enforces the same sensitive/binary/root limits, derives the total budget from the actual routed model, and sends source/warning receipts back through the channel. Queued messages retain their original profile/session scope. Tests prove cross-profile and out-of-root references cannot bleed, followed by one live configured-channel round trip.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12: extracted surface-neutral context preprocessing shared by the local composer and gateway. Allowlisted inbound messages expand @file ranges, @folder, @diff, @staged, @git:N, and @url before queueing under the received message's root/profile identity and routed-model character budget. The existing hardened expander still enforces root, sensitive-file, binary, per-reference, and aggregate limits; source and warning receipts return through the channel, and media turns preserve the expanded block. Executed proof: a configured production WebChat adapter round trip exercised the full grammar; a queued multi-profile fixture proved profile A/B isolation and refused an out-of-root read. Focused gate: 9 files / 88 tests passed. Full suite: 1,331 files / 13,033 tests passed / 3 skipped. TypeScript, git diff check, and the production Docusaurus build passed.

items.title: Authenticated runtime readiness - bounded health for remote supervision
items.summary: Hermes current delta (f9728af5, 6142203b) adds an authenticated detailed readiness surface over config, state DB, model, disk, gateway, active runs, process completions, and delegations while keeping public liveness cheap. Vanta Public API /status initializes a live conversation and returns only kernel/model/tool/session/goals, so a remote supervisor cannot distinguish liveness from degraded readiness without shell access.
items.done: The public API exposes a cheap liveness route that performs no setup or mutation and an authenticated readiness route with bounded checks for kernel, provider/config, state stores, disk pressure, gateway/channel state, active turns, background completions, and delegated workers. Responses expose status/counts only - no secrets, config values, paths, commands, payloads, or raw errors. Degraded readiness remains a successful transport response with explicit status. Integration tests prove auth, non-mutation, redaction, corrupt-store degradation, low-disk injection, and queue counts.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12: extended the existing public API/desktop server with unauthenticated GET /api/v1/live and bearer-authenticated GET /api/v1/readiness plus the /status compatibility alias. Probe routing occurs before session allocation; liveness performs no setup/auth/store work, and readiness verifies tokens without touching lastUsedAt. The bounded collector caps kernel wait time, file count, and bytes per file, then returns only status enums and counts for kernel, provider config, state stores, disk, fresh gateway/channel snapshots, active turns, background tasks/completions, and delegated workers. Gateway ticks persist status-only snapshots with raw channel errors removed. Executed HTTP proof covered auth rejection/acceptance, zero session allocation, zero token-store mutation, corrupt-store and low-disk degradation, configured-but-unobserved channel degradation, queue counts, and response redaction. Focused gate: 7 files / 26 tests passed. The Teams JWT tamper fixture was made deterministic and passed 20/20 repeated runs. Full suite: 1,333 files / 13,038 tests passed / 3 skipped. TypeScript, SDK build, desktop production build, Docusaurus production build, file-size limits, and git diff check passed.

items.title: System prompt presets - switch Vanta's operating role per session
items.summary: Let the operator select a named system-prompt preset for the current Vanta conversation without editing source files or weakening the kernel boundary. Reuse the existing markdown agent-definition format so prompts are portable, inspectable, and shared with spawned workers.
items.done: Named prompt definitions are discovered from the project and Vanta home; /prompt list|show|use|reset switches a bounded prompt overlay in the live session; switching preserves Vanta's base safety and kernel instructions; invalid or missing presets fail as values; focused tests prove switching, replacement, reset, and project-over-user precedence.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12. /prompt list|show|use|reset loads project .vanta/agents, compatible .claude/agents, then Vanta-home agents. Marker-bounded replacement preserves the base prompt and other live additions; empty, oversized, multiline-name, and reserved-marker definitions are refused. Focused command/preset tests and the full TypeScript suite pass.

items.title: Prompt-routed agent spawn - launch workers with different operating prompts
items.summary: Allow delegate and subagent spawn to select a built-in or operator-defined agent type whose prompt, tool allowlist, and optional model shape that worker while every action remains kernel-gated.
items.done: The delegate tool accepts agent_type; built-in and markdown-defined agent types resolve through one loader; the resolved prompt is appended to the worker's immutable Vanta base prompt, its tool list only narrows available tools, and its optional model override is honored unless the call explicitly chooses a model. Tests execute a worker and inspect its persisted sidechain system message, prove tool narrowing and precedence, and prove unknown types fail closed to the documented general-purpose default.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12. delegate accepts agent_type and resolves the same built-in/custom definitions. Prompt overlays remain subordinate to the Vanta base prompt; tool declarations only narrow the present registry, including tools mounted later by MCP; explicit delegate.model overrides a definition default. An executed spawn test inspected the persisted sidechain system message and agentType, focused routing tests pass, and the final full suite passed 1324 files / 13004 tests.

items.title: Needs-human queue — one actionable ticket instead of repeated failure
items.summary: When Vanta reaches a missing-tool, repeated-failure, or explicit human-decision boundary, create or update one deduplicated internal ticket with the reason and next action rather than retrying or losing the blocker.
items.done: A repeated-failure run creates exactly one unread needs-human ticket; the same blocker increments that ticket instead of duplicating it; the agent can explicitly queue an unclear decision; and the operator can list and resolve the queue from the CLI.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12. The shared conversation path detects explicit missing-tool, permission, decision, and repeated-failure boundaries; fingerprints the blocker; and creates or updates one unread operator ticket with a reason and next action. The tickets tool can queue a needs-human decision explicitly, while vanta maintenance queue lists and resolves it. Executed proof: a real conversation turn created one ticket, repeated occurrence deduplicated it, and ordinary questions did not queue. Focused tests and the final full suite passed 1,338 files / 13,055 tests / 3 skipped.

items.title: Documentation router health — measure loads, use, staleness, and contradictions
items.summary: Context documentation currently loads without an evidence trail. Record what is loaded or imported, track later references, flag stale and conflicting instructions, and surface never-consulted files before any self-heal rewrites them.
items.done: Real prompt assembly records top-level and imported context files; completed turns record file references; vanta maintenance docs reports loaded, referenced, never-consulted, stale, missing, and contradictory documents with evidence and no writes.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12. Prompt assembly records top-level and recursively imported context documents, completed turns record explicit document references, and vanta maintenance docs reports loads, references, stale and never-consulted files, missing imports, and exact opposite instructions without rewriting source documents. Executed proof: a real AGENTS.md to docs/INDEX.md prompt import and five conversation turns produced measured load/reference evidence. Focused tests and the final full suite passed 1,338 files / 13,055 tests / 3 skipped.

items.title: Maintenance budget — keep meta-work from competing with delivery
items.summary: Record each turn as delivery or maintenance with elapsed time and tokens, report the ratio over a selected window, and raise one deduplicated needs-human ticket when maintenance dominates a meaningful sample.
items.done: Each real agent turn records work class, elapsed time, token use, and outcome; vanta maintenance budget reports delivery versus maintenance; override and threshold controls are documented; and a >=5-turn over-threshold fixture creates one actionable warning ticket without duplicates.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12. Every identified real agent turn records a task hash, conservative delivery/maintenance class, elapsed time, tokens, tool iterations, and stop reason. vanta maintenance budget exposes text/JSON reports, threshold and time-window controls, CI enforcement, and VANTA_WORK_CLASS overrides. Executed proof: five real maintenance turns crossed the 60 percent budget and created exactly one deduplicated needs-human ticket with a concrete next action. TypeScript, file-size limits, Docusaurus production build, focused tests, and the full suite passed 1,338 files / 13,055 tests / 3 skipped.

items.title: Desktop app v1 — dependable native Vanta for daily use
items.summary: Turn the existing React and Electron shell into a production-grade desktop application with reliable startup, project ownership, complete connection/error/empty states, keyboard-accessible navigation, and distributable macOS artifacts.
items.done: A clean machine artifact installs and launches without a source checkout; project selection persists; the primary chat/session/model/approval/rail workflows remain usable across desktop widths; startup failures are actionable; native smoke, UI integration tests, packaged artifact inspection, and visual screenshots pass.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12. Rebuilt the React/Electron surface as a fixed one-viewport workspace: the outer document never scrolls, while only genuine session/chat/inspector content does. Added searchable sessions, prompt starters, explicit loading/recovery, command/model/setup dialogs, safer approvals, compact panel navigation with close controls, resilient API parsing, and in-app provider/model/API-key setup stored privately in project .vanta/.env. The native host now persists and switches project roots, selects a free port, shows a startup splash, handles single-instance/menu/tray lifecycle, bundles renderer/TypeScript runtime/Rust kernel paths, unpacks executable transform dependencies, and produces ARM64 app/DMG/ZIP artifacts. Executed proof: focused desktop/setup gates, TypeScript, and size gates passed; the full suite passed 1,339 files / 13,059 tests / 3 skipped; Playwright at 1440x960 and 760x700 measured html/body/shell exactly one viewport with no outer scroll; signed app passed strict codesign verification; DMG checksum verified; the root-level Vanta.app launched directly from the mounted DMG against an empty temporary project with no Vanta checkout and reported renderer assets ready, first-run setup ready, and bundled kernel online. Artifacts: 176 MiB DMG and 176 MiB ZIP. Public Apple notarization is captured separately as DESKTOP-MAC-NOTARIZATION because notary credentials are not configured.

items.title: Desktop viewport composer fix — keep the empty state and input visible
items.summary: Harden the desktop conversation layout so startup, recovery, and empty-session states cannot create an implicit grid row that pushes the composer below the native window.
items.done: The packaged renderer keeps the shell at one viewport and the composer fully visible at the reported wide/tall window size, in normal and connection-error states; visual bounds checks and the signed app smoke pass.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12. The workbench now always owns exactly three rows: header, one bounded conversation stage, and composer. Loading and connection-recovery content live inside the conversation stage, so an error banner cannot create an implicit row below the native viewport. Added desktop:layout:smoke with healthy and forced-error bounds assertions and packaged-app support. Executed proof against both the rebuilt signed ARM64 Vanta.app and the Vanta.app launched directly from the mounted DMG at the reported 1778x1136 content viewport: document/shell stayed 1136px high; conversation ended and composer began at 1002.15625px; composer ended exactly at 1136px in both states. TypeScript, file-size gate, 24 focused desktop tests, and the full 1,339-file / 13,059-test suite passed; strict codesign and DMG checksum verification passed.

items.title: Desktop Files panel responsive fix — restore rows and contain long paths
items.summary: Keep the inspector's two-row grid when it opens as a narrow overlay, and constrain file paths to stable single-line rows without horizontal overflow.
items.done: At the reported narrow inspector width, Files keeps tabs above content, every filename row remains readable, long paths ellipsize, only the file list scrolls vertically, and the packaged-app responsive bounds smoke passes.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12. Fixed the compact inspector selector that replaced the right rail's required two-row grid with a horizontal flex layout below 1080px. Files now keeps an explicit header/list grid, hides horizontal overflow, gives each path a stable 28px row, ellipsizes long paths, and exposes the full path as a native title tooltip. Extended desktop:layout:smoke with 220 long-path fixtures at 760x900. Executed against source, rebuilt signed ARM64 Vanta.app, and Vanta.app launched from the mounted DMG: inspector display grid; tabs bottom 71.5px; panel top 83.5px; panel client/scroll widths 391/391; list widths 388/388; sampled rows all 28px. TypeScript, size gate, 24 focused desktop tests, full 1,339-file / 13,059-test suite, strict codesign, and DMG checksum passed.

items.title: Desktop session management — rename, archive, restore, and delete conversations
items.summary: Give each desktop conversation a compact, keyboard-accessible management menu so the left session rail can be kept useful without losing recoverable history.
items.done: The desktop sidebar can rename a session inline, archive and restore it without changing its transcript or routing metadata, and permanently delete it only after an explicit confirmation. Loopback mutation routes reject invalid requests, the active-session path resets safely, focused persistence and HTTP tests pass, and a native interaction smoke proves the controls are usable.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-13. The sidebar now provides a compact per-session action control with inline rename, reversible archive/restore, and confirmed permanent deletion. Persisted archive state preserves the complete transcript plus project/provider/model metadata; normal session listings exclude archived conversations while the desktop API returns them for the restore disclosure. Added loopback POST routes for rename, archive, and delete with request validation, and deleting the active persisted session clears runtime state before a new session is created. Executed proof: 20 focused persistence/API/desktop tests passed; TypeScript and production renderer build passed; `npm run desktop:sessions:smoke` launched Electron and completed rename, archive, restore, and delete through the rendered UI. The rebuilt ARM64 `release/mac-arm64/Vanta.app` also passed strict Developer ID codesign verification and the same packaged-app smoke.

items.title: Managed runtime installer — one command provisions CLI and optional desktop from the same Vanta home
items.summary: Adopt the useful Hermes installer shape without importing its Python stack: a source-agnostic bootstrap clones or updates a managed Vanta runtime, delegates to Vanta's existing verified setup, installs the global CLI, and can build the Electron desktop app from that exact runtime.
items.done: Running the public installer from outside a checkout provisions a clean managed runtime under VANTA_HOME, preserves an existing dirty managed checkout instead of overwriting it, records the runtime path, installs a usable global vanta command, and optionally builds/launches desktop from the same runtime. The source-checkout installer remains supported. Shell fixtures prove clean install, idempotent rerun, dirty-checkout protection, and no-project bootstrap routing; native desktop smoke proves the optional app artifact uses that runtime.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-13. Added a source-agnostic public bootstrap that clones atomically into VANTA_HOME/app, updates only clean managed checkouts, preserves dirty ones, delegates to the existing verified installer, records the runtime, and installs the global launcher. `--desktop` packages from that same runtime; desktop packaging now reuses the verified runtime kernel before requiring Cargo. The desktop sidebar now retains loaded sessions even when provider status fails, so chats remain manageable during setup. Executed proof: 5 managed-installer fixtures cover clean provision, clean update, dirty protection, and standalone raw-installer routing; the real isolated managed CLI smoke and source-checkout smoke passed; the managed desktop smoke packaged and launched Vanta.app and completed rename, archive, restore, and delete. TypeScript passed; full suite passed 1,342 files / 13,075 tests (3 skipped).

items.title: Desktop macOS notarization — staple a Gatekeeper-clean public build
items.summary: Submit the signed desktop DMG to Apple, wait for acceptance, staple the ticket, and prove Gatekeeper acceptance on a quarantined clean Mac before publishing a public download.
items.done: Apple notarytool accepts the exact release DMG; stapler validate passes; spctl accepts the installed app after quarantine on a separate Mac; the published checksum matches the notarized artifact.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-07-12. Added npm run desktop:release: build and Developer ID-sign the app, build ZIP/DMG, sign the DMG container, submit with the saved notarytool Keychain profile, wait for Apple, staple, validate, and require spctl source=Notarized Developer ID. The first manual submission exposed that an accepted app inside an unsigned DMG still fails Gatekeeper; the automated path fixes that order. Executed release submission 82513167-9ee2-451e-896a-bde16e21ea15 returned Accepted. Public v0.8.0 DMG: https://github.com/jpoindexter/Vanta/releases/download/v0.8.0/Vanta-0.8.0-arm64.dmg; SHA-256 aa39b49307c5fe70fb197c2bba6fd4caa7695d384c2317ca7448a043d40e85b6, matching GitHub's server digest and the published .sha256 asset. Fresh separate macos-14 GitHub-hosted runner proof https://github.com/jpoindexter/Vanta/actions/runs/29208066598 passed checksum, stapler validate, DMG codesign, DMG Gatekeeper, mounted install, quarantine attribute, deep app codesign, and installed-app Gatekeeper; both assessments reported Notarized Developer ID.
